volume.h1:
TEXTS
OF
SOUTH-INDIAN INSCRIPTIONS
VOL. IX PART II

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1913.)
ON THE PEDESTAL OF A JAINA IMAGE KEPT IN THE TALUK OFFICE
AT RAYADRUG, RAYADRUG TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record which is in Sanskrit verse is damaged and dated Śaka 1277,
Manmatha, Mārgaśira, pūrṇimā, Bhṛigu[dina] corresponding to A.D. 1355 November 20
Friday. It refers itself to the reign of the Vijayanagara king Harihara and
records that, by the command of the king, Bhōgarāja set up the god Ananta-Jinēś-
vara. His preceptor was Māghaṇandi, disciple of Amarakīrtti of the Nandi-saṅgha,
Balātkāra-gaṇa, Sārasvata-gachchha and Kundakund-ānvaya.

l.ka:
śrīmanma[nma]thavatsarē [girinaga]dvaṁdvēṁdu • • • • • māsē mārgga[śirā]bhidhē
bhṛgu • • • satpūrṇnamāyāṁ tithau | rājyaṁ śāsati [rāyadurga]nagarē
• • • • • • • • • • nāthaḥ svāṁgasamudbhavē hariharekṣmāpē

tadājñāvaśāt || śrīmūlasaṁghējani naṁdi[saṁ]ghastasminbalātkāragaṇōtiramyaḥ gacchētha
sārasvatanāmadhēyē [śrī]kuṁdakuṁdā[nvaya]vārddhi [caṁdraḥ] || ·ācāryyāmara-
kīrttisaṁ[ya]mi-

[sutaḥ] śrīmāghaṇaṁdivratī siddhāṁtābdhisudhākarō gururabhūdyasyaiṣa dākṣiṇyabhūḥ |
[māḷā]yi[priyarāmi]seṭṭitanayaḥ śrībhōgarājō vyadhātpūtānaṁta-

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(A.R. No. 402 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD NEAR KAIYANI, A HAMLET OF SANKARANARAYANA,
COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1278, Durmukhi, Māgha, śu. 10, Ma[ṅgaḷavāra]
corresponding to A.D. 1357 January 31 Tuesday, in the reign of Vīra-Bukkaṇṇa-
Voḍeya. It records that, under the orders of the king, his Mahāpradhāna
Maleya-Daṇṇāyaka made a gift of a new land, the old grant having been lost,
for the service of the god Śaṅkaranārāyaṇadēva.

l.ka: mūlastaṁbhāya saṁbhavē || śrīmatu sakavaruṣa 1278 neya dur(ṁ)mukhisaṁva-
tsarada māgha śu 1[0] ma śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvaraṁ ·arirāyavibhāḍa [bhāṣege tappuvarā]-

rāyaṇadēvara ·amṛtapaḍige

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(A.R. No. 371 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE SOUTH VERANDAH OF THE INNER PRAKARA OF THE
KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, A SUBURB OF COONDAPUR, COONDAPUR
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1279, Hēmaḷambi, Āshāḍha, ba. 5, Śu[kravāra], corresponding
to A.D. 1357 July 7 Friday, in the reign of Bukkaṇṇa-Voḍeya. It records that
Maleya-Daṇṇāyaka's minister Ravaḷadēva, son of Sabutiya-Nāyaka, and grandson
of Mādarasaya, purchased a bāḷu (field) from Vishṇu-Tiruḷa and granted it for the
service of the god Kōṭēśvaradēva.

l.note: The rest of the inscription is built in.

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(A.R. No. 374 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE WEST SIDE OF VOLAGA-MANDAPA IN THE
SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1287, Krōdhin, Bhādrapada, ba. 6, Sō[mavāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1364 August 19 Monday (Adhika-Bhādrapada), in the reign of
Bukkaṇṇa-Voḍeya. It records that, when the Mahāpradhāna Malliya-Daṇṇāyaka
was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya, the thirty families of the village Chakkare,
and others, made a gift of 12 honnus and plots of dry and wet fields (specified),
for the service of the god Kōṭīśvara.

l.ka: krōdhisaṁvatsarada bhādrapada ba 6 sō dalu śrīmanma-
gadde mū 8 ha 30 makkiya gadde mū 3 ha 30
su bhaṁḍāri tā koṁḍu bāḷu nīru tōṭa
mane makki ma[ra] nekki niḍilu yiṁtini
dalu koḍuta baharu mattaṁ bāḷu tōṭada me

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(A.R. No. 384 of 1928.)
ON A STONE IN A FIELD CALLED POLATIMURUKAMALA AT BUNTWALMUDA,
MANGALORE TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1287, Krōdhi, Bhādrapada, ba. 6, Maṅ[gaḷavāra]
corresponding to A.D. 1364 September 17 Tuesday (Nija-Bhādrapada), in the reign of
Vīra-Bukkaṇṇa-Voḍeya. It records that, under the orders of the king, Mādarasa
of Maṅgalūru made a gift of the village Pudu grāma, to certain Brāhmans (named)
free of all taxes. The Brāhmans were to give 12 mūḍes of rice for the service of the
god Mañjināthadēva.

l.ka: ḍaḷēsvara ·arirāyavibhāḍa bāsege tappūva rāyara gaṁḍa pū[rvvapa]-
rada • • • janaṁgaḷige koṭṭa śilāsāsana kramaveṁteṁdare ·ā pudu

nāḍe vāraṇāsiyalu sāvira gōvanu brā(ṁ)hmaranu koṁda dōsadali

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(A.R. No. 376 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BY THE SIDE OF THE EAST VERANDAH OF THE PRAKARA OF THE
KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, A SUBURB OF COONDAPUR, COONDAPUR
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1287, Viśvāvasu, Āśvayuja, ba. 2, Gu[ruvāra], corresponding to
A.D. 1365 October 2 Thursday, in the reign of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Bukkaṇṇa-
Oḍeya. It records that, under the orders of the king, Dharaṇidēva, son of Muddeya-
Nāgappa, purchased four plots of land, yielding 124 mūḍes of paddy and 18 honnus,
in Kōṭēśvara and made a gift of it for feeding every day six Brāhmans including a
cook in the Kōṭīśvara temple. Malleya-Daṇṇāyaka who was ruling the Bārakūra-
rājya under the orders of the king, made the plots of land free from taxes.

l.ka: garāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē || svasti śakavarṣa 1287 neya vi-
śvāvasusaṁvatsarada ·āśvayuja ba 2 gu daṁdu śrīmanmahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ śrīvī-

dalli śrīmatu muddeya nāgappanavara ma[kka]ḷu dharaṇidēvaṁgaḷu kōṭīśvaradēvaralu ·ayi-
varu

ra baḍagada kereya ·adhivāsadalu kārabayala bāḷina catusīme ·ūra vōṇiyiṁ paḍuvalu
jōḷiyara gaḍiya bēliyimdaṁ baḍagalu harida hoḷeyiṁdaṁ mū-
ḍalu ·ūra vōṇiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu ·a(ṁ)ntu catusīmeya voḷage bittuva bāḷu

mūḍalu sahasraṁṇakkaḷa bāḷa gaḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu yiṁtī catussīmeyo-

da mūḍe 12 ·ānegaḷiya kaḷiṁjayalu • • • garuḍage vōṇiyiṁda va-

ppattunālku rokka hadineṇṭu ho(ṁ)nnu ·aṁtu yī payika nālkaṟa mēle

ḍu ·ā bāḷugaḷa mēle ·uḷa pūrvvāya ·akara ·a(ṁ)nyāyavanū kā[du sarvva]-

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE MAIN SHRINE OF THE SIVA
TEMPLE AT KOTTASIVARAM, MADAKASIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 12[87], Viśvāvasu, Kārtika, śu. 10, Śu[kravāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1365 October 24 Friday, in the reign of Bukkaṇa-Voḍeya, ruling
at Hosapaṭṭaṇa. It records that Būcheya-Nāyaka, son of Benakeya-Nāyaka, who
was ruling at Sivara, the chief town of Nidugalla-rājya, made a gift of the village
Gōpasamudra for the service of the god Gōpinathadēva and placed it in charge of
Rāmakṛishṇadāsa and Kēśavadāsi, sons of Śivadāsi.

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(A.R. No. 246 of 1913.)
ON A STONE IN FRONT OF THE NANDI-MANDAPA OF THE DODDAPADESVARA
TEMPLE AT MODALLI, KOLLEGAL TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 12[8]8, Parābhava, Chaitra, ba. 10, Mam[galavāra].
In Parābhava, Chaitra ba. 10 was a Sunday = April 5, A.D. 1366. In Viśvāvasu, how-
ever, the tithi fell on a Tuesday = A.D. 1365 April 15. The inscription refers itself to
the reign of Kampaṇṇa-Oḍeya, son of Vīra-Bukkaṇṇa-Oḍeya. It records that
during the administration of Perumāḷa-Bhaṭṭa, a certain Nāyaka made a gift of land
and a part of toll revenue of the villages Baṇḍehaḷḷi and Mōḍehaḷḷi, with the con-
sent of the nāḍu, nagara and nānādēśis, for the service of the god Mallināthadēva
of Mōḍehaḷḷi.

l.ka: bhatta bha 1 kkaṁ herjuṁkaṁ puṇse nele niṁda • • • •
vā yō harēti vasuṁdharā[ṁ*] | saṣṭirvarṣasahasrāṇi vi-
ṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimi[ḥ*] || maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(A.R. No. 779 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WASTE WEIR OF THE TANK AT CHILUMUTTURU,
HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1289, Plavaṅga, Pratham-Āshāḍha, śu. 14, Śukravāra corre-
sponding to A.D. 1367 June 11 Friday, in the reign of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-
Bukkarāya. It records that, when Irugappa-Voḍeya, son of the Mahāpradhāna Bayi-
cheya-Daṇṇāyaka, was ruling over Chelumuttūru, the stone-stand (kallu-mañchige) was
constructed newly.

l.ka: svasti śrī [||*] jayābhyudaya śakavarṣa 1289 neya plavaṁgasaṁvatsarada prathamāṣāḍha

su 14 sukravāradalu svasti śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēsvaraṁ ·arirāyavibhāḍa bhāsege tappuva rāya
ra gaṁḍa
naṁ bayiceyada(ṁ)ṇnāykara kumāra yirugappavoḍeyaru celumutūranu stiravāgi
·āḷutiddalli hostāgi māḍisida kallamaṁcige[ge] maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(A.R. No. 374 of 1921.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE ALANKARA-MANDAPA BEFORE THE TRIPURASAMHARA
IMAGE IN THE TIRUVIRATTANESVARA TEMPLE AT TIRUVADI, CUDDALORE TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Sādhāraṇa, Pushya, śu. 13, Maṁ[gaḷavāra] corre-
sponding to A.D. 1370 December 31 Tuesday. It records that Dugaṇṇadēva, (manager)
of the house of Kaṁpaṇṇa-Voḍeya, son of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bukkaṇṇa-Oḍeya,
made a gift of the village of Sātipaṭu in Kīḷe-Āṇimjegāḍipaṭṭu for the service of the
god Modalīsvaramoḍeya-Nāyanār of Tiruvadi and placed it in charge of the sthānika
of the temple.

l.ka: tipaṭu nāyaparipālanāda grāma 1 kaṁ prāku hāḷāgidda
vanu madhupānaṁ māḍidavanu • nobanu gūṇa || kali

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(A.R. No. 259 of 1918.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP NEAR THE KALLESVARASVAMI TEMPLE AT BASARAKODU,
A HAMLET OF SIVALINGANAHALLI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1295, Parīdhāvi, Prathama-Bhādrapada, ba. 12,
Gu[ruvāra], corresponding to A.D. 1372 August 26 Thursday, in the reign of
Bukkarāya. It records that when the Pradhāni Kēcharidēva led an army, Chandappa
died.

l.ka: • • • bida kalu maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(A.R. No. 375 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BY THE SIDE OF THE EAST VERANDAH OF THE OUTER PRAKARA
OF THE KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, A SUBURB OF COONDAPUR,
COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1295, Parīdhāvin, Kārtika, śu. 5, Gu[ruvāra]. In Parīdhāvin
Kārttika, śu. 5 corresponded to A.D. 1372 November 1 Monday. But in the next
year, i.e., Pramādin the tithi did fall on a Thursday = A.D. 1373 October 20; f.d.t.
·55. The inscription refers itself to the reign of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Bukkaṇṇa-
Voḍeya. It records that when the Mahāpradhāna Gōparasa-Voḍeya was ruling the
Bārukūra-rājya, Nandinātha, Bhṛiṅginātha, Vīrabhadradēva and Gaṇakumāra-Heggaḍe,
purchased some plots of land, of specified boundary, in Kōṭēśvara and made
a gift of it to the temple of Kōṭēśvara for feeding Ubhaya-Jaṅgamas during the
festival called kaṭiya-habba; the yield of the land was 12 mūḍes of rice and 40 honnus.

l.ka: bhāḍa bhāṣege tappuva rāyara gaṁḍa śrīvīrabukkaṁṇa-
ya dharmake koṁḍa bāḷu koṭa bāḷatiya bageyalu dani-
yaru mūlavāgi koṁḍa bāḷu mane maneya ṭhāu bāvi
bāgila gadde ·alli paḍuva makke yellaū kaṁnuvakaṭṭeya
na ga[dde] bidiraḍiya gadde ·aṁttu nāgaṁḍugadalu bitta
bittuva hattu mūḍe gaddege bārakūra parivartta-
nake saluva gadyāṇa nūṟu hoṁnaṁ daniyara
kaiyalu koṭṭaru ·ā bāḷina mēle baha gēṇi-

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(A.R. No. 381 of 1928.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN MODANKUPPA BANTWAL-MUDA, MANGALORE TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1299, Piṅgaḷa, Siṁha 10, Śanivāra, corresponding to A.D.
1377 August 8 Saturday, in the reign of Hariyappa-Oḍeya, son of Bukkaṇṇa-Oḍeya.
It records that when Paṇḍaridēva was ruling over Maṅgalūru-rājya, certain śeṭṭis
made a grant of some plots of land for the service of the god Timirēśvaradēva.

l.ka: • • • tipāḍi ba • ṁtivaru yeṁba bhūmi lakṣakkaṁ
• • vara phala mane bhāvi kuṁṭe beṭṭu yiṁtī sarvvasvava
ligarē [te]ṟu siddhāya naṭṭu beḍe yese ·osage jōḍi yivanellava-

ppisuva ·aḍake | yī dharmavanu pālisidavarige sāvira ka-
vileyanu dānava māḍida phala yī dharmakāvanānū
vira kapileyenū koṁda dōṣa yī dharmava māḍi-
davarige maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī guru śaraṇā timerē-

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(A.R. No. 384 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE MARGIN OF THE EAST VERANDAH OF THE PRAKARA OF
THE KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, A SUBURB OF COONDAPUR, COONDAPUR
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1300, Piṅgaḷa, Kārtika, śu. 1, Mam[davāra], corresponding to
A.D. 1377 October 3, Saturday; f.d.t. ·34, in the reign of Hariyappa, son of Vīra-
Bukka. It states that Bommarasa was ruling the Bārakūra-rājya and records that
the Mahāpradhāna Mudde-Daṇṇāyaka purchased three plots of land and made a gift
of them and some gold for the offerings and other services of the god Kōtīśvara, for
feeding 13 Brāhmans every day in the temple and for the wages of two women
who waved chauris before the god. It also records that Muddaya-Daṇṇāyaka presen-
ted a frame of golden rods to the temple.

l.ka:
namastuṁgaśiraścuṁbicaṁdracāmaracāravē | trayilōkyanagarāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁ-
bhavē | svasti śrī

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(A.R. No. 388 of 1928.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SUBRAHMANYA AT SUBRAHMANYA
(KUKKE), PUTTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1301, Siddhārthi, Mārgaśira, ba. 10, Va[ḍḍavāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1379 December 4 Saturday, in the reign of Hariyappa-Voḍeya.
It records that Mādarasa made a gift of 200 gadyāṇas for the service of some god
(name lost).

l.ka: ga 170 kīḷokkaliṁga 30 vubhayaṁ ga 200 teṟuvaru yī

l.note: Two lines after this are much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(A.R. No. 401 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD NEAR HIREBAIL, A HAMLET OF
SANKARANARAYANA, COONDAPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This seems to be a record of a grant made in the reign of Bukkarāya but probably
given effect to and put on stone in Hariyappa-Oḍeya's reign which is mentioned at the
end. It is damaged and dated Śaka 1302 (current), Siddhārthi, Chaitra, śu. 1,
Ādityavāra, corresponding to A.D. 1380 March 19 Saturday (not Sunday). It records
that the Mahāpradhāna Maleya-Daṇṇāyaka had made a grant of land in the reign of
Vīra-Bukkaṇṇa-Oḍeya for the service of the god Koḍagiya-Śaṅkaranārāyaṇa-
dēva. The land was now made over to Kimiseṭṭi who was made responsible for
conducting services to the god.

l.ka: rirāyavibhāḍa bhāsege tappuva rāyara gaṁḍa gajarājarā-
ḷakanu kayivaṇi tāṁḍeya makkiyali māḍida dharmadoḷage ·a
du koṭa patra ·ā tāṁḍeya makkiya yikida kalloḷu • • •
ra niki niṭilu nidhi nikṣēpa jala pāśāṇa sidha sādhya ·ākṣiṇi
varuśa 1 dakaṁ yeṁṇe hāne 36 nu naḍasuvaru yi • • •
ya koṁḍa pāpadali hōharu [||*] sadataṁ paradataṁ vā yō harē-

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1910.)
ON THREE SECTIONS OF A VIRAGAL NEAR A PIPAL TREE AT ERAGANAHALLI,
GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1308, Kshaya, Vaiśākha, śu. 1, Va[ḍḍavāra], corresponding to
A.D. 1386 March 31 Saturday, in the reign of Vīra-Hariyappa-Oḍeya. It records that
Chikkatamma, son of Kalli-Gauḍa of Eṟaganahaḷḷi, died after slaying thieves with an
axe in Mūḍaṇabeṭṭa and that his younger brother Kētaṇṇa set up this hero-stone.

l.ka:
svasti śrī [||*] sakavaruṣa 1308 saṁda kṣayasaṁvatsarada vaiśākha su 1 va daṁdu śrīmamanṛ-
hāmaṁ-
haḷḷiya kalligauḍana maga cikkataṁmanu mūḍaṇabeṭṭadalli ·asahāyasūranāgi koḍali-

yalli kaḷḷara huydu keḍahi tānu bidda kāraṇakke ·ātana tamma kēta(ṁ)ṇna huysida vīraga-
llu maṁgaḷa

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1913.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN A FIELD AT MODALLI KOLLEGAL TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1313, Prajōtpatti, Phālguṇa, śu. 15,
Man[davāra ?], corresponding to A.D. 1392 March 9, Saturday, in the reign of
Harihara-Mahārāya. It records that the people and Gāvuṇḍas restored the fields
and houses formerly granted by Hiriya-Kamparāya in Mōḍehaḷḷi, surnamed Kampa-
rājapura, to Brāhmans of various gotras, and placed them in charge of the Mahā-
janas of the village.

l.ka: kaṁba 200 kke kṣētra kha 1 yo mariyāde-
nige kha 1 vatsada periyarige kha 1 vādhūlada dēva
• • rige kha 1 kausikada mādavarige kha 1 vā-
ḷege kha 1 kāśyapada dharaṁbarige kha 1 bharadvāja
rumāḷe kha 1 kapila gauraṇa kha 1 ha-
ru kha 1 vaśiṣṭha māyaṇa kha 1 kausika •

inscription.head:
No. 422.
(A.R. No. 734 of 1916.)
ON A ROCK IN THE REVANNA TEMPLE AT HARESAMUDRAM, MADAKASIRA
TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1318, Īśvara, Kārtika, śu. 12, corresponding to A.D. 1397
November 2 Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Harihararāya. It records that
Dhūḷeya-Nāyaka, son of Basaveyanāyakana-Chauṇḍappa, made a gift of land irrigated
by Chaṭayanakuṇṭe in Kōḍaiyanahaḷḷi to gods, Brāhmans and Bhaktas.

l.ka: kuṁṭeya keḷage dēvarige brāṁharigeyu nelu kṣētra 1 bhaktarige
yalu kapileya koṁda pāpa brāhmaṇava[dheya] māḍida pāpa

inscription.head:
No. 423.
(A.R. No. 408 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE MAHALINGESVARA TEMPLE
AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1322, Vikrama, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 6, Sau[myavāra], corresponding
to A.D. 1400 August 11 Wednesday, in the reign of Harihara-Mahārāya ruling at
Vijayanagarī. It records that, when Basavaṇṇa-Oḍeya was ruling over Bārakūra-
rājya under the orders of the king, Mādaṇṇaseṭṭi made a gift of land to Siṅgaṇṇa-Ayya,
son of Chakravarti-Upādhyāya, a Vidvān of the maṭha of Śṛiṅgēri, for daily
offerings to Nandikēśvara in front of the temple of Nakharēśvara at Basrūr and a
gift of gold for the service of the god Uḷḷūradēva. The celebrated Vidyāraṇya is
praised at the beginning of the record.

l.ka: kurutē mūkaṁ mūkaṁ vācāḷapuṁgavaṁ [|*] vidyāra(ṁ)ṇyagurōścitraṁ cari-
traṁ caturānana(ṁ) [||*] svasti śrījayābhyudayaśakavaruṣa 1322 saṁdu varttamā-
na vikramasaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa ba[6sau]dalu svasti śrīmadrājādhirāja rājaparamē-
śvara śrīvīrapratāpa hariharamahārāyaru vijayanagariyali sukhadiṁ sā-
bārakūra rājyavanū dhar(ṁ)mmadiṁ paripālaneya māḍutaṁvirdda kāladalu
siṁgaṁṇa·ayyagaḷige bārakūra taṁmaṁṇa tolādhāriya maga basurū-
ra seṭṭikāṟa mādaṁṇaseṭṭikoṭṭa śilāśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdaḍe kaṁdā-
ddha brahmadāyada mūlada bāḷina catuḥsīmeya vivara mūḍalu seṭṭiya be-
ṭṭina siṁgaṁṇana gaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu | teṁkalu ·uḷḷūradēvara dēvasva-
da gaḍiyiṁdaṁ baḍagalu | paḍuvalu mūḍilakkaḷa gaḍiyiṁdaṁ mū-
ḍalu | baḍagalu harida sāliṁdaṁ teṁkalu | yiṁtī catuḥsīmeyiṁdoḷaguḷḷa
mane bāvi manebāgila gadde maneya hiṁdaṇa beṭṭu tippe makki mekke bayalu ka-
ḷe keṟe makki mara nekki niḍilu nidhi nikṣēpa jala pāṣāṇa ·ākṣīṇi ·āgāmi siddha sā-
yyagaḷige ·ā seṭṭakāṟa mādaṁṇaseṭṭi ·uḍuvara baḷiya taṁna kīḷuniṁ-

·ā bāḷiṁge basarūra nakharēśvaradēvara muṁdaṇa naṁdikēśvaradēvara nai-
vēdyakke dinaṁprati nāḍahaneyalu 1 hāne ·akkiya lekkadalu varuṣaṁ-
naḍasi baharu | ·uḷḷūradēvarige kaṭṭuṁdeṟāgi varuṣaṁprati 1 hoṁna na-
ḍasuvaru yī bāḷiṁge ·oḷage baṁdu hoṟage ·a(ṁ)nyaru ·ārobbaru vakrisida-

inscription.head:
No. 424.
(A.R. No. 418 of 1928.)
ON ANOTHER SLAB SET UP IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1323, Vishu, Vaiśākha, ba. 5, Sō[ma*]vāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1401 May 2 Monday, in the reign of Harihara-Mahārāya. It records that, when
Basavaṇṇa-Oḍeya was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya under the orders of the king, a gift
of land was made by Tuḷuvaka-Heggaḍiti for the service of the god Tuḷuvēśvara
and for feeding nine Brāhmans in the sattra of the temple.

l.ka: cāravē trayilōkyanagarāraṁbha mūlastaṁbhāya staṁbhavē [||*] svasti śrīja-
yābhyudaya śakavaruṣa 1323 saṁdu vartamāna viśusaṁvatsarada vayiśākha ba 5
sō[ma*]vāradalu svasti śrīmadrājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa hari-
(ṁ) viralu ·ā rāyara nirupadiṁ basavaṁṇa·oḍeyaru bārakūra rājyava sa-
ddhar(ṁ)madiṁ pratipālaneya māḍutaṁvidda kāladalu basarūra paḍuva-

ruva hadi ·oḷage dharmake biṭa bāḷina vivara ·oḷamāḍina ·oḷage muḍalu

inscription.head:
No. 425.
(A.R. No. 400 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD NEAR HALE-AGRAHARA AT SANKARANARAYANA,
COONDAPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1324, Vishu, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 12, Ā[divāra]. Śrāvaṇa
śu. 12 of Vishu corresponded to A.D. 1401 July 22 Friday (not Sunday), but in
Vikrama the tithi did fall on a Sunday = A.D. 1400 August 18. The inscription
refers itself to the reign of Harihararāya who was ruling from Dōrasamudra.
It records that when Basavaṇṇa-Oḍeya was governing the kingdom from
Bārakūru under the orders of the Mahāpradhāna Gōpayadaṇṇāyaka-Oḍeya,
Mādāḍi-Toḷaha made a grant of a fixed quantity of rice per day for the service
of the god Śaṅkaranārāyaṇadēva.

ka·oḍeyara svakāruṇyadiṁ śrīmatu basavaṁṇa·oḍeyaru bārakūra rājadhāni-

inscription.head:
No. 426.
(A.R. No. 396 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE TEMPLE OF JANARDANA AT
YERUMAL, UDIPI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1324, Chitrabhānu, Vaiśākha, śu. 3, Gu[ruvāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1402 April 6, Thursday. It refers itself to the reign of
Harihararāya and records that a gift of some plots of land was made by Gaṇapaṇṇa,
son of Lakkhappa of Kaḷalige, for the service of the god Janārdanadēva at
Yermmāḷa, after purchasing the land from others. It states that Basavaṇṇa-Oḍeya
was then governing the Bārakūra-rājya.

l.ka: svasti śrī [||*] pāṁtu vō jaladaśyāmā śārṅgajyāghātakarkaśā trailōkyamaṁṭapa-
va kāladalu ·avara nirūpadiṁda basuva(m)ṇna·oḍeyaru bārakūra rājya-

ṁṇagaḷu yermmāḷa śrījanārddanadēvara sthānadali māḍida dharmmada krama-
ṇagaḷu ·ā pādamūli viṣṇunelli nāraṇanelli kēśavanelli yivaru-
gaḷa kayyalli ·ardhaparicchēdavāgi mūḷiya haṇa sahitavāgi ko-
ṭṭu mūlaparicchēdavāgi dhārāpūrvvakavāgi koṁḍa kṣētrada vi-
vara yermmāḷadēvālyada yośā(ṁ)nyada mūleya pāḷa dēvara
majalalu ·ā bhomiya catuḥsīme mūḍalu heggaḍeya bāḷiṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 427.
(A.R. No. 386 of 1920.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT TIMMANCHERLA,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is in Sanskrit and not dated. It refers itself to the reign of Harihara.
It states that he made gifts according to Hēmādri-śāstra and records that he made a
grant of the village Chimmanakeṟe, situated in Gutti-vaḷita in Ghanādri-rājya to the
learned Brāhman Gōpalārādhya, son of Viṭhalārādhya. The king referred to here was
probably Harihara II.

l.ka: bhaginī lōkē (|) sarvvēṣāmēva bhūbhujāṁ || na bhōjyāna

inscription.head:
No. 428.
(A.R. No. 572 of 1906.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE PUNDISVARA TEMPLE AT
MUGAVADI, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1328, Pārthiva, Jyēshṭha, ba. 7, [Budhavāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1405 May 20 Wednesday, in the reign of Bukkarāya
(II). It records that, under the orders of Jommaṇṇa-Oḍeya, a gift was made
to the god • • • Nāyanāradēva at Mūgavāḍi and mentions Dēvarāya-Oḍeya.

inscription.head:
No. 429.
(A.R. No. 320 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB ON MALLAYYA-KONDA NEAR TAMBALLAPALLE,
MADANAPALLE TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1327, Pārthiva, Āshāḍha, śu. 1, corresponding to A.D. 1405
May 29 Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Devarāya-Mahārāya. It records
the nirūpa issued by the king to Mayilappa, stating that the mānya village
Tammaḍiyahaḷḷi, together with 25 varāhas, being the kāṇike received on Śivarātri
and other occasions in the temple of Mallikārjuna at Chandragiri in Mārja-
vāḍi-nāḍu, be restored and continued as in the time of Kaṁpaṇṇa-Voḍeya, Bukkaṇṇa-
Oḍeya and Chikkoḍeya and that the same be given effect to commencing from
Tāraṇa, Kārtika, śu. 1 (= A.D. 1404 October 5). It also states that the sum of the
kāṇike be distributed among the sthānapatis of the temple whose names and the
shares they were to get are written in Telugu.

l.ka: ra kāla mo[da*]lāgi·u hiṁde naḍedu baṁda mari-
nu māḍi sarvvamānyavāgi naḍasi bahadu yo

inscription.head:
No. 430.
(A.R. No. 369 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A PLAIN CALLED BARAHADA-KALLU-GUDDE AT
VADERAHOBLI, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1328, Pārthiva, Phālguna, śu. 10, Sō[mavāra] corresponding to
A.D. 1406 February 28, Sunday (not Monday). It refers itself to the reign of
Bukkarāya and states that Bāchaṇṇa-Oḍeya was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya under
his orders. After praising Vidyāraṇya-guru, it records that the two Dhaṉis
(chiefs) of Kundāpura, and others purchased a plot of land (brahmadāya)
at the same village and made a gift of it to Nārasiṁhabhārati, the guru of Śṛiṅgēri.
Out of the produce of the land, 3 mūḍes of paddy were to be given for the service
of the god Kōtīśvaradēva and 60 hānes of rice for the service of the god Chikkadēva,
every year.

l.ka: dayaśakavaruṣa 1328 neya vartamāna pārthivasaṁvatsarada phālguna śuddha
rāyara gaṁḍa śrīvīrapratāpa bukarāyaru rājyābhyudayadalu taṁnirūpa-

inscription.head:
No. 431.
(A.R. No. 735 of 1922.)
ON A ROCK TO THE WEST OF RAMASAGARAM, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: This is not dated. It mentions Bukkarāya (II ?) and Penugoṇḍe.

inscription.head:
No. 432.
(A.R. No. 401 of 1920.)
ON A STONE IN A FIELD TO THE EAST OF THE VILLAGE GULAPALYAM,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1329, Sarvajit, Jyēshṭha, śu. [1]5, Śanivāra,
Sōmagrahaṇa corresponding to A.D. 1407 May 21, Saturday, on which day there was a
lunar eclipse. It refers itself to the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya and records that
Haṇḍepa-Nāyaka made a grant of land to some Bhaṭṭa (name lost). The engraver
of the record was Liṅgōjayya, son of Timmōja.

l.ka: śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī • • • • • sama-

inscription.head:
No. 433.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1910.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE BASAVESVARA TEMPLE AT
SINGANALLUR, COIMBATORE TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is a little damaged and dated Kali 4522 (4509), Śaka 1330, Sarvadhāri,
Śrāvaṇa, śu. 13, Ma[ndavāra ?], corresponding to A.D. 1408 August 4, Saturday, in the
reign of Vīra-Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, son of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Harihara-Mahārāya.
The king is described as being well-versed in music and literature. The inscription
records an agreement entered into by the Gauḍas, Seṭṭis, Brahmachāris, Brāhmans,
Sēnabōvas and the Eighteen Samayas of Baṇḍiyahaḷḷi-Mōdeyahaḷḷi which belonged
to Vīraṇṇa-Ayya, a subordinate of the Mahāpradhāna Nāgaṇṇa Nāyaka-Oḍeya.
The terms were that the number of the families of cultivators would be according to
the number of houses occupied; the amount of taxes payable on grains, bullocks,
clarified butter, sugar and other things, to the palace by each family would be fixed
and regularly paid; if any family or families ran away, the Gauḍas responsible would
bring them back or pay the amount due from them; if the head of a family died, his
successor would pay the amount due; if necessary, family jewellery would be sold to
pay off the sum due to the palace; and if any one stole any thing in the house of
the chief, the same rule held good, that is to say, those who were responsible for
him would make good the loss.

l.ka: 1330 neya sarvvadhārisaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa śu 13 ma svasti sama-
stha sālu mūleyoḷagāda padineṁṭu samayakke mukhyavā-

inscription.head:
No. 434.
(A.R. No. 803 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT GAUNIVARIPALLI,
A HAMLET OF KONDAPURAM, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1304 (mistake for 1330), Sarvadhāri, Mārgaśira,
śu. 7, Budhavāra. The date is not regular; Sarvadhāri corresponded to A.D. 1408-09.
The inscription refers itself to the reign of Hiryia-Dēvarāya (I) and records the grant
of a village (name lost) with its toll-revenue for the service of god Hanumantadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 435.
(A.R. No. 378 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BY THE SIDE OF THE EAST VERANDAH IN THE OUTER PRAKARA
OF THE KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA
DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is very much damaged and dated 133[3] • • • Pushya (= A.D. 1411) in the
reign of the Vijayanagara king • • Oḍeya. It records that when the Mahāpradhāna
Bāchaṇṇa-Oḍeya was in charge [of Bārakūra-rājya], Hariyaparasa and others made a
grant of land for a perpetual lamp to the god Kōṭināthadēva and for the feeding of a
fixed number of Brāhmans in the temple.

l.ka: baraṁ salu·uttirdda śakavaruṣa 133[3] • • • • • rada puśya śu[dha]
ya • • • • • gade biṭa kaṁma 1 || lu hoḷeyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu

inscription.head:
No. 436.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE AT MALAPANGUDI, HOSPET
TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1333, Khara, Māgha, ba. 5 Bu[dhavāra], corresponding to A.D.
1412 February 3, Wednesday. It refers itself to the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya
and records that the Heggaḍe Sōvaṇṇa-Aṇṇa, son of Buḷḷe-Nāyaka constructed a
water-shed at the junction of the roads leading to Bisilahaḷḷi-kaṇuve and Baḍavaliya-
kaṇuve and, purchasing three vṛitti lands from three Brāhmans (named), made
provision for repairing the well and the water-shed, for purchase of utensils and
for the remuneration of the persons employed.

inscription.head:
No. 437.
(A.R. No. 584 of 1912.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE COMPOUND OF THE SUB-COLLECTOR'S OFFICE,
PENUKONDA, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and the date is partly lost with the exception of Śaka 1339 (A.
D. 1417-18). It records the setting up of the lamp-post by a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
(name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 438.
(A.R. No. 521 of 1906.)
ON FOUR FACES OF A PILLAR LYING AT THE WESTERN ENTRANCE OF THE LAKSHMI-
NARASIMHASVAMI TEMPLE AT KADIRI, KADIRI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged and dated the cyclic year Hēmaḷambi, Phālguṇa, śu.
10, in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, corresponding to A.D. 1418 February 16
Wednesday (not verifiable). It records that the king confirmed the exemption,
granted formerly to the Pañchālas in the four Nāḍus by king Harihara-Mahārāya,
from taxes on marriages, bullocks, buffaloes, carts loaded with paddy, and the
like.

inscription.head:
No. 439.
(A.R. No. 804 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD TO THE EAST OF THE VILLAGE GAUNIVARIPALLI,
HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 100 30 39 (i.e., 1339.) Hēmaḷambi, Phālguṇa, ba.
10, G[uruvāra] corresponding to A.D. 1418 March 2 Wednesday (not Thursday), in the
reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, son of Harihara-Mahārāya, who was the son of
Bukkarāya. It records that the king confirmed the exemption granted by
Harihara-Mahārāya, as a reward for having manufactured the throne in the
royal court (hajāra-siṁhāsana), to the Pāṁchālas from taxes on bullocks, buffaloes,
carts, jewels, etc.

l.ka:
ba 10 gu svasti śrīmanmahārājādhi-
rāja rājapa-

l.note: The slab is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 440.
(A.R. No. 283 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ANJANEYASVAMI TEMPLE AT RAJABAVANAHALLI,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1341, Vikāri, Kārtika, śu. 11, Ādivāra corres-
ponding to A.D. 1419 October 29 Sunday, in the reign of Dēvarāya-
Mahārāya. It records that, while Hariyaṇṇa, son of Lakhappa, was ruling
the division of Noṇabana-Arasiyakeṟe in Pāṇḍya-nāḍu under the orders of the king,
the Mahājanas headed by this Hariyaṇṇa were granted as a śrōtriya-agrahāra the
village of Hariyasamudra for having founded it by reclaiming the forests, and having
constructed a tank. The land under the new tank was made free from taxes
such as tax on wet land, dry land, arecanut, coconut, jack-fruit, mango-trees,
sugar-cane, plantain-trees, betel creepers, salt-pan, harvest-grain, ploughs of bullocks
and buffaloes, sēse, siddhāya, Daṇāyaka-sāmya and other imposts. It also records
that the Mahājanas granted rent-free lands to other Brāhmans (named).

inscription.head:
No. 441.
(A.R. No. 365 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE KUNDESVARA TEMPLE AT
COONDAPUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1348, Viśvāvasu, Chaitra, śu. • • Maṁ[gaḷavāra],
corresponding probably to A.D. 1425 March 27, Tuesday, in the reign of
Dēvarāya-Mahārāya (II). It records that Narasiṁhadēva-Voḍeya, who was governing
the Bārakūra-rājya by the command of the king and, Hariyappa-Daṇṇāyaka-
Voḍeya restored, without diminishing the revenue to the palace, the land, once
granted by Dēvaṇṇa-Sēnabōva of Kuḍākūr for the service of the god Kundēśvara,
after making a thorough inquiry among the inhabitants of Kundāpura who had
assembled at his call.

l.ka: varṣa 1347 saṁdu 8 neya viśvāvasuseṁvatsarada caitra su

inscription.head:
No. 442.
(A.R. No. 389 of 1928.)
ON A STONE LYING IN FRONT OF THE RUINED TEMPLE OF GOPALAKRISHNA
AT BILINILI, PUTTUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is very much damaged and the preserved portion of the date is the cyclic
year Sādhāraṇa, Kārtika, śu. 12. Vaḍḍavāra, corresponding to A.D. 1430 October 28,
Saturday. It refers itself to the reign of the Vijayanagara king Vīrapratāpa-
Dēvarāya-Mahārāya (II). It seems to record the gift of a village (name lost) and
mentions Dēvarāja-Oḍeya and the village Rāmanāthapura. It is written in Nāgarī
characters and the Kannaḍa language.

vi • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 443.
(A.R. No. 765 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI-PILLAR SET UP NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT KOTIPI,
HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1354, Parīdhāvi, Kārtika, śu. 1, Śu[kravāra], corresponding to
A.D. 1432 October 24, Saturday, f.d.t. ·38, in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that the king abolished the cess on marriages among all castes in
Koṭapi. It also states that those who marry and go out, keeping the Basava (on
the pillar on which the inscription is engraved) to their right, will be blessed with
many children.

inscription.head:
No. 444.
(A.R. No. 414 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE MAHALINGESVARA TEMPLE AT
BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1356, Pramādi, Kārtika, corresponding to A.D. 1433 October-
November, in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya. It records that while the
Mahāpradhāna Chandarasa-Oḍeya was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya a grant of land
was made by Īsara-Seṭṭi for keeping a perpetual lamp before the god Nakharēśvara
and feeding a fixed number of Brāhmans in the temple on special days.

inscription.head:
No. 445.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1904.)
ON A ROCK NEAR THE KALASAPURA-ANJANEYA TEMPLE ON THE HOSPET-
KAMPILI ROAD, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1356, Pramādīcha, Māgha, śu. 10, Gu[ruvāra],
probably corresponding to A.D. 1434 January 20, Wednesday, not Thursday. It
refers itself to the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya and records that the king made a
gift of a (village ?) to Rāmayya, manager of the temple of god Hanumantadēva
set up by [Sā]ḷuva-Śaṅkara[dēva ?].

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 446.
(A.R. No. 430 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE BACK-YARD OF A PRIVATE HOUSE TO THE SOUTH OF THE
TANK, DODDAKEREKATTE, AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA
DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1358, Naḷa, Āśvīja, śu. 10, Gu[ruvāra] corresponding to A.D.
1436 September 20 Thursday, in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya. It records that,
while the Mahāpradhāna Aṇṇappa-Oḍeya was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya under the
orders of the king, Baḷabijjana-Bemmaseṭṭi-Nāraṇaseṭṭi of the Baḷegāra community
made a gift of some plots of land of specified boundary for feeding Brāhmans in
the maṭha at Paḍuvakēri in Basarūru.

inscription.head:
No. 447.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1904.)
ON THE MANDAPA IN THE FIELD CALLED PAPAJAGALURA-HOLA NEAR KAMALAPUR,
HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Siddhārthi, Āśvīja, śu. 10, corresponding to A.D.
1439 September 18, Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that the king's servant Kaṭigeya Āhamuda-Khāna caused the construction
of a rest-house and a well, for the merit of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(A.R. No. 407 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE MAHALINGESVARA TEMPLE
AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1363, Ḍurmati, Phālguṇa, śu. 10, Ma[ṅgaḷavāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1442 February 20 Tuesday, in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that Kōṭiyaṇṇa-Seṭṭi made a gift of land for the service of the god
Mahādēva at Paḍuvakēri of Basarūru, when the Mahāpradhāna Chandarasa-Voḍeya
was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya under the orders of the king.

l.ka:
śrīgaṇādhipatayē nama[ḥ*] śrīsarasvatyayē nama[ḥ*] śrīgurubhyō nama[ḥ*] ·avighna-
mastu
durmmati saṁ | phālguṇa śu 10 maṁ lū śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamē-
ṁda śrīmanmahāpradhāna caṁdarasavoḍeyarū bārakūra rājyavanū pratipāli-

saṁnidhiyali māḍida dharmada vivara rudrapūje yibbaru dīvaḷigeya habbada-

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(A.R. No. 417 of 1928.)
ON ANOTHER SLAB SET UP IN THE SAME PRAKARA.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1366, Raktākshi, Āshāḍha, śu. 10, Gu[ruvāra], corresponding
to A.D. 1444 June 25 Thursday, in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya. It mentions
Timmaṇṇa-Oḍeya as ruling over Bārakūra-rājya under his orders and records that
the several Seṭṭikāras of Paḍuvakēri in Basarūru gave lands to Nāraṇa-Seṭṭi
for offerings and service in the temple of Nakharēśvara. Nāraṇa-Seṭṭi is also stated to
have made a gift of land to the same temple.

l.ka: śrīgaṇādhipatayē namaḥ śrīsarasvatyāyaṁ namaḥ śrīgurubhyōnamaḥ nirvigña-
66 neya raktākṣisaṁvatsarada ·āśāḍha śuddha 10 gu lū śrīmanmahārājā-
riya siṁhāsanadalli sāṁbrājyavanāḷutiha kāladali ·avara nirūpadiṁ tiṁ-
maṇa·oḍeyaru bārakūrarājyavanu pālisuva kāladali basuṟūra paḍuva-

ra baḷiya nāraṇaseṭṭige koṭṭa mūlaśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare hārunāḍa kayima-
ra ·odare koṁgāḷiya beṭina bayalina catussīmeya vivara mūḍalu ·aṁṇaheggaḍeya
gaḍi kānūrana makkiya gaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu teṁkalu telaūravara gaḍi ye-
ttida ·agaḷiṁdaṁ baḍagalu | paḍuvalu bayicahaḍavaḷana gaḍiyiṁdaṁ mūḍa-
lu | baḍagalu baḍābeṭiṁdaṁ teṁkalu yiṁtī catussīmeyiṁda ·oḷagaṇa ba-
yalu bittuva bede gaṇagilu nāghaṁḍugadalu muḍi 12 maneya bāgila gadde makki
saha bittuvadu muḍi 6 ·aṁtu bayalu makki saha bittuvadu muḍi 18 ·akṣāradalū ha-
dineṁṭu mūḍe gadde mane hakkalu haḍu beṭṭumeṁdu mara phala nidhi nikṣēpa jala
pāśāṇa ·akṣīṇi ·āgāmike siddha sādhya yēnuḷḷaṁthādu sahitavāgi halaru seṭikā-
rvvakavāgi mūlavanu yeṟadu koṭaru yo bāḷa mēle halaru seṭikāṟara haḍiya-
lu kaṭṭikūṭṭa teṟu ¥ 2 modalu halaru seṭikāṟaru yiḷihikoḍuva jī-
ta ga 4 hoṁnu yī yeraḍu haṇavina modala teṟina ·oḷage yī nālku
hoṁna jītavanu yiḷihikoṭṭu mēlāda hoṁnanū koṁḍuhōharu yo
hadineṁṭu mūḍe gaddeyamēle hoṟagaṇa ·āppaṇe ·uṁṭu yaṁdu baṁda-
re halaru seṭṭikāṟaru tettu koḍuveveṁdu ·oḍaṁbaṭṭu barasida śilāśāsana
yo bāḷa mēle saṁkaraseṭṭiya maga nāraṇaseṭṭi māḍida dharmada vivara nakharēśvara-
da śrīmahādēvara śāṁtidēva pūjeya māḍu[va*] ·aḍigaḷige naḍasikoḍuva bhatta nālvaṁ-
ḍeyalu muḍi 27 kāṭiga 2 hoṁnu rudrapūje 1 kke bhatta muḍi 20 yī dhar(ṁ)mmava-
nu ·ā bāḷa mēle nāraṇaseṭṭi nāraṇaseṭṭiya kuṭuṁbadavaru ·ācaṁdrārkkasthāyi-
yāgi naḍasikoḍuveveṁdu ·oḍaṁbaṭṭu barasida śāsana yī bāḷa mēle ·ārobba-

yō harēta vasuṁdharāṁ | śaṣṭirvaruṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi[ḥ*] ||

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(A.R. No. 404 of 1928.)
ON ANOTHER SLAB SET UP IN THE SAME PRAKARA.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1366, Raktākshi, Pushya, śu. 5, Sō[mavāra], corresponding to
A.D. 1444 December 14 Monday, in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya. It records
that, when Timmaṇṇa-Oḍeya was ruling over the Bārakūra-rājya, the Nakharas and
Seṭṭis of Dharmapaṭṭaṇa agreed to pay to the temple of Mahādēva a portion of
toll-revenue raised in the village and use it for repairing the gold pinnacle of the
temple and that the balance, if any, would be used for the service of the god and for
no other purpose. The gift was made to expiate the sin commited by them in having
murdered two men (named) of their community.

l.ka: śakavaruṣa 1366 raktākṣisaṁvatsarada puśya śu 5 sō lu
varu kaṁḍiṁda dharmmapaṭaṇada nakharadavara kayyalū māḍi-
hattu honu suṁkavādare ·oṁdu hoṁna lekkadalu kāla-
ciyiṁda ·oḍaṁbaṭu barasida śāsana yo honu mahādē-
vara ceṁbina ·oḍilu honna kaḷaśake māḍida dharma ·ālliṁ-
śāsana yidake dharmapaṭṭaṇada nakharadavara suhastada ·oppa śrīsa-
dāśiva paḍuvakēryi halaru seṭṭikāṟara suhastada ·oppa na-
taṁ padaṁ [||*] vāgu[da]ttaṁ manādattaṁ dhārādattaṁ dinē dinē | śa-

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(A.R. No. 377 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BY THE SIDE OF THE EAST VERANDAH IN THE PRAKARA OF THE
KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, A SUBURB OF COONDAPUR, SAME TALUK
AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka [1]36[9], Prabhava, Vaiśākha, śu. 15, correspon-
ding to A.D. 1447 April 30 Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that Ruppaṇṇa-Oḍeya who was governing the Bārakūra-rājya made a
grant of the village, Karuvakunda, yielding 208(1/2) honnus per annum, in order to
conduct some charities instituted by Gururāya-Oḍeya, in the name of the king,
for the service of the god Kōṭināthadēva and for feeding ten Brāhmans every day.
This income is stated to have been transferred from the accounts of Government
revenue to the temple revenue.

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(A.R. No. 429 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A HOUSE-SITE SOUTH OF THE TANK CALLED DODDAKERE-
KATTE AT BASRUR, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 13[7]3, Pramōda, Vaiśākha, śu. 13, Śukravāra, corresponding
to A.D. 1450 April 24 Friday, in the reign of Mallikārjuna-Mahārāya. It states that,
under the orders of the king, Liṅgapa-Oḍeya was ruling the Bārakūra-rājya and
records that Kōṭeyaka-Nāyakitti, wife of Kōṭeya-Nāyaka, and her son, Meravaṇṇa-
Seṭṭi made a gift of land and gold for the worship of gods and feeding some Brāh-
mans in the maṭha of the place.

l.ka:
śrīgaṇādhipatayē nama[ḥ*] śrīsarasvatyai nama[ḥ*] śrīgurubhyō nama[ḥ*] nirvighnamastu
[||*]
neya pramādasaṁvatsarada vaiśākha śu 13 śukravāradalū śrīmanmahā-
yakitti taṁna maga meravaṁṇaseṭi paḍuvakēṇali barasida śilāśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare yo ma-
ṭhakke barasida bāḷu ·ānagaḷiya voḷage ·ā bāḷu catussīmeya vivara mūḍalu dēvakaseṭi-

inscription.head:
No. 453.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1904.)
ON A ROCK NEAR THE KAPILASRAMA AT NIMBAPUR, HOSPET TALUK,
BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1372, Pramāthi, Pausha, ba. 7, Śukravāra corre-
sponding to A.D. 1450 December 25, Friday. It gives the genealogy of the Vijaya-
nagara kings from Saṅgama down to Prauḍhadēva-Mahārāya who was ruling from
Vijayanagara. It records that, under the orders of the king, Rāyapaṇṇayya founded
a village called Sōmalāpura and that the king erected a temple, a lamp-pillar, and a
Sandhyā-maṇḍapa and consecrated in the temple the god Saumya-Sōmēśvara. He is
also stated to have made a gift of Kariyahaḷḷi for the service of the god and placed
it in charge of Nāgaṇṇayya, son of Huligere Maṅgaṇṇa-Ayya. The writer was
Vīraṇa, son of Muddaṇāchārya. The record is engraved in Nāgarī characters and
Sanskrit and Kannaḍa languages.

gaṇṇa·ayyagaḷa makkaḷu nāgaṁṇa·ayyagaḷa kartara māḍi koṭṭaru ·ī dharmavanu pālistavaru vāraṇāsiyalu

inscription.head:
No. 454.
(A.R. No. 382 of 1928.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN A FIELD CALLED KALLUMARU AT BANTWAL-MUDA,
MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged and the preserved portion of the date is the cyclic year
Pramōda in the reign of the Vijayanagara king Prauḍha-Dēvarāya-Mahārāya (= A.
D. 1450). It records some gift for offerings on the Dīpāvaḷi festival to a god (name
lost).

inscription.head:
No. 455.
(A.R. No. 366 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE KUNDESVARA TEMPLE AT
COONDAPUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged and dated Śaka 1373, • • • Śrāvaṇa, ba. 10, Ādivāra, corre-
sponding to A.D. 1451 August 21, Saturday (not Sunday), in the reign of Mallikārjuna-
dēva. It records that, when the Mahāpradhāna Vallabhadēva-Daṇāyaka was ruling
the Bārakūra-rājya, a gift of 48 honnus, which was the income from salt pans in
Kundāpura, was made to Jōyisa Hariyappa of Maudgalya-gōtra and Ṛik-śākhā.

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(A.R. No. 412 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE TEMPLE OF MAHALINGESVARA
AT BASRUR, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1374, Prajōtpatti, Pushya, śu. 1, corresponding to A.D. 1451
December 24 Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Mallikārjuna-Mahārāya. It
records that, under the orders of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, Bhānappa-Oḍeya, who was
ruling over Bārakūra-rājya, made a gift of 121 kāṭi-gadyāṇas out of the siddhāya
of Basarūru, for the service of the god Nakharēśvaradēva of the place.

l.ka: bhavē [||*] svasti śrījayābhyudaya śakavaruṣa 1374 neya prajōtpatyasaṁvatsarada puśya śu-

dha 1 lū śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa mallikārjunamahārā-
ya-
ra nirūpadiṁ bhānappa·oḍeyaru bārakūra rājyavanu pālisuti halli ·ā bhānappa-
śrīmahādēvarige bhōgapātrada dharmake māḍidu basuṟūra divarakēriya
tōṭada heḍeṟina sidhāyada kāṭiga 111 ·akṣāradalu nūṟahanoṁdu honu
basuṟūra yibbaru taḷada sesabikada bageya sōṇe mariyādeya
yippata·oṁdu honanū kālakālakke yettikoṁḍu yeraḍu ho-
re nirūpadiṁda bhānapa·oḍeyaru yo bhōgapātrada dharmmavanū ·āca-

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(A.R. No. 405 of 1928.)
ON ANOTHER SLAB SET UP IN THE SAME PRAKARA.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1377, Bhāva, Māgha, śu. 5, corresponding to A.D.
1455 January 23 Thursday (not verifiable). It refers itself to the reign of Malli-
kārjunarāya, son of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, ruling from Vijayanagarī. It records that,
when the Mahāpradhāna Paṇḍaridēva was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya, under the
orders of the king, an agreement was effected between the Śeṭṭis of the two
streets viz., Paḍuvakēri and Mūḍakēri in Basarūr, regarding the boundary and the
rights of their respective localities, and the paths through which the Śeṭṭis of each
locality had to take sheep and areca-nut trees to the temple of Dēvī on occasions
of festivals. The interest of the inscription lies in the amicable settlement made by
the people themselves to avoid bloody religious feuds.

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR (NORTH FACE) IN THE NANDI-MANDAPA OF THE MALLIKARJUNA TEMPLE
AT SRISAILAM, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1379, Īśvara, Māgha, ba. 5, Guruvāra, corresponding to A.D.
1458 January 19 Thursday, in the reign of Prauḍha-Dēvarāya-Mahārāya. It records
that the lady reader of the palace named Honnammavva purchased a plot of land
and made a gift of it for offerings to the god Mallikārjuna and for feeding five
Jaṅgamas in the temple.

l.ka:
śvarasaṁvatsarada māgha ba 5 gu lū
śrīmaṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(A.R. No. 406 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE MAHALINGESVARA
TEMPLE AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1387, Pārthiva, Vaiśākha, ba. 5, Bu[dhavāra] corresponding to
A.D. 1465 May 15 Wednesday, in the reign of Prauḍha-Immaḍi-Dēvarāya Malli-
kārjuna-Mahārāya. It records that when Paṇḍaridēva-Oḍeya was ruling over
Bārakūra-rājya under the orders of the Mahāpradhāna Rāmachandra-Daṇāyaka, he
made a gift of the gold, which the Hañjamāna community was paying, for the service
of the god Mahādēva at Paḍuvakēri, on the occasion of a lunar eclipse.

l.ka: śrīgaṇādhipatayē namaḥ | śrīsarasvatyāya namaḥ | nirvighnamastu | namastuṁ-
varttamāna pārthivasaṁvatsarada vayiśākha bahuḷa 5 bu lū śrīmanma-

mrutapaḍi naṁdādīpti yo mūṟu bageya dhar(ṁ)mmake dhāreyaneṟaddu
ge koṭa ·a paḷimariyādeyali bahaṁthā kāṭigadyāna nūṟa-
ṟadu koṭu taṁma svarūciyiṁda ·oḍaṁbaṭu barasi koṭṭa
taṁ padaṁ [||*] vāgduttaṁ manōdattaṁ dhārādattaṁ dinē di-
svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharā[ṁ*] | ṣaṣṭhi-

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(A.R. No. 376 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD NEAR THE AMMUNAJEGUTTA IN POLALI AMMUNAJE,
MANGALORE TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1387, Pārthiva, Siṁha-māsa, 15, Budhavāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1465 August 13, Tuesday (not Wednesday). It refers itself to the reign of
Prauḍha-Mallikārjunadēvarāya-Mahārāya. It records that when Rāmachandra-
Daṇāyka was administering the kingdom under the royal seal and Viṭṭharasa
was governing the Maṇgalūru-rajya, Āllappa-Śēkhara Chauṭa and his three
brothers gave a land to Mañjaṇaśēkhara and his sisters, for offerings to the goddess
Poḷaladēvī.

l.ka: ṁ svasti [||*] śrījayābhyudayaśakavaruṣa 1387 neya vartamāna pārthivasaṁ-
dramadhyarājyava pratipālisuvalli ·āvara mudre·uṁgurava dharisi śrīmanmahāpradhāna rā-
macaṁdradaṇāyakkaru samastarāṇuve pārupathyavanu citteyisi yalli ·āvara nirūpa-
diṁda viṭharasaru maṁgalūra rājyavanāḷuva kāladalu śrīmatu puttigeya jana-
nadalu bāḷuva ·āllappasēkharāda cau[ṭa]ru ·āvara taṁma dēvaruśēkharu bīmaṁ-
rmakkanāyattiyavarige barasikoṭṭa śilāśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare khāṁḍenāḍa baṁgamaṁ-
ju[va] modalāda hiriyaru lōkabeṭṭeṁba bāḷikeyanu bāḷi baṁdalli ·ālliya teru [si]-

ddāya baḍḍi vāsi hoṁnu bhattavanu kālakālake naḍasatīrade yiralāgi ·āruvārava biṭṭu
mu-
ṁde naḍasuvaṁtāgi kaṭikoṁḍa siddhāya gadyāṇa 17 bhata sīme sahaba 30lka mu 236 ba 6
nu varuṣaṁprati naḍasōdake haruvilada nimitta nivage nāvelaru ·oppi yēḷu hoṁnanū bha-
ttadiṁda nūrayeppatāru baḷe ·āranu guṇavamāḍidevu muṁdakke niṁma bāḷikeyalu paṭṭi-
labida kāru bitu baḷḷa 30 ·adalu mu 61 makki kallimaru mu 6 beṭṭu mu 5[3]
·a(ṁ)ntu muḍi tāde mu 120 ·ākṣā[ra]dalu mūḍe nūrayippatanu nīu bāḷi ·ā bāḷi-
keyiṁda poḷaladēviya śrīgaṁdha naṁdādīptige varuṣaṁprati naḍava sīme saṁṇa pratāpa-
gadyāṇa 10 ·ā hattuhonnu dēvara vruṣāpūjeya ·āmṛtapaḍiya bagege sīme bhatta[ba]-

du kaṭṭikoṭṭevu yī hoṁnu bhattavalade mattāvasīme siddhāyada hoṁnu bhattadalagga vi-

māyalaheggaḍe baḷiya birumaṇa koṭāri kōvāri baḷiya pārakāntu yiṁtivarellaroppi
ba-

rasi koṭa śilāśāsana yī śāsanada ·āḍakakke tappidavaruḷḷare poḷaladēviya pratimeyyanu
bhi(ṁ)nnava māḍida

dōṣa kavileyanu kayyāre vadhisida dōṣekke hōharu ·ādu nimitta ·ā bāḷikeya sīme-
yanunaḍa-

si sukhadalu bāḷūvudu | śrī | dānapālanayōrmmadhyē dānāt śrīyōrupālanaṁ |
dānāt svargga-
mavāpnōti pālanādaccutaṁ padaṁ [||*] śubhamastu | maṁgaḷaṁ mahāśrī śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(A.R. No. 390 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE HARIHARA TEMPLE AT KUMBHAKASI,
COONDAPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1389, Sarvajit, Prathama-Bhādrapada, śu. 15
corresponding to A.D. 1467 August 15, Saturday with a lunar eclipse, as stated. It
refers itself to the reign of Virūpāksharāyā-Mahārāya and records that Viṭharasa-
Voḍeya, who was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya under the orders of the Mahāpradhāna
Kāchappa-Daṇṇāyaka, made a grant of gold for the service of the god Mahādēva
at Kuṁbhāsi (the present Kuṁbhakāśi). The gift was made for the prosperity of
the king.

l.ka: śrīgaṇādhipatayē namaḥ | śrīsarasvatyainamaḥ | nirvighnamastu | namastuṁga śiraścu[ṁ]-

gacaṁdracāmaracāravē | trailōkyanagarāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē || svasti śrī-
jayādbhudayaśā-
tsarada prathamabhādrapada śu 15 lu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja paramēśvara śrī-
śrīmahādēvarige nityaśrībaliya kaṭṭaḷege dhāreyaneṟada dharmaśāsanada kramaveṁ-
geyiṁda kāṭi ga 55 kāṭi ·ayivatta·ayidu paḍuva maneya ha[tvā] ra-
hadi modalu ·appaṇeya bageyiṁ kāṭi ga 45 kāṭi nālvatta·a-
yidu kāṭi ·ubhayaṁ kāṭi ga 100 kāṭi nūṟu dēvara paṁjina ·eṁṇeya
honninoḷage kāṭi ga 9 ·akṣaradalu ·oṁbhattu kāṭi paḍuva maneya [hatvā]ranū naṁma
ṇeya bageyiṁ kāṭi ga 6 ·āṟu ·ubhayaṁ paṁjina ·eṁṇeya kaṭaḷege kāṭi [ga]
ṇeya kaṭṭaḷege kāṭi ga 115 ·akṣaradalū nūṟa[hadi]naidu kāṭiyanū nāu [sa]-
• • • • gaḷu ·aṁṇahatvāra paḍuva maneya hatvāra yibarū kūḍi yo dharmma

• • • • lla tappade naḍasi baharu • • • • • • • • • • mahādēva-
rige haṁnera

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(A.R. No. 762 of 1916.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE GOVINDARAJAPERUMAL
TEMPLE AT TIRUPATI, CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1389, Sarvajit, Pushya, śu. 1, Ā[divāra] corresponding to
A.D. 1467 December 27, Sunday. It records the order given by the Sāḷuva Mahāmaṇḍa-
lēśvara Narasiṅgayyadēva-Mahā-arasu that the worship in the Gōvindarāja-Perumāḷ
temple at Tirupati must be conducted on the same lines as those of Tiruvēṅgaḷa-
nātha on the Tirumale hill and that Kandāḷa Rāmānujayya and his pupils should
supervise the distribution of the prasāda to Śrīvaishṇavas, both foreign and
local, in the Gōvindarāja-sannidhi and in the Rāmānujakūṭa (feeding-house)
newly constructed at Tirupati by himself. It also records that the king established a
Rāmānujakūṭa on the hill and fixed the scale of offerings to the god Tiruvēṅgaḷa-
nātha, to be supervised by the same Kandāḷa-Ramānujayya and his disciples.

l.ka:
• • • stu [||*] svasti śrīśakābda 1389 ra mēle naḍeva sarvajitusaṁvatsarada puṣya
śuddha 1 ·ā lū śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvara mēdinī[mīsa]ragaṇḍakaṭhārisāḷuva sāḷuva
narasiṁgayyadēva mahāarasugaḷu tirupatiya stānikarige ·aṭṭida kārya niṁma stāna
ke gōviṁdarājadēvara prasādaprapāptiyanū tirumaleya mariyādeyale nāvellarū
haṁcikoṁḍu jīvisabēkeṁdu nīu namage hēḷalāgi nāu pūrvāparavanu vicārisi
kalveṭṭi pra[mā]ṇanū ·ōdisi

• • • • • [ga]ḷanāthadēvara mariyādeyale gōviṁdarājadēvargge·u vayibhava
naḍeyabēku nalku bageyavaru tirumaleya ·ūḷigavanū māḍabēku gōviṁdarāja-
dēvara naivēdya prasādavano tirupatiyavarige kāṇiparāgi nālku harivāṇaū naṁbi-
gaḷige ·oṁdu harivāṇaū pūrvadalū ·ārōgaṇeya māḍuva paḍikaṭaḷeya ·ārōgaṇeya
harivāṇadalū koṭṭu mikka harivāṇagaḷanū tirumaleya saṁdhiya ·aḍapinaviḷukāḍina-
lū haṁcikoṁbudu nā gōviṁdarājadēvarige makarasaṁkrānti puṁṇyakāladalu
namma hesaralū hostāgi

• • • vēdyada kaṭṭaḷage paḍavīḍarājya muppaḍuveṭu pūṁgoḍuvelada grāmagaḷalū
aramanege ·etti baha jōḍi śūlavarigaḷanū biṭṭu tirupatiya kerege mūnūru hoṁ
nanū koṭu kaṭṭaḷeyanū māḍida nayivēdyake harivāṇa yippattārū ·apapaḍi harivāṇa
voṁdu tirukaṇāmaḍe [1] ke yaraḍara lekhadalū dēvara nayivēdya naḍavahāge kaṭṭaḷeya
māḍida harivāṇada prasādada harivāṇagaḷa voḷage viṭṭavana viḷkāḍige harivāṇa ·ārūvare
appaprasāda hadimūrū ·akāḷiya prasāda

• • • dhivanū namage dharmavāgi tirupatiyalū dakṣiṇatirupatiyalū gōviṁdarāja-
dēvara sa(ṁ)nnidhiyalū nāu kaṭṭista śrīrāmānujakūṭadalū pratidivasagaḷalū svadēsi
paradēsigaḷū śrīvayiṣṇavaragaḷige yikkuvahāge kaṭṭaḷeya māḍidevāgi yo prasāda-
vanū kaṁdāḷa rāmānujayyagaḷa śiṣyapāraṁpareyāgi naṁma rāmānujakūṭake naḍasi
bahudu tirumaleya mēle nāu kaṭista rāmānujakūṭadalū tiruveṁgaḷanāthadēvara
tirumajjanakālakke nāu kaṭaḷeya māḍida karpūra kastūri kuṁku

• • • rivaṭi ·āratiya karpūra ·āḍake ·amudu muṁtāda ·upacāragaḷu naṁma sēveyāgi
kaṁdāḷa rāmānujayyagaḷa śiṣyapāraṁpareyāgi cātāda śrīvayiṣṇavaru sēveya mā-
ḍi bahahāge nāu kaṭṭaḷeya māḍida prakāradali ·ā gōviṁdārājadēvarige tirumajja-
nakāladalū naṁma sēveyāgi naḍasuvahāge rāmānujayyagaḷige kaṭṭaḷeya māḍidevāgi
avara śiṣyapāraṁpareyāgi naḍasi bahadu yidumodalāgi gōviṁdarājadēvaru ·ārō-
gaṇeyanū māḍuva nayivēdyavanellavanū tirumaleya mariyādayale haṁcikoṁbu-
du tirunāḍu tiṁgaḷu divasa modalāda vayibhavagaḷanella

• • • mariyādeyali naḍasikoṁḍu bahudu tirucānūra ·aḷaghiyaperumāḷadēvarige
nāu tiruveṁgaḷanāthadēvara bhaṁḍāradali kaṭṭaḷeya māḍida yaraḍu harivāṇa nayi-
vēdyavanū naḍisibahudu tirupatiyalū pūrvadali tirupatiyavarige naḍeda sāmyagaḷanū
pūrvamariyādeyale naḍeva hāge kaṭṭaḷeya māḍidevāgi nīvellarū tiruveṁgaḷa-
nāthadēvaru gōviṁdarājadēvara sēveyanū māḍikoṁḍu sukhadalu yiruvudu
śrīrāmacaṁdra ||

inscription.head:
No. 463.
(A.R. No. 372 of 1921.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE ALANKARAMANDAPA BEFORE THE TRIPURASAMHARA
IMAGE IN THE TIRUVIRATTANESVARA TEMPLE AT TIRUVADI,
CUDDALORE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The inscription is dated Vikṛiti, Āśvīja, śu. 13, Sōmavāra corresponding to A.D.
1470 October 8, Monday, and records the grant of the villages Satyābharaṇanallūr
and Suṇṇāūru by Aṇṇamarasayya, an officer of the Sāḷuva Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Narasiṅgayyadēva-Mahā-arasu, for offerings to the god Vīraṭadēva of Tiruvadi
during the early morning service and for conducting the car-festival in the month
of Tai. The villages are stated to have been situated in Naḍuvimaṇḍalada-vēṇṭhe,
a sub-division of Vaḷidilambaṭina-chāvaḍi, granted to Aṇṇamarasayya by the king
for amaranāyakatana. It also records the gift of some land to the person supervising
the worship.

l.note: Every line begins with a circle.
l.ka:
vikṛtisaṁvatsarada ·āśvīja śu 13 sōlū śrīma(ṁ)nmahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara mēdinīmīsaragaṁḍa
kaṭhārisāḷuva sāḷvanarasiṁgayyadēvama-

hāarsugaḷa ·avasarada ·aṁṇamarsarū tiruvadiya vīraṭadēvarige koṭṭa dharmaśāsanada paṭe
narasiṁgarājavoḍeyara dharmavāgi nimage na-

yivēdyakke ·udayakāla nayivēdyakke dina ·oṁdakkaṁ ·akki ·oṁdu kalau mēluvecake voṁdu
haṇau yī maryyāde lekhadalū varuṣa 1 kke mūnūṟu ·aṟuvatta kala ·akki·u
mēluvecakke mū-

vatta·āṟuhoṁnū tayi māsa • • cāra nitya māḍisuvaṁtāgi naḍava tirunāḷige yī
tirunāḷada tirunāḷavina haṁneraḍuke saluva veca ·aṟuvattu hoṁnu ·u

bhayaṁ veccatoṁbhattu ·āru honnū ·ā muṁnūra ·aṟuvattu kalake sērisi koṭidu na(ṁ)mma
amaranāyakatana vaḷidilaṁbaṭinacāvaḍiya naḍuvimaṁḍalada vēṁṭeya-

da tribhuva[na] mahadēvipaṭana satyābharaṇanallūra grāma 1 kaṁ rēkhe ga 46 naḍuvikara paṭṭina
suṁṇāūru kāluvaḷi saha grāma 1 rēkhe ga 50 ·ubhayaṁ grāma 2 kaṁ

rēkhe ga 96 ·akṣāradalu grāma yeraḍakkaṁ toṁbhattu ·āṟu hoṁnigaṁ yo grāmada catu-
ssīmege saluva naṁje punje vānpayiru punpayiru vāsiluvari vāluva-

ri sekukaḍame taṟikaḍame ·āsuvapudu makaḷakaḍime māvaḍe maravaḍe kuḷavaḍe sāraḍe duḷira
vaṭa kaṭaḷevaṭa ·ēri vonabele saṁte ·uṁṭāda hala ·upādhigaḷū saha sērsi
koṭṭevā-

gi yā grā[ma]dali [u*]ḷa ścatussīmegaḷalli ·uṁṭāda nidhi nikṣēpa jala pāṣāṇa ·akṣīṇi
āgāmi siddha sādhyaveṁba ·aṣṭabhōga tēja svāmyavanū ·āgumāḍikoṁḍu dēva-

ra nityaneyivēdyavanū tayimāsada rathōtsahavāda tirunāḷanū naḍasi sukhadalū ·anubha-
visūdu saṁka ·eḍadoṟe ·ubhayamārga horagāstānamānya

pūrvvamaryyāde ·endu koṭṭa dharmmaśāsanapaṭe yo ·ubhayava naḍasuvadakke sutaṁtravāgi
periya·oḍeyāru namaśśivāyadēvastānada kōyilu kēḷkumoda

• • • • • • • • • • naṁjaneladalū [mara] hoṟatāgi ·ā vūra ·aḷateyali suṁṇā-
ūrali mā 2 satyābharaṇanalūra

naṁjenelanu mā 2 ·ubhayaṁ nela • • • • • yāgi ·anubhavisikoṁḍu pūje nama-
skāravanū māḍikoṁḍu sukhadali yihudu [||*] dānapālanayōrma-

ubaḷayittu ·annamarsana baraha [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 464.
(A.R. No. 427 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF A HOUSE TO THE SOUTH OF THE TANK,
DODDAKEREKATTE AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1395, Nandana, Bhādrapada, ba. 8, Bu[dhavāra] corresponding
to A.D. 1472 August 26, Wednesday, in the reign of Virūpāksha-Mahārāya ruling
from Vijayanagara. It records that, while Viṭṭharasa was ruling over Bārākura-
rājya, Duggaṇa-Seṭṭi of Paḍuvakēri at Basurūr and his three nephews made a grant
of land to Siriyappa on the latter's return from his pilgrimage to Benares, for the
worship of the image of the god Kāśī-Viśvanātha which he had brought with him.

l.ka:
śrīgaṇādhipatayē namaḥ | śrīgurubhyō(ṁ)nnamaḥ | śrīsarasvattyai namaḥ | nirvvighnamastu
namastuṁgaśiraścuṁbicaṁdracā-

maracāravē trayilōkyanagarāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē || svasti śrījayābhyudaya-
śakavarṣa 1394 saṁdu ·a-

yidaneya naṁdanasaṁvatsarada bhādrapada ba 8 bu lū śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamē-
śvara śrīvīrapratāpa virupā-

kṣamahārāyarū vijayanagarada siṁhāsanadaliddu sakalasāṁbrājyavanū ·āḷuttiha kāla-
dalli ·avara nirū-

padiṁda viṭhṭharasavoḍeyarū bārakūra rājyavanu pratipālisutaṁviha kāladalli | basuṟūra
paḍuvakēriya sāva-

ṁṇasēnabōvara maga baḷḷābaḷiya duggaṇaseṭṭiyaru ·avara ·aḷiyaṁdiru ·akkaseṭṭitiya
maga sōvaṇaseṭṭi bāli-

yakkaseṭṭittiya maga nāgaṇaseṭṭi [sā]vakkaseṭṭitiya maga dēuseṭṭi ·iṁtī mūvaru
tāya makkaḷu ·ā duggaṇaseṭṭiyu

·iṁtī nālvarū voḍaṁbaṭṭu javarupāliya tirumalenāthaseṭṭara makkaḷu siriyappagaḷu
vāraṇāsige hōgi ·ā

vāraṇāsiyiṁda viśvanāthadēvaranu paṁcapūje sahitavāgi vijayaṁgeyisikoṁḍu baralāgi
ā vāraṇāsi siriyappaga[ḷi]-

ge sūryyōparāgapuṇyakāladalli ·ā duggaṇaseṭṭiyarū ·avara ·aḷiyaṁdirū dānadhārā-
pūrvvakavāgi dhāreyanū ·e-

ṟadukoṭṭa dharmmada bāḷina vivara | guḍḍaṁpāḍiya [ka]ṟikeya kaḷiyalli nālvaṁḍeyali
muvattanālku muḍi ·akki gēṇi yikkuva bā-

ḷu ·ā bāḷina catussīmeya vivara mūḍalu daṇṇira sāliṁdaṁ paḍuvalu teṁkalū daṇṇira
sāliṁdaṁ baḍagalu | pa[ḍu]-

[va]lu ·uppunīra hoḷeyiṁdaṁ mūḍalu baḍagalu kaṟikeya ·a(ṁ)ṇṇahebāruvana gaḍiyiṁdaṁ
dharmmada bāḷi-

ṁdaṁ teṁkalu ·iṁtī catussīmeya ·oḷaguḷa sarvvabāḷu mara phala ·anubha[kkaṁ] mūvatta-
nālku muḍi • • • •

nālku muḍi ·akki teṟanū kaḷeci śuddha mūvattu muḍi ·akki mattaṁ hatāvarada ·oḷage ·adhi-
vāsada • • • mūvattu muḍi

bhatta gēṇi bittuva bāḷu ·ā bāḷina catussīmeya vivara mūḍalu [e]ḷaṟeya sāvaṁtana
gaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu teṁka-

lu • • • • • toḷahara baḷiya māṇikkaseṭṭiya gaḍiyiṁdaṁ baḍagalu | paḍuvalu
mudda haḷiya nāyakana [ga]ḍi-

yiṁdaṁ mūḍalu | baḍagalu kāḍiṁdaṁ teṁkalu | ·iṁtī catussīmeya ·oḷaguḷa sarvvabāḷu
mane ṭhāu mara

phala ·anubha | mattaṁ kaṁdāvarada ·oḷage halakāveyalli duggaṇaseṭṭiyara mūlada bāḷu
hiriyagadde 1 kka-

ṁ nālkuvare muḍi mattaṁ kruṣṇaupādhyara kayyali koṁḍadu • • • ṇikaneṁba
gadde 1 kkam ·oṁduvare muḍi mattaṁ ·ā beṭṭina ·o-

ḷage • • • • ·akki saha hanneraḍu muḍi tāṟuvāriya bageya beṭṭina ·oḷage ·āṟu
muḍi ·a(ṁ)ntū ·ippattanālku muḍi gade

• • • ṇiya bhatta nālvaṁḍeyalū muḍi ·ayivattu mattaṁ rāmana koḍalu saha |
a(ṁ)ntu yo mūṟu bageya bāḷige gēṇiya ·akki

muḍi 70 ·eppattu muḍi ·akki gēṇi yikuva bāḷanū ·ā siriyappagaḷē teṟu dēvasvavanū
tettu • • • va pūjeyanū māḍi-

koṁḍu dīvaḷigeyahabbada mūṟudinada raṁgapūjeyanū naḍasikoṁḍu bāḷuvaru mattaṁ
ā viśvanāthadēvara naṁdādīptige ·ā

inscription.head:
No. 465.
(A.R. No. 428 of 1928.)
ON ANOTHER SLAB SET UP IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1395, Nandana, Bhādrapada, ba. 8, Budhavāra, corresponding
to A.D. 1472 August 26, Wednesday, in the reign of Virūpāksha-Mahārāya. It
records that, when Viṭṭharasa-Oḍeya was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya, Baḷḷābaḷi
Duggaṇa-Seṭṭi son of Nāraṇa-Sēnabōva of Basarūr, and his three nephews, built a
maṭha and endowed it with some plots of land.

l.ka:
śrīgaṇādhipata·eṁ nama[ḥ*] śrīsarasvatyāeṁ nama[ḥ*] śrī gurubhyō nama[ḥ*] nirvighnama-
stu | namastuṁ-

bādrapada ba 8 bu

lū śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrivīrapratāpa virupākṣamahārāyarū vijaya-
nagariya siṁhāsanadalliddu saka-

lasā(ṁ)mrājyavanū ·āḷuttiha kāladalli ·avara nirūpadinda viṭhṭharasa·oḍeyarū bārakūra
rājyavanū pratipālisutti-

hāne gaddeyiṁ heggana

gaddeya gaḍiyiṁ paḍuvalu teṁkalu keḷagaṇa mallagaddeyiṁ baha ceṁmārara ·ōṇiyiṁ mū |

inscription.head:
No. 466.
This No. should be read(A.R. No. 371 of 1921.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE ALANKARAMANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
IN THE TIRUVIRATTANESVARA TEMPLE AT TIRUVADI, CUDDALORE TALUK, SOUTH
ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1391 (mistake for 1393), Khara, Vaiśākha, śu. 10, corresponding
probably to A.D. 1472 April 29, Monday (not verifiable). It records the gift of the
taxes collected from the village Mosukoḷattūr, hamlet of Tribhuvana-Mahādēvipaṭṭana
in Vaḷidilambaṭṭu-chāvaḍi, by Timmi-Nāyaka, the agent of Aṇṇamarasayya, who was
the deputy of Sāḷuva Narasiṅgayyadēva-Mahā-arasu, for the expenses connected with
the Vasanta-festival of the god Vīraṭadēva at Tiruvadi.

l.ka:
svasti [||*] śrīvijayādbhudayaśakavaruṣa 1391 neya mēle naḍeva kharasaṁvatsarada vayi-
śākha śu 10 lū śrīmanmahāmaṁḍaḷēśvara mēdinīmēsaragaṁḍa kaṭhārisāḷuva sā-
ḷuva narasiṁgayyadēvama-

hāarasugaḷu ·avasarada ·aṁṇamarasayyana kāryyake karttarāda ti(ṁ)mmināyakaru tiru-
vadiya vīraṭadēvarigū koṭṭa dharmaśāsanada paṭe ·aṁṇamarssayyana dharmmavāgi vayi-
śākhamāsadalū

naḍava vasaṁtatirunāḷa ·ubhayakke dhvajārōhaṇa ·uṭara vayiśākha śu 5 ·ārabhya ·a śu 15

172 || 1 || 3 kaṁ rē visiṣṭavāgi guttige rēkheyāgi naḍadu bahudu 125 ·akṣā-

ḷa ·ubhakke sērisi koṭṭevāgi ·ī grāmada catussīmege saluva naṁje puṁje vānpayiru
pupanṛ-

yiru vāsiluvari vāluvari śakukaḍime taṟikaḍame māvaḍe maravaḍe kaḷuvaḍe nāraḍe baḷuku
nīru ·ūrasante modalāda hala ·upādhigaḷu saha nidhi nikṣēpa jala pāṣāṇa ·akṣīṇi

siddha sādhyaveṁba ·aṣṭabhōgatējasāmyagaḷanu sarvamānyavāgi ·ācaṁdrārkkasthāyi-

deṁdu koṭa dharmaśāsanapaṭṭe | dānapālanayōrmmadhyē dānāśrēyōnupālanaṁ dā-
[nā*]tsarggamavāpnōti pālanādaccutaṁ padaṁ [||*] svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō
harēti vasuṁdharā[ṁ |*] ṣaṣṭiṁ varṣasahasrā-

inscription.head:
No. 467.
(A.R. No. 371-A of 1921.)
IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is dated in the cyclic year Khara and records the gift of some plots of land
by the same individual as the one mentioned in No. 465 for burning five perpetual
lamps in the same temple.

l.ka:
vikṛtisaṁvatsarake saluva kharasaṁvatsara padinēḷane śrīmatu ·aṁṇamaresayyana kāryyake
kartarāda tiṁmanāya[ka*]ru ·alliya vīradēvara guḍiya sthānikarige samasta
• • • • kāryya nāu vīraṭadēvarige baṁdu tiru-

nāḷige biṭṭa tribhuvanamahādēvipaṭṭana mosaguḷattūru grāmadalu tiṁgaḷige tirinaṁdādī
vigege naṁjene[la*]dali koṭṭudu nāḍa ·aḷu·uva kōlu mā 5 ·akṣāradalū ·ayidu
māu naṁje nelavanū sarvamānyavāgi

koṭṭevāgi ·ī dīvige ·ayidanū tapade yikki yo ·ayidu mā nelanū niṁma putrapautra-
pāraṁparyyavāgi ·ācaṁtrarkkasthāyiyāgi ·anubhavisikoṁ[bu*]du ·eṁdu koṭṭa
śile saubharayi[tu] ti(ṁ)mmana vrālu ||

inscription.head:
No. 468.
(A.R. No. 393 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BY THE SIDE OF A HOUSE TO THE EAST OF THE TANK,
DEVARAKERE AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1395, Nandana, Bhādrapada, ba 8, Bu[dhavāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1472 August 26, Wednesday, in the reign of
Virūpāksha-Mahārāya. It records that, while Viṭṭharasa-Voḍeya was ruling
over Bārakūra-rājya, Baḷḷābali Duggaṇa-Seṭṭi, and his nephews Sōvaṇa-Seṭṭi, son of
Akka-Seṭṭiti, Nāgaṇa-Seṭṭi, son of Bāliyakka-Seṭṭiṭi, and Dēvu-Seṭṭi son of Nāvakka-
Seṭṭiti, made a grant of some plots of land to Siriyappa, son of Tirumalanātha-Seṭṭi,
for the worship of the god Viśvanātha, which he had brought from Benares, when he
returned from his pilgrimage to that place. The gift was made on the occasion of a
solar eclipse.

l.ka:
śrīgaṇādhipatayē namaḥ | śrīgurubhyōnnamaḥ | śrī sarasvatyai namaḥ | nirvighnamastu || nama-
stuṁgaśiraścuṁbicaṁdra cā-

maracāravē | trayilōkyanagarāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē || svasti śrījayābhyudaya-
śakavarṣa 1394 saṁdu ·a-

kṣamahārāyarū vijayanagarada siṁhāsanadaliddu sakala sāṁbrājyavanū ·āḷuttiha kāladalli
avara nirū-

padiṁda viṭhṭharasavoḍeyarū bārakūra rājyavanū pratipālisutaṁviha kāladalli | basuṟūra

ge sūryyōparāga puṇyakāladalli ·āduggaṇaseṭṭiyarū ·avara ·aḷiyaṁdirū dānadhārā-

inscription.head:
No. 469.
(A.R. No. 380 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BY THE SIDE OF THE EASTERN VERANDA IN THE OUTER
PRAKARA OF THE KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, SAME TALUK AND
DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This much damaged inscription is dated Śaka 1400, Viḷambi, Kanyā, 21,
Ādivāra, corresponding to A.D. 1478 September 20, Sunday, in the reign of
Dēvarāya-Mahārāya-Virūpāksharāya. It records that, when Viṭṭhararasa-Oḍeya
was governing Bārakūra-rājya, a certain Mādavayya made a grant of land, for the
service of some god (name lost).

l.ka:
• • • • • • • • • • [intī] catuḥsīme sa • • • • ba • •
niya naḍuve • • • viṭhṭharsavoḍe

inscription.head:
No. 470.
(A.R. No. 403 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB, BROKEN IN THREE PIECES AND LYING IN THE CHOULTRY ATTACHED
TO THE TEMPLE OF THE GODDESS MUKAMBIKA AT KOLLUR, COONDAPUR
TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1404, Plava, Māgha, śu. 15, Ādityavāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1482 February 3 Sunday, in the reign of Virūpāksharāya ruling from
Vidyānagarī. It records a gift of land made by the Sāvanta Baṅki-Arasa and Honna-
kaṁbaḷi for offerings to the goddess Mūkāṁbikādēvī at Kollūru, while Paṇḍaridēva
was governing Bārakūra-rājya under the orders of the Mahāpradhāna Siṅgaṇa-
Daṇṇāyaka. Sihga-Oḍeya the Sthānakarta of the temple is praised for his ascetic
qualities.

l.ka: ṁ śrīgaṇādhipatē namaḥ | śrīsarasvatyāyai namaḥ śrīguru-
stāṁ triṇētrāṁ hēmābjasthāṁ pītavastrāṁ prasa(ṁ)nnāṁ | dēvīṁ durgā-
kavaruśa 1404 neya saṁda vartamāna plavasaṁvatsarada mā[gha]
śrīmūkāṁbikādēvi taṁna sthānake kartara māḍikoṁḍu sthānavanu ·ū-
ḷeyara baḷige samarththaru kadariya mageya beṁmacasāvaṁta-
da kramaveṁteṁdare yo ·amrutapaḍige biṭṭa bāḷine catussī-
meya vivara mukādēvikāsthānada samopadali muḍalu hari-
da hoḷeyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu teṁkalu halasina māni kūla-
reguṁḍiya harida hoḷeyiṁdaṁ muḍalu baḍagalu hari-
da hoḷeyiṁda teṁkalu yiṁtī catussīmeya ·oḷaguḷḷa dēvā-
lyada beṭṭina baḍagalu bittuva bījavari hāne nālvattaṟa lekka-
dalu nālvattu muḍe gadde kalukaṭige makki bittuva bījavari mu
la pāṣāṇa siddha sādhya yēnuḷaṁthādanu sarvamānyava • • • •
ṁ naḍadu baha ·akki mukāṁbikādēviya sthānakke kartaru si[ṁ]-
gappa·oḍeyaru maṭhakke mu 2 gaddege naḍadu baha ·akki mu • •
hottina śrībalige māḍida kaṭṭaḷeya vivara mēluśāṁti kī-
ḷuśāṁti bali dēvara hoṟuva brahmaṇa saha ·āṁtu muvaru
·aḍigaḷu ·avarige biṭṭa bāḷu kaṁbaḷa gadde kusiya tarada gade-
gade gēṇi ·akki mu 54 bali kalacaruvige hirigade ·ottina
talabuva muḍalu hoḷe ·ottina hāḷigaḷu 7 saha ha 60 ga-
de gēṇi ·akki mu 6 || dēvaḍigaḷige yeṟadu koṭṭa vivara mo[da]va-
ṁtebaḷiya yekkanāthige mu 8 gade ·ā gadeyiṁda nitya nālvā-
ne ·akki naivēdya naḍasuva vādya haḷage maddaḷe 2 gaṁgara baḷiya
duggaṇage mu 8 gade ·ā gaddeyiṁda nitya nālvāne ·akki naivēdya
naḍasuva vādya maddaḷe kiṟugāḷe 2 moḷaku baḷiya dēuge
mu 4 gadde ·ā gaddeyiṁda nitya yippāne ·akki naivēdya naḍasu vā-
dya tāḷa 1 toḷahara baḷiya cimmaṇage mu 8 gadde honnaṇi
1 ke makkiya ·oḷage mu 3 ·ā gaddeyiṁda nitya nālvāne ·akki naivē-
dya naḍava vādya jēgaṭe kiṟugāḷe 2 ·aṁtu nitya ha 22 ·akki
naḍaudu yo dharmasthaḷada bāḷige maṭhake śāṁtige naḍava ·akki ga-
nūṟaṁndu kapileya koṁda pāpakke hōharu sāvira ghaṭṭa-
dali naḍava hēṟinettina pāpakke hōharu vēdapālakarāgidaṁ-
thā sāvirada[nū]ṟa·oṁdu maṁdi brāhmaranu gōkarnadali koṁ-
da pāpakke hōharu yiṁdraḥ pru(ḥ)chatu cāṁḍālī kimidaṁ pa-
cyetē mayā | svamā[ṁ*]saṁ surayā siktaṁ kapālēna citāgninā | dēva-
[bhītyā] carmaṇā pihita(ṁ) mmayā [||*] svadattaṁ vā paradattaṁ vā yō
yatē krimi[ḥ*] || yo dharmasthaḷada ·amrutapaḍige dēvaḍigaḷige biṭṭa
yo dēvaḍigaḷu dēvālyake kalumarana hoṟabēku hōda baṁ-
dalli kaṭṭi hoḍasuvaru biṭṭi yettu kaṭṭina hullusalla [||*] dānapā-
ruḍu kōla baḷiya ·oppa maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrīśrīśrī

inscription.head:
No. 471.
(A.R. No. 421 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE TEMPLE OF VENKATARAMANA
AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1404, Pramōdūta, Vaiśākha, śu. 1. Śaka 1404 corresponded
to the cyclic year Śubhakṛit in which year the details cited give the equivalent
A.D. 1482 April 19 Friday (not verifiable). It refers itself to the reign of
Bhujabala-Narasiṅgarāya-Mahārāya and states that when Mallapa-Nāyaka,
brother of Sōmaṇṇa-Nāyaka of Kuṟugōḍ, was ruling over the Bārakūra-rājya, a
number of residents of Basaṟūr made gifts of land and money for the service of the
god Tirumaladēva at the Paḍuvakēri of Basaṟūr.

l.ka: saṁdu nālkaneya vartamāna pramōdūtasaṁvatsarada vayiśākha śu 1
ṇanāyakara ta(ṁ)mma mallapanāyakaru bārakkūra rājyavanāḷuva kāla-
gi paḍuvakēriya halarū māḍida [dha]rmada vivara hiriyakuduralū paḍuva kudu-
ralū ·a • ra catusīmeya vivara mūḍalu harida hoḷeyiṁda paḍuvalu teṁkalu
ṁda mūḍalu baḍagalu harida sāliṁda teṁkalu yī nālku catuḥsīmeyiṁda vo-
ḷage ·uḷḷa monera maṁjana kayyali mūlava koṁḍa kaḷi sahavāgi halara bageya kaḷi
hoḷe ·uṁṭādadanu | gaddeya māḍi bittuva bījavari muḍi 30 ·a mūvattu mu-
ḍi gadege gēṇi nālvaṁḍeyalū ·akki ha [ē]ḷuvare hāneyali muḍi 75 ·a-

vattuhāne gadege gēṇi ·akki mu 2 mattaṁ tirumaladēvarige tiṁmayyagaḷu ·uḍupabha-

ṟu varahanavōpādiyalū ·ā ṭhāvili sasiya naṭṭare sasige nālku haṇa geṁḍege vo-
ṁdu hoṁnina vōpādiyalū ·uḍupabhaṭṭarū kommayabhaṭṭarū koṭu ba-
haru hiriyakuduroḷage halaru koṭṭa hosakaḷiyoḷage kokoci tiṁma·oḍeya-
rige gadde muḍi 3 ·adaṟa paḍuvottira sōmōparāgapuṁṇyakāladalu tiṁmayya-

ra gade ma 1 || 0 ge ·ārdhavaraha ga 16 || mataṁ mūḍakuduralū ·uḍupabhaṭṭara hoṁ-

seṭi ·aṣṭaguṇavāgi māḍikoṁḍa gade mu 6 ke gēṇi ·akki mu 9 mattaṁ ·ayiduvare tāre

inscription.head:
No. 472.
(A.R. No. 710 of 1917.)
ON THE SOUTH BASE OF THE RAMASVAMI TEMPLE AT RAMAPURAM,
MADAKASIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1407, Viśvāvasu, Āśvīja, śu • • (= A.D. 1485
September). Rāyaparāja, son of Bayarājadēva-Chōḷa-Mahā-arasu, is said to have
made a gift of land and the channel called Śaṅkaradēvana-kāluve to Narasiṁha-
Sōmayāji, Prasanna-Sōmayāji and Lakshmaṇa-Sōmayāji, sons of Daśagranthi-Nara-
siṁha-Sōmayāji of Āśvalāyana-sūtra, Kāśyapa-gōtra and Ṛik-śākhā on the condition
that they would convert the valley adjoining the field into a tank and call it
Narasāmbudhi after the name of king Narasiṅgarāya-Mahārāya and that they would
allot a portion of the land for the midday offerings to the god Rāmachandradēva.
The land was situated in Agaḷi in the Rāyadurga-sthala.

l.ka:
śubhamastu | svasti śrījayādyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1407 neya viṣvāvasu-

sugaḷu makaḷu rāyapparājagaḷu ·āśvalāyanasūtrada kāsyapagōtrada rukhuśākheya
madhusūdanapurada daśagraṁthi narasiṁhasōmayājigaḷa makkaḷu narasiṁhasōmayāji-
gaḷu prasaṁnasōmayājigaḷu lakṣmaṇasōmayājigaḷige koṭṭa

dharmmaśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare namma nāyakatanakke saluva rāyadurgada staḷada ·agaḷiya
kereya hiṁdaṇa kariya • • • yalu • • • • • vittu ·a haḷavanu nīu
kereya kaṭṭikoṁḍēvenalāgi ·a haḷavanu ·a sīmeyalu hadinayidu hoṁnina holānu
śaṁkharadēvana kāluveyanu sūryyagrahaṇapu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalu raghunāthana saṁnidhiyalu
dhāreneredu koṭṭevāgi ·ā staḷadalu narasiṁgarāyama-

hārāyara hesaralu sarasāṁbudhi ·eṁba hesaralu kereya kaṭṭikoṁḍu ·ā kereya gaddegaḷa ·aḷate-
yalu nākhaṁḍuga gadenu • • • varugaḷige bhāga mūrakke mūghaṁḍuga rāma
caṁdradēvarige madhyā(ṁ)hnadavasarake bhāgi vūṁdakke khaṁḍuga gaddeyanu ·anubhavisalu-
ḷavaru | ·a kāladalu dhāreneṟadu koṭṭa holananu dīkṣitaru tāvē ·anubhavisaluḷavaru
kereya voḷagaṇa bomagoṁ-

ḍana kāluve kōḍiseṭi māḍida hariyasamudrada holananu nimage grāmagrāsavāgi
grāsa vāsasthaḷagaḷige naḍayade yiralāgi nāu nimage dhāreyaneṟadu koṭṭevāgi
a hariyasamudrada holadali huṭṭida ·accukaṭṭu niṁma nāghaṁḍuga gaddeya keḷage
muṁde huṭṭidaṁtha ·accukaṭṭu hariyasamudrada hola dīkṣitaru rāmacaṁdradē-

varu sariyāgi ·anubhavisaluḷavaru | gaḷe ·aḷateya cāturbhāgake viṁgaḍisida nāghaṁḍuga gade
hoṟatāgi modalu dīkṣitarige viṁgaḍisida hola hoṟatāgi • ta mayali huṭuva
accukaṭṭanu sariyāgi ·anubhavisaluḷavaru gadeya veccavanu sari

bhāgeya gadeyavoḷage koḍaluḷavarū ·eṁdu koṭṭa dharmaśāsana | rāyaparājagaḷa voppu
śrīrāmacaṁdra ||

inscription.head:
No. 473.
(A.R. No. 391 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE MAHALINGESVARA TEMPLE AT
BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1409, Plavaṅga, Kārtika, śu. 5, Ravivāra, corresponding to A.D.
1487 October 21, Sunday, in the reign of Pratāparāya-Mahārāya son of Virūpāksha-
rāya-Mahārāya. It records that, while Virūpākshadēva-Oḍeya was governing the
Bārakūra-rājya, Rudrākshi-Oḍeya, the guru of the Seṭṭikāṟas, built a maṭha at the
Paḍuvakēri of Basarūr and that the Seṭṭis endowed the maṭha with plots of land.
The boundaries of the lands and their annual income are given.

l.ka: gasaṁvachsarada kārtika śu 5 ravivāradalū śrīmaṁnumahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrī-
kāladalli ·ā rāyara nirūpadiṁda virūpākṣadēva·oḍeyaru bārakūra rājyavanu
pratipālisutaṁviha kāladalli basūṟūra paḍuvakēriyya ·oḷage śrīmahādēvara sa-
ṁnidhiyali paḍuvakēriyya halaru seṭṭikāṟarige gurustanavāgidda rudrakṣi·oḍeyaru
maṭhavanu kaṭṭisi ·ācaṁdrārkkavāgi ·ā maṭhada dhar(ṁ)mmake barasidaṁthā śilāsāsanada viva-
ya mūlada gaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalū teṁkalu hoḷeyiṁdaṁ baḍagalū paḍu-
valu hoḷeyiṁdaṁ mūḍalu baḍagalū nāgaṁṇameraṭṭana maṭhada dharmada
śilāśasanada gaḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu paḍuvalu yiṁtī catussīmeyiṁdo-
ḷaguḷa hiriyya ·āgara ·oṁ 1 du ccika ·āgara ·oṁ 1 du yidaṟa kaṭṭu kaṟike mara pha-
la sahavāgi teṁginamara sahavāgi paḍuvakēriya halarū rudrakṣe·oḍeyari-
ge mūlava koṭṭa ·āgarada mēle ·ayivara dharma naḍavadu halukaḍiya ·oḷage ·ā
hitila cattusīmeya vivara muḍalu ·ūrōṇiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu teṁkalu ye[ḷa]-
da dhareyiṁdaṁ baḍagalu paḍuvalu naḍuva ·ōṇiyiṁda ·aṁṇamuliya hitilagaḍiyi-
ṁdaṁ muḍalu baḍagalu jagadārādhyara gaḍi ·āṁṇamuliya hitila gaḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu

yiṁtī catusīmeyiṁda ·oḷage ·uḷa hittilu yēnu ·uḷaṁthādu yi maṭṭhake mataṁ māruva
gōḷi-
ya ·oḷage ·ā hittila catusīmeya vivara muḍalu narutivabhaṭṭana maneya gaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍu-
valu teṁkalu ·agaḷa gaḍiyiṁdaṁ baḍagalu paḍuvalu ·āgaḷa gaḍiyiṁdaṁ muḍalu baḍagalu
keṟeyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu yiṁtī catusīmeya ·oḷage ·uḷa hittilu ·aṁtte yi yeraḍu mūlada

hittilu ·oḍeyara maṭṭake muvara dharmake māḍidudu mattaṁ ·oḷamāḍina ·oḷage nāyi nāre-
ya

dharmada baḷina kaṁṭṭada mēlaṇa teṁginamara rudrākṣi·oḍeyara maṭṭake ·obana dharmma naḍavadu
matta
[u]daya tāḷuvana baḍaga tayipāḍiya ·oḷage mūli mādaṁṇaseṭiya ·aḷiya beṁmase-
ṭṭi rudrakṣeya·oḍeyara maṭhake māḍida dhar(ṁ)ma yīcanā biṁnāṇiya maga kēśabiṁnāṇi
subramāṇyabiṁnāṇiya maga sakruṣnabiṁnāṇi yivaribara mūla rudrakṣe·oḍeyara ·a-

ṁdāpurada ·oḷage koṭṭa ·adhivāsada ·oḷage muḍi mādaṁṇaseṭiya beṁmaseṭṭi rudrākṣe·o-

ḷe yera-

akṣa-
radalu ·ayivattu ho[ṁ*]nu yi bāḷalarddha baṁdarū ·urati baṁdarū ·oḍeyamaṭṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 474.
(A.R. No. 719 of 1917.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SANKARESVARA TEMPLE AT AGALI,
MADAKASIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1420, Piṅgala, Chaitra, śu. 15, Śanivāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1497 March 18, Saturday. It records the grant of the village Paḷiganahaḷḷi,
surnamed Immaḍi-Kāchāpura, situated in Agaḷi-sthala in Rāyadurga-Chāvaḍi, by
Immaḍi-Kāchapa-Nāyaka, son of Kāchapa-Nāyaka of Ādavāni, for the offerings of the
god Śaṅkaradēva at Agaḷi. The gift was made in the presence of the god Rāmēśvara
at Sētubandha-Rāmēśvara, whither he had gone with Narasaṇa-Nāyaka. It is stated
that Kāchapa-Nāyaka held the division of Agaḷi-sthala as a fief from Narasiṅgarāya-
Mahārāya who had bestowed on Narasaṇṇa-Nāyaka the Rāyadurga-Chāvaḍi for
his nāyakatana.

inscription.head:
No. 475.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1915.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE FLOOR OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE MALLIKARJUNA-
SVAMI TEMPLE AT SRISAILAM, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is not dated. It records that king Vīra-Narasiṅgarāya-Mahārāya is serving
at the feet of the god Mallikārjunadēva. It is not possible to say whether the king
to whose reign the inscription belongs was a Sāḷuva or Tuḷuva. Below this inscrip-
tion, are written names of some devotees, in Dēvanāgarī characters.

·ānaṁdarāvabhagavaṁta
kā pītāṁbaraśyāma

inscription.head:
No. 476.
(A.R. No. 423 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE TEMPLE OF VENKATARAMANA
AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1428, Prabhava, Kārtika, śu. 15, Śa[nivāra], corresponding to
A.D. 1506 October 31, Saturday; Prabhava is a mistake for Kshaya which was
Śaka 1428 expired. The inscription refers itself to the reign of Vīra-Narasiṅgadēva-
rāya-Mahārāya. It records that Basavarasa-Voḍeya raised, as subscription from the
villagers of Baḍākere in Kandāura, some money and made a grant of it to the
temple of the god Tirumaladēva for burning a perpetual lamp before the god out
of the interest on the sum. It also states that some other persons made a grant of
gadyāṇas 25, being the annual income of a garden, for the service of the same god;
it further registers a gift made by the Halaru of Baḍākere of varāhas 7 got as
interest from the Brāhmans of the place on a certain sum outstanding on them as
a loan, for the service of the god.
