inscription.head:
No. 477.
(A.R. No. 703 of 1919.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE LAKSHMI TEMPLE AT GULYA, ALUR TALUK,
BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1430, Śukla, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 10, Guruvāra, corresponding to A.D.
1509 July 26 Thursday; the Śaka year, however, was 1431 expired. The record refers
itself to the reign of the Vijayanagara king Kṛishṇadēvarāya-Mahārāya. It states
that Narasarāja, son of Gōparāja and Kasavamāmbā, grandson of Nāgarāja and
Gavuramāmbā, of Kauṇḍinya-gōtra, Yajuś-śākhā and of the Kamme sect of
Brāhmans, the chief of the town Yēṭūru in Gaṇḍikōṭe-sīme and the minister of
Vīraṇa-Nāyaka, son of the Sēnādhipati Karaṇada Sōmaṇṇa-Nāyaka, caused
the construction of the temple of Virūpāksha consisting of the garbha-gṛiha, sukhanāsi
and raṅga-maṇḍapa, had a well sunk and a mango-garden planted. He also made a
grant of land in Virupasamudra for the service of the god.

l.ka: na tōpi kāya pala bāviya baḷi-

inscription.head:
No. 478.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED BEHIND THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT BELAGUPPA,
KALYANADRUG TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated • • • • Śukla, Pushya, ba. 2, the Śaka year being
badly damaged. It refers itself to the reign of Vīra-Narasiṅgadēva-Mahārāya and
records that Haḍapada-Gaura-Nāyaka made a grant of the village Beḷuguppe,
surnamed Vīra-Narasiṅgadēva-Mahārāyapura, situated in the division of (?) Būduguppe
in Kundurupe-sīme to the aseetic Mallikārjunadēva as a maṭha-pura. The gift was
made for the merit of the king.

siṁgadēvama-

inscription.head:
No. 479.
(A.R. No. 410 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE MAHALINGESVARA
TEMPLE AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1433, Śukla, Māgha, śu. 5 corresponding to A.D. 1510 January
14 Monday (not verifiable), in the reign of Bhujabala-Narasiṁga-Mahārāya ruling
from Vijayanagarī. It records that, under the orders of the king, Mallapa-Nāyaka,
younger brother of Sōvaṇa-Nāyaka of Kuṟugōḍ, who was ruling over Bārakūru-rājya
collected kāṇike from the merchants of Paḍuvakēri in Basarūr for offerings and other
services of the god Mahādēva and himself made a grant of the village Paḍuva-Belatūr
in Hāru-nāḍu and Mūḍa-Belatūr. It also records that on Tuesday, ba. 8 of
Chaitra, Ī'svara, Saṅkamma-Modaliti, daughter of the grand-daughter of Bemmaṇa-
Bhaṇḍāri, made a gift of 60 varāhas and an yearly grant of a fixed quantity of
rice for the service of the same god.

inscription.head:
No. 480.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1913.)
ON A STONE NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT YALPI, BELLARY TALUK,
BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1431, Śukla, Māgha, ba. 14, Śivarātri, correspond-
ing to A.D. 1510 February 7 Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-
Mahārāya. It records the abolition of the cess on marriages in the Yaḷape-sīme, made
for the merit of the king.

l.ka: gaḷu 1431 neya śuklasaṁvatsara māgha ba 14 śivārātre puṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 481.
(A.R. No. 227 of 1913.)
ON A STONE LYING NEAR THE MALLESVARA TEMPLE AT MOKA,
BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka [1431], Śukla, Māgha, ba. 1[4], Śivarātri corre-
sponding to A.D. 1510 February 7 Thursday (not verifiable). It records that
Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya made a gift of the village Malukaṇa for his own well-being.

l.note: The rest of the inscription is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 482.
(A.R. No. 717 of 1917.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SANKRESVARA TEMPLE AT AGALI,
MADAKASIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1432, Pramōda, Āśvayuja, śu. 12, correspond-
ing to A.D. 1510 September 15 Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya.
It records that in the districts of Ghanagiri, Gutti, Ghaṇḍikōṭa, Siddhavaṭṭi,
Siddhāpura, Chandragiri, Nāgamaṅgala, Muḷuri and Rāyadurga the king abolished
the marriage-cess, irrespective of caste, for all time. The record is partly in
Sanskrit.

l.ka: kaṁ muktivā(ṁ)n mudā ghanagiryākyarājyē ca guttirājyē ca viśrutē rā-
jyē ghaṁḍikōṭastaḷē śrutē siddavaṭṭi • • • yā siddhāpurasīmākhyaviśrutē
rājyē rāyadurggē mahatyapi rājyē tēṣu ca rājyēṣu tēṣu tēṣu vasaṁti yō
nānājātibhidhēstasya vivāhasamayaṁ nijō vadhūvarajanai sarvvardhimānaṁ karaṁ ci-

inscription.head:
No. 483.
(A.R. No. 685 of 1919.)
ON A STONE SET UP BY THE SIDE OF THE ROAD TO ALUR AT HATTI-
BELAGALLU, ALUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1432, Pramōdūta, Māgha, ba. 2, Guruvāra corresponding to
A.D. 1511 January 11 Thursday. It records that Hanumappa, a servant of king
Kṛishṇadēvarāya, set up Pēṭeya-Basava, and mentions Hattiya-Beḷugallu and Jakke-
Nāyaka.

l.ka: da māgha ba [2] gu lu śrī[vī]-

inscription.head:
No. 484.
(A.R. No. 724 of 1922.)
ON TWO PIECES OF A BROKEN SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE HANUMANTARAYA
TEMPLE AT HAMPEDEVANAHALLI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1433, Prajāpati, Chaitra, śu. 13, Budhavāra corresponding to
A.D. 1511 March 12 Wednesday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records
the gift of the village Haṁpadēvahaḷḷi, surnamed Kṛishṇarāyapura, in Nāri-sīme to
various Brāhmans (named), in the presence of the god Virūpākshadēvā on the
bank of the Tuṅgabhadrā river. It refers to a copper-plate grant issued in this
connection.

l.ka: saṁvachsarada caitra śu [1]3 bu lu śrīma(ṁ)nmahārājādhirā-
bēkū yeṁdu yo tathātithiya manvādipu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalu sa-
ra makkaḷu sōmābhaṭṭarige vritti 4 kāśyapagōtrada yajuśā-
ṭṭara makkaḷu dēmābhaṭṭarige vritti 3 bhāradvājagōtrada ya-
ya ceṁnābhaṭṭara makkaḷu rēvaṇabhaṭṭarige vritti 3 va-
taṁ padaṁ || maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī ||

inscription.head:
No. 485.
(A.R. No. 742 of 1916.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE NARASIMHASVAMI TEMPLE AT
UPPADIHALLI, MADAKASIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1434, Āṅgira, Vaiśākha, śu. 15, corresponding to A.D.
1512 April 30 Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇadēvarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that Ellaya, a servant of the king made a gift of the village Upuḍihaḷḷi,
situated in Rodda-nāḍu, a district of Māraṭūr-sīme, for the service of the god
Ahōbala-Nārasiṁha at Hiriya-Dhāḷivaṭa. The gift was made for the merit of the
king.

da vayiśākha śu 15 lu śrīma(ṁ)n-

gade

inscription.head:
No. 486.
(A.R. No. 96 of 1913.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE MALLIKARJUNA TEMPLE
AT KAMBADURU, KALYANDRUG TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1434, Āṅgirasa, Kārtika, śu. 12, corresponding to A.D. 1512
October 23 Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records
that Rāyasada Koṇḍamarasayya made a gift of land in Kaṁbhadūru situated
in Kundurupe-sīme for the service of the god Mallikārjuna of the village. The gift
was made for the merit of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 487.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1922.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE THIRD PRAKARA OF THE KALAHASTISVARA
TEMPLE AT KALAHASTI, CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1434, Āṅgirasa, Phālguṇa, śu. 5, corresponding to A.D. 1513
February 10, Thursday (not verifiable). It records that Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya made
a gift of pearl neck-laces with a gold-string and precious stones, together with gold
and silver plates, for ārati to the god Kāḷahastīśvara at Kāḷahasti.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrīvijayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1434 saṁdu mēle
naḍeva ·āṁgirsasaṁvatsarada phālguṇa śu 5 lu

inscription.head:
No. 488.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1924.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE THIRD PRAKARA OF THE KALAHASTISVARA TEMPLE AT
KALAHASTI, CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1434, Āṅgirasa, Phālguṇa, śu. 5, corresponding to A.D. 1513
February 10 Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that the king made a gift of a pearl neck-lace and a neck-lace of precious
stones, a gold cup inlaid with precious stones, giriy-ārati inlaid with precious stones,
a gold plate and 25 silver plates for ārati, to the god Kāḷahastīśvara. The
inscription is written in Nāgarī characters.

l.de:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrīvijayādbhudayaśālivāhanasakavarṣaṁgaḷu 1434 saṁdu mele naḍava ·āṁgirasa-
saṁvatsarada pālguṇa su 5 lu śrīmaṁmnahārājādhirāja rājaparameśvara śrīvīrapratāpa

śrīvīrakriṣṇarāyamahārāyaru srīkālahastiśvaradevarige samarppista muttu 61 madhyanāyadanila 1
kusuri 2 saha tūka 181 ge kaṁṭhamāle 1 mattaṁ [kaṁ*]ṭhamāle 1 ke muttu 20
māṇike 18 nila 2 tuṁbida

kusuri 38 ke tuka 174 ge māṇikada kaṁṭhamāle 1 ·ubhaya kaṁṭhamāle 2 madhyamāṇikavikkida
saṁtardāreya cinnada baṭṭalu 1 kke 226 cinnada purṣāmṛgada ·ārati 1 giriyarati 1 kke
muttu 22 keṁpu 24 pacce 5 sahā ·ārati 1
beḷiya ·ārati 25 yiṣṭanu taṁma seveyāgi samarpisi cittayistaru maṁgala mahā
l.ka:
śrī śrī śrī ṟo-
yūri rāmana vrāta śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 489.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1924.)
ON THE NORTH GOPURA OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1435, Śrīmukha (= A.D. 1513-14), in the reign of
Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya. It records the gift made by the king of a golden prabhāvaḷi
set with precious stones and the grant of some villages for the service of the god
Kālahastīśvara. The gift was made for the merit of his parents. The inscription
gives the genealogy of the Tuḷuva dynasty down to Kṛishṇarāya in Sanskrit.

inscription.head:
No. 490.
(A.R. No. 687 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE GARDEN OF KARANAM NARAYANA RAO AT MUDLAPURAM,
NEAR HOSPET, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1435, Śrīmukha, Chaitra, śu. 5, corresponding to A.D. 1513
March 12 Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records
the grant of the village Virupāpura in Hosaūra-māgaṇi made by the king in the cyclic
year Āṅgirasa on Phālguṇa ba. 30 Sōmavāra, Sūryagrahaṇa corresponding to A.D.
1512 March 7 Monday, for offerings to the god Viṭṭhaladēva, car-festivals, daily,
fortnightly, monthly and yearly festivals. The gift was made for the merit of
his father Narasaṉṉa-Nāyaka-Voḍeya and his mother Nāgājidēvi-Amma.

l.ka: rada grāma 1 nū naṁma taṁde narasaṇanāya-

inscription.head:
No. 491.
(A.R. No. 500 of 1907.)
ON A SLAB DISCOVERED IN THE UNDER-GROUND TEMPLE OF PRASANNA-
VIRUPAKSHA AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1435, Śrīmukha, Chaitra, śu. 5, corresponding to
A.D. 1513 March 12 Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that the king made a gift of certain villages (named), together with
two gardens, for the service of the god Prasanna-Virūpākshadēva. The gift was
made for the merit of his father, Narasaṇṇa-Nāyaka, and mother Nāgāji-Amma.

l.ka: bha mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē || svasti śrījayādbhudayaśālivāhanasakavaruśa 1435 ne-

ya śrīmukhasaṁvatsarada cayitra śu 5 lu śrīmma(ṁ)nmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrī-
vīrapratāpa śrīvīra-

paṁcapa-

inscription.head:
No. 492.
(A.R. No. 161 of 1924.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE THIRD PRAKARA OF THE KALAHASTISVARA TEMPLE AT
KALAHASTI, CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1435, Śrīmukha, Jyēshṭha, śu. 7, Saumyavāra corresponding
to A.D. 1513 May 11 Wednesday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records
the gift of a pendant (padaka) consisting of various precious stones presented by
the king to the god Kāḷahastīśvara.

l.ka:
svasti śrī [||*] vijayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1435 saṁdu mēle naḍava śrī-
mukhasaṁvacharada jēṣṭha śudha 7 saumya-

vāradalu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīrakruṣṇarāya-
mahārāyarū śrīkāḷahastiyośva-

radēvarige samarpista padaka 1 kaṁ muttu 61 pace [3] [keṁ]pu 12 vaiḍūriya 4 nīḷe-
ya ga 37 saha tūka ha 1 kaṁṭha • • • 1 kaṁ

tūka ga 24¥ 2 ·ubhayaṁ tūka ga 71¥ 2 kaṁ padaka 1 yo padakavanū tamma sēveyāge
samarpi[si] cittaistaru maṁgaḷa mahāśrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 493.
(A.R. No. 714 of 1922.)
ON BOTH SIDES OF THE SOUTHERN ENTRANCE OF THE TEMPLE OF VIRUPAKSHA
AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1435, Śrīmukha, Āshādḥa, śu. 5, corresponding to A.D. 1513
June 8 Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇadēvarāya-Mahārāya. It
records the grant of some villages (named) and of the gift of gold vessels and plates by
the kin for the service of the god Virūpākshadēva. The gift is stated to have been
made on the occasion of a solar eclipse for his own well-being as well as for the
merit of his father Narasaṇa-Nāyaka and his mother Nāgāji-Amma.

inscription.head:
No. 494.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1905.)
ON A PILLAR WITHIN THE ANTARALA-MANDAPA OF THE CHANNAKESAVASVAMI
TEMPLE AT PUSHPAGIRI, CUDDAPPAH TALUK, CUDDAPPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1436, Bhāva, Chaitra, śu. 1, Sōmavāra corresponding to A.D.
1514 March 27, Monday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records that the
king made a gift of the village China-Māṁchupalli in Charnnūra-sīme, a subdivision
of Mulikināḍa-sīme for the service and offerings of the god Channakēśava of
Pushpagiri.

l.ka: ge voḷagāda gadde bedalu tōṭa tuḍiki nidhi nikṣēpa jala
svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavēt || svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēta

inscription.head:
No. 495.
(A.R. No. 330 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT CHINAMASAPALLE,
CUDDAPAH TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1436, Bhāva, Chaitra, śu. 1, Sōmavāra corresponding to A.D.
1514 March 27 Monday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records that the
king made a gift of the village Chika-Māñchupalli in Chenūri-sīme a sub-division of
Mulukināḍu-sīme, for the service of the god Channakēśavadēva at Pushpagiri.

l.ka: mage ·oḷagāda gade bedalu tōṭa tuḍike nidhi nikṣēpa
hārēṇa svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavēt || svadattaṁ para-
dataṁ vā yō harēta vasuṁdharāṁ ṣaṣṭirvarṣasa-

inscription.head:
No. 496.
(A.R. No. 385 of 1904.)
ON TWO FACES OF A PILLAR SET UP IN THE RAMALINGESVARA TEMPLE AT
RAMESVARAM, NEAR PRODDATURU, PRODDATURU TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1436, Bhāva, Chaitra, śu. 1, Sō[mavāra] corresponding to A.D.
1514 March 27 Monday. It records that king Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya made a gift of
the village Payiṇḍāle in Chernūra-sīme for the service of the god Rāmayadēva at
Mahā-Rāmēśvara in Ghaṇḍikōṭeya-sīme.

inscription.head:
No. 497.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE MARAMMA TEMPLE AT TAGARAPURAM,
KOLLEGAL TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1437, Bhāva, Chaitra, ba. 5, Śukravāra corresponding to A.D.
1514 April 14 Friday, in the reign of the Vijayanagara king Kṛishṇa-Dharma-Mahādhi-
rāya (Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya). It records the grant of the village Tagavūru to
Sōmayyadēva-Voḍeya, son of Bāloḍeya of Mōgūru, by Sāḷuva Gōvindarāja, made with
the consent of his elder brother, the Mahāpradhāna Sāḷuva Timmarasa, under
the orders of the king. The king is stated to be the son of Narasiṁha-Dharma-
Mahārāja. The donee's grandfather Sōmēśvarāchārya, also called Mallikārjuna, was
the guru of Vīra-Rudra of Ōruṅgal.

l.ka: nmahāpradhāna sāḷuva timmarasavarmmānumatadiṁ tadanuja sāḷuva gōviṁdarājaga-
ḷu śakavarṣa 1437 neya bhāvasaṁva[tsa]rada caitra ba 5 śukravāradalū [||*] ·ōruṁgallā-
ḍiyarige dānārthamāgi śilāsthāpanava māḍi koṭṭa tagavūra grāmada catussī-
meya vivara mūḍalu kuṁtūra cilakavāḍiya tagavūra trisaṁdhikalu 1 purada
cilakavāḍiya tagavūra trisaṁdhikalu 1 voḷageṟeya kētedēvara kalu 1 bāliha-
ḷḷiya hūvinakaṭṭeya kallu 1 keṁgaḍūra ko(ṁ)mmegeṟeya tōkuḷiya haḷḷada kallu
1 bōgekeṟeya mūḍaṇa kōḍiya kallū 1 keṁgaḍūra mālaṁggiya tagavūra trisaṁ-
ydu liṁgamudreya kalloḷagāda tagavūra grāmada sunka suvarnnādāya gadde

tōṭa viśēṣa carādā[ya*] modalāgi nidhi nikṣēpa jala pāṣāṇa ·akṣīṇa ·āgāmi siddhi
sādhya-
visi bahiri yeṁdu māḍida dharmasādhana || svadattaṁ dviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ pū-
t || vanē vanagatō vahni[ḥ*]svayaṁ dahati • • • • trikulaṁ mūlāni daha-
ti dharmasyāgatapāragāt | svadattā • • • • • • vā yō harēti vasuṁdha-
yo dānavanu ·aḷihidavaru vāraṇāsiyali taṁma guru taṁde tāya

inscription.head:
No. 498.
(A.R. No. 558 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE KESAVASVAMI TEMPLE AT CHERUVU-BELAGALLU,
KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka [143]6, Bhāva, Vaiśākha, śu. 15, corresponding
to A.D. 1514 May 8 Monday (not verifiable), in the reign of the Vijayanagara king
Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records that Karaṇam Basavarasa, son of Sōmarasa of
Tirapatūr who belonged to the Gautama-gōtra and Āśvalāyana-sūtra, made a gift of
a plot of land in Keriya-Beḷugallu in the division of Ādavāni-durga, to the actor
Nāgayya, whose father Chāgaya had become famous in the drama called Tāyikuṇḍa-
nāṭaka and to the actress Pātri, daughter of the Naṭṭuva Timmayya of Pōtavara.
The gift was made in the presence of the god Jalasiṅgadēva for the merit of the king.

l.ka:
da naṭuva tiṁmayana magaḷu pātri-
gū-

inscription.head:
No. 499.
(A.R. No. 513 of 1919.)
ON THE BASE OF THE EAST, SOUTH AND WEST VERANDAHS ROUND THE
‘ROCK’, ARULALA-PERUMAL TEMPLE, LITTLE, CONJEEVERAM,
CONJEEVERAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1436, Bhāva, Āshāḍha, śu. 10, Budhavāra Uttarā-nakshatra.
The date is irregular. In Bhāva=Śaka 1436, Āshāḍha śu. 10 was neither a Wednes-
day nor was the nakshatra Uttarā current on that day. The record refers itself to
the reign of the Vijayanagara king Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It gives in Sanskrit
verse the genealogy of the king and records that for the merit of himself his father,
Narasaṇa-Nāyaka, and his mother, Nāgalādēvi-Amma, the king had the Puṇyakōṭi-
vimāna of the god Varadarājadēva gilt with pure gold.

l.ka:
śubhamastu | harērllīlāvarāhasya daṁṣṭrādaṁḍassa pātu vaḥ | hēmādrikalaśā yatra
dhātrī chatraśriyaṁ dadhau | kalyāṇāyāstu taddhāma pratyūhatimirāpahaṁ | yat
gajōpyagajōtbhūtaṁ hariṇāpi ca pūjyatē [||*] ·āsti kṣīramayāddēvairmmathyamā-
nānmahāṁbudhē[ḥ] | navanītamivōtbhūtamapanītatamōmahaḥ | tasyāsīt tanayasta-
pōbhiratulairanvarththanāmā budhaḥ pu(ṁ)ṇyairasya purūravā bhujabalairāyu[rdvi]ṣā-
nnighnataḥ | tasyāyurnahuṣōsya tasya puruṣō yuddhēyayāti[ḥ] kṣitau khyāta-
stasya tu turvvasurvvasunibhiḥ śrīdēvayānīpatēḥ | tadvaṁśē dēvakījānirddidīpē
timmabhūpatiḥ | yaśasvī taḷuvēndrēṣu yadōḥ kriṣṇayivānvayē | tatōbhūt
bukkamājānirīśvaraḥ kṣitipālakaḥ | ·atrāsamaguṇabhraṁśaṁ mauliratnaṁ mahī(ṁ)bhu-
jām | padmākṣō balijinnijaistribhuvanakrāntikṣamairvvikramaiśśaṁkhaṁ cakramapi
śriyaṁ nijakarē śrīdēvakīnaṁdanaḥ | bhūtvāpyatbhutavośvarōyamiti yō bhū-
yastarāṁ paprathē | bhūtiṁ cākhilapūjyatāmatibhruśaṁ bhūbhrutsutāmāśritaḥ | bhūvā-
saikaparōparō raviriva prāptōdayōharniśaṁ | yō maṁdēharipūnahan kalibudhō-
pētō na pētō raṇāt | ·āpūrvāparavārirāśinitarāmāsētuhēmācalaṁ vikhyātō
vihasamścayō vitaraṇairvidyōtatē dyōtarūn | sarasādudabhūttasmānnarasāvani
pālakaḥ | dēvakīnaṁdanā[tkā]mō dēvakīnaṁdanādiva | kāvērīmāśu baddhvā
bahaḷajalarayāṁ tāṁ vilaṁghyaiva śatruṁ jīvagrāha[ṁ] gaihītvā samiti bhujabalā-
ttaṁ ca rājyaṁ (ma)tadīyaṁ | kritvā śrīraṁgapūrvaṁ tadapi nijavaśē paṭṭaṇaṁ yō ba-
bhāsē kīrtistaṁbhannikhāya tribhuvanabhavanastūyamānāpadānaḥ | cēraṁ cōḷaṁ
ca pāḍyaṁ tamapi ca madhurāvallabha(ṁ) mmānabhūṣaṁ vīryyōdagraṁ turuṣkaṁ gajapati-
nripatiṁ cāpi jitvā tadanyān | ·āgaṁgātīralaṁkāprathamacaramabhūbhrittadaṁtaṁ nitā-
ntaṁ khyātakṣōṇīpatīnāṁ srajamiva śirasāṁ śāsanaṁ yō vyatānīt | vividha-
sukrutōddāmē rāmēśvarapramukhē muhurmaditahrudayasthānē sthānē vyadha-
tta yathāvidhi | budhaparivritō nānādānāni yō bhuvi ṣōḍaśa tribhuvanajanō-
dgītaṁ sphītaṁ yaśaḥ punaruktayan | tippājīnāgalādēvyōḥ śrīkausalyāsumi-

trayōḥ | dēvyōriva nrusiṁhēṁdrāttasmātpaṁktirathādiva | vīrau vinayinau rāma-
lakṣmaṇāviva naṁdanau | jātau vīranrusiṁhēndrakriṣṇarāyamahīpatī | vīraśrī-
nārasiṁhaḥ sa vijayanagarē ratnasiṁhāsanasthaḥ kīrtyā nītyā nirasyan nruganaḷanahu-
ṣānapyavanyāmathānyān | ·āsētōrāsumērōravanisuranutaḥ svairamācōda-
yādrērā pāścātyācalāṁtādakhilahrudayamāvarjya rājyaṁ śaśāsa | nānādānā-
nyakārṣīt kanakasadasi yaḥ śrī virūpākṣadēvasthānē śrīkāḷahastīśiturapi nagarē
vēṁkaṭādrau ca kāṁcyāṁ | śrīśailē śōṇaśailē mahati hariharēhōbalē saṁgamē
ca śrīraṁgē kuṁbhaghō • • • • • • • • • • yō hiraṇyāśvarathamapi tulā-
pūruṣaṁ gō sahasraṁ | hēmāśvaṁ hēmagarbhaṁ kanakakarirathaṁ paṁcalāṁgalyatānīt |

prājyaṁ praśāsya nirvighnaṁ rājyaṁ dyāmiva śāsituṁ | tasmin guṇēna vikhyātē kṣitēriṁdrē
divaṁgatē | tatōpyavāryyavīryyaśrīḥ kriṣṇarāyamahīpatiḥ | vibhartti maṇikē-
yūranirviśēṣaṁ mahīṁ bhujē | kīrtyā yasya samaṁtataḥ prasrutayā viśvaṁ rucai-
kyaṁ vrajēdityāśaṁkya purārirabhavat phālēkṣaṇaḥ prāyaśaḥ | padmākṣōpi caturbhu-
jōjani caturvaktrōbhavat padmabhū[ḥ] kāḷī khaḍgamadhādramā ca kamalaṁ vīṇāṁ
ca vāṇī karē | śatrūṇāṁ vāsamētē dadhata yiti ruṣā kinnu saptāṁburāśīn nānā-
sēnāturaṁgatruṭitavasumatī dhūḷikāpāḷikābhiḥ | samśōṣya svairamētat pratinidhi-
jaladhiśrēṇikā[ṁ] yō vidhattē bra(ṁ)hmāṁḍasvarṇamērupramukhanijamahādānatō-
yairamēyaiḥ | maddattāmarthisārthāśśriyamiha suciraṁ bhuṁjatāmityavētya
prāyaḥ pratyūhahētō[ḥ*] tapanarathagatērālayān dēvatānāṁ | dattaddigjaitra-
vrityāpi ca birudapadairaṁkitāṁstatra tatra staṁbhān jātapratiṣṭhānvyatanuta bhuvi yō
bhūbhrudabhraṁkaṣāgrān | kāṁcīśrīśailaśōṇācalakanakasabhāvēṁkaṭādripramukhyē-
ṣvāvarttyāvarttya sarvēṣvatanuta vidhivat bhūyasē śreyasē yaḥ | dēvasthānēṣu
tīrthēṣvapi kanakatulāpūruṣādīni nānādānānyanyōpadānairapi samamakhilairāga-
mōktānitāni | rōṣakrutapratipārthivadaṁḍa[ḥ*] śēṣabhujakṣi tirakṣaṇaśauṁḍaḥ | bhāṣege-
tappuvarāyaragaṁḍastōṣakrudarthiṣu yō raṇacaṁḍaḥ | rājādhirājayityuktō yō
rājaparamēśvaraḥ | mūvarāyaragaṁḍaścapararāyabhayaṁkaraḥ | yiṁdurāyasuratrā-
ṇō duṣṭaśārdūlamardanaḥ | gajaughagaṁḍabhēruṁḍa yityādibirudānvitaḥ | ·ālō-
kaya mahārāja jaya jīvēti vādibhiḥ | ·aṁgavaṁgakaḷiṁgādyai rājabhissēvyatē
ca yaḥ | stutyaudāryya[ḥ] sudhībhissa vijayanagarē ratnasiṁhāsanastha[ḥ] kṣmāpā-
lān™ kruṣṇarāyakṣitipatiradharīkrutya nritya nrugādīn™ | ·āpūrvādrērathāstakṣi-
tidharakaṭakādā ca hēmācalāṁtādāsētōrarthisārthaśriyamiha bahaḷīkrutya
kīrtyā samiṁdhē | ·udayādrimupārūḍhē kriṣṇarāyadivākarē | tamōbhūtō gaja-
patissaṁtassaṁjīvitā bhruśaṁ | ·iha kila jagati nikhilakavikulābhinaṁdyamānaudāryyadhai-
ryyaśauryyādijanitayaśaḥpūrakarpūrapūritabra(ṁ)hmāṁḍakaraṁḍēna samaracaṁḍēna
vihasitanaḷanahuṣanābhāgaduṁdhumāramāṁdhātrubharatabhagīrathadaśaratharāmādicaritēna ma-
hīsucaritēna krutabhūsuratrāṇēna paribhūtasuratrāṇēna gajapatigaja • • • •
• • • • • • tasāṁparāyēṇa kruṣṇadēvamahārāyēṇa kanakamayamakāri
nikhilamaṁḍalagaṁḍa toṁḍīramaṁḍalamaṁḍanāyamāna- • • • • • nū pādarasa tūkakke bele ga • • • • •
nū kūli mēlu veccāgaḷige muṭṭidu ga nnu ·antu

kāṁcīpurīkamalinīmadhyē viharan madagajāyamāna mataṁgajagiriśirōmaku-
ṭāyamānamakhilajanābhivaṁdyamānaṁ ·atisurācalabhāsamānaṁ ·asamānaṁ puṇya-
kōṭivimānaṁ | tuṁgasvāyaśakābdabhāsuragatau bhāvē śubhē vatsarē jyeṣṭhē
māsi budhōttarāmaladaśamyāptē dinē vriścikē | sauvarṇāṁ kariśailabhartura-
karōt śrīpuṇyakōṭimmuda sarvēṣānnarasāvanīṁdratanayaḥ śrīkruṣṇarāyō
mahān™ | bhrutyēṣu prathitēṣu satsvapi tadāgnātaḥ svayaṁ sahyajāgādhāṁ-
bhōnidhipāvaniryyadi saha prāṇairgrihītvā dviṣaṁ saṁgrihyōdhdhāmahīdradu-

rggamaparāṁcitaṁ ca bhātyōjasā | suvarṇaṁ sa hi kriṣṇarāyanrupatiḥ śrīpuṇya-
kōṭīṁ vyadhāt || svasti śrīvijayātbhudayaśālivāhanaśakābda 1436 ṟa mēle
saluva bhāvasaṁvatsarada ·āṣāḍa śu 10 budhavāralū ·uttarānakṣatradalū jayaṁgoṁḍa-
cōḷamaṁḍalada ·ūttukāḍupattiva kāṁcīpurada tiruvattiyūralu niṁtu bijaya māḍi
yiha varadarājadēvara bhaṁḍārake śrīman mahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapra-
thāpa śrīvīrakruṣṇadēvamahārāyarū | dhāreyeṟadu sa[ma*]rppista dharmmaśāsana
tamagū tamma taṁdegaḷu narasaṇanāyakavoḍeyarigū tamma tāyigaḷu nāgalādēvi
ammanavarigū dharmmavāgi puṇyakōṭiya vimānava ciṁnava hāsuvadakke lōveyiṁda
mēle kaḷasa pariyaṁtaralū muṭṭida ·aparaṁjiya tūṁkada • • ceṁbina taḍige ceṁbu
halakke bele ga

koṭṭu puṇyakō-
ṭiya vimānavanū ·aparaṁjiya ciṁnadalū hāśiste·u | yo dharmaū ·ācaṁdrārkkasthā-
yiyāgi nama sēveyanu varadarājadēvarū kaikoḷaluḷavaru yo dharmake tappidavarū
vāraṇāśiya [gaṁgā]tīradalū tamma taṁdetāyigaḷa koṁda pāpakke hōgaluḷavaru
kapileya koṁda pāpakke hōgaluḷavaru ·aśvatti bra(ṁ)hmatti gōhattiya māḍida pā-
pakke hōgaluḷavaru | dānapālanayōrmmadhyē dānāśrēyōnupālanaṁ | dānāt
svarga mavāpnōti pālanādacyutaṁ padaṁ ||

Some space is left blanktu & ko•

inscription.head:
No. 500.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1915.)
IN THE EASTERN PORCH, LEFT OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE TEMPLE AT
SRISAILAM, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1438, Yuva, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15, Budhavāra, Chandra-grahaṇa,
corresponding to A.D. 1515 July 25, Wednesday with a lunar eclipse, in the reign of
Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya. After mentioning the king's conquest of Udayagiridurga,
the capture of Tirumalarāutarāya-Mahāpātra, the subjugation of Addaṅki, Vini-
koṇḍe, Bellaṁkoṇḍe, Nāgārjuṇikoṇḍe, Taṅgeḍa, Kētavara and Koṇḍavīḍudurga, the
capture of Vīrabhadrarāya, son of Pratāparudra-Gajapati, Naraharidēva, son of
Komarā-Pātra, Mallūkhāna of Rāchūru, Uddaṇḍakhāna and others, it records
the gifts of tulāpūrusha, ratnadhēnu and saptasāgara made by the king and
his queens Chinnādēvamma and Tirumaladēvamma in the presence of Amarēśvara-
dēva on the bank of the river Kṛishṇavēṇī and his order for the construction of
a stone maṇḍapa in the car street at Śrīparvata after his arrival there. It also
states that he had granted the villages, Pārumañchāla and Ātakūru for offerings to
the god Mallikārjuna in the year Śrīmukha, Vaiśākha, śu. 11, Guruvāra corresponding
to A.D. 1513 April 15 Friday, not Thursday. Further he is stated to have remitted
tolls on kāvaḍis, packhorses, bullocks, etc.

l.ka: saṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa śudha 15 budhavāradalū candragrahaṇa pu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalli śrīmalli-

inscription.head:
No. 501.
(A.R. No. 412 of 1920.)
ON A ROCK BEHIND THE LINGAYAT-MATHA AT URAVAKONDA, GOOTY TALUK,
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1439, Yuva, Phālguṇa, śu. 10, corresponding
to A.D. 1516 February 13, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-
Mahārāya. It records the gift of some plots of land made by Amarada Timmarasa,
the Kāryakartta of the king, to Kaṭṭige Tippayya, for constructing a tank to the
south of the village of Uruvakoṇḍe.

l.ka: ḷāda ·āmarada timmarasaru kaṭṭigeya tippayyage koṭṭaśilāśā[sanada] kramaveṁ[teṁdare]nī-
nu naṁma ·urvvakūṁḍege dakṣiṇadikkinalu ·ānadaleya dāriyalu keṟeyanu ho-

bittida bījavari kha 1 kke ¥ 2 || ·akṣāradalu khaṁḍugakke yikkuḷada yibaḷa ·ā keṟeya ·oḷage

holanū nimage mānyavāgi koṭṭau ·eṁdu koṭṭa śilāśāsana ·iṣṭanū niṁna putrapautra-

vōbayyanū [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 502.
(A.R. No. 711 of 1922.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE HUNDRED-PILLARED MANDAPA IN THE VITTHALASVAMI
TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1438, Dhātu (= A.D. 1516-17) with no further details. It
records that king Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya caused the construction of the hundred-
pillared maṇḍapa to god Viṭhaladēva in the year mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 503.
(A.R. No. 457 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE GARDEN OF THE SANTIVIRASVAMI TEMPLE AT
SOGI, HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Dhātu, Kārtika, śu. 5, corresponding to A.D. 1516
October 30, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records
that, under the orders of Pārvati-amma, mother of Immaḍi Basavappa-Voḍeya, an
officer of the king, Aruha-Timmaṇa-Nāyaka who was the Pradhāna at Koṭṭūru made
a gift of the village of Daṇṇāyakapura for the charity water-lift at Sōge, for the
merit of his master (i.e., Basavappa-Voḍeya). The balance, if any, out of the revenue
of the village, after meeting the expenses of the water-lift, was to be used for the
service of the god Kalināthadēva at Sōge.

l.ka: śrī sōgeya śrīkalināthadēvaru [||*]dhātusaṁvatsarada kārtika śu 5 lu śrī-
raṇāsiyalli taṁma tāyi taṁde guruva koṁda pāpa-
dali hōharu siva sākṣi | śrī śrī śrī yīdha-

inscription.head:
No. 504.
(A.R. No. 686 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL IN THE ROOM TO THE LEFT OF THE MANDAPA
IN THE RANGANATHA TEMPLE AT NAGENAHALLI, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1438, Dhātu, Kārtika, śu. 15, Ādivāra corresponding to A.D.
1516 November 9, Sunday, in the reign of Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya. It records that
Raṅganātha-Dīkshita, son of Nañjinātha-Bhaṭṭa of Jāmadagnyāvatsa-gōtra,
Āśvalāyana-sūtra and Ṛigvēda, and the Purōhita of the king, received a mānya
village from the king and caused therein the construction of a tank called Nāgasamudra
after the name of Nāgalādēvī, the mother of the king, and of the temples of Nāgēśvara
and Nāgēndraśayana. He made the village an agrahāra giving it the name Nāgalādēvī-
pura and gave vṛittis to Brāhmans.

l.ka: sa ca haraḥ kailāsakōṇē sthitaḥ | kailāsōpi sa lakṣitaḥ kṣititaḷē-
sau yasya mrudbiṁduvat | daṁṣṭrāgrē vilasatyasāvavatu vaḥ krō [ḍā]-
ṣōsya tasya puruṣō yuddhē yayātiḥ kṣitau khyātastasya tu turva-
vijaya[vāḍa]māśu hatvātha jitvā yudhi gajapatisainyaṁ prāpya

• • puraṁ purā | tamimaṁ nāgalādēvīpurābhikhyāṁ vidhāya ca | ·agra
• • • tataḥ kṛ[tvā] nāgāṁbāyā vibhūtayē | nāgēśvaraṁ ca nāgēṁdra [kāṁ]
[dhā]tusaṁvatsara kārttika śu 15 ·ā lū śrimatu rājādhirāja rājapara-
• • • • • hākisida śilāśā[sana] maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 505.
(A.R. No. 177 of 1924.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE THIRD PRAKARA OF THE KALAHASTISVARA TEMPLE AT
KALAHASTI, CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1438, Dhātu, Phālguṇa, ba. 10, Maṅgaḷavāra, Śravaṇa, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1517 March 17, Tuesday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It
records a gift of money made by the Sthānikas of the temple to Māde-Nāyaka for
the service of the god Kāḷahasti-Nayinār at Kāḷahasti, also called Mummuḍi-Chōḷapura,
in Āttūr-nāḍu, a sub-division of Jayaṁgoṇḍachōḷa-maṇḍala. The gift was made for
the merit of the king. The manner of distributing the offerings among the servants
of the temple is also specified.

l.ka:
śubhamastu svasti [||*] śrīma(ṁ)nmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīra-
kruṣṇarāyamahārāyaru pruthuvīrājyaṁ māḍuta yiralāgi śālivāhanaśakavaruṣa
1438 neya

dhātusaṁvatsarada phālguṇa ba 10 maṁgaḷavāra śravaṇanakṣatradalu jayaṁgoṁḍacōḷamaṁḍalada
ātūranāḍa trikāḷahastiyāda muṁmaḍicōḷapurada nayinārū śrīkāḷaha-

stinayināradēvara śrībhaṁḍārada stānadavarū mādenāyakarige dharmaśāsanava māḍikoṭṭa
pariyeṁteṁdare naṁma svāmi kruṣṇarāya-

inscription.head:
No. 506.
This inscription should be(A.R. No. 457 of 1923.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE BAZAAR STREET OF THE VILLAGE, KANEKKAL,
RAYADRUG TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1438, Dhātu, Āshāḍha, ba. 30, Ādivāra corres-
ponding to A.D. 1516 June 29, Sunday, in the reign of Kṛishṇadēvarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that the king exempted the donees of the mānya village Kaṇiyakallu in
Rāyadurga-[sīme] from taxes and permitted them to enjoy the dēvadāya and brah-
mādāya lands without any disturbance.

l.ka: • • ṭe·ū [||*] svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēta vasu • • •

inscription.head:
No. 507.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1912.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE CHAUDESVARI TEMPLE AT
CHOLASAMUDRAM, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is incomplete and dated Śaka 1439, Īśvara, Mārgaśira, ba. 3, corresponding
to A.D. 1517 December 1 Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahā-
rāya, king of Karṇāṭaka. It mentions his queen Tirumalāmbikā and records
that Rāyasada Koṇḍamarasayya, son of Mantri Timmarasa and grandson of
Śrīpatyāchārya, of Bhāradvāja-gōtra, Āśvalāyna-sūtra and Ṛik-śākhā, made some
gift for the service of the goddess Chauḍēśvari in Chōḷasamudra in order that the king
may be blessed with children. Koṇḍamarasayya is stated to have been an expert
in reading various scripts. His grandfather is described as a very learned man and
observer of the four religious orders, Brahmacharya, Gṛihastha, Vānaprastha and
Yati, as the ornament of the Udayagiri-Kannaḍiga-kula and as the chief of Podaṭūru
in the Draviḍa-ḍēśa.

l.ka: tadupari varttamāna ·īśvarasaṁvatsarada mārgaśira ba 2 lū śrīmatu sadharmmacāritraṁ

ramēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa vīranarasiṁharāyasahōdaraṁ sākṣāt pratyakṣakṛṣṇāvatāraṁ ·a-
dhar(ṁ)-

inscription.head:
No. 508.
(A.R. No. 529 of 1915.)
ON ONE OF THE PILLARS OF A MOSQUE ON THE HILL AT ADONI, ADONI TALUK,
BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1439, Īśvara, Māgha, śu. 5, corresponding A.D. 1518 January
16, Saturday (not verifiable). It records that Rāmaṇa-Nāyaka, son of Kaṭhāri Tippaṇa-
Nāyaka, who was an officer of Prauḍha-Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, constructed the temple
of the god Prasanna-Rāmayadēva and consecrated the image of the god in it.

inscription.head:
No. 509.
(A.R. No. 225 of 1913.)
ON A STONE IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT YETTINA-BUDEHALU,
BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete record. It is dated Śaka 1445 (mistake for 1440) Bahu-
dhānya, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 10, corresponding to A.D. 1518 August 1, Sunday (not verifiable),
in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It stops with the name of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 510.
(A.R. No. 697 of 1922.)
ON A ROCK NEAR KUMBHARAKUNTE TO THE NORTH-WEST OF KAMALAPURAM,
HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1[4]3[9], Bahudānya, Kārtika, śu. (mistake for ba.?) 12, Śa[nivāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1518 October 30, Saturday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahā-
rāya. It records that the senior queen Tirumaladēvi-Amma made a gift of (the
land called) Kummaraguṇṭe near Vijayanagara for the service of the god Tiruvēṅ-
gaḷanātha of Añjanagiri in Vijayanagara. The gift was made for the merit of Tiru-
malarāya-Mahārāya. Mention is made of Rāmānuja-muni, the reformer of Vaishṇa-
vism.

l.ka:
śubhamastū | śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara ·arirāyavibhāḍa bhāṣegetappuva
rāyaragaṁḍa mū-

vvarāyara gaṁḍa kaṁḍanāḍa koṁḍu koṁḍanāḍa koḍada pūrvadakṣiṇapaścimōttaracatussa-
mudrādhipati śrīvī-

tanirā[sabhā]surakharō jainībhakaṁṭhīrava māyāvādabhujaṁgabhaṁgagaruḍa trayividyacū-

inscription.head:
No. 511.
(A.R. No. 425 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A PRIVATE BUILDING-SITE TO THE NORTH OF DODDAKERE-
KATTE AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1441, Pramādi, Kārtika, śu. 2, Maṅgaḷavāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1519. October 25, Tuesday, in the reign of Kṛishṇārāya-Mahārāya. It records
that while Vijayappa-Voḍeya, son of Ratnappa-Voḍeya, was ruling over Bārakūra-
rājya, Tirumaka-Seṭṭiti, wife of Nāraṇa-Seṭṭi and daughter of Joki-Seṭṭi, caused the
construction of a maṭha in the Paḍuvakēri of Basrūr and endowed it with lands and
gold.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 512.
(A.R. No. 424 of 1928.)
ON ANOTHER SLAB SET UP ON THE SAME SITE.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1442, Vikrama, Chaitra, śu. 14, Ādivāra, corresponding to A.D.
1520 April 1, Sunday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records that, while
Vijayappoḍeya was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya, Śaṅkara Seṭṭi, son of Allu Seṭṭi of
Seṭṭivāḍi, built a maṭha, out of the gold of his grand-daughter, Tirumakka-Rāvutiti,
who had no children and endowed it with some plots of land. He also made a gift for
the service of the god, Nakharēśvara.

l.ka:
svasti śrīgaṇādhipata·ēṁ nama[ḥ*] śrīsarasvatyāēṁ nama[ḥ*] śrīgurubhyōṁ nama[ḥ ||*]
namastuṁgaśiraścuṁbi-

caṁdracāmaracāravē | trayilōkyanagarāraṁbhē mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē [||*] svasti śrī
jayādbhudaya-
śālivāhanaśakavaruṣa 1441 saṁdu nālvatta·eraḍaneya vartamāna vikramasaṁvatsarada cayi-

tra śu 14 ·ādivāradalū śrīma(ṁ)nmahārājādhirāja paramēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa kṛṣṇarāya-

yarū vija[ya*]nagarada siṁhvāsanadalū sukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁda sakala sāṁbrājyavanu
pratipā-

inscription.head:
No. 513.
(A.R. No. 707 of 1919.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP NEAR BASAVESVARA TEMPLE AT BOLAGOTI,
ALUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1443, Vṛisha, Chaitra, śu. 1, corresponding to
A.D. 1521 March 9, Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that while Kaṭṭige Kāmaṇa-Nāyaka was in charge of the fortress of Ādavāni,
his Maṇehagāra, Chiṅkala Tirumalabōva, and Sēnabōva Timmarasa issued certain
orders regarding the manufacture of salt in some villages (named), belonging to Aḍa-
pada Mācha of Mīnagondi in Ādavāni (division).

inscription.head:
No. 514.
(A.R. No. 727 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MANDAPA OF THE ISVARA TEMPLE AT KURIKUPPE,
HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1445, Svabhānu, Vaiśākha, śu. 5, Sō[mavāra], corresponding to
A.D. 1523 April 20, Monday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records that
Benakarasa, son of Heggarasa of Vasishṭha-gōtra, Drāhyāyaṇa-sūtra and Sāma-śākhā,
caused the construction of a temple at Kuṟiyakuppe surnamed Anantasētu and, setting
up the god Virūpāksha there, made a gift of some vṛittis which he purchased from
Basavā-Dīkshita, for worship, etc. to the god. The Mahājanas of the agrahāra also
made a gift of a garden land for the same purpose.

l.ka: varttamānavāda svabhānusaṁvatsarada vaiśākha śu 5 sō lu śrīma-
vā[la*]yavanū kaṭṭisi virupākṣadēvara pratiṣṭheyanu māḍi ·ā dēva-
rige naivēdya pūje punaskāra nityasēvegaḷige basavādīkṣitara ·ā-
nēṟilu saṁpage muṁtāda nānāurakṣagaḷanū yikkisi sama-
maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 515.
(A.R. No. 288 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT CHATNAHALLI,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1446, Svabhānu, Kārtika, śu. 12, Sōmavāra, correspond-
ing to A.D. 1524 November 7, Monday. The inscription refers itself to the reign of
Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records that Visaṇa-Rāvutta, son of Murāri-Rāvutta
of Āriya-Kshatriya-kula, Kāśyapa-gōtra, Āśvalāyana-sūtra and Ṛik-śākhā made a
grant of Chaṭanahaḷḷi in Kaviḷichervina-sīme, a sub-division of Uchchaṅgi-Vēṇṭhe
under his nāyakatana, to Nārāyaṇa-Bhaṭṭa, son of Dēvaru-Bhaṭṭa of Gautama-
gōtra, Baudhāyana-sūtra and Yajuś-śākhā, free of all taxes, for the merit of the king.

l.ka: vike heggāru marana guttage hēru gāṇa
kala kārāya [modalāda] nidhi nikṣēpa jala pā-
ttaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavētu || yēkaiva bhaginī lōkē

inscription.head:
No. 516.
(A.R. No. 450 of 1920.)
ON A ROCK TO THE WEST OF VELPAMADUGU, GOOTY TALUK,
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1446, Tāraṇa, Māgha, śu. 7, Sō[mavāra], corresponding to
A.D. 1525 January 30, Monday, in the reign of Kṛishṇadēvarāya-Mahārāya. It
records that Liṅgarasa of Dhārāsura issued orders to Seṭṭis, Sēnabōvas and local
and foreign merchants residing in Velupaḍige, also called Kṛishṇarāyapura, fixing the
rate of taxes in the new bazaar street opened in the name of the king as follows:
no taxes are to be levied during the first three years; then a varāha per oil-mill;
3 paṇas for a weaving machine; 3 paṇas for a stall; if ten weaving machines are
kept by a single family, only nine are to be taxed; a paṇa for a stall of minor
articles; all these are to be collected once a year.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrīvijayābhdudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṁṣaṁgaḷu 1446 neya mēle
saluva tāraṇasaṁvatsarada māgha śu 7 sō lu śrīmanmahā-

rājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrivīrapratāpa śrivīrakruṣṭṇadēvarāyamahārāyaru pruthvī
rājyaṁ geyi·uttaṁ yiralu śrīmatu dhārāsurada liṁgarasaru

bhaṁḍāravāḍake saluva mūḍanāḍa modala grāma velupaḍigeya staḷada voḷagaṇa kruṣṭṇa-
rāyapurada pēṁṭheya gauḍasēnabhōvaru seṭṭipaṭṭaṇasvāmi nānāprajegaḷige nānā-
dēśa-

da seṭi paṭṭaṇasvāmi nānāprajegū māḍikoṭṭa śilāśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare naṁma kru-
ṣṭṇarāyadēvamahārāyarugaḷa hesarali kaṭṭida pēṭeyali baṁdu ·iha

vokkalugaḷige mūṟu varuṣa sarvamā(ṁ)nya mūṟu varuṣada mēle gāṇa 1 ke varuṣa 1 kke
gāṇada vokkalu voṁdu varaha varuṣa 1 ke magga 1 ke ¥ 3 ·aṁgaḍi 1 ke ¥ 3 magga
hattake

voṁdaṟa mā(ṁ)nya ciluvāṁna binugugaḷige nānājātiya vokkalugaḷige varuṣa 1 ke ¥ 1
yo mariyādēlu koṁbe·u yiṣṭallade tappidare kruṣṇadēva-

mahārāyara pādada ·āṇege tappidavaru muṁde ·āvānānobba baṁdare·ū yi mariyādeya-
lu naḍasikoḍade ·idara kruṣṇadēvamahārāyara pādada ·ā-

ṇe taṁma taṁma sukrutavanu ·a(ṁ)nyajātigaḷige koṭṭa dōṣake hōgaluḷḷavaru ·eṁdu
māḍikoṭṭa śilaśāsana [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 517.
(A.R. No. 431 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF A HOUSE TO THE SOUTH OF THE TANK CALLED
SANNAKAREKATTE AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and is dated Śaka 1447 = A.D. 1525 (the details being lost), in
the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It states that while Viṭharasa-Voḍeya, son of
Lakshmīnārāyaṇa-Karaṇika, was ruling the Bārakūra-rājya, Nāraṇamunila of Chenna-
vāra, son of Tammimunila-Saṅkaraseṭṭi of the Paḍuvakēri of Basrūr and Kupaṇa-
Seṭṭi caused the construction of a maṭha to Lakshmīnārāyaṇadēva and endowed it
with lands and interest-bearing grants of money outstanding on many persons
(named).

inscription.head:
No. 518.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1913.)
ON A PILLAR LYING IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE MALLIKARJUNA TEMPLE
AT KAMBADURU, KALYANADRUG TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1447, Pārthiva, Kārtika, śu. 12, corresponding to A.D. 1525
October 28, Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya. It re-
cords the gift of the village Jāluvaṭi to the temple of the god Mallikārjuna at KaM-
badūru in Kuṁdurupeya-rājya made by Rāyasada Ayyapparasayya, for the merit
of Rāyasada Koṇḍamarasayya.

l.ka: ma kṣētra nidhi nikṣēpa jala pāṣā-

inscription.head:
No. 519.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1912.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE BHOGESVARA TEMPLE AT KONAKONDLA,
GOOTY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1448, Pārthiva, Kārtika, ba. 7, Maṅgaḷavāra,
corresponding to A.D. 1525 November 7, Tuesday, in the reign of Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that, under the orders of the king, Kottanara Rāmarāja made a gift of
land to certain persons (names lost) of Koṇḍakundi for constructing a tank in the
village and for its supervision.

l.ka: saṁvatsarada kārtika ba 7 maṁgaḷavāradalu svasti samastabhūva-
• • • koṭa dasavaṁda • • • yakaṁge māḍida gade kaṁḍuga
• • • dasavaṁdha 1 yikuḷada gade • • • • gade·u nimage bhūdāna

māḍisikoṁḍu niṁma kereyanu re[ka]me māḍikoṁḍu nīu niṁma putra • • • •
pitrugaḷa vāraṇāsiyalu vadhiyisida pāpakke hōharu gōbrāhma-
ra koṁda pāpake hōharu śrīrāmanāmake tappida pāpakke hō[haru]
śrīrāmanāmada sākṣi [||*] svadattāṁ [paradattāṁ] vā yō harēta vasuṁdharāṁ śaṣṭhi-
rvaruṣasahasrāṇi vriṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi[ḥ*] || svadattādviguṇaṁ pu(ṁ)ṇyaṁ pa-

sāvantanakōṭe rāmarājagaḷa voppa rāmayavrālu ||
śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 520.
(A.R. No. 368 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE GANAPATI SHRINE AT VODERHOBLI, A HAMLET
OF COONDAPUR, ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE ROAD TO UDIPI, COONDAPUR TALUK,
SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1448, Pārthiva, Phālguṇa, śu. 1, Sōmavāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1526 February 12, Monday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya ruling from
Vijayanagarī. It records that Liṅgarasa-Voḍeya, appointed as governor of the
Bārakūra-rājya by the king, placed Etirāya-Voḍeya in charge of the province and
that the latter made a gift of the income derived from Kōṭa in Kundāpura to
Raghupatitīrtha-Śrīpāda and Śrīnivāsatīrtha-Śrīpāda of Ānegondi (Vyāsarāya Maṭha),
for the prosperity of the king, on the occasion of a lunar eclipse.

l.ka:
śrīgaṇadhipatayē(ṁ) nama[ḥ*] śrīgurubhyō(ṁ) nama[ḥ*] śrīsarasvatyaṁ nama[ḥ*] nirvvi-
ghnamastu
rū bārakkūra rājyavanū liṁgarasavoḍeyarige pālsi ·a nirūpadiṁ [eti]rā-
vēdya naṁdādīpti ·avara bhikṣāsvāstege koṭṭa dānaśilāśāsanada kramaveṁ-
teṁdara | bārakkūra rājyada kuṁdāpurada grāmada voḷage bhaṁ[ḍā]rastaḷakke
saluva kōṭada ·adhivāsa 1 kkaṁ prāku kuḷa va ga 176¥ 3 ·akṣāradalu nūṟa
·eppattāṟu ho(ṁ)nnu mūṟu haṇa ·a ·adhivāsada suṁka va ga 10 ·ubhayaṁ
nūṟa·eṁbhattāru ho(ṁ)nnu mūṟu haṇavanū suvar(ṁ)nādāya saha kuḷava-
nu kaḍidu sōmōparāga pu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalū sahira(ṁ)ṇyōdaka saha dānadhārā-
pūrvakavāgi dhāreyaneṟadu koṭe·u yī nū[ṟa]eṁbhatāṟu hoṁnu
mūṟu haṇavanū niṁma śiśyapāraṁpareyāgi taṁdukoṁḍu sukhadalli ·anu
ḷāgi bāḷuviri yeṁdu sōmōparāgapu(ṁ)ṇyakā[lada*]lū sahiraṁṇyōdaka sa-
sarvvamā(ṁ)nya staḷavāgi kuḷavanū kaḍidu koṭṭadanū nimma śiśyapāraṁparayā-
voḍe[ya*]rigū śrīnivāsavoḍeyarige dhāreneṟadu koṭṭa dānaśilāśāsana || dānapāla-
taṁ padaṁ || bārakūra karaṇika tiṁmarasara baraha kōṭa ·adhivāsada ·u-
yara voppa maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 521.
(A.R. No. 559 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT CHERUVU-
BELAGALLU, KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the cyclic year Sarvajit, Āshāḍha, śu. 6, in the reign
of Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya, corresponding to A.D. 1527 June 6, Thursday (if
the month was Adhika) or July 5, Friday (if Nija). The date is not verifiable.
The inscription records that Suṅkaṇa-Nāyaka, son of Dāḍi-Nāyaka, made a gift of a
field in Keṟe-Beḷugallu in Ādavāni-durga under his amara, for the service of the
god Vīrabhadra at Beḷugallu. The gift was made for the merit of the king and of the
donor's father.

inscription.head:
No. 522.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1918.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE FRONT MANDAPA OF THE ISVARA TEMPLE AT GUDIHALLI,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1449, Sarvajit, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 10 corresponding to A.D. 1527
August 7, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya ruling
from Vijayanagarī. It records that while Rāyasada Nārāyaṇappa, son of Chinna-
Bhaṇḍārada Timmarasa, was ruling Noṇabanarasikeṟe in the voḷita of Koṭūra-sīme
and while his agent Nāgarasaya had the pārupatya over Koṭūra-sīme, Timmarasa,
son of Raṅgadāsa, who was administering Arasikeṟe under the orders of Nāgarasaya,
had the damaged sluice of the tank at the village rebuilt.

l.ka: ya purada tuṁbu muṟidu bidu keṭiralāgi
·ā tūṁbanu bicisi sōdhisi balumānada kalanu kaṁ-
ṭisi gāreyanu hākisi ceṁnāgi kaṭisi jīrnōdhā-
yelā nuḍidarvakau nilau nīroḷage lekhada kaṟadaṁdi ba-
llida raṁgaya tiṁmanu mellane mātāḍe kalaśāṣanamakuṁ [||*]
ḍali kaḍalukki haridu hōgali nuḍidare tapila bā-

inscription.head:
No. 523.
(A.R. No. 323 of 1925.)
ON A STONE PLANTED IN A FIELD AT MARABA, KUDLIGI TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1450, Sarvadhāri, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15, Śukravāra, corresponding
to A.D. 1528 July 31, Friday, in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya. It records that
Mallapaṇṇa of Elabarige caused the construction of a shop or stall and made a gift
of the same, together with the toll-revenue of the fair held in Malapura, and some
land at Moraba to the god (?) Sūryanārāyaṇadēva, for the merit of his mother,
Vīramma. Moraba is stated to have been situated in Pāṇḍ[y*]a-nāḍu.

l.ka: nāḍa vaḷi[ta*]ke saluva morabadali mallapaṁṇavarige puṇya-
purada ·aṁgaḍiya kaṭṭisi saṁtheya vasigeya sarvvamānyava bi[ṭṭu]
ra sēveyanu māḍikoṁḍu sukhadalli yihudu yeṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 524.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1904.)
ON A ROCK TO THE SOUTH OF THE BASAVESVARA TEMPLE AT KURUGODU,
BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1450, Sarvadhāri, Bhādrapada, ba. 1, correspond-
ing to A.D. 1528 August 30, Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇadēva-
Mahārāya. It records that Bāgila Kṛishṇarāya-Nāyaka made a gift of land for
offerings to the god Doḍḍa-Basavaṇṇa.

l.ka: koṭṭadu kuṟugōḍa mūḍaṇa hurigala • • • • • ḍehāḷi paramagoṁḍanu māḍi

inscription.head:
No. 525.
(A.R. No. 411 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE TEMPLE OF MAHALIN-
GESVARA AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1451, Sarvadhāri, Kārtika, śu. 1, corresponding to A.D. 1528
October 14, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It
records that, while Aḷiya Timmaṇṇa-Voḍeya was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya under the
king's orders, Śaṅkaraseṭṭi, son of Sōvaṇaseṭṭi, built a maṭha and that Mallapaseṭṭi,
son of Saṅkaraseṭṭi's first wife, and Saṅkamanāyakiti, Saṅkaraseṭṭi's second wife, and
her sister, Dēku Aḍeyiti, endowed the maṭha with some plots of land for its main-
tenance.

l.ka:
svasti śrīgaṇādhipata·eṁ namaḥ | śrīsarasvatye·eṁ nama[ḥ*] śrīgurubhyō(ṁ)nama[ḥ*] nirvvi-
ghnamastu | namastuṁgaśi-

saṁdu ·ayivattavoṁdaneya sarvadhārisaṁvachsarada kārtika śu 1 lū śrīmadrājādhirāja
rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa kru-

ṣṇarāyamahārāyarū vijayanagariya siṁhāsanadalū sakala sāṁbrājyavanu pratipāli-
sutiha kāladalli ·avara nirūpa-

diṁda ·aragada ·aḷiya timmaṁṇavoḍeyarū bārakūra rājyavanū ·āḷuvali basaṟū[ra*] paḍu-
kēryya nakharēśvarada śrīmahāliṁgadēvara badakaṭṭi-

nalli sōvaṇaseṭṭiya maga saṁkaraseṭṭiyū maṭhavanū kaṭṭisi māḍida dharmake ·ātana saṁ-
tānadavaru mūvaru tāyamakkaḷo-

ḷage hiriya tāya maga malpaseṭṭi biṭṭa sarvvasvada vivara māṇikoḷala hadahinoḷage hū-
liya ·aṁṇappahebbā-

rana makkaḷu yośvarahebbāra mādappahebbāranavara mūlada bāḷa catusīmeya vivara baḍaga
lu ·arevosēriya gadeya

gaḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu mūḍalu tolādhāriya gaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu teṁkalu hūliya kaḷame
keyana gaḍiyiṁ baḍagalu paḍu-

valu harida sāliṁda mūḍalu yī nālku catu[ssī*]meya voḷage beḷugaleṁba gadde nā-
gaṁḍugadalu mu 5 baya-

lagade yidaṟa kūḍaṇa mekke hūliya makkaḷa gaḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu gadde 2 kaṁ muḍi 2
yo mekkeya teṁkaṇa teṁgina tō-

ṭade catussīmeya vivara baḍagalu ·aṁṇappahebbāruvana hoyimaṁṇa mekkeya gaḍiyiṁdaṁ
teṁkalu mūḍalu hū-

liya makkaḷa mūlada tōṭada gaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu teṁkalu tolādhāriya hālagadeya
gaḍiyiṁdaṁ baḍagalu paḍuva-

lu ·aṁṇapahebārana tōṭada gaḍiyiṁdaṁ muḍalu yiṁtī nālku gaḍiyiṁdoḷagaṇa teṁgina
tōṭa

bayalu mekke saha nāghaṁḍugada ba | mu 7 yī sarvvasvavanu biḍāruvāravāgi cikadaṁ-
ṇaṁdaru bārakūra parivartana-

ke saluva doḍa va ga 81 mata kuṭige teko[ṁ*]ḍa va ga 5 hodekaṭige tekoṁḍa va ga 5
mulādige va ga 10 mayisāla va ga 10 ·aṁtu

• • • • tegedukoṁḍaru • • • • haṁnoṁdu varahananu saṁkaraseṭiya teṁma
mallapaseṭṭi koṭanu

• • • • • • hebāra tegedukū[ṁ*]ḍanu yo bāḷa kuḍaṇa tōṭadali teṁginasasiya
naṭadake geṁḍege

voṁdu hoṁnina maryādeyali ·āruvārada ·ārthava koḍuvāga koṭṭu baharu yo bāḷige
yeḷaura ·aṁṇuse-

ṭiya hadibayala gaddege mukkālu vosari mekeya gade mu 2 ke bēpaḷeya hadi kālu
vosari teṟu ·ubhaya

voṁdosari teṟa tettu bāḷuvaru tōṭake hadihārana hadikaṭṭu teṟu nālku haṇa yo
patrakāra hoṇe ·eḷa-

·ura ·aṁṇuseṭṭi sākṣigaḷu tiṭiya śaṁkharanārāyaṇa hebāra saṁṇaṁṇa bhaṭakartara vopa
huliya

bhaṁgāru hoṇegāṟana vopa maṁḍekaṭṭina braṁmaru sākṣi doregaḷa vopa yiṁtivarubhayā-
nmatadiṁ ·avaḷa-

ttiya vāmana nārāyaṇabhaṭṭara baraha mataṁ mallapaseṭṭiya kiriyatāyi saṁkaṁmanā-
yakiti ·ākeya taṁgi dēku-

·aḍeyati yī yibbaraṁ taṁma pālige baṁda dhar(ṁ)make biṭṭa bāḷu hāranāḍa voḷagaṇa
haḍahu hareyaṁgōḍu ·u-

ppina kuduru yo yeraḍu gaḍi yelaṁ pōtarasara maga śankaradēvana mūla talākaheggaḍi-
tiya ·ārūvāra ·ā talākahega-

ḍitiya makaḷa kayyali nāu ·aṁtarārūvāra māḍikoṁḍa bāḷa catusīmeya vivara mū
ḍalu sōmayājiya gaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu

teṁkalu kaṭiṁdaṁ baḍagalu paḍuvalu siṁgēriya voḍeyara kaḷiya kaṭṭiṁdaṁ mūḍalu baḍa
galu hareyaṁgōḍa kaḷakaḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu yiṁ-

tī catusīmeyiṁda voḷagaṇa śankaradēvana kaḷiyavoḷage bayalu muḍi 12 yidaṟa kū-
ḍaṇa beṭṭu mu 18 ·ubhaya mu 30 gadeya

voḷage kōṭyappa haḍavāḷage mūṟoṁdu bhāgige baṁda hattu mūḍe gadeya mēlaṇa ga
60 varahananū kaḷadu naṁma yibbara pālu gade mu-

ḍi 20 ṟa mēle koṭṭa ·ardha bārakūra parivartanakke saluva doḍa varaha ga 120 ·akṣāradalū
nūṟayipatu doḍavaraha yo bāḷige

teṟu sabhāyitana harikōṭeya kēriya nāruseṭige kāla 1 ke naḍasuva hareya bhatta mu
1 | ya kaḷaci naḍava gēṇi nāghaṁḍugadalū ·a-

ki mu 24 yo patrake ·āda hoṇe kōṭeya kēriya nāruseṭṭi sākṣigaḷu jaṁni ·āṁṇamē-
lāḍi hebbārubhaṁḍāri siṁgēriya voḍe-

yara manuṣya rāmasēvabōva • • • • kalamaḷāḍiya madhyasta nārāyaṇa-
bhaṭṭara baraha mattaṁ ·anegaḷiya kēśava mūḍakere-

ya mūla manjanapāṭiṁ baḍagaṇa • • • ya vaḷiyavara gaḍiyiṁ teṁkkalu kuṁbaḷa saṁ-
kara mūḍakereyakkaḷa gaḍiyiṁ paḍavalu mēlāḍi

• • • seṭiya gaḍiyiṁ baḍagalu • • • • • yakkaḷa gaḍiyiṁ mūḍalu yo
nālku gaḍiyi[ṁ*]doḷage me mu 1 ko 10 mattaṁ bhānapana gaḍi-

yiṁdaṁ paḍuva kōṭihebārana gaḍiyiṁda mūḍalu kōṭiṁbagada hā 30 mattaṁ hiri-
bāḷa

makaḷa gaḍiyiṁ paḍuvalu • • • • • mūḍalu kallagade ·eṁbudu mu 1 mattaṁ
hiriya bāḷa makaḷa gaḍiyiṁ paḍuvalu harida sā-

liṁda mūḍalu • • • • • • • • • • mattaṁ hiribāḷa makaḷa gaḍiyiṁ teṁkalu nāraṇa-
dēvana gaḍiyiṁ baḍagalu kaṭaṇahāḷi hā

10 mattaṁ kōṭi kaṁḍavara gaḍiyiṁ teṁkalū • • • • • • kereya kaḷagaḍiyiṁ
baḍagalu mūḍaṇa hitilahāḷi mu hā 10 mattaṁ kōṭi kaṁḍavara

gaḍiyiṁ teṁkalu • • • • • • paḍuvalu mūḍaṇa gaḍiya hāḷi hā 10 ·aṁttu
bittuva me mu 4 gadeyanū dēkuseṭiti

biḍāruvāravāgi māḍikoṁḍu koṭa doḍavaraha kunduvoḍekaṭige koṭa ga 3 yo bāḷige
teṟu kāṭi kaṁdahabārana ha-

di vosari = heraḷihebārana hadi vosari |—sabācana hadi vosari = ·aṁtu vosari || —yo
kaṭina hoṇe heraḷiya kēśahebāra sākṣi-

gaḷu ·aṁṇahebārana maga gōviṁdahebāra kāhāṭiya śaṁkharahebāra madhyasta śrīdhara-
bhaṭana baraha
śrīgaṇādhipata·ēṁ nama | mata mahāliṁgadēvarige naṁdādīptigū purāṇada dīpti-
gū saṁkarahegaḍeya ·āḷiya mallappaseṭi māḍida dharma taṁma mūlada teṁgina tōṭa-

da catusīme mūḍalu naḍuvaccara baḷiyavara gaḍiyiṁda paḍuvalu teṁkalu halara gaḍiyali
baḍagalu paḍuvalu

inscription.head:
No. 526.
(A.R. No. 619 of 1904.)
ON THE SOUTH, EAST AND NORTH WALLS OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE
VEDANARAYANASVAMI TEMPLE AT NAGALAPURAM, PONNERI TALUK, CHINGLEPUT
DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the cyclic year Sarvadhāri, Māgha, śu. 3, correspond-
ing to A.D. 1529 January 13, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-
Mahārāya. It records that the services of the god Vēdagiri-Perumāḷ should be
conducted as detailed in the copper-plate document granted for the purpose and that
the expenditure should be met from the income of the villages granted for the
purpose. It also states that the fine levied from the culprits who removed the
boundary stones should be given to the temple.

l.ka:
sarvvadhārisaṁvatsarada māgha śu 3 lu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparemēśvara śrīvīrapra-
tāpa śrīvīrakṛṣṇarāyamahārāyaru virupayaṇa • yappa nāgalādēvi ·aramanepura
dēvastānakke·ū ·aṣṭamāṁśadanitakke·ū naṁma rāyasa prakāradale hākida śāsana
yelleka • • • da kallugaḷanu kittuhākida durjanaru mūvvarige takka kaṭṭu
māḍi ·avara mēlaṇa lekkada hoṁnanū koṁḍu guḍige koḍisūdu ·aṣṭamāṁśakke mo-
dalu viṁgaḍisi koṭṭa gadde mānyanelanū ·allade mikkāda nelanū guḍiya viniyōgada-
vara jītakke viṁgaḍisūdu viṣṇustānadalli durjanaru māḍida ·akrutyagaḷanu biḍisi saṁni-
dhimēlaṇa vēdagiriperumāḷige tiruṇāḷanu sāgisi pūjeyanu māḍisūdu guḍige ·uṁ-
ṭāda kaṭaḷḷege·ū tiruvaḷiyāṭa grāmagaḷige·ū cātina cātāḷavayiṣṇavarige·ū
kriṣṇarāya modali dinadalli ·avanige naḍada stānanirvāha ·ubhaya grāmagaḷige·ū hiṁ-
dakke naṁma rāyana tāṁbraśāsana pālisi naḍada mariyāde yogalu naḍava hāge kaṭṭa-
ḷeya māḍe cittayiste·u ·ā mariyādele naḍasibahudu ·ā mariyādele yellavanu naḍasi
bahāge kaṭṭaḷeya māḍuvudu ·aṣṭamāṁśadavarige hiṁdakke viṁgaḍisi koṭṭa staḷadale
yihāge kaṭṭaḷeya māḍūdu dēvara pūja punaskāra ·aṁgaraṁgavayibhōgagaḷanū

sāṁgavāgi naḍasūdu nāū hiṁdakke dēvastānakke ·ubhayakaṭṭaḷe modalāgi tiruvaḷeyāṭa-
kaṁ kaṭṭaḷeya māḍe cittayista śīmegaḷu voṁdaralū kaḍameyillada hāge naḍasūdu
āgi ·ā stitiyanu [ni]rūpa cittayistau śrīvirupākṣa [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 527.
(A.R. No. 626 of 1904.)
AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE SECOND GOPURA OF THE VEDAVALLI-TAYAR SHRINE
IN THE VEDANARAYANASVAMI TEMPLE IN THE SAME VILLAGE.
inscription.p: This is a copy of A.R. No. 619 of 1904 above.

l.ka:
svasti śrī [||*] sarvvadhārisaṁvatsarada māgha śu 3 lu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rāja paramē-
śvara śrīvīrapratāpa
l.note: Thesvasti zrī are written at the beginning of the second line.

l.ka:
ya pura dēvastānakke·ū ·aṣṭamāṁ(va)śadanitakke·ū taṁma rāyasa prakāradale hākida
sāsaneyallekaṭu • • • da kallu-

gaḷanu kittuhākida durjanaru yibaru mūvarigetakka kaṭṭupāṭu māḍi ·avara mēlaṇa lekhada
hoṁnanū

tegadu guḍige koḍisuvudu ·aṣṭamāṁ(va)śake modalu viṁgaḍisi koṭa gadde mānyanelanu
allade mikkaṁ-

thā nelanu guḍiya viniyōgadavara jītakke viṁgaḍisūdu viṣṇustānadalli durjanaru māḍida
akrutyagaḷanu biḍi-

si girimēlaṇa vēdagiriperumāḷige tiruṇāḷanu sāgisi pūjeyanu māḍisūdu guḍa[ge*]
uṁṭāda kaṭṭa-
ḷege·u tiruvaḷiyāṭa grāmagaḷige·u cātina cātāda vayiṣṇavarige·u kriṣṇarāya mo

dali dinadalli ·avanige naḍada stāna nirvāha ·ubhayagrāmagaḷige·u hiṁdake naṁma rāyasa
tāṁbra-

sāsana pālisi naḍada mariyādele yogalu naḍeva hāge kaṭṭaḷemāḍi cittayiste·u ·ā mari-
yāde-

li naḍasibahudu ·ā mariyādele yellavanū naḍisi bahāge kaṭṭaḷaya māḍuvudu ·aṣṭamāṁ-
(va)śadava-

rige hiṁdakke viṁgaḍisikoṭṭa staḷadale • • • • • • • • • • ḍūdū dēvara pūje puna-
skāra ·aṁga-

raṁgavayibhōgagaḷanu sāṁgavāgi naḍasūdu nāvu hiṁdakke dēvastānakaṁ ·ubhaya kaṭṭaḷe
modalā-

gi tiruveḷeyāṭakū kaṭṭaḷeya māḍe cittayistagrāmagaḷu voṁdaralu kaḍameyillada hā-
ge naḍasūdu

inscription.head:
No. 528.
(A.R. No. 194 of 1913.)
ON A STONE IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT HAVINAHALU-
VIRAPURA, BELLARY TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1450, Sarvadhāri, Māgha, śu. 6 corresponding to A.D. 1529
January 15, Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya. It records
that the people of the village of Doṇḍavaṭe made a grant of land to Malesāṇi, son of
Guṇḍapa for repairing the tank at the village. The gift is stated to have been made
under the orders of Mudaṇa-Nāyaka to whom the king had given the village as an
umbaḷi.

l.ka: saṁvacharada māgha śu 6 lu śrīmatu śrīma[n]-
parasara maga vonagoṁḍadēvanu vokalu maṟaḍi ·usiku grāma
dasavaṁdada śāśanada kramaveṁtaṁdare naṁma doṁḍavaṭeya grā-
mau nīruvari yilade grāma khilavāgiralāgi nāu ·ellarū
mudaṇanāyakarige biṁnahaṁ māḍi nāvu niṁmanu voḍaṁba-
ḍisida vivara[u] naṁma doṁḍavaṭeya grāmadalu niṁma kaiya ma
• • • nu yiki keṟeya kaṭisidare na(ṁ)mmu seragrāma sirigeṟeya gō-
vaṇagauḍa muruḍanūra hanumaṁtagauḍa yivarige naḍada kaṭumā-
nyada dasavaṁda ·āta[nu] kaṭisida keṟeya keḷage hāga naḍadu bahudu hāga
māḍi niṁma makaḷa makaḷa thara • • • • bahe·u yida
ḍi baṁdare·u naṁma grāma • • • biṁnahaṁ mā-
dare yo ·ūra mādāṟa makaḷu ·ēṁdu ho[yda] śāśanada kalu
dharmavē jaya maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 529.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1913.)
ON A STONE IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT YALPI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1451, Virōdhi, Māgha, śu. 1[1], corresponding to
A.D. 1530 January 9, Sunday (not verifiable). It records that Daṇāyakara Timmaras-
made a grant of varāhas 36 payable out of the revenue of the village Yaḷape, which
was granted to him by Kṛishṇarāya-Mahārāya, for feeding in the satra 12 Brāmans
that may come to the village. The charity was made for the merit of king Achyuta-
rāya-Mahārāya.

l.ka: lu śrīmatu daṇāyakara tiṁmarasaru māḍida sa-
namage mukhāse kudurege pālisida yaḷapeya grāma-
ga 3 ra lekhadalu varuṣa 1 kaṁ gha[ṭṭivara]ha ga 36 mū-
lu naḍasuvaru naḍasade tapidare taṁma tāyi taṁdegaḷanu vā-
taṁ padaṁ [||*] yēkaikabhaginī lōkē sarvēṣāmēva bhūbhu-
ja na bhōjya na karagrāhyā vipradattā vasuṁdharā [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 530.
(A.R. No. 716 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE HANUMANTARAYA TEMPLE AT KAMPLI,
HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and the date is partly lost. It mentions the cyclic year Virōdhi
(= A.D. 1529-30) in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya and records the gift made by
him of the revenue raised in the bazaar street of Vijaya-Virupāpura for the
service of the god Mallikārjunadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 531.
(A.R. No. 453 of 1920.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD BY THE SIDE OF THE CART-TRACK TO VAJRAKARUR
FROM THE VILLAGE POTTIPADU, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1452, Vikṛiti, Chaitra, ba. 1, Sōmavāra, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1530 March 14, Monday, in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya. It
records that Bāsappa-Nāyaka made a gift of some plots of land for the service of the god,
Channakēśava, at Karūr. The gift was made for the merit of the king.

l.ka: phalaṁ bhavētu || svadattāṁ paradattāṁ [vā yō harē]-

inscription.head:
No. 532.
(A.R. No. 200 of 1910.)
ON SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE ANKALAMMA TEMPLE AT ADHAMANKOTTAI,
DHARMAPURI TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This incomplete record is dated Śaka 1452, Vikṛiti, Bhādrapada, śu. 5, correspond
ing to A.D. 1530 August 27, Saturday or 28 Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of
Immaḍi Achyutadēvarāya-Mahārāya. It records a gift of the toll-revenue raised in
the fair held in the presence of the god Bhairavadēva, for the service of the same god
by Kāmiyappa-Nāyaka, son of Bommiyappa-Nāyaka, by reviving the fair.

l.ka: ru kaṭṭaḷeyanikki dēvadāya hoṟagāgi sarvvamānyavāgi bi-
siyalli brāhmaṇana koṁda pāpadalli gaṁgeyalli ka-
pileya koṁda pāpadalli tamma guru daiva mātru pitru-
gaḷige tappida pāpadalli hōharu ·athavā kakkulike bi-
ḍi baḍita taṁdavana bāyi kōṣṭa yo bhāṣege voṟida-
du bareda śāsana kāmappanāyakara hēḷikeya baraha

inscription.head:
No. 533.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1904.)
ON A ROCK EAST OF THE TRAVLLEER'S BUNGALOW ON THE BELLARY ROAD
AT KAMALAPURAM, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1453, Khara, Chaitra, śu. 15, Sthiravāra corre-
sponding to A.D. 1531 April 1, Saturday. It refers itself to the reign of Achyutarāya-
Mahārāya and records that Sāḷuva-Daṇāyaka made a gift of land for burning a
perpetual lamp before the god Narasiṁhadēva in Koṇḍamarasayana-Pāḷya.
The writer was Timmapa, son of Liṅgarasa of Cheraṭimaṅgala.

l.ka: kharasaṁvatsarada cayitra śu 15 stiravāsaradalu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rāja-
tiralu sāḷuvadaṇāyakaru namage naḍadu baṁda kāmalāpurada staḷadalu koṁḍamarasaya-

navara pāḷayadali śrīnārasiṁhadēvara nityadīpārādhanege koṭṭa bhūdānada kramaveṁteṁda
dī 1 ke vīsa 10

naṁdādīpa 1 kāni yo parimāṇa pālisi koṭa staḷa (mūḍaluḷa) mūḍalu koṁḍamarasaya
uriyapau-
ḷiyoḷagaṇa mūleyali cakrakalu 1 ·adake teṁkalu nīra hoḷeya dārige bhaḍagalu
vīramāsuvalige teṁkalu cakrakalu 1 yidake paḍuvalu hūvina tōṭa
baḍagalu nele ga • • mēle cakrakalu 1 yidake baḍagalu rājabī-
tōṭa nīruvarida • • • • lu yo catusīmege voḷagāda
kapileya bhāvi yo catusīme voḷagāda staḷavellavanū

inscription.head:
No. 534.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1904.)
ON THE SOUTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE VITTHALASVAMI
TEMPLE. AT HAMPI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1453, Khara, Jyēshṭha, śu. 15, corresponding to
A.D. 1531 May 30, Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya. It
records that a village was granted by the king for offerings to the god Viṭṭhaladēva.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] śrīviṭhaladēvara nityanaivēdyada kaṭaḷege ·acutarāyamahārāyaru samarpi-
sida grāma

svasti śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruśa 1453 ṟaneya kharasaṁvacharada jyēṣṭha śu
15 lū

prāku samarpisida harugōla hoḷe virupākṣa viṭha[lā]hu[ti]ge hoṁnuruke koḷagaṭe
kaṁpilāpura • • •

inscription.head:
No. 535.
(A.R. No. 579 of 1912.)
ON THE ROCKY FLOOR OF THE SECOND PRAKARA OF THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE
AT LEPAKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1453, Khara, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 8, corresponding to
A.D. 1531 August 6, Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyuta.
After giving the genealogy of the Vijayanagara kings in Sanskrit verses it records
that at the request of Virapaṇṇa, son of Nandi-Lakkiseṭṭi and Muddamāmbā, the king
made a gift of the village Chelaviṇḍla, surnamed Achyutēndrapura, in the presence of
the god Virūpāksha on the bank of the Tungabhadrā, for the service of the gods
Vīrabhadra, Raghunātha and Pāpavināśa of Lēpākshi.

l.ka:
śrīgaṇādhipatayē namaḥ | namastuṁgaśiraśuṁbicaṁdracāmara • • • trailōkya-
nagarāraṁbha • • • • yaśaṁbhavē [||*] harē • • • • • • • • • •
• • pātu vaḥ | hēmādrikalaśā yatra dhātrīchatraśriyaṁ dadau || [kalyāṇā-
yāstuta]ddhāma pratyūhatimirāpahaṁ | ya[dga]jōpyagajō[dbhūtaṁ] hariṇāpi
ca pūjyatē [||*] ·asti kṣī[ramayāddēvyai]rma-

thyamānāṁ mahāṁbudhēḥ | navanīta[mivōdbhūtamapanītatamōmahaḥ [||*] [ta]syāsīttana-
yastapōbhira[tulairanva]rthanāmā bu[dhaḥ puṇyairasya purūravā bhu]jabalairāyu-
rdviṣāṁ nighnataḥ | tasyāyurnahuṣōsya tasya puruṣō yuddhē yayāti[ḥ*] kṣi-
tau khyātastasya tu turvasurvasunibhaḥ śrīdēvayānipatēḥ [||*] tadvaṁśē dēvakījā-
nirdidēpē tiṁ[mabhūpatiḥ |]

yaśasvī tuḷuvēṁdrēṣu yadō[ḥ*] kruṣṇayivānvayē [||*] tatōbhūdbukkamā-
jāni[rīśvaraḥ] kṣitipālakaḥ | ·a[trāsama]guṇabhraṁśaṁ mauḷira[tnaṁ] ma[hī]
bhujāṁ [||*] sarasādudabhūttasmānnarasāvanipālakaḥ | dēvakinaṁdanātkāmō dēvakī-
naṁdanādiva [||*] vividhasukrutōddāmē rāmēśvara[pramukhē] muhurmuditahru-
dayasthānē sthānē vyadhatta ya[thāvidhi |]

budhapari·uradhō nānādānāni yō bhuvi ṣōḍaśa tribhu[vanajanōdgī]taṁ sphītaṁ yaśaḥ
punaru[ktaya]n™ [||*] kāvērīmāśu badhvā ba[ha]ḷaja[lara]yāṁ yō vilaṁghyaiva
śatruṁ jīvagrāhaṁ grihītvā samiti bhujabalāttaṁ ca rājyaṁ ta[dī]yaṁ | krutvā
śrīraṁgapūrvaṁ tadapi nijavaśē paṭṭaṇaṁ yō babhāsē (|) kīrttistaṁbhaṁ nikhāya
tribhuvanabhavanastūya-

mānāpadānaḥ [||*] cēraṁ cōḷaṁ ca pāṁḍyaṁ tamapi ca madhurāvallabhaṁ mānabhūṣaṁ
vīryō[da]graṁ [turuṣkaṁ ga]japatinrupatiṁ cāpi jitvā tadanyān | ·āgaṁgātīra-
laṁkāprathamacaramabhūbhṛttaṭāṁtaṁ nitāṁtaṁ khyā[taḥ] kṣōṇīpatīnāṁ srajamiva
śirasā śāsanaṁ yō vyatānīt [||*] tippājīnāgalādēvyōḥ kaṁsalyāśrīsumi[tra]-

yōḥ | dēvyōriva nrusiṁhēṁdrāttasmātpaṁktirathādiva [||*] vīrau vinayinau rāma-
lakṣmaṇāviva naṁdanau | jautau [vīranṛsiṁhē]ṁdrakruṣṇarāya mahīpatī [||*] vīra-
śrīnārasiṁhassa vijayanagarē ratnasiṁhāsanasthaḥ kīrtyā nītyā nirasyannruganaḷanahu-
ṣānapyavanyāma[thā]nyān | rāsētōrā sumērōravanisuranutaḥ svairamācō-
dayādrērāpāścātyācalāṁ-

tādakhiḷahṛdayamāvarjya rājyaṁ śaśāsa [||*] nānādānānyakārṣītkanakasadasi yaḥ śrī-
virūpākṣadēvasthānē śrīkāḷahastīśiturapi nagarē vēṁkaṭādrō ca kāṁcyāṁ | śrī-
śailē śōṇaśailē mahati hariharēhōbalē saṁgamē ca śrīraṁgē kuṁbhaghōṇē
hatatamasi mahānaṁditīrthē nivṛttau [||*] gōkarṇē rāmasētau jagati taditarēṣva-
pyaśēṣēṣu pu(ṁ)ṇyasthānē-

ṣvārabdhanānāvidhabahaḷama[hā]dānavāripravāhaiḥ [|*] yasyōdaṁcatturaṁgaprakarakhurara-
jaśśuṣya[daṁbhōdhimagnakṣmābhṛtpa]kṣacchidōdyatkuliśadharōtkaṁṭhitā kuṁṭhitābhūt |
[|*] braṁhāṁḍaṁ viśvacakraṁ ghaṭamuditamahābhūtakaṁ ratnadhēnuṁ saptāṁbhōdhīṁśca
kalpakṣitiruhalatikē kāṁcanīṁ kāmadhēnuṁ | svarṇakṣmāṁ yō hiraṇyāśvarathamapi
tulāpū-

ruṣaṁ gōsahasraṁ hēmāśvaṁ hēmagarbhaṁ kanakakarirathaṁ paṁcalāṁgalyatānīt [||*]
prājyaṁ pra[śāsya nirvighnaṁ] rājyaṁ dyāmiva śāsituṁ | tasminguṇēna vikhyātē
kṣitērindrē divaṁ gatē [||*] tatōpyavāryavīryaśrīkruṣṇarāyamahīpatiḥ |
bibharti maṇikēyūranirviśēṣaṁ mahīṁ bhujē [||*] kīrtyā yasya samaṁtataḥ
prasṛtayā viśvaṁ

rucaikyaṁ vrajēdityāśaṁkya purā purārirabhavadbhālēkṣaṇaḥ prāyaśaḥ | padmākṣōpi catu-
rbhujō[jani caturvaktrōbhavatpadmabhūḥ] kāḷī [khaḍgama] dādramā ca kamalaṁ
vīṇāṁ ca vāṇī karē [||*] śatrūṇāṁ vāsamētē dadata ·iti ruṣā kiṁnu saptāṁbu-
rāsīn nānāsēnāturaṁgatruṭitavasumatīdhūlikāpālikābhiḥ | saṁśōṣya
svairamētatpratinidhijala-

dhiśrīṇikāṁ yō vidhattē(|) brahmāṁḍasvarṇamērupramukhanijamahādānatōyairamēyaiḥ
[||*] [maddattāmarthisārthāśśriyami]ha suciraṁ bhuṁjatāmityavētya prāyaḥ pratyū-
hahētōstapanarathagatērālayān dēvatānāṁ | tattaddigjaitravṛttyāpi ca birudapa-
dairaṁkitāṁstatra tatra staṁbhān jātapratiṣṭhānvyatanuta bhuvi yō bhūbhṛdabhraṁkaṣā-

grān [||*] kāṁcīśrīśailaśōṇācalakanakasabhāvēṁkaṭādripramukhyēṣvā vṛtyāvṛtya
sarvēṣvatanuta [vidhivadbhūyasē śrīyasē yaḥ | dēvasthānēṣu] tīrthēṣvapi kanaka-
tulāpūruṣādīni nānādānānyēvōpadānairapi samamakhiḷairāgamōktāni tāni |
[||*] rōṣakṛtapratipārthivadaṁḍaḥ śēṣabhujakṣitirakṣaṇaśauṁḍaḥ | bhāṣegetappuvarāyara
gaṁḍastōṣakrudarthiṣu yō raṇa-

caṁḍaḥ [||*] rājādhirāja ·ityuktō yō rājaparamēśvaraḥ | mūrurāyaragaṁḍaścapararā-
[yabhayaṁkaraḥ [||*] hiṁdu]rāyasuratrāṇ[ō] duṣṭaśārdūlamardanaḥ | vīrapratāpa
ityādi birudairucitairyyutaḥ [||*] ·ālōkaya mahārāja jaya jīvēti vādibhiḥ
aṁgavaṁgakaḷiṁgādyai rājabhissēvyatē ca yaḥ [||*]

stutyaudāryassudhībhiḥ sa vijayanagarē ratnasi[ṁ*]hvāsanasthaḥ kṣmāpālān kruṣṇarā-
yakṣitipati[radharīkṛtya nītyā nṛ]gādīn | ·āpūrvādrērathāstakṣitidharakaṭakādā ca
hēmācalāṁtādā sētōrarthisārthaśriyamiha bahaḷīkrutya kīrtyā babhāsē ||
krutavati suralōkaṁ kruṣṇarāyē nijāṁśaṁ tadanu tadanujanmā puṇyaka-

rmācyutēṁdraḥ [||*] prakaṭamavanilōkaṁ svāṁśamētyārijētā vilasati ha[ri*]jētā
vidvadiṣṭapradātā || śrutavarakavicētōraṁjanē kaṁjanētrē krutavati suralōkaṁ kru-
ṣṇarāyē svavaṁśaṁ | tadanu tadanujastaṁ prāpya bhūlōkamindhē vitanumadanudaṁgaśrībha-
rairacyutēṁdraḥ [||*] yatkīrticaṁdraścarati kṣamāyāṁ tithiṣvaśēṣēṣu

vivardhatē ca | tanōti cakrasya mudaṁ samiṁdhē divā ca sāyaṁ kumudairviruṁdhē [||*]
madaṁ manasi mā[rutaṁ] śithilayatyamēyairayairyadaśvapaṭalīkhurai[ḥ*] kṣitirajō-
bhirutthāpitaiḥ | ·ajījanaditi krudā kiṁ viśōṣayatyaṁbudhiṁ balapramathanasya nō
rayamirōdhinaṁ[vājinaṁ ||*] kārāgruhākalitavāsavirōdhibhūpadhārāva-

ḷīkaravicālitacāmarasya [|*] rājādhirājapararājabhayaṁkaraikavīrādhikāni birudāni [ba
hūni] yasya || gōkaṁrnasaṁgamani·urattisuvarnasaṁsacchōṇādriparvataviriṁcipurēṣu
kāṁcyāṁ | śrīkāḷahastinagarē pi ca kuṁbhaghōṇē dānāni ṣōḍaśa bahūni krutāni
yēna || ·aṁbhōdēna nipīyamānasalilō-

gastyēna pītōjjhitaḥ taptō rāghavasāyakāgniśikhayā saṁtapyamānaḥ sadā | ·aṁtasthai-
rvaḍabānalasya ca[śikhājālai]rviśuṣkōtha vā yaddānāṁbu ghanāṁburaṁbudhirayaira-
harniśamahō pūrṇaḥ samudyōtatē [||*] ·aṁgēnāpi kaḷiṁgēna vaṁgēna ca
parairnṛpaiḥ | jaya jīva mahārājētyaniśaṁ gīyatē ca yaḥ [||*] sa jayati nerapā-

lō ratnasiṁhāsanasthō vijayanagaravāsī kīrtipūrtyā vilāsī [||*] nruganaḷanahuṣā-
dīṁ nīcayanrājanītyā nirupamabhujavīryaudāryabhūracutākhyaḥ || śakābdē-
śśālivāhanasya sahasrēṇa catuśśataiḥ | paṁcāvatā ca saṁkhyātē trayābhyadhikayā
kramāt [||*] kharanāmani varṣē ca māsi srāvaṇanāmani | pu(ṁ)ṇyāyāṁ

gōkulāṣṭamyāṁ śubhaṁgyau samayēpi ca || tuṁgabhadrāpagātīrē śrīvirūpākṣasaṁni-
dhau | nijēna bhujavīryēṇa nirjitākhilarakṣasā [||*] pūjitasya purā bhaktipūrvakaṁ
raghusūnunā | prītyā pāṁgayataḥ pāriśvāvāsinīṁ śvavilāsinīṁ [||*] tāvubhau
sanidhasthau ca vīrabhadraraghūdvahau | svarṇamukhyā tayā guptagāminyā ca manō-
harē [||*] kū-

rmākṛtau mahāśailē sadāvāsamupēyuṣaḥ | trayāṇāmasi lōkānāṁ bhartuḥ pāpa-
vināśinaḥ [||*] • • • dāsthānavāsinasmaraśāsinaḥ dhūpairdīpairanēkaiśca vādyai-
rhṟaidyaissagītibhiḥ [||*] nrutyaiśca bahubhiryuktāṁ cakṣurānaṁdadāyibhiḥ | ma-
hābhiṣēkanaivēdyaviśiṣṭairupaśōbhitāṁ [||*] kartuṁ triṣvapi kālē-

ṣu mahāpūjāṁ dinē dinē | ghanagiriyāhvayē rājyē prājyē vāsamupāsitaṁ [||*]
roddanāḍuvarāṁtasthaṁ prācyāṁ śrītōṭahaḷḷitaḥ | grāmādvaḷidarīkākhyaṁ dakṣiṇāṁ
diśamāśritaṁ | paścimāṁtē kulōḍasya vonakuṁṭāgrahārataḥ | ·uttarasyāṁ diśi prauḍha-
vāsaṁ khyātimupāśritaṁ [||*] gollahaḷyā ca pērmakkahaḷyāpi ca samanvitaṁ |

śrīpētippanahaḷyā ca [rācahaḷyā ca*] saṁyutaṁ [||*] hanumaṁtanahaḷyā ca lokki-
kuṁṭēna cānvitaṁ | seṁyutaṁ bommireḍyākhyahaḷyāpi ca manōgñayā [||*] cenna-
mmanāyakābhikhya haḷyāpi ca samanvitaṁ | gaṇapatyākhya[haḷḷī*]ti grāmagrāsēna
saṁyutaṁ [||*] ·acyutēṁdra puraṁ cēti pratināmnā samāśritaṁ | celaviṁḍlāhvaya-
grāmōpāṁ-

tavartimahīdharaṁ [||*] sarvamānyaṁ catussīmāsaṁyutaṁ ca samaṁtataḥ | nidhinikṣēpapāṣāṇa-
siddhasādhyajalānvitaṁ [||*] ·akṣīṇyāgāmisaṁyuktaṁ dēvabhōgyaṁ sabhūruhaṁ | vā-
pīkūpataṭākaiśva kacchēnāpi samanvitaṁ [||*] dānasyādhamanasyāpi vikrayasyāpi
cōcitaṁ | parītaḥ prayataiḥ snigdhaiḥ purōhitapurōgamaiḥ [||*] [vi*]vidhairvibudhai
śśrau-

tapathikairadhikairgirā | mātuśśrī muddamākhyāyā balēna tapasāṁ dhruvaṁ [||*] naṁdiśrī-
lakkiseṭṭyabdhipūr(ṁ)ṇacaṁdrēṇa • • • | vīrabhadraikacittēna virapaṁṇēna cō-
ditaḥ [||*] ·acutēṁdramahārāyō mānanīyō manīṣiṇāṁ | sahira(ṁ)ṇyapayō-
dhārāpūrvakaṁ dattavānmudā [||*] grāmasyāsyaiva sīmānō dikṣu prācyādiṣu
kramāt | tattaccihnasamāyuktāḥ

likhyaṁtē dēśabhāṣayā [||*] celaviṁḍlagrāmada holamēre dēśābhāgada paḍuvalādi mala-
gūra holakke naṁma [naḍau śāśanada nilu·ugallu] ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu rācahaḷḷiya
araḷīmarakke paḍuvalu cikkabeṁce mēre ·alliṁdaṁ [ba*]ḍagalu tōṭiholake ·aṁkavāle
niḍuvinahāḷa mēre ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu ciṁtalakuṁṭeya baḍa-

gaṇa kōḍi mēre ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu ceṁnamanāyakana haḷige [pratināmavāda narasiṁha-
samudrada ·agrahārada*] voṁṭiguṁḍa mēre | ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu • • • • •
mēre | ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu lokkakuṁṭehaḷḷige paḍuvalu kappalabeṁce mēre ·alliṁdaṁ
baḍagalu cikkapilliguṁḍlu mēre ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu cilukūra hola suddakuṁṭeya
karagu mēre ·alliṁ-

daṁ baḍagalu śā[sa*]nada nilu·ugallu mēre ·alliṁdaṁ maridoḍakeya tirumalanāyakana
hola sāḷabayala huṭṭu[gallu mēle hā]kida ·ā śāśanada kallu mēre ·alliṁdaṁ
mūḍalu baṁdarigiḍuvina hiṭṭugallubaṁḍe mēre ·alliṁdaṁ mūḍalu [virupāji]ya
bhāviya baṁḍeya śāsana mēre | ·alliṁdaṁ mūḍalu mai-

śināyakanahaḷḷige baḍagalu kariyabeṭṭada baṁḍeya mēre ·alliṁda mūḍalu cikkakariya
kallu mēre ·alliṁda • • • • mēre | ·alliṁda mūḍalu gaddeguṁḍu mēre
alliṁdaṁ mūḍalu tugilamarada mēlugaḍeya beṭṭada baṁḍe mēre ·alliṁdaṁ mūḍalu
vaḍagēnahaḷḷiya dāriya ·aḍḍatippeya mēlaṇa cikkahuṭṭugallu

mēre ·alliṁdaṁ vaḍagēnahaḷi naṁma mēre yeṟagoṁḍege paḍuvala cikkaguḍada mēlaṇa ·aṟe-
śāsana mēre ·alliṁdaṁ teṁkalu [biḷiya] beṭṭake baḍagalu ·araya mēlaṇa śāsana mēre
alliṁdaṁ teṁkalu biḷiya beṭṭada mēlaṇa hiriya guṁḍina śāsana mēre ·alliṁdaṁ teṁkalu
biḷiya beṭṭake mūḍalu ·areya mēlaṇa śāsane mē-

re ·alliṁdaṁ teṁkalu tēkulōḍina voḍḍina muguḍḍeya hola paṁṭugalla śāsana mēre |
alliṁdaṁ paḍuvalu ·agrahāra vonakuṁṭeya kariyaguḍḍada mēle śāsanada kallu mēre
alliṁdaṁ paḍuvalu ·araḷīmarada mēlugaḍeya hegguṁḍina mēlaṇa śāsana mēre |
ālliṁdaṁ paḍu-

valu rācahaḷḷige teṁkalu ·areya mēlaṇa śāsana mēre | śrīsāsanaprati samāptaṁ maṁgaḷa
mahāśrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 536.
(A.R. No. 785 of 1917.)
ON A ROCK IN THE GAVI-RANGASVAMI HILL AT CHALIVENDLA, SAME
TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is a copy of No. 579 of 1912 above. The only difference is the marginal
note added to the inscription at the right top-corner, which states that Virapaṇṇa
was the Taḷavāra of the Vijayanagara city.

l.ka:
śrīgaṇādhipatayē namaḥ | namastuṁgaśiraścuṁbicaṁdra cāmarācāravē (|) trailōkyanaga-
rārambhamūlastambhāya śaṁbhavē || harērlīlāvarahasya dauṣṭrādaṁḍassa pātu vaḥ |(|)
hēmādrikalaśā yatra

dhāśrī chatraśriyaṁ dadhau [||*] kalyāṇāyāstu taddhāma pratyūhatimirāpahaṁ | yadga-
jōpyagajōdbhūtaṁ hariṇāpi ca pūjyatē [||*] ·astikṣīrama[yāddē*]vairmathya-
mānā(ṁ)nmahāmbudhēḥ | navanītamivōdbhūtamapanītatamō mahaḥ ||

tasyāsīttanayastapōbhiratuḷairanvartanāmā budhaḥ puṇyairasya purūravā bhujabalairā-
yurdviśāṁ nighnataḥ | tasyāyurnahuṣōsya tasya paruṣō yuddhē yayātiḥ
kṣitau khyātastasya [tu*] turvasurvasunibha[ḥ*] śrīdēvayānīpatēḥ ||

tadvaṁśē dēvakījānirddidīpē tiṁmabhūpatiḥ | yaśasvī tuḷuvēṁdrēṣu yadōḥ kṛṣṇa
yivānvayē [||*] tatōbhūdbukkamājānirīśvaraḥ kṣitipālakaḥ | ·atrāsamaguṇabhraṁśaṁ
mavuḷiratnaṁ mahībhujāṁ [||*] sarasādudabhūttasmānnarasāvanipālakaḥ | dēvakī-

naṁdanātkāmō dēvakīnaṁdanādiva [||*] vividhasukrutōddhāmē rāmēśvarapramukhē
muhurmāditahrudayāstānē vyadhatta yathāvidhi | budhaprarivṛtō nānādānāni
yō bhuvi śōḍaśa tribhuvanajanōdgītaṁ sphītaṁ yaśaḥ punaruktayan || kāvērīmāśu
baddhvā bahaḷa ja-

larayāṁ tāṁ vilaṁghyaiva śatruṁ jīvagrāhaṁ gṛhītvā samiti(ḥ) bhujabalā(ṁ)ttaṁ ca
rājyaṁ tadīyaṁ [||*] kṛtvā śrīraṁgapūrvaṁ tadāpi nijavaśē paṭṭaṇaṁ yō babhāsē
kīrtistaṁbhaṁ nikhāya tribhuvanabhavanastūyamānāpadānaḥ [||*] cēraṁ cōḷaṁ ca
pāṇḍyaṁ tamapi ca madhurā-

vallabhaṁ mānabhūṣaṁ vīryyōdagraṁ turuṣkaṁ gajapatinrupatiṁ cāpi jitvā tathānyā[n |*]
āgaṁgātīralaṁkāprathamacaramabhūbhruttaṭāntaṁ nitāṁta[ṁ*] khyāta[ḥ*] kṣōṇīpatī-
nāṁ srajamiva śirasā[ṁ*] śāsanaṁ yō nyatānīt || tippājīnāgalādēvyō[ḥ*]
kaṁsalyāśrīsumitrayōḥ

dēvyōriva nrusiṁhēndratasmātpaṁktirathādiva [||*] vīrau [vi*]nayinau rāmalakṣmaṇā-
viva naṁdanau | jātau vīranṛsiṁhēṁdrakṛṣṇarāyamahīpatī [||*] vīraśrīnāra[siṁ*]-
hvassa vijayanagarē ratnasiṁhāsanastha[ḥ*] kīrtyā nītyā nirasyanruganaḷanahuṣā-
napyavanyāmayānyān | rāsētōrāsumērōra-

vanisuranutaḥ svairamā cōdayādrērā pāścātyācalāntādakhilahrudayamāvarjyarā-
jyaṁ śasāsa [||*] nānādānānyakārṣī[t*] kanakanakasadasi yaḥ śrī virūpākṣa-
dēvāsthānē śrīkāḷahastīsiturapi nagarē veṁkaṭādrō ca kāṁcyām | śrīśailē
śōṇaśailē mahati hariharē-

hōbalē saṁgamē ca (|) śrīraṁgē kuṁbhakōṇē hatatamasi mahānaṁditīrththē nivṛttau |
[|*] gōkar(ṁ)ṇē rāmasētau jagati taditarēṣvapyaśēṣēṣu pu(ṁ)ṇyasthānēṣvārabdha-
nānāvidhabahuḷamahādānavāripravāhaiḥ | yasyōdaṁcatturaṁgaprakarakhurarajaśśuṣya-

dambōdhimagnakṣmābhṛtpakṣacchidōstadukuliśadharōtkaṁṭhi[tā*] kuṁṭhitābhūt || brahmāṁ-
ḍaṁ viśvacakraṁ ghaṭamuditamahābhūtakaṁ ratnadhēnuṁ | svarṇakṣmāṁ yō hiraṇyāśvara-

thamapi tulāpūruṣaṁ gōsahasraṁ hēmāśvaṁ hēmagarbhaṁ kanakakarirathaṁ paṁcalāṁgalyatā-
nīt [||*] prājyaṁ praśāsya nirvighnaṁ rājyaṁ dyāmiva śāsituṁ | tasmi(ṁ)nguṇēna
vikhyātē kṣitēriṁdrē divaṁ gatē [||*] tatōpyavāryyavīryaśrīḥ kṛṣṇarāyamahī-
pati[ḥ*]

bibharti maṇikēyūrnirvisēṣaṁ mahī[ṁ*] bhujē [||*] kīrtyā yasya samaṁtataḥ prasru-
tayā viśvaṁ rucaikyaṁ vrajēdityāśaṁkya [purā*] purārirabhava[t*] bhāḷēkṣaṇa[ḥ*]
prāyaśaḥ | padmākṣōpi caturbhujōjani caturvaktrōbhavatpadmabhūḥ kāḷī khaḍga-
madhādramā ca kamalaṁ vī-

ṇāṁ ca vāṇī karē [||*] śatrūṇāṁ vāsamētē dadata ·iti ruṣā [kiṁ nu*] saptāṁburā-
sīnnānāsēnāturaṁgatruṭritavasumatīdhūḷikāpālikābhiḥ | saṁśōṣya svairamēta-
[t*] pratinidhijaladhiśrēṇikā[ṁ*] yō vidhattē brahmāṁḍasvarṇamērupramukhanijama-
hādānatōyairamēyaiḥ [||*] maddattā-

marththisārthāśśriyamiha śuciraṁ bhunjatāmityavētya prāyaḥ pratyūhahētōḥ tapanara-
thagatērālayān dēvatānāṁ | tattaddigjaitravṛttyāpi ca birudapadairaṁkitāṁstatra
tatra (|) sta[ṁ*]bhān(ṁ) jātapratiṣṭhānatanuta bhuvi yō bhūbhṛdabhr(ō)ṁkaṣā-
grā(ṁ)n [||*] kāṁcīśrīśailaśō-

ṇācalakanakasabhāvēṁkaṭādripramukhyēṣvāvartyāvarttya sarvēṣvatanuta vidhivadbhūyasē
śrēyasē yaḥ | dēvastānēṣu tīrthēṣvapi kanakatulāpūruṣādīni nānādānā-
nyaivōpadānairapi samamakhilairāgamōktāni tāni [||*] rōṣakrutapratipārthi-
vadaṁḍaḥ śēṣabhujakṣi tirakṣaṇaśauṁ-

ḍaḥ | bhāṣegetappuvarāyaragaṁḍastōṣakrudarthiṣu yō raṇacaṁḍaḥ [||*] rājādhirāja
ittyuktō yō rājaparamēśvaraḥ | mūrurāyaragaṁḍaśca pararājabhayaṁkaraḥ [||*]
hiṁdurāyasuratrāṇaḥ duṣṭaśārddū[la*]marddanaḥ | vīrapratāpa yityādi biru-
dairucitairyyutaḥ [||*] ·ālō-

kaya mahārāya jaya jīvēti vā[di*]bhiḥ | ·aṁgavaṁgakaḷiṁgādyai rājabhissēvyatē
ca yaḥ [||*] stutyaudāryya[ḥ*] sudhībhissa vijayanagarē ratnasi[ṁ*]hvāsanasthaḥ
kṣmāpālānkruṣṇarāyakṣitipatiradharīkrutya nītyā nrugādīn | ·āpūrvādrērathā-
