śriyamiha bahaḷīkrutya kīrtyā babhāsē [||*] krutavati suralōkaṁ kruṣṇarāyē
nijāṁśaṁ tadanu tadanujāṁhnā puṇyakarmācyutēṁdraḥ | prakaṭa ·avanilōkaṁ śvāṁsa-
mētyārijētā vilasati harijītā vidvadiṣṭapradātā [||*] krutavarakavicētōraṁjanē
kaṁjanētrē (|) krutavati suralōkaṁ

kruṣṇarāyē svavaṁśaṁ | tadanu tadanujōśaṁ prāpya bhūlōkamiṁdhē vitanumadanudaṁga-
śrībharairacyutēṁdraḥ [||*] yatkīrticaṁdraścarati kṣamāyāṁ tithiṣvaśēṣēṣu vivardha-
tē ca | tanōti cakrasya mudaṁ samiṁdhē divā ca sāyaṁ kumudairviruṁdhē [||*]
madaṁ manasi mārutaṁ śithilayaṁtya-

mēyairayairyadaśvapaṭalīkhurai[ḥ*] kṣitirajōbhiruktāpitaiḥ | ·ajījanaditi krudhā kiṁ
viśōṣayatyaṁbudhiṁ balapramathanasya nō rayavirōdhinaṁ vājinaṁ [||*] kārāgru-
hākalitavāsavirōdhibhūpa(ḥ)dhārāvaḷīkaravicālitacāmarasya [|*] rājādhirājapara-
rājabhayaṁkarai-

kavīrādhikāni birudāni bahūni yasyā [||*] gōkarṇṇasaṁgamani·urattisuvarnasaṁsa-
śōṇādriparvataviriṁcipurēṣu kāṁcyāṁ | śrīkāḷahastinagarēpi ca kuṁbhakōṇē
dānāni ṣōḍaśa bahūni krutāni yēna [||*] ·aṁbhōdēna nipīyamānasalilō-
gastyēna pītōddhitaḥ taptō

rāghavasāyakāgniśikhayā saṁtapyamānaḥ sadā [||*] ·aṁtasthairvaḍabāmukhānalaśikhājālai-
rviśuṣkō dhruvaṁ yaddānāṁbughanāṁburambudhirahō pūrṇā(ḥ)ssamuddyōtatē
[||*] ·aṁgēnāpi kaḷiṁgēna vaṁgēna ca varaiḥ nrupaiḥ | jaya jīva mahārājētyaniśaṁ
gīyatē ca yaḥ [||*] sa jayati

narapālō ratnasiṁhvāsanasthō vijayanagaravāsī kīrtipūrtyā vilāsī | nruganaḷanahu-
ṣādīṁ nīcayanrājanītyā nirupamabhujavīryōdāryabhūracyutākhyaḥ [||*]
śakābdēśśālivāhasya sahasrēṇa catuḥśataiḥ | paṁcāśatā ca saṁkhyātē trayābhyadhi-
kayā kramāt [||*] kharanāmani varṣē ca māsi

srāvaṇanāmani | puṇyāyāṁ gōkulāṣṭamyāṁ śubhangyau samayēpi ca [||*]
tuṁgabhadrāpagātīrē śrīvirūpākṣasannidhau | nijēna bhujavīryēṇa nirjitākhilarakṣasā ||
pūjitasya purā bhaktipūrvakaṁ raghusūnunā | prītyā pāra[ṁ*]ga(ṁ)taḥ pārśvavāsi-
nīṁ śvavilāsinīṁ | tāubhau sanidhasthau ca vīra-

bhadraraghūdvahau | svarnamukhyā tayā guptagāmi(ṁ)nyā ca masōharē [||*] kūrma
krutau mahāśailē sadā vāsamupēyuṣaḥ | trayāṇāmapi lōkānāṁ bhartuḥ pāpa-
vināśinaḥ [||*] lēpākṣyākhyamahāsthānavāsina[ḥ*] smaraśāsinaḥ | dhūpairdīpairanē
kaiśca vādyairhṛdvaissagītibhiḥ [||*] nṛtyairapi ca saṁ-

yuktāṁ cakṣurānaṁdadā[yi]bhaḥ | mahābhiṣēkanaivēdyavisēṣairupaśōbhitāṁ [||*]
kartuṁ triṣvapi kālēṣu mahāpūjāṁ dinē dinē | ghanagiriyāhvayē rājyē prājyē
vāsamupāsitaṁ [||*] ṟoddanāḍuvarāṁtasthaṁ prācyām śrītōṭahaḷḷitaḥ | grāmā-
dvaḷidaḍīkākhyaṁ dakṣi-

ṇāṁ diśamāśritaṁ [||*] paścimaṁtē kulōḍasya vonakuṁṭāgrahārataḥ | ·uttarasyāṁ diśi
prāptavāsaṁ khyātimupāśritaṁ [||*] gollahaḷḷyā ca peṁmukkahaḷyāpi ca samanvitaṁ |
śrīpētippanahaḷyā ca rācahaḷyā ca saṁyutaṁ [||*] hanumaṁtanahaḷyā ca lokki-
kuṁṭēna cānvitaṁ | saṁyutaṁ bommireḍākhyaha-

ḷyāpi ca manōgñayā [||*] caṁnaṁmanāyakābhikhyahaḷyāpi ca samanvitaṁ | gaṇapatyā-
khyahaḷḷīti grāmagrāsēna saṁyutaṁ [||*] ·acyutēṁdrapuraṁ cēti pratināmasamāśri-
taṁ | celiviṁḍlāhvayē grāmōpaṁtavarttimahīdharaṁ [||*] sarvamānyaṁ catussīmā-
saṁyutaṁ ca sa-

maṁtataḥ | nidhinikṣēpapāṣāṇasiddhasādhyajalānvitaṁ [||*] ·akṣīṇyāgāmisaṁyuktaṁ dēva-
bhōgyaṁ sabhūruhaṁ | vāpīkūpataṭākaiśca kacchēnāpi samanvitaṁ [||*] dānasyā-
dhamanasyāpi vikrayasyāpi cōcitaṁ | parītaḥ prayataissnigdhaiḥ purōhitapurōga-
maiḥ [||*] vividhairvibhudhaiśśrautapathikairadhi-

kairgirā | mātuśśrīmuddamākhyāyo balēna tapasāṁ [dhruvaṁ] [||*] naṁdiśrīlekkiśeṭya-
bdhipūrnacaṁdrēṇa bhūṣṇunā | vīrabhadraikacittēna virapaṁṇēna cōditaḥ [||*]
acutēṁdramahārāyō mānanīyō manaśvināṁ | sahiraṇyapayōdhārāpūrvvakaṁ
dattavānmudā [||*] grāmasyāssyaiva sīmānō dikṣu prā-

cyādiśu kramāt | tataścihnasamāyuktaṁ likhyaṁtē dēśabhāśayā [||*] calluviṁḍligrā-
mada holamēre dēśōbhāga paḍuvalādhi malugūra holakke na(ṁ)mma naḍu·u śāsanada
nilu·ugallu mēre ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu rācapahaḷiya ·araḷīmarakke paḍuvalu ci-

• • • cemēre ·alliṁda baḍagalu tōṭṭi holake ·aṁkavāle niḍuniḍuvinahāḷa mēre
alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu ciṁtalakuṁṭeya baḍagaṇa kōḍi mēre ·alliṁda baḍagalu ceṁnemanā-
yakana haḷige pratināmavāda narasiṁhvasamudrada ·agrahārada voṁ

• • • • • • ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu lokkakuṁṭehaḷḷige paḍuvalu kappalabeṁce mēre
alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu cikkapilliguṁḍlu mēre ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu cilukūra holakke sudda-
kuṁṭeya karugu mēre ·alliṁdaṁ baḍagalu śāsanada nilu·ugallu mēre ·alliṁdaṁ baḍaga

• • • • • • • • • • tirumalanāyakaru biṭa hola sāḷalabayala huṭṭugalla
mēle hākida śāsana mēre·alliṁdaṁ mūḍalu narasiṁhvasamudrada baṁdarigiḍuvinūḷagaṇa
hiṭugalla mēlaṇa śāsana mēre ·alliṁdaṁ mūḍalu muddalapurakereya kō

• • • • • • • • • • śāśana mēre ·alliṁdaṁ mūḍalu virupājīya bāviya mēlu
gaḍeya ce·uḍi mārikuṁṭege pratināmavāda hiriya tirumalasāgarada mādhavayana ·agra-
hārada hola hiriya bōḷabaṁḍeya mēlaṇa śāsana | ·alliṁda mūḍalu haḷadoḷagaṇa
areya mēlaṇa śāsana mēre

mēre ·alliṁdaṁ mūḍalu gadde guṁḍu mēre ·alliṁdaṁ mūḍalu tuggilamarada mēlu
gaḍeya beṭṭada ·areya śāsana mēre ·alliṁda mūḍalu vaḍagēnahaḷiya dāriya ·aḍḍa-
tippeya mēlaṇa

• • • • • • • • • • teṁkalu vaḍagēnahaḷi namma mēre yeragoṁḍege paḍuvalu cikka
guḍada mēlaṇa ·areya śāsana mēre ·alliṁdaṁ teṁkalu biḷiya beṭṭake baḍagalu ·areya
mēlaṇa śāsana mēre ·alliṁdaṁ teṁkalu biḷiyabeṭṭada mēlaṇa hiriya guṁḍina śāsana
mēre ·alliṁ

edition.h4: On the right side of the rock at the top.

inscription.head:
No. 537.
(A.R. No. 578 of 1912.)
ON THE ROCKY FLOOR AT THE FOOT OF THE EAST WALL OF THE SECOND PRAKARA
OF THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT LEPAKSHI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Khara, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 12 in the reign of the Vijaya-
nagara king Achyutarāya-Mahārāya, corresponding to A.D. 1531 August 9, Tuesday
(not verifiable). It records the royal order to the Gauḍas of Cheluviṇḍla stating that
the king made a gift of the village Cheluviṇḍla surnamed Komāra-Veṅkaṭādriyapura
to the god Pāpavināśana and Gaṇapatihaḷḷi to Virapaṇṇa of Penugoṇḍa and that the
ownership of both the villages was given to the latter.

l.ka:
kharasaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa ba 12 lū śrīmāmahārā[jā*]dhirājan™ rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapra-
tāpa śrīvīra·acyutarāyamahārāyaru hāḍulakhayyana hōbaḷiya celuviṁḍla
grāmada gauḍaprajegaḷige nirūpa niṁma grāmavanū lēpākṣi pāpavināśanadēvarige ko-
māraveṁkaṭādriyapuraveṁdu penugoṁḍada śrī

virapaṁṇage gaṇapatihaḷiyanū celuviṁḍlagrāmake pratināmadhēyavāda veṁkaṭādriyapurava
pāpavināśalēpākṣada vīrabhadradēvarigū • • • dāna koṭe·u vijenaga[ra]da paṭṭa-
ṇada taḷavāṟa virūpaṁṇage śāsanastavāgi pāliside·u virupaṁṇana ·āṇe ·āgnegoḷagāgi
naḍaudāda kāraṇa ·ā stitiyanu nirūpadi cittayiside·u śrī

inscription.head:
No. 538.
(A.R. No. 786 of 1917.)
ON A ROCK ON THE GAVI-RANGASVAMI HILL AT CHALIVENDLA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: This is a copy of No. 537 above, but it does not mention the village Gaṇapatihaḷḷi.

l.ka:
kharasaṁvacharada śrāva[ṇa] ba 12 lu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa
śrīvīra·accutarāyamahārāyaru hāḍuvalakhayana hōba[ḷi*]ya celuviṁḍla
grāmada gauḍuprajegaḷige nirūpa niṁ-

ma grāmavannu lēpākṣada pāpavināśanadēvarige komāraveṁkaṭādriyapuraveṁdu vidyā-
nagarada paṭṭaṇada taḷavāra penugoṁḍeya virupaṁṇage śāsanastavāgi pālisi cittayiside-
u | ·ā virapaṁṇana ·ā-

ṇāgnege voḷagāgi naḍaudādakāraṇa ·a stitiyanu nirūpa citayiside·u śrīvirūpākṣa maṁ-
gaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 539.
(A.R. No. 679 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE TEMPLE OF SANNAKKI-VIRABHADRA AT
HOSPET, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1453, Khara, Āśvīja, śu. 1, corresponding to A.D. 1531
September 11 Monday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya. It
records that Kampadēvarasa-Aṇṇa and the Nānādēśa merchants agreed to pay at the
rate of one kāsu per load of paddy and paṇas 2 on every marriage for the service of
the god Gaurēśvara at Tirumaladēvi-ammana-paṭṭaṇa.

l.ka: ḍiga payiru hēru 1 kaṁ kāsu 1 nella
ra koṁda samā taṁma dharmake tāvē tapida

inscription.head:
No. 540.
(A.R. No. 424-A of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE BUILDING SITE NORTH OF THE DODDAKEREKATTE
AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Khara, Mārgaśira, śu. 1, Guruvāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1531 November 9, Thursday. It records that Sadānandaseṭṭi and Dēvuseṭṭi
made gifts of land and money for the service of the god Mahādēva, consecrated
by the former and for feeding Brāhmans and Sumaṅgalīs in the temple, and for the
worship of the goddess Durgādēvī during the Navarātri festival. This date falls in
the reign of Achyutarāya.

l.ka:
svasti [||*] kharasaṁvatsarada mārgaśira śu 1 gu lū mahāliṁgadēvara savopadalū durgādē-
viya-

ranū lajjeya sadānaṁdaseṭi pratiṣṭheya māḍidake vineye • ke naivēdyake mu 22 ||
śāṁti mu 18 ·eṁṇe mu 10

hūvige mu 1 | gaṁdha mu 1 hāli mu 1 ·aṁna sivīḷyake mu 1 || raṁgapūje ·abhyaṁgada ·eṇe
ma 2 || ·ī ·akkige biṭa

inscription.head:
No. 541.
(A.R. No. 360 of 1920.)
ON A SLAB LYING NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT MEDIMAKULAPALLI,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This mutilated record is dated Śaka 1452, Khara, Mārgaśira, ba. 5, corresponding
to A.D. 1531 November 28, Tuesday (not verifiable). It refers itself to the reign of
Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 542.
(A.R. No. 690 of 1919.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE BHOGESVARA TEMPLE AT CHIPPAGIRI, ALUR
TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the cyclic year Khara, Pushya, ba. 6, Śukravāra, Sun
in Makara corresponding to A.D. 1531 December 29, Friday. It states that Koṇḍa-
bhūpa made a grant of land to the temple of Bhōgēśvara. It also registers another
gift to the same god by Chikka-Timmappayya with the permission of the king,
Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.

l.ka:
satyagirirājyē sōmasukhapradhvaghē khara[vatsarē] puṣyēmāsyasitē ravau makaragē ṣaṣṭī-
dinē

bhārgevē bhaṁḍārāppayajēna koṁḍavibhunā • • bhōgiliṁga • • • bhaṁḍāra ·a-
ppayasarōvaraḥ

śrīkoṁḍabhūpō makarē gatē ravau śrīcippagiryā • • • śrī ·appaya • • •
• • bhōgīśvarāyā • • •

śaṁbhavē [||*] bhōgēśvara • • • • paścimadaluṁ | śrīda • • • • • vāgi
naḍedu baṁda kṣētra • • •

biṁnāha māḍi svāmi[ya] nirūpadiṁdalū • • • rāyarige pu(ṁ)ṇyavāgi yo
tathātithipu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalu dē-

varige sarvamā(ṁ)nyavāgi dhāreyaneradu koṭa kṣētra ·ādavāniya nagarada bījavari yereya
hola pūrva-

yiguḷa kṣētravanu sahira(ṁ)ṇyōdaka • • • • • • • • • • koṭṭaru yī
kṣē-

ṭāda nidhi nikṣēpa jala pāśāṇa ·akṣīṇi • • • • • [aṣṭa]bhōga tējasvā(ṁ)myaṁ-
gaḷu ·ācaṁ-
va bhūbhujāṁ na bhōjyā na karagrāhyā vipradattā • • • • • • • • • • braṁhasvaṁ

viṣamucyatē | viṣamēkākinaṁ haṁti braṁhasvaṁ • • • • • • dānapālanayō-
rmadhyē dānā-

inscription.head:
No. 543.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1904.)
ON THE WEST BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE VITTHALASVAMI
TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1453, Khara, Māgha, ba. 10 corresponding to
A.D. 1532, January 31, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahā-
rāya. It records the gift of the villages (1) Hepe, (2) Cheḷuvanahāḷu and (3) Ānegana-
hāḷu for offerings to the god Viṭṭhaladēva made by Tāḷapāka Tirumalayya of Bhārad-
vāja-gōtra, Āśvalāyana-sūtra and Ṛik-śākhā.

l.ka:
svasti [||*] śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣa 1453 ṟaneya kharasaṁvatsarada māgha
ba 10 lū śrīmatu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara

śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīra·acyutarāyamahārāyarū pṛthvīrājyaṁ gaiuttaṁ ·iralu śrīma-
dvēdamārgapratiṣṭhācāryarāda ·āśvalāyanasūtrada bhāradvājagōtrada rukuśā-
khādhyāyigaḷā-

da tāḷapāka tirumalayyaṁgaḷu śrīviṭhaladēvarige samarpisida grāma | namage ·acyutarāya-
mahārāyarū dhārāpūrvakavāgi tāṁbraśāsana • • • • • ṇa sīme voḷage
vikhyāta • • •

ceḷuvanahāḷa grāma 1 kāluvaḷi ·āneganahāḷa grāma 1 hepeya grāma 1 ·i mūru grā-
make saluva catussīmevoḷagāda ·aṣṭabhōgatējassvāmya sakalanidhinikṣēpādisahita-
vāgi ·ī mūru grāmavanū
hepeya grāma 1 caḷuvanahāḷa grāma 1 ·āneganahāḷa grāma 1 ·ā mūṟu grā-

makaṁ ro rēkhe va ga 1350 kaṁ saluvadu nityanaivēdya dīpārādhane • • harivāṇa

·ā va ga 1350 ·ī mari[yāde*] yallinaivēdyavanū māḍi putrapautrapāraṁpari

inscription.head:
No. 544.
(A.R. No. 387 of 1920.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE ANJANEYASVAMI TEMPLE AT GADEKALLU, GOOTY
TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1453, Khara, Māgha, ba. 14, Śivarātre corresponding to A.D.
1532 February 3, Monday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya. It
records that Lakumiseṭṭi, son of Muttina Lakumiseṭṭi, the officer in charge of the
vajra-bhaṇḍāra at Karūru in the Uruvakoṇḍe-sīme which was granted to Yallapaṇṇa
by the king made a gift of land in the village Gaḍēkallu to Koṇḍā-Jyōsya, son
of Purushōttama-Bhaṭṭa of Polaki, and also some other land in the same village to
Bhūsi-Bhaṭṭa, son of Kantrike Siri-Bhaṭṭa.

l.ka: mahārāyaru yallapaṁṇagaḷige pāllisida vuruvakoṁḍe sīme vo-
ḷagaṇa karūra vajrabhaṁḍārake karttarāda muttina llakimiseṭṭiyara makaḷu lakamise-
ṭigaḷu tavu ·āḷuva gaḍekala gramadalu polaki koṁḍājyōśyarige pūrvadali yikuḷada
hola sarvamānyavāgi naḍavutihudu ·ā lakmiseṭigaḷu yalapaṁṇagaḷige pu(ṁ)ṇyavāgi

·ā sivārātri puṇyakāladallu polakiya puruṣōttamabaṭra makaḷu koṁḍājyōśyarige
yikuḷada hola-

nū dhāraneredu grāmadalli kuṭuṁbavāsavāgi yirabēku yeṁdu vubhayaṁ nāgguḷada bīja-
vari-
ya holanū grāmake nayirutyabhāgadalu gaviraḍi māḍida kṣētravanu ·ā kṣētradallu bā-

haṁthā ·aṣṭabhōga tēja svā(ṁ)mya muṁtāgi sarvamā(ṁ)nyavu koṭu ·ācaṁdrārkastā-
yiga-
du koṭa muttina lakimiseṭi[ya*]ru māḍida bhū-
mātrupitrugaḷanū gōbrā(ṁ)hmaranū vāraṇāsi voḷage koṁda dōṣadali hōda

·avāru [||*] dānapālanayōrmmadhyē dānāścrēyōnrupālanaṁ [|*] dānasvargama-
vāpnōti pālanādacyuthaṁ padaṁ [||*]
kharasaṁvatsarada māga bahuḷa 14 sivarātrepu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalu mutina lakimiseṭa-

ra makkaḷu lakimiseṭigaḷū kaṁtrikeyā siribhaṭāra makaḷū bhūsibhaṭarige yikuḷada kṣētrava-
nu dhāreneradū tā-

·u ·āḷuvaṁta gaḍiyakala grāmadalu baḍagalu kōṭerāmainū māḍuva holadoḷage yiku-
ḷada bījavari

yo kṣētravanū ·aṣṭabhōga tēja svāmya muṁtāgi sarvamānyau sahira(ṁ)ṇyōdaka
sahitavāgi

dhāreneredu koṭevu nīu ni(ṁ)mma putrapavutrapāraṁpariyavu ·āceṁdrārkkastāyigaḷāgi
grāmadalu kuṭu[ṁ]-

basthāyiyāgi sukhadalu yihūdu yaṁdu koṭaṁthā mutina lakumiseṭaru māḍida bhū-
dānada silāli-

ṣebaṭṭu tekoṁḍare taṁma mātrupitrugaḷanu kāsikṣētradoḷage vadhisidaṁthā pātakadalu hō-
haru [||*]

svadattā[ṁ*] paradattā[ṁ*] vā yō haraṁti vasuṁdharā[ṁ ||*] ṣaṣṭirvvaruṣasahasrāṇi
vriṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē

inscription.head:
No. 545.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1910.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE CENTRE OF THE VILLAGE ERAGANAHALLI,
GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is a little damaged and dated Śaka 1454, Nandana, Jyēshṭha, śu. [5, Guruvāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1532 May 9, Thursday, in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that, under the orders of Kāṇikeya Kampadēvarasaṇṇa and in the
presence of Beṇṇeya, the Sthānikas of eight villages and Pārapatyagāra Koṇḍappayya,
Sēnabōva Gummaṭayya and the Gauḍas of the Baḍakoṅga-nāḍu, the apportionment of
some plots of land in Eṟaganahaḷḷi was made among Bhōganañjayya, son of Nañjeya-
dēva, Annadāni-Voḍeya, son of Parvata-Voḍeya of the feeding-house of the
Jaṅgamas, and Dēvaṇṇa-Ayya, son of Liṅgappayya of the feeding-house of the
Brāhmans.

l.ka: ḷa sthānikara muṁdiṭṭu gājanūra sthaḷakke pārupatyava māḍu-
ḷada gavuḍagaḷa muṁdiṭṭu kapilā kauṁḍinī saṁgamakṣētradali pra(ṁ)saṁ-
vara makkaḷu dēvaṁṇa·ayyanavarige koṭṭa grāmada hasigeya vivara ye-
ṟaganahaḷḷiya grāmada bīrappagauḍana ma[ne]ge mūḍaṇa ha[la]geya grāma
baḍagaṇa tiṭṭina bhūmi paḍavalu basavanakaṭṭeya vōṇiya vobbe mēre-
yāgi beḷageṟeya paḍuvaṇa kōḍi mēreyāgi salluvudu teṁkalu daṁḍina dā-
riya mūḍaṇa vōṇi mēreyāgi gōvana hoṁḍa doṇe bāvi voḷagāgi haḷḷa mēre-

yāgi mūḍaṇa toṭṭada • • • bbe mēre nārigaṭṭada sīmege nēṟalumara hola voḷa
gā-

gi māvina tāḷakaitōṭa voḷagāgi salluvudu nārigaṭṭada mūḍaṇa • • • • bbe
mēre sappa-
nadaṟeya vobbe mēre kāḷuva kkuṁmariya vobbe mēre saṁbudēvana takkilavo-
bbe mērayāgi mattitāḷada • • • vobbe mēre beḷageṟeya mūḍaṇa kō-
ḍi mēreyāgi baḍagaṇa kaṭṭige salluvudu • • • kōḍipurada grāmakke salluva
sīme teṁkalu beḷageṟeya dāri mēreyāgi paḍuvaṇa kōḍiya paṭṭada ·arasiya vōṇi
mēreyāgi toṟeya haḷḷa mēreyāgi salluvudu mūḍalu tūbina tāḷavōṇi-
ya teṁkaṇa vobbemēreyāgi • • • purakke giḍu salluvudu ||
cillahaḷḷiya grāmakke salluva pura kuṁmaripura lakharasanapura sīgallachatra
yo mūṟu puragrāmagaḷu cillahaḷḷige salluvudu cillahaḷḷiya grāmakke salluva
cattusīme kuṁmariyapurakke salluva catussīme lakharasanapurakke salluva catussīme

inscription.head:
No. 546.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1910.)
ON A SECOND SLAB SET UP NEAR THE POND WEST OF THE SAME VILLAGE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and is of the same date and reign as A.R. No. 175 of 1910,
above. It specifies the plots of land which were given to Annadāni-Voḍeya in
charge of the Jaṅgama-satra.

l.ka: ·u hadināru sthānikara muṁdiṭṭu gājinūra sthaḷava pā-
ya grāmadalli 2 bhāgavāgi • • • yanu jaṁgama chatrada naṁ-
ja dēvara [ma*] kkaḷu bhōgana • • • • • • • • • • voḍeyara makkaḷu
bhāgi 10 nū ·aṟeya grāmadalū baḍagaṇakēri paḍuvalu karadē • • • • •
la hākida yelle mēre • • sahavāgi baḍagalu saluvudu keṟeya
karagaḷa tōṭa saribhāge saluvudu baḍagarataṭana yelle mēre muḍalu maṁci-

na teṁkalu maṟehō • • • • • • • • • • varu mēreyāgi salu·udu brammaṇabhā-
geya
yeṁdu baṁdu svarucikeyiṁdoḍaṁbaṭṭu koṭṭa kalluśāsana voṁdu • • • • •

rige • • koḷḷaṁthā sakṣigaḷu • • honnaṇagauḍaru parvvatigauḍaru • • •
gaya

inscription.head:
No. 547.
(A.R. No. 541 of 1919.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SECOND PRAKARA OF THE ARULALA-PERUMAL TEMPLE
AT LITTLE CONJEEVARAM, CONJEEVARAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1454, Nandana, Jyēshṭha, ba. 12 Guruvāra, corresponding
to A.D. 1532 May 30 Thursday, f.d.t. ·66, in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya.
After enumerating the king's conquests, it states that the king, queen Varadādēvi-
Amma and prince Veṅkaṭādri performed the ceremony of tulā-pūrusha and granted
jewels and seventeen villages (named) to the god Varadarājadēva.

l.ka:
śubhamastu svasti [||*] śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa mūrurā-
yara gaṁḍa ·arirāyavibhāḍa ·aṣṭadikurāyamanōbhayaṁkara pūrvadakṣiṇapaścimasamu-

drādhīśvara ·acyutarāyamahārāyarū pṛthvīrājyaṁ geyi·uttaṁ yiralū śālivāhana-
śakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1454 saṁdu mēle naḍeva naṁdanasaṁvatsarada jyēṣṭha ba [1]2 gulū
acyutarāyamahārāyaru virōdhisaṁvatsarada kārtika ba 5 lū paṭṭābhiṣēkastarāgi
annayahastarāyasakkāgi śaraṇāgatarāgi baṁda nugihaḷi rāyaṇarājagaḷu ·u(ṁ)mma-
tūra mallarājagaḷu veṁkaṭādri muṁtāda pāḷayadha nāyakamakkaḷanū pālisi bhoga-
tarāgidda maṁneyaragaḷanu rakṣisi tiruva-

ḍi rājyakke maṁneyaranū daḷavāḍavanū kaḷiha citteyisi tiruvaḍi kayya kappavanū tekadu-
koṁḍu pāṁḍyarājana komāratiyanū tegadukoṁḍu tāṁbrapar(ṁ)niyali jayastaṁbhava
hākisi tuṁbica sāḷuvanāyakanū ·ājñeyanū yirisi naṁdanasaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa śu
11 stiravāradalu tulāpuruṣa mahādānakke hadivāsavanū māḍa cittēsi 12 ·ā dalū

gaḷanū māḍi cittaisi varadarājadēvarige samarppista kāṇike vivara virōdhisaṁvatsarada
kārtika ba 5 ·ārabhya naṁdanasaṁvatsarada śrāva-

inscription.head:
No. 548.
(A.R. No. 549 of 1919.)
ON THE SAME WALL
inscription.p: This is a copy in Nāgarī of No. 541 of 1919 above.

l.de:
śubhamastu | svasti [||*] śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparameśvara śrīvīrapratāpa mūrurāyaragaṁḍa ·arirāyara
vibhāḍa ·aṣṭadi[ku]rāyamanobhayaṁkara catu[sa]mudrādhīśvara ·accutarāyamahārāyaru pruthvī-
rājyaṁgeyi·utaṁ yiralu śālivāhanaśakavaruṣaṁgahu 1454 saṁdu mele naḍava naṁdana-
saṁvaccarada jeṣṭha ba 1[2] guru ·acutarāyamahārāyaru virodhisaṁvaccarada kārtika ba 5 llu
paṭābhiṣekastharāgi ·annya hasta rāyasagaḷige śaraṇāga[ta]rāgi baṁda nugihaḷḷi rāyaṇa-
rājagaḷuṁ ·uṁmatūru mallaṇarājagaḷuṁ veṁkaṭādri muṁtāda pāḷayada nāyakamakkaḷanu pālisi bhūgata-
rāgi yi[da] maṁneyarugaḷanu rakṣi[si] tiruvaḍirājyake maṁnyeranu daḷavāḷavanu kaḷuha
citaisi tiruvaḍi kaiya kappava-

nu tekadukoṁḍu pāṁ[ḍya]rājana komāra[ti]yanu tegadukoṁḍu tāṁbraparṇiyalli jayastaṁbhavanu hākiśi
tuṁbicci sāluvanāyakana ·ājñenu yirisi naṁdanasaṁvaccarada śrāvaṇa śudha 11 sthira-
vāradalu tulāpuruṣa mahādāna hadivāsavanu māḍisi 12 [ā]divāradalu muttina tulāpuruṣa-
vanu varadādevaṁmanavaru komāraveṁkaṭādri cikavoḍeyaru tulāpuruṣa mahābhūta ghaṭadāna [sa]hasra-
godāna mahādāna muṁtāda ma[hā]dānaṁgaḷanu māḍi citaiyi[si] śrīvaradarājadevarige samarpi-
[stha] kāṇikeya vivara virodhisaṁvaccarada kārtika ba 5 ·ā[rabhya] naṁdanasaṁvacarada śrāvaṇa-
śuddha 12 varege ca[kra] 415 cerupu puḷugugāpige sama[rpi]stha ca[kra] 150 ·ubhayaṁ cakra
565 pītāṁbra 1 kke taṭuseragu ḷatehūgaḷu saha tūka 119 ke vivara [su]tta[ṇa]

inscription.head:
No. 549.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1924.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE THIRD PRAKARA OF THE KALAHASTISVARA TEMPLE AT
KALAHASTI, CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1455, Nandana, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 12, Ravivāsara corresponding to
A.D. 1532 July 14 Sunday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya. It records that
the king, his queen, Varadambikā, and his son, Chika-Veṁkaṭādri, severally per-
formed the Muktā-tulābhāra ceremony in the presence of the god Hari at Kāñchī.
The record is written in Sanskrit verse.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] śākē bhūtaśarāṁbudhīṁdugaṇitē varṣē punarnaṁdanē māsē śrāvaṇanāma-
kē ca vimalē pakṣē ra[vē]rvāsarē | dvādaśyāṁ harisannidhau vyaracayanmaktātulā-
pūruṣaṁ(|) dānaṁ śrīnarasācyutakṣitipatiḥ kāṁcīpurābhyaṁtarē ||

muktātulāpūruṣadānavidhau vikī(ṁ)rnnān | muktāmaṇīn samadhikaṁ varadāṁbikā-
yāḥ | dānāṁbupūraparitān rayatassamētya ratnākaratvamabhajallavaṇākarōpi ||
kāṁcyāṁ śrīcikaveṁkaṭādrimaṇinā dānē tulābhārakē | saṁpratyā-

racitē dvijāssamabhava(ṁ)nnarthātibhārākulāḥ | dhātrīyaṁ kalikalmaṣādadhigatā • • •
• • • kṣōṇīpālakumārakā(ḥ)staditarē bhārāya jātā bhuvaḥ ||

inscription.head:
No. 550.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1924.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE THIRD PRAKARA OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is a little damaged and dated Śaka 1454, Nandana, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 10, Manda-
vāra, Rōhiṇī-nakshatra, corresponding to A.D. 1532 July 27 Saturday in the reign
of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya. It records the gift made by the king on the day of his
coronation in the year Virōdhi, Kārtika, ba. 5, corresponding to A.D. 1529 October
21, Thursday, in the presence of the god Kālahastīśvara at Kālahasti, of seven
and three-fourths villages (named) and the export and import duties from certain
specified ports, for worship and offerings to the god on festival days. It mentions
the levy of tribute from Tiruvaḍi (Travancore), the subjugation of Tumbichchi and
Sāḷva-Nāyaka, the taking (in marriage) of the Pāṇḍya princess, and the planting of a
pillar of victory on the bank of the Tāmbraparṇī river by the king. The king's
birth-star is stated to be Mṛigaśīrsha. The inscription is written in Nāgarī characters
and Kannaḍa language.

l.de:
śubhamastu | svasti śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparameśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīra·accutarāyamahā-
rāyaru prithvīrājiyaṁ māḍuttalu yiralāgi śakābda 1454 saṁdu mele naḍava naṁdanasaṁvatsarada
śrāvaṇa bahula 1[0] maṁdavāradalu rohiṇi nakṣatradalu ·accutarāyamahārāyaru virodhisaṁvatsarada-
kārtika bahula 5 lu kālahāstige vijayamāḍisi kālahastīśvarana seveyanu māḍi
śrīkālahastīśvarana sannidhi[ya*]lu paṭṭāabhiṣekavanu māḍikoṁḍu melunāḍa dhoregaḷu nugge-
haḷḷi rāyaṇarājagaḷu ·ummattūru mallarājagaḷu veṁkaṭādrināyaka muṁtāda pāḷayada nāyakamakkaḷanu
śaraṇāgatarādavaranu pālisi bhūgatarāda manneyaranu rakṣisi tiruvaḍirājyakke manneyarunu [da]-
ḷavāḍavanu kaḷuhisalāgi

nāyakaranu ·ājñeyanu māḍisi tāṁbraparṇiyalu jayastaṁbhavanu hākisi [śrīkā]laharatīśvara-

inscription.head:
No. 551.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1924.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
The date and the contents are the same as those of No. 182 of the same year
published above. The characters are Kannaḍa.

l.ka:

tamma ·ubhayada naivēdyada viṭṭavana viḷakkāḍu prasāda harivāṇadali yeraḍu harivā-

inscription.head:
No. 552.
(A.R. No. 546 of 1919.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SECOND PRAKARA OF THE ARULALA PERUMAL TEMPLE
IN LITTLE CONJEEVERAM, CONJEEVERAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1454, Nandana, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 12, Ā[divāra] corresponding to
A.D. 1532 July 28, Sunday, f.d.t. ·42, in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that, when the king went to Visḥṇu-Kañchi with his queen Varadādēvī and
son Chika-Veṅkaṭādri and made the tulāpūrusha and gōsahasra gifts, he granted
17 villages with an income of gadyāṇas 1500 for offerings to the god Varadarāja.

l.ka:
śubhamastū svasti [||*] śrīvijeyābduda[ya*]śālivāhanaśakhavaruśa 1454 [sa]ṁdu [mē]le
na[ḍa]va naṁdanasaṁvatsarada [śrā]vaṇa ba 12 ·ā lū śrīmāṁmahārājādhirāja rājapara-
mēśvara ·arirā[ya]vibhāḍa ·aṣṭadikurāyamanōbhayaṁkara pūrvadakṣiṇapaścima
samudrādhīśvara bhāśege tapuva rāyara gaṁḍa śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīra·acutarāyamahā-
rāyarū viṣṇukaṁcige cittaisi tāu [va]ra[dā*]dēviyarū komāra cikaveṁkaṭādri-

voḍeyarū muttina tulāpurusavanu tūgi [bhū]dāna sahasragōdānanū māḍi nāu
varadarājadēvarige sama[r]ppista ciyanāvarada sīme grāma 3 kku pa 14 saha grāma
17 ke rēkhe ga 1500 ke nayivēdya harivāṇa 54 dadhyanna harivāṇa 2 tiruvatisā hari-
vāṇa 2 ke ·aki 11 ·atirasa paḍi 2 yiṣṭake biṭavanaviḷukāḍu nālkaraloṁdu neyivēdya
harivāṇa 13 || dadhyaṇa 1 tiruvatisāṁ harivāṇa || ·atirasa 26 yiṣṭa-

nnu rāyasarāmacaṁdradīkṣitarige pāliste·u yo dharmmau ·ācaṁdrārkastāyi ·āgi putra-
pautrapārampareyāgi naḍeyaluḷadu ·ī tithiyalu haṁdikerāyaru saha biṭe·u kaṁnaḍa
• • jāri satiyarāyarū biṭṭa harivāṇa 24 ke biṭavanaviḷukkāḍu harivāṇa 62 ge
nara[siṁ]garāyaru biṭa harivāṇa 5 ke 1 || [kriṣṇa]-
l.note: Space for about 25 letters is left blank.

l.ka:
rāyarū ·a[cuta]rāya[rū] • • • • harivāṇa 14 || yo mūru taḷigege • •
• gaḷu koṭa • • • • 12 ke ·atirasa • • • • • • • • • • yēnuṁṭā-
danū rāyastarāmacaṁ(caṁ)dradīkṣitarige pālistevu yo dharmau ·ācaṁdrārkastā-
yāgi putrapavutrapāraṁpareyāgi naḍeyaluḷadu yi dharmake ·ārobaru hānimāḍidarū
vāraṇāsili kapile

inscription.head:
No. 553.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1904.)
ON THE PEDASTAL OF THE NANDI NEAR THE SHRINE OF VIRABHADRA
AT KUDATANI, BELLARY TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1454, Nandana, Adhika-Bhādrapada, ba. 30, Śu[kravāra], corre-
sponding to A.D. 1532 August 30, Friday. It refers itself to the reign of Achyutadēva-
Mahārāya and records that Koṇḍapa, brother-in-law of Bhaṇḍārada Timmapa-Ayya,
made a gift of land, together with some toll revenue, for offerings to the god
Vīrabhadra at Kuḍutaṇi. The gift is stated to have been made for the merit of
Timmapa-Ayya.

l.ka: kabhādrapada ba 30 śu lu śrīma(ṁ)n mahārā-
dēvara nayivēdyakke jīrnōdhārake koṭa kariya ·ereya ho-
rggada mūle vīsa ·etige kāsu saṁteya pasige paḷige-
baru tapidavaru taṁma taṁdetāya vāraṇāsili koṁda pā-

inscription.head:
No. 554.
(A.R. No. 492 of 1915.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT KAVUTALAM, ADONI TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1454, Nandana, Phālguṇa, ba. 7, Sō[māvara],
corresponding to A.D. 1533 March 17, Monday, in the reign of Achyutarāya-
Mahārāya. It records the migration of the people of Kavatāḷa-sīme to Māsaveya-
sīme to escape from the tyranny of the palace officials and their return, at the
invitation of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Salakayadēva Chika-Tirumalarāja Mahāarasa,
to Kavatāḷa on promise of relief. The terms of the order granting relief are set
forth in detail.

l.ka: charada phālguṇa ba 7 sō lu śrīmatu
meya samasta gauḍu prajegaḷige koṭa silā-
ge biṭu hōgi yiralāgi nāu ·ādavānige baṁdu
niṁma kavatāḷada sīmeya samasta gauḍa pra-
ṭṭe·u sīmeya stāna prajegaḷū tapade naḍi
nya kuḷapāla bhata kha 1 kaṁ birāḍa kuḷapāla bha
·arasa dhā(ṁ)nya kha 1 kaṁ biḍāra ga 10 || yo ma-
seya masuvaṇa yo sāsanada pramāṇadalū tapa-
de naḍasi bāhe·u yo pramāṇadalū naḍasalaṟiya-
ke hōharu || yi[da*]naṟitu pāpake guri ·āgade tapa[de]
teya tuṁṇe kalupu surāpāna mādegarasara • niḍuvu-
du dharmavē jaya maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī
kaṇiyakala mallaya bareda peruvōjana maga rā[mōja] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 555.
(A.R. No. 422 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE TEMPLE OF VENKATARAMANA
AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1457, Vijaya, Āshāḍha, śu. 11, corresponding to A.D. 1533
July 2, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya. The king
appointed Suṅkaṇṇa-Nāyaka as governor of Bārakūra-rājya and the latter in his turn
appointed Koṇḍa-Voḍeya to rule over the province. The inscription records that
Suṅkaṇṇa Nāyaka made a gift of the village, Haṭiya-Kuduru, for the service of the
god Tirumaladēva of Paḍuvakēri of Basrūr. The gift was made for the prosperity of
the king.

l.ka:
śrīgaṇādhipata·eṁ namaḥ | namāmi suguṇāvāsaśritalēkhāya śārṁgiṇē janasaṁnutakaṁṭhāya

re bhaṁḍārastaḷada haṭikudura grāma 1 ke salūva kāṭi 59 kkaṁ nija varaha 23 • •
akṣāradalū yippatta-

mūṟu varahanu ·āṟu haṇavanu teṁginatōṭa kudivara • • • teṟu sahavāgi śrītirumala-
dēvara ·amru-

tapaḍi naṁdādīptiyanu naḍasi sukhadali ·anubhavisi bahiri ·eṁdu koṁḍappavoḍeyaru tiru-
maladēvarige

koṭṭa dānapaṭṭe | dānapālanayōrmadhyē dānāśrēyōnrupālanaṁ dānāt svargama-
vāpnōti pālanādacutaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 556.
(A.R. No. 745 of 1916.)
ON A ROCK NEAR THE RANGANATHASVAMI TEMPLE AT MELAVAY,
MADAKASIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1456, Vijaya, Āśvayuja, ba. 12 corresponding to
A.D. 1533 October 15, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutadēva-
Mahārāya. It records that the king made a gift of three villages, Anantapura
• • male and Meḷavāyi for the service of the god Tiruvēṅgaḷanātha.

l.note: Four more lines are very much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 557.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1904.)
ON THE NORTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE VITTHALESVARA
TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1455, Vijaya, Mārgaśira, śu. 11, Guruvāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1533 December 11, Thursday, in the reign of Achyutadēvarāya-Mahārāya. It
records the Sanskrit verse sung by Tirumalamma, about the gift of suvarṇa-mēru
made by the king in the presence of the god Viṭṭhalēśvara. It is interesting to
note that Tirumalamma was a Sanskrit poetess.

l.ka:
śubhamastu | śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīra ·acyutadēva-
rāyamahārāyaru vijeyasaṁvatsarada mārgaśīrṣa śu 11 guruvāradali tuṁgabhadrā-
tīradali śrīviṭhalēśvarana saṁnidhiyali māḍida suvarṇamērudānakke vōduva tirumalaṁ-
manavaru hēḷida ślōkavidu ||

bāṇaprāṇapayōdhiśītakarasaṁkhyātē śakavatsarē vikhyātē vijayē ca māsi sahasi svacchē
ca pakṣē gurōḥ vārē viṣṇudinē śubhēparisarē śrīviṭhalādhīśituḥ dhīraḥ kāṁcanamēru-
dānamakarōdvīrōcyutakṣmāpatiḥ ||

inscription.head:
No. 558.
(A.R. No. 708 of 1922.)
ON THE NORTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p: This is a copy, engraved in a different place, of No. 9 of 1904, above.

l.ka:
śubhamastu | śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīmadaccutadēvamahārāyaru
vijeyasaṁvatsarada mārga[śira] śu • • [guruvāradali] • • • • tīradali
śrī viṭhalēśva-

rana sanidhiyalli māḍida suvarṇamērudānake vōduva tirumalammanavaru hēḷida ślōka-
vidu [||*] bāṇaprāṇapayōdhi[śītakarasaṁkhyātē] śakevatsarē vikhyātē vijayē [ca] mā-

si sahasi svacchē [ca] pakṣē gurōrvārē viṣṇudinē śubhē parisarē śrīviṭhṭhalādhīśituḥ
dhīraḥ kāṁcanamērudānamakarōdvīrāccyutakṣmāpatiḥ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 559.
(A.R. No. 580 of 1912.)
ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SECOND PRAKARA OF THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE
AT LEPAKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged at the end. The Kanarese statement at the beginning says that
this is a copy of the copper-plate inscription registering the grant of certain villages
for the service of the god Vīrēśvara, i.e., Vīrabhadradēva of Lēpākshi. The rest of the
record is in Sanskrit verse giving the genealogy of the Tuḷuva line of kings and
mentioning their various conquests and gifts. The numerous gifts made by king
Achyutarāya to whose reign the record belongs are also mentioned. The verse that
was recited by Ōduva Tirumalamma about the gift of suvarṇa-mēru in Śaka 1455,
Vijaya, is also found here.

inscription.head:
No. 560.
(A.R. No. 789 of 1917.)
ON THREE PIECES OF A BROKEN SLAB LYING NEAR THE ANJANEYA
TEMPLE AT CHAGALERU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1455, Vijaya, Mārgaśira, ba. 15, Sōmavāra
corresponding to A.D. 1533 December 15 Monday. It records that Kampadēvarasa-
Aṇṇa of Chāgalēra-Kāntēri made a gift of a wet field, a khaṇḍuga in sowing capacity,
for the service of the god Avubhaḷadēva at Chāgalēru. The gift was made for the
merit of the king Achyutarāya-Mahārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 561.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB LYING NEAR A SLUICE OF THE TANK AT BUKKAPATNAM,
PENUKONDA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1455, Vijaya, Phālguṇa, śu. 15, Sthiravāra corresponding to
A.D. 1534 February 28, Saturday, in the reign of Achyutadēvarāya. It records that
Timmarasa, son of Bhaṇḍārada Apparasaya, restored the dēvadāya and brahmadāya
lands below (the tank) Hosakere in the division of Penugoṇḍe, in accordance with
the order issued by the king on the completion of the lakshahōma rite performed at
Varadarāja-Ammana-pēṭhe.

l.ka: gaḷa gaddegaḷu cikka·oḍeyara kāla modalāgi sarvva-
koṁdu narakada voḷage nūkida pāpadali hōgaluḷavaru
·ēkaiva bhaginī lōkē sarvēṣāmēva bhūbhujāṁ | na bhō-
jyā na karagrāhyā vipradattā vasuṁdharā [||*] yeṁbuthā śā-
ṭṭidavarige tāvē tapidavaru [||*] svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā
viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi[ḥ *] || yo dharmake tapidavaru

inscription.head:
No. 562.
(A.R. No. 547 of 1919.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SECOND PRAKARA OF THE ARULALA-PERUMAL
TEMPLE AT LITTLE CONJEEVERAM, CONJEEVERAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1455, Vijaya, Pḥālguṇa, ba. 4, corresponding to A.D. 1534
March 4, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya. It re-
cords that in the year Virōdhi, Kārtika, ba. 5, corresponding to A.D. 1529 October
21, Thursday, the day of his coronation, the king made a gift of some villages (named)
to the gods, Varadarājasvāmi and Ēkāmbranātha, to be equally divided and that on
finding that Sāḷuva-Nāyaka who was appointed for the purpose made unequal distri-
bution, the king set it right. It also records the manner of distribution of the
offerings among the priests and officials of the temple. The inscription is
Damaged.

l.ka:

biḍuva naivēdya kaṭṭaḷe prāku

svāmi kaṭṭaḷe māḍiddu harivāṇa 54 ·atirasa paḍi 2 dadhyōdanada harivāṇa • • • •
• harivāṇa 2 dīpa 50 yiṣṭake prāku kaṭṭaḷe māḍidu ga 1000 • • tathātithi-
yallū kaṭṭaḷeya māḍi rāyasava cittayistalli varadā[jaṁ]mmanavara ·ubhayakke •

ga 360 ·ēkāṁta ·avasara naivēdyakaṭṭaḷe tiruvattisāma harivāṇa 2 kaṁ saṁṇakki 1 hālu
tuppa sakhare bāḷehaṁṇu teṁginakāyi • • • • • • 1 kaṁ ga 3 || lekhadalū

lekha varuṣa 1 ke hala 3600 ka¥ 1 • • • hala 6 lekhadalū ga 6 ·ā kaṭṭaḷege ga
126 yo naivēdyada prasāda ·atirasa dadhyaṁna tiruvattisāma gaṁdha ·aḍakele ·ellavanūnṛ
biṭṭavaviḷukāḍu nālkaṟali voṁdu pātrāvisēṣanu kaḷadu mikkādadanū nālku
bageyavarū haṁcikoḷaluḷavarū yo dharmmake yo pramāṇadallū ·ācaṁdrārkkasthā-
yiyāgi naḍisaluḷadū ||

inscription.head:
No. 563.
(A.R. No. 548 of 1919.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p: This is a copy in Nāgarī characters of No. 547 of 1919 above.

l.de:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrimanmahārājādhirāja rājaparameśvara mūrurāyaragaṁḍa ·arirāyavibhāḍa ·aṣṭa-
dikkurāyamanobhayaṁkara bhāṣegetappuvarāyaraṁgaḍa pūrvadakṣiṇapaścimasamudrādhīśvara śrīvīrapratāpa
śrīvīra·accutarāyamahārāyaru pruthvīrājyaṁ geyuttamiralu śālivāhanaśakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 14[55 saṁdu]
mele naḍava vijayasaṁvatsarada phālguṇa ba 4 (4) śrīvīra·accutarāyamahārāyaru prāku virodhi-
saṁvatsarada kārtika ba 5 lu paṭṭābhiṣekastarāgi viṣṇukaṁciya varadarājadevarigū ·ekāṁbranātha-
devarigū samanāgi tamma paṭṭābhiṣekadali biṭṭa grāmagaḷanu sariyāgi biḍaheḷi sāḷuva-
nāyakari[ge*] ·appaṇeya cittaisalāgi ·ā sāḷuvanayakaru taṁna kāṇiyāciyāgalāgi
grāmagaḷanū

·ekāṁbranāthadevarige vaggaḷavāgi biṭṭu varadarājadevarige kaḍameyāgi biḍalāgi ·āmele svāmi grāma-
gaḷanu heccukuṁdāgi biṭṭiralāgi parāmarisi samanāgi samarpista grāmagaḷa vivara | varadarāja-
devarige [ci]yavarada kāluvaḷigaḷū sahita grā 1 ke rekhe ga 450 ·a[kkā]lavelūru
grāma kāluvaḷisaha grā 1 ke ga 60 peṭe grā 1 kaṁ 85 [are]yaneri grā 1 kaṁ ga
930 pāleyūru kāluvaḷisaha grā 1 kaṁ ga 420 gaṁgavarada grā 1 kaṁ ga 155 ·aṁtu gnā
7 [kā]luvaṁḷigaḷu saha rekhe ga 2550 svāmiya rāyasta pramāṇakke ·ekāṁbranathadevarige
biṭṭa ·akkāḷi • • • grā 1 kaṁ ga 450 • • grā 6 kaṁ ga 2000 kkaṁ biḍuva
naivedyakaṭṭaḷe prāku svāmi kaṭṭa

ḷe māḍidu harivāṇa 54 ·atirasa paḍi 2 dadhyaṁnada harivāṇa • • • • harivāṇa 2 dipa 50 yiṣṭakke
prāku kaṭṭaḷe māḍidu ga 1000 • • tathātithiyalu kaṭṭaḷeya māḍi rāyasava cittayistalli
varadāja·aṁmanavara ·ubhaya puḷukāppu ·ekādaśi 1 ke ga 40 lekhadalū varuṣa 1 kaṁ
ekādaśi 25 kaṁ ga 1000 komāra veṁkaṭādri·ayyanavara ·ubhayaṁ dadhyaṁnada harivāṇa 2
kaṁ saṇaki 2 mosaru śuṁṭhi paccaḍi • • • ·uppu meṇasu jīrige meṁtheya paccaḍi 4 saha
tiṁ 1 kaṁ ga 3¥ 2 || lekhadalū baruṣa 1 kaṁ ga 39 svāmiya ·ubhayaṁ pallakkisevege
bijayamaḍūsu saṁmaṁdha tiruveḷakku paṁcige yaṁṇe di 1 kaṁ ma • • • lekhadalū varuṣa 1 kaṁ
ga 360 ·ekāṁtada

·avasarake naivedya kaṭṭaḷe tiruvattisāma harivāṇa 2 kaṁ saṁṇakki 1 hālu tuppa sakhare bāḷe
haṁṇu teṁginakāyi • • • • • • • • • • 1 kaṁ ga 42 ·aḍake • • 1 kaṁ 50 ·ele
100 lekhadalū varuṣa 1 kaṁ ·aḍake 18000 ga 1 || 3 ·ele 36000 ga 1 || 3
ubhaya ga 3 || 1 di 1 kaṁ gaṁdha hala 1 lekhadalū varuṣa 1 kaṁ hala 3600 ka
¥ 1 ke hala 6 lekhadalū ga 6 ·ā kaṭṭaḷege ga 126 yī naivedyada prasada ·atirasa dadhyaṁna
tiruvattisāma gaṁdha ·aḍikele ·ellavanū viṭṭavanuviḷukkāḍu nālakarali ·oṁdu pātraviseṣanu

sthāyiyāgi naḍasaluḷadu ||

inscription.head:
No. 564.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1904.)
ON THE LEFT SIDE OF THE NORTH GOPURAM OF THE ACHYUTARAYASVAMI
TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1456, Jaya, Vaiśākha, śu. 13, Bhā[nuvāra], cor-
responding to A.D. 1534 April 26, Sunday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya
It records that the Mahāmaṇḍalēsvara Hiriya-Tirumalarāja-Voḍeya, son of Salaka-
rāja-Voḍeya, set up the god Tiruvēṅgaḷanātha on the bank of the Tungabhadrā and
granted three villages for the service of the god and ordered that the offerings made
to the god should be distributed among 14 persons (specified).

l.ka: hanaśakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1456 neya jayasaṁvatsarada vayiśākha śu 13 bhā
[bhōga] māsauchāha snapana paṁcaparvva rathōchaha koḍatirunāḷu saha
ya grāma yo catamsīmeya voḷagāda cōramanūra saha cannahaṭiya grā-
paḍuvalu mataṁgaparvata mūḍalu tiparājatō • baḍaga tuṁgabhadrātīrada sī-
tākoṁḍa [ṭeṁka]lu bhūpatikeṟe yo catusīme voḷagādha pēṭeya tō • •
ya hasuru vāṇi • • • tōṭa • • • sarvamā(ṁ)nyada pēṭe 1 salaka-
rājagaḷa hiriya tirumalarājagaḷu krayava koṁḍu biṭa koḍage • • 1 dhar(ṁ)ma sā

• • rada grā 1 kōṭinahāḷa grāma 1 ·aṁtu kraya • • yalu biṭa grāma mū-
ṟu ·ī sīme-
ya pēṭa saha sarvamā(ṁ)nyavāgi ·ācaṁdrārkastāyāgi dēvarige samarpisi purōhita

sūtrada gautamagōtrada rāmaṇācāryara makaḷu nāraṇācārya gautamagō-
trada

[da] ja 2 purāṇikara ja 2 yitihāsada ja 1 ghaḷiyārada ja 1 bhāgavata ja 2 kaṭigeya ja
2 yo dēvastāna pēṭe grāma sīmeda staḷada sēnabōva

maudgalagōtrada ·āślāyanasūtrada ·ādavāniya karaṇika habarasara moṁma basavarasara ma
kaḷu sātarasana jana 1 kaṁ jīvita prasāda ·aṣṭa-

tēja svāṁmya pēṭeya mūlavīsa saha staḷada sēnaboyyikeyanū śrītiruvēṁgaḷanāthana
sannidhiyalu pratiṣṭhākāladalū dhāreyaneradu koṭarāgi

l.note:
The following lines are inserted on the left hand
dēvara vaidya
maudgalyagō-
trada ·āśvalā-
yanasūtrada
·apājipaṁ-
ḍitara ja 1

l.ka:
dhārāpūrvakavāgi ·ācaṁdrārkastāyigaḷāgi putrapautraparaṁpareyāgi ·anubhavisuvudu
eṁdu koṭa staḷada sēnabōyike dēvara paricā-

raka svayaṁpāka ·ūḷigadavaru kauṁḍalyagōtrada ·āpastaṁbasūtrada gōmaḍa ·apayana maga
apayana ja 14 yo brā(ṁ)hmaṇaviniyōga

jīvitaprasāda ·aṣṭatēja syā(ṁ)mya sahā kaṭṭaḷeyanu māḍi ·acutadēvamahārāyaru
yo tathātithi pu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalu sahira(ṁ)ṇyōda-

kadānadhārāpūrvakavāgi śrītiruvēṁgaḷanāthage tuṁgabhadrātīradalu dhāreyaneradu koṭaṁ-
thā sīme grāma pēṭiya śilāśāśanake maṁgaḷa ma-

hāśrī śrī śrī [||*] yo dharmake ·āru tapidarū taṁma taṁde tāyigaḷanu vāraṇāsiyalu
vadhisida pāpake hōgaluḷavaru gō brā(ṁ)hmara vadhi-
sidaṁthā pāpake hōgaluḷavaru || śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 565.
(A.R. No. 195 of 1913.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO A WATER-CHANNEL NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE
AT HAVINAHALU-VIRAPURA, BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1455, Jaya, Vaiśākha, śu. 15, corresponding to
A.D. 1534 April 28, Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that the king erected the siṁhāsana of the god Bukkēśvara to the west of
the temple of Virūpākshadēva and made a gift of the village Doṇḍavaṭi for the service
of the god. The gift is stated to have been made for the merit of his grandfather
Īśvara-Nāyaka and grandmother Bukkāji-amma and that a copper-plate charter was
granted to that effect and liṅgamudre stones set up on the boundaries of the village.
The village was given to Rāmachandra-Dīkshita for conducting the worship.

l.ka: rāyarū taṁma taṁde narasaṇanāyakara tāyi bu[kkāji]-
mege liṁgamudreya kalanū naṁdiya kalanū hākisi taṁ-
samarpisi biṭṭa doṁḍavaṭiya grāma yo grāmake
māḍida dōśakke kōharū || vāgdattaṁ (|) manōdattaṁ (|) dhā-
jāyatē krimi [ḥ*] || ·ēkaiva bhaginī lōkē sarvēsāṁmōva bhū-
bhujāṁ | na bhōjyāna karagrā(ṁ)hyā vipradattāvasuṁdharā [||*] nūnā •

inscription.head:
No. 566.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1904.)
ON THE WESTERN BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE VITTHALASVAMI
TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1456, Jaya, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 12, Bu[dhavāra], corre-
sponding to A.D. 1534 July 22, Wednesday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that Gandhada Tippaseṭṭi made a gift of gold for the worship of the
Āḷvārs, 13 in number, including Tirukachinambiyāḷvār, consecrated by himself. The
gift was made for the merit of the king.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrīvijayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣa 1456 ṟaneya jayasaṁva-
charada śrāvaṇa śu 12 bu lū śrīmatu śrīma(ṁ)nmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrī-
vīrapratāpa śrīvīra·acyutadēvamahārāyaru pruthvīrājyaṁgeyi·uttaṁ yiralu
śrīviṭhaladēvara

stānadalu ·āvaraṇada ·oḷage baḍagaṇa bhāgadalu kaṭṭi ·idda nālku ·aṁkaṇadoḷage vāṇijyavai-
śyanagarada śeṭyaṇadaruśigōtrada ·a[dhi*]kāriya tippiseṭṭira maga gaṁdhada tippiseṭṭiyaru
accutarāyamahārāyarige pu(ṁ)ṇyavāgabēkū ·eṁ-

[du] pratiṣṭheya māḍida ·āḷvāra dēvaru 12 tirukacinaṁbiyāḷvāru 1 ·ubhayaṁ dēvaru
hadimūṟu dēvarige ni[tya]dīpārādhane naivēdya hūvina daṁḍeyū śrīviṭhaladēvara
amrutapaḍiya kaṭṭaḷeya voḷage saluva kaṭṭaḷegāgi śrīviṭhaladēvara bhaṁḍārake pulivaṭe
āgi sama-

rpisida rokka varaha ga 100 ṟu varahaṁnu koṭṭu dēvara guḍiyalū māḍisida puliveṭṭu
kaṭṭaḷe dina 1 lu saluva kaṭaḷe ·akki paḍi hadimūṟu paḍige saluva prasāda baṭṭalu 36
dīpārādhanege yeṁṇe paḍi voṁdū ·akki hūvina daṁḍe 13 ·ikaṭṭaḷe pramāṇinalu dēva-
stānadalū naḍasu

dharmake ·āru tapidarū śrīviṭhaladēvara pādakke tapidavaru | gōhatya bra(ṁ)hmahatya mahā-
pātakava māḍida pāpake pōgaluḷavaru || śrī

inscription.head:
No. 567.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1912.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE PRAKARA OF THE LAKSHMIDEVI TEMPLE AT
GORREPALLE, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1456, Jaya, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 8, Kṛishṇajayantī, corresponding to
A.D. 1534 August 1, Saturday (not verifiabie), in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that, at the suggestion of Vīrapaṇṇayya of Penugoṇḍe, Timmarasa, son of
Bhaṇḍārada Apparasa of Bhāradvāja-gōtra, Kātyāyana-sūtra and Śukla-Yajuś-
śākhā, made a gift of the village Muttakadahaḷḷi, surnamed Mahālakshmīpura, for
the service of the goddess Mahālakshmīdēvī at Haruhe-Lakkhaṇāpura in Rodda-
nāḍu, a sub-division of Penugoṇḍe-rājya.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] vaṁdāmahē mahālakṣmīṁ vaṁdāruvaradāyinīṁ | lakhkhaṇāpuravikhyāta
puṇya-

ṇāpuramahālakṣmyāḥ susampāditaṁ | naivēdyādisamṛddhayē pratidinaṁ yadgrāmasī-
mādikaṁ
tatsarvaṁ pravilikhyatē sakalavikhyātyai śilāśāsanē || svasti śrījayābhyudayaśāli-
māgaṇināyakatanakke pālisida penugoṁḍerājya rodanāḍa haruhe sthaḷadoḷagaṇa | viṭha-

vanahaḷḷige paḍuvalu modeyagā | make baḍagalāda catusīmāsahitavāda nidhi nikṣēpa jala
pāṣā-

ṇa ·akṣīṇāgāmi sidha sādhyaṁgaḷeṁba ·aṣṭabhōgatējasvāmyadoḍane kūḍida muttakada-
haḷige prati-

nāmavāda mahālakṣmīpuraveṁba sarvamānyada grāmavanū penugoṁḍe vīrappaṁṇaṁyanavaru
bōdhi-

salāgi haruhe lakhaṇāpurada mahālakṣmīdēvarige dhūpa dīpa ·amṛtapaḍi naivēdya nṛtya
gīta vādya muṁ-
radu koṭṭa muttakadahaḷḷi grāmada dharmaśāsanada prati | svadattādviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ parada-

inscription.head:
No. 568.
(A.R. No. 729 of 1916.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYASVAMI TEMPLE AT ANANTAPURAM,
MADAKSIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1456, Jaya, Āśvayuja, ba. 12, corresponding to
A.D. 1534 October 18, Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya.
It registers a gift for the service of the god Hiriya-Tiruvēṅgaḷanātha of
Mūdaṇagiri. The gift was made for the merit of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 569.
(A.R. No. 527 of 1915.)
ON A STONE SET UP AT THE VILLAGE-GATE AT BHATTARABOMMALAPURAM,
ADONI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1457, Jaya, Pushya, śu. 3, Sōmavāra, Saṅkramaṇa, correspon-
ding to A.D. 1534 December 7, Monday, which was not, however, a day of any saṅ-
kramaṇa. It records that king Achyutadēva-Mahārāya, who was ruling at Vidyānagara,
made a gift of the village Bommalāpura on the bank of the Tungabhadrā and situated
in the Sātanūru-sīme, a division of Ādavāni-rājya, to Bhaṭara Nāgaya, son of Kaḍabūra
Reḍyapaya of the Bhaṭ-ānvaya.

l.ka: bhavē [||*] svasti samastapṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara vīrapratāpa
ya jayasaṁvatsarada puṣya śuddha [3] sōmavārada saṁkramaṇapu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalu śrīma-
dbhaṭā[nva]ya kaḍabūra reḍyapayana maga bhaṭara nāgayage koṭa dharma | ·ā-
davāniya rājyake saluva sātanūra sīmeyoḷagaṇa tuṁgabhadrātīrada boṁ-
bhōga tēja svāmya hadineṁṭumaha sakalōtpatti sakala suvarnādā-
ya sakala dhānyada kūranū nīu niṁma putrapautrapāraṁpara
yeṁdu māḍida dharma yo dharmake ·āru tapidare ·avaru
taṁma mātā pitrugaḷa kāśiyalu koṁda dōśakke hōharu

inscription.head:
No. 570.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1904.)
ON THE SOUTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE VITTHALASVAMI TEMPLE
AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1456, Jaya, Pushya, śu. 6, Gu[ruvāra], corresponding to A.D.
1534 December 10, Thursday, f.d.t. ·09, in the reign of Achyutadēvarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that Varadappa-Nāyaka, son of Tiruvēṅgaḷa-Nāyaka, made a gift of gold to
the treasury of the Viṭṭhalēśvara temple for the daily offerings of the god. The gift
was made for the merit of the king and of Chikkarāya.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣa 1456 ṟa neya jayasaṁvacha-
rada puśya śu 6 gu lū śīmatu

ralu | caturthagotrada talibaṇada baḷiya tiravēṁgaḷanāyakara maga [va]radappanāyakarū
barasida dharmaśāsana ta(ṁ)mma voḍeyaru

·accutadēvarāyamahārāyarigū cikkara yarigū pu(ṁ)ṇyavāgabēkū ·eṁdu śrīviṭhalē-
śvaradēvarige nityanaivēdya naḍevadakke

dēvara ·ugrāṇadalu koḍuva dānada kaṭaḷege dēvara bhaṁḍārake samarpisida ghaṭṭi·i varahakaṁ
ba[ḍi]

tiṁgaḷuvoṁdake ghaṭṭivaraha hattū varahakke sale cakra ga 11 ¥ 4 ṇakkaṁ ·ugrāṇadalū
koḍuva davasa kaṭaḷe

dina 1 dakke ·akki paḍi 40 mēlōgara ·uppu meṇasu sambhāra hesaṟu harivāṇake tuppa
sakhare bāḷehaṇṇu[hālu]

mosaru vīḷeya dīvige 2 ·i pramāṇina davasavanū koṭṭu naḍasi baraluḷadu ·i naivēdya
nirmālya vecca-
da dharmaū tamma voḍeya ·acutadēvarāyamahārāyarige pu(ṁ)ṇyavāgi māḍida dharma

·i dharmakke ·āru tappidarū śrīviṭhaladēvara pādakke tappidavaru gōhatya bra(ṁ)hmahatya
bhrūṇahatyādi mahāpātakava māḍida pāpakke hōharu ||

inscription.head:
No. 571.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1913.)
ON A STONE IN FRONT OF THE NANDI-MANDAPA OF THE DODDAPADESVARA
TEMPLE AT MODALLI, KOLLEGAL TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1456, Jaya, Phālguṇa, śu. 5, Ravivāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1535 February 7, Sunday, in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya. It records
that Mādappayya, agent of Rāmappayya, a minister to the king, made a grant of the
income from ārati-kāṇike and bēḍige for the service of the god Mallikārjunadēva in
Haḷe-Mōḍahaḷḷi near Mōḍahaḷḷi in Hadināḍu-sīme, thus reviving the defunct service.
The gift was made for the merit of the king and of Rāmappayya.

l.ka:
hanū bramaṇana koṁda pāpakke hō-
ha-

inscription.head:
No. 572.
(A.R. No. 575 of 1912.)
ON THE EASTERN WALL OF THE SECOND PRAKARA OF THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT
LEPAKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1456, Manmatha (= A.D. 1535-36), in the reign
of Achyutarāya. It records that the king made a gift of a village (name lost) for
the service of the god Vīrabhadra at Lēpākshi.

l.ka:
disamraddhayē | śrīvīrācyutarāyēṇa praṇamyaiva samarpitaḥ || salagrā • māda
yaṁ grāmaḥ catussīmā samanvitaḥ jagatprasiddha-

inscription.head:
No. 573.
(A.R. No. 681 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE BRIDGE ON THE ROAD LEADING TO THE SUB-COLLECTOR'S
BUNGALOW AT HOSPET, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1457, Manmatha, Vaiśākha, śu. 1, corresponding to A.D. 1535
April 3, Saturday (not verifiable.) It refers itself to the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahā-
rāya, and records that Abbarāja Timmapa, agent to the Pradhāna Tirumalarāja,
made a gift of the mūlavīsa (cess) with the consent of the merchants of Tirumala-
dēviyara-paṭṭaṇa, Varadarājammana-paṭṭaṇa, and Kṛishṇāpura for the service of the
god Tiruvēṅgaḷanātha in Tirumaladēviyara-paṭṭaṇa. Bisilahaḷḷi pass is mentioned.

l.ka: da seṭi paṭaṇasvāmigaḷigū kruṣṇāpurada seṭi pa-

l.note: The slab is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 574.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1904.)
ON THE SOUTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE VITTHALASVAMI TEMPLE
AT HAMPI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1458, Durmukhi, Jyēshṭha, śu. 11, Bu[dhavāra] corresponding
to A.D. 1536 May 31, Wednesday in the reign of Achyutadēvarāya-Mahārāya. It records
that Hiriya-Tirumala-Nāyaka made a gift of varāhas 200 to the temple-treasury for
daily offerings to the god Viṭṭhalasvāmi. The gift was made for the merit of the
king and Chikkarāya.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣa 1458 ṭaneya durmukhisaṁva-
charada jēṣṭa śdhu 11 bu lū śrīmatu śrīma(ṁ)n-

taṁmināyakarige • • • • • • ·ūḷigada hiriya mallappanāyakanū barasida dharma

varaha 5 kkaṁ sele cakra ga 50 || kaṁ • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 575.
(A.R. No. 5-A of 1904.)
IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1458, Durmukhi, Jyēshṭha, śu. 11, Bu[dhavāra],
corresponding to A.D. 1536 May 31, Wednesday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that Tirumala-Nāyaka made some gift by the order of the king.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrīvijayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavarṣa 1458 neya durmukhisaṁva-
tsarada jyēṣṭha śu 11 bu lū śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīravīpratāpa
śrī ·acyuta-

inscription.head:
No. 576.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD TO THE WEST OF THE MALLIKARJUNA TEMPLE
AT KAMBADURU, KALYANADRUG TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1458, Durmukhi, Jyēshṭha, ba. 30, Ādivāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1536 June 18, Sunday, in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya. It records that
Baiyapa-Nāyaka confirmed the grant formerly made of the 6 villages, Jālavaṭi, Hosa-
haḷḷi, Ammakere, Tegegala, Gāḍidipāṟa and Baṇḍēnahaḷḷi, and certain wet fields at
Kambhadūru for the service of the god Mallikārjunadēva.

l.ka: ḷu 1458 neya durmukhisaṁvatsarada jyēṣṭha ba 30 ·ādivāradalu śrīma-
vaka prāku saluva grāmagaḷa vivara jālavaṭiya grāma 1 hosahaḷi grā 1
ḍēnehaḷi grāma 1 ·aṁ[tu*] grāma 6 kaṁbhadūra keṟe keḷage sa-
luva gade kha 3 gaṁ hūvina kāluve dēvanīlabhaṭara ho-

nyavāgi biṭukoṭu śāśanavanu barasi koṭe·u yo grāmagaḷige

inscription.head:
No. 577.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1912.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE PRAKARA IN THE LAKSHMIDEVI TEMPLE AT
GORREPALLI, HINDUPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1458, Durmukhi, Māgha, śu. 7, Budhavāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1537 January 17, Wednesday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya. It records
that, at the suggestion of Virapaṇṇa-Nāyaka of Penugoṇḍe, Kṛishṇapa-Nāyaka, son of
Sāṇi Chinnapa-Nāyaka, made a gift of the village, Goravanahaḷḷi, surnamed Lakshmī-
sāgara, in Guyyalūra-sīme, a sub-division of Rodda-nāḍu which was a district in
Penugoṇḍe-rājya, for the service of the goddess, Mahālakshmīdēvi, of Haruhe-
Lakkhaṇāpura.

l.ka: kavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1458 neya durmukhisaṁvatsarada māgha śu 7 budhavāra ratha-
tanake pālisida penugoṁḍerājya rodanāḍa guyyalūra sīme voḷagaṇa
haḷige teṁkalu koḍagihaḷige paḍuvalu | modheyake baḍagalāda ca-
tussīmāsahitavāda nidhi nikṣēpa jala pāṣāṇa ·akṣīṇāgāmi sidha sādhya-
da lakṣmīsāgaraveṁba sarvamānyada grāmavanū penugoṁḍe virapaṁṇayya-
dhūpa dīpa ·amṛtapaḍi naivēdya nrutya gīta vādya muṁttāda vaibhavaṁgaḷige ta-

inscription.head:
No. 578.
(A.R. No. 381 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BY THE SIDE OF THE EAST VERANDAH IN THE OUTER PRAKARA
OF THE KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, A SUBURB OF COONDAPUR,
COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is very much damaged and dated Śaka 1458, Durmukhi, Māgha, ba. 14,
Guruvāra, corresponding to A.D. 1536 February 8, Thursday, in the reign of Achyuta-
dēvarāya-Mahārāya who was ruling at Vijayanagara. It records a gift of land and
gold made by Koṇḍappa-Voḍeya for the service of the god Kōṭināthadēva and the
goddess Saṅkamādēvī. The king is stated to have appointed Suṅkaṇa-Nāyaka
as the governor of Bārakūra-rājya.

l.ka: rada māgha ba 14 gu lu śrima(ṁ)nmahārājādhi-
• • • • • svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharāṁ
l.note: The slab is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 579.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1912.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE PRAKARA OF THE LAKSHMIDEVI TEMPLE AT
GORREPALLE, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1459, Hēmaḷambi, Chaitra, śu. 15, Sōmavāra corresponding to
A.D. 1537 March 26, Monday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya. It records that
Vīraṇa-Nāyaka, brother of Penugoṇḍe Virapaṇṇayya, son of Lēpākshi Nandi-Lakiseṭṭi
and Muddamāmbā, made a gift for the service of the goddess Mahālakshmīdēvi, of
Lakkhaṇāpura, of the kāṇike, gold and clothes, offered by devotees of various count-
ries to the goddess, on festival and other days.

l.ka: marpisuvaṁthā kāṇike rokha vastra bhaṁgāra muṁttāda sa-
masta vastugaḷa kāṇikeyanū ·i dēvarige dhūpa dīpa
·amrutapaḍi naivēdya nrutya gīta vādya muṁttāda vaibha-
neradu koṭṭa prativaruṣa rathōtsava tīrthadivasagaḷa pratidi-

inscription.head:
No. 580.
(A.R. No. 576 of 1912.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SECOND PRAKARA OF THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT
LEPAKSHI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1459, Hēmaḷambi, Āshāḍha, śu. 12, Sōmavāra corresponding to
A.D. 1537 June 20, Wednesday, not Monday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that Achyutarāya-Mallapaṇṇa purchased from Timmapa, son of
Bhaṇḍārada Apparasa of Bhāradvāja-gōtra, Kātyāyana-sūtra and Śuklayajuś-
sākhā the mānya village, Nandicherla, and presented it for the service of the god
Vīrēśa. This village, surnamed Virūpāksharāyapura, is stated to have been granted as
a mānya to Timmappa's grandfather Vīramarasa by Virūpākshadēva-Mahārāya in
Śaka 1389, Sarvajit, Pushya, śu. 2, Sōmavāra corresponding to A.D. 1467 December
28, Monday. The sale price was fixed by the Madhyasthas. The record is damaged
at the end.

inscription.head:
No. 581.
(A.R. No. 781 of 1917.)
ON A BOULDER TO THE WEST OF THE VILLAGE CHAUTAKUNTAPALLI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1459, Hēmaḷambi, Āśvayuja, śu. 11, corresponding to A.D.
1537 September 15, Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.
It states that a copper-plate grant was issued recording the gift of Kañchākarahaḷḷi
for the service of the gods Pāpavināśa, Vīrēśa and Raṅganātha at Lēpakshi by
Virapaṇṇaya, son of Nandi-Lakkiseṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 582.
(A.R. No. 577 of 1912.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SECOND PRAKARA OF THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT
LEPAKSHI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1459, Hēmaḷambi, Āśvīja, ba. 5, Sōmavāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1537 September 23, Sunday, not Monday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that Penugoṇḍe Virappaṇṇa purchased from certain Brāhmans some
vṛittis in the village of Kalanūru, surnamed Triyambaka-Nārāyaṇapura, and presented
them for the service of the god Vīrēśvara of Lēpākshi.

inscription.head:
No. 583.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1912.)
ON THE SOUTHERN WALL OF THE PRAKARA OF THE LAKSHMIDEVI TEMPLE AT
GORREPALLI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1460, Viḷambi, Chaitra, śu. 5, Maṅgaḷavāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1538 March 5, Tuesday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya. It records
that Vīraṇa-Nāyaka, brother of Penugoṇḍe Virapaṇṇayya, son of Nandi-Lakkiseṭṭi,
constructed a canal called Nūtana-Tuṅgabhadrā from a spring to the north of the
village Modaya, surnamed Achyutarāyapura, which had been granted by the king for
the service of the gods Vīrēśvara, Pāpavināśadēva and Rāghavēśvara, and made a
gift of the land under the new canal for the service of the goddess Mahālakshmīdēvi
at Haruhe-Lakkhaṇāpura.

l.ka:
śubhamastu | svasti śrīśakavaruṣēbhrarutvabdhiśaśabhain™ mitē viḷaṁbināmakē caitrē | śuddha-
śrīpaṁcavotithau || penugoṁ-

hārāyēṇārpitasya svanāmataḥ [||*] grāmasya modayākhyasya sthalē yatnēna nirmitā |
[||*] śrīmanṛ-

purī(ṁ)lakṣmyāḥ naivēdyādisamaiddhayē | tuṁgabhadrā(ṁ)hvayā kulyā tuṁgabhadraphala-
pradā [||*] sahira(ṁ)ṇyapayōdā-

nadhārāpūrvaṁ samarpitā ·ācaṁdrārkaṁ bhaktajanapāraṁparyēṇa vardhatāṁ [||*] svasti śrī-
jayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaru-

dhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrī-

ārobbaru tappidarū yo dē-

inscription.head:
No. 584.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1912.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE PRAKAṚA OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1460, Viḷambi, Chaitra, śu. 15, Sthiravāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1538, March 15, Friday (not Saturday), in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that Vīraṇa-Nāyāka, brother of Penugoṇḍe Virapaṇṇa-Nāyaka, got a gift
made of the toll and other revenue raised in the fair held on every Friday at
Lakkhaṇāpura by the Seṭṭis and Paṭṭaṇasvāmis, for the service of the goddess Mahā-
lakshmīdēvī of Haruhe-Lakkhaṇāpura.

l.ka: ge dhūpa dīpa ·amrutapaḍi naivēdya nrutya gīta vādya muṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 585.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1912.)
ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is of the same date and reign as No. 584 above. It enumerates the various
buildings and shrines erected in the temple of Mahālakshmīdēvi at Haruhe-Lakkhaṇā-
pura by the brothers Virapaṇṇa and Vīraṇa-Nāyaka of Penugoṇḍe.

l.ka: ṇa cappara vōlagada ghāḷimaṁṭṭapa suttaṇa taḷa-
tadēvara guḍi bhayiruvadēvara guḍi viśvēśvarana gu-

inscription.head:
No. 586.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1904.)
ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE EASTERN GOPURA OF THE VITTHALASVĀMI TEMPLE
AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka, 1459, Viḷambi, Adhika-Jyēshṭha, śu. 15, Sōma-
vāra corresponding to A.D. 1538 May 13, Monday. It records that, in the reign of
Achyutadēva-Mahārāya, Hiriya Timmappa-Nāyaka, son of Udhiya Timappa-Nāyaka,
gave a sum of gold coins for putting up pinnacles on the eastern gōpura of the
Viṭṭhaladēva temple.

l.ka: mahārāyaru pruthvīrājyaṁgaiuttaṁ yiralu cātrada śrīvaiṣṇava tiru[pa]
ke hēḷalāgi ·udhiya tiṁmappanāyakara maganu rāyara ca[parada] hiriya-
ti[mma]ppanāyakaruṁ taṁma caparada raghavapanu taṁma voḍeya ·acyutarāyamahā-

inscription.head:
No. 587.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1912.)
ON THE WALL OF THE VERANDAH ROUND THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT LEPAKSHI,
HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1460, Viḷambi, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 10, Sō[mavāra] corresponding to
A.D. 1538 August 19, Monday in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya. It records
that Penugoṇḍe Virapaṇṇayya made a gift of the village Chikkanandichervu,
surnamed Dēvarāyapura, which he purchased from certain Brāhmans (named), for
the service of the god Vīrabhadra of Lēpākshi. The village is stated to have been
granted as a sarvamānya to an ancestor of these Brāhmans by Prauḍha-Dēva-
Mahārāya.

l.ka: sādhu samarpitō vijayatāma[sminṛ] śilāśāsanē [||*] svasti śrījayā-
vatsarada śrāvaṇa ba 10 sō dallū śrīmanṛmahārājādhirāja paramēśvara śrīvīrapra-
śvaradēvara nijabhaktarāda lēpākṣi naṁdilakkiśeṭṭi mudamāṁbā varakumāra penu-
sarvakratu sōmanāthadīkṣitara makkaḷu koṁḍudīkṣitariṁda cikanaṁdicervu yeṁ-
ba grāmadalli caturthāṁśavanu | ·ā gōtrada ·ā sūtrada ceṁnidīkṣitara makkaḷu duggaṇadī-
kṣitaru namaśivāyadīkṣitaru jana yibbariṁdalū ·i grāmada caturthāṁśava-

nu | ·ā gōtrada ·ā sūtrada sōmanāthasōmayājigaḷa makkaḷu sōmaṇasōmayāji-
gaḷiṁ-
ḷa makkaḷu tiṁmappa ·aṁjanaiyya jana yibariṁdalū ·i grāmada caturthāṁśava-

llū nānūru ghaṭṭivarahānū sallisi koṭṭu grā-
mada pūrvada tā(ṁ)mraśāsana sahavāgi krayavanū tekkoṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 588.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1912.)
IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is of the same date and reign as No. 587. It records that Penugoṇḍe Vira-
paṇṇayya purchased from certain Brāhmans (named) half of the village Sadāśivapura,
surnamed Dēvarāyapura for varāhas 108 and made a gift of it for the service of the
god Vīrabhadra of Lēpākshi. The village is stated to have been granted to Dēvara-
Sōmayāji by king Prauḍha-Dēva-Maharāya.

l.ka: ruṣaṁgaḷu 1460 neya viḷaṁbisaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa ba 10 sō dallū
bēlūra cāvaḍage saluva deppaṟu sthaḷadalli ·i prauḍhadēvamahārāyariṁdalū ·i dēva-
ṇayyanavaru krayamūlya dravya ghaṭṭivaraha ga 108 ·akṣāradallū nūrayeṁṭu varahakke
krayavanū tekoṁḍu yo vīrēśvaradēvara ·amṛtapaḍi naivēdya muṁtāda vaibhavaṁga-
ḷige dhāreneradu koṭṭa sadāśivapura ·ardhagrāmada śāsanada prati ||

inscription.head:
No. 589.
(A.R. No. 704 of 1922.)
ON THE NORTH GOPURA OF THE VITTHALASVAMI TEMPLE AT HAMPI,
HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1460, Viḷambi, Bhādrapada, ba. 10, corresponding to A.D.
1538 September 18, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahā-
rāya. It records that Rāghavappa-Nāyaka, son of Uddi Timmappa-Nāyaka, and
brother of Chapparada Timmappa-Nāyaka put up a pinnacle on the north gōpura of
the temple of Viṭhaladēva.

l.ka: rāyarige pu(ṁ)ṇyavāgi śrīviṭhaladēvara baḍagaṇa gōpurake ·uddi tiṁmappanāyakara ma-

ga rāyara caparada tiṁmappanāyakara ta(ṁ)ma caparada raghavappanāyakaru ·acutarāya-
mahā-
rāyarige puṇyavāgi māḍida kaḷa[śa]da sēveya śāsanaṁ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 590.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1912.)
ON THE WALL OF THE VERANDAH ROUND THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT
LEPAKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1460, Viḷambi, Mārgaśira, ba. 3, Sō[mavāra] corresponding to
A.D. 1538 December 8, Sunday (not Monday), in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that Penugoṇḍe Virapaṇṇayya along with his brother Vīraṇa purchased
some vṛitti lands from certain Brāhmans and made a gift of them for the service of
the god Vīrēśvara of Lēpākshi. The vṛittis are stated to have been situated in
Nagaragere, surnamed Kṛishṇarāyasamudra, which was situated in Tumbekalla-sthala
in Rodda-nāḍu, a sub-division of Penugoṇḍe-rājya.

l.ka: saṁvatsarada mārgaśira ba 3 sōdallū śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājapara[mēśvara]
ḍerājya rodanāḍu tuṁbekalla sthaḷada nagaragerege pratināmavāda kṛṣṇa-
rū ·ā[tana] taṁma vīraṇanāyakarū krayadānavāgi tekoṁḍu samarpisida vṛttigaḷa vivarā

kuppaṇarsariṁda vṛtti 1 kke kraya varaha ga 150 nūra·ayivattu varaha kālabhāvagōtrada
ā-
pastaṁbhasūtrada yajuśākheya malagūra rāmarsara makkaḷu śaṁkarayana kaiyyalu
vṛttiṁ || ·ardhake kraya varaha ga 75 yappattayidu varaha jāmadagñāvatsagōtra-
da bōdhāyanasūtrada yajuśākheya gaṁgādharadīkṣitara makkaḷu kṛṣṇabhaṭṭara kailū
vṛttiṁ || ·ardhake kraya varaha ga 75 yappattayidu varaha | ·ā gōtrada ·ā sūtrada
llū vṛttiṁ || ·ardhakaṁ kraya varaha ga 75 yappattayidu varaha [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 591.
(A.R. No. 794 of 1917.)
ON A BOULDER UNDER THE TANK-BUND TO THE SOUTH-EAST OF THE VILLAGE,
BUDILI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1459, Viḷambi, Māgha, śu. 2, Bu[dhavāra] corres-
ponding to A.D. 1539 January 22, Wednesday, in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahā-
rāya. It records that Chikka-Narasappayya, aḷiya (son-in-law) of Ananta-Ayya,
repaired the tank at Būdali and, purchasing some land under the same tank, made a
gift of it for the service of the god Kēśavadēva of the village, consecrated at the time
of Narasaṇa-Nāyaka.

l.ka: gaḷu 1456 neya viḷaṁbisaṁvatsarada māgha su 2 bu śrīmatu rājādhirāja rājaparamēsvara

·anaṁta·ayyanavara ·aḷiya cikkanarasappayyanavarū bhūdaliya kēśavadēvarige krayakke koṁ-
ḍu koṭṭa

dharmaśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare prāku narasaṇanāyaka·ayyanavara[kāladalli] kaṭisida narasa-
yana • • •
• būdaliya kēśavadēvarige kuṁṭeyaliha mūḍa gaddeyaṁ dēvara nayivēdyake ·aṁgaraṁ-
kaṭisi ·ā kuṁṭeya keḷage hostāgi ·acukaṭṭanū kaṭṭisi dēvara nayi-
pūrvadali naḍava dē va ga 20 hoṁnu

inscription.head:
No. 592.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1910.)
ON A SLAB SET UP TO THE EAST OF THE BASAVESVARA TEMPLE
AT KUNTHURU, KOLLEGAL TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is damaged and dated Śaka 14[61], • • • Śrāvaṇa, śu. 2, Guruvāra,
corresponding to A.D. 1539 July 17 Thursday in the reign of Achyuta-Mahārāya. It
records the gift of certain incomes from the village Kuntūru belonging to Sālūra-
Naṁjeyadēva made by Rāmābhaṭṭayya, an officer of the king. The gift was made for
his own merit in the presence of the god Virūpākshadēva at Paṁpākshētra.

l.ka: ḍalāgi ·ā saṁbaḷa sarati kāṇike bēḍige varu birāḍa ·āvu-
ṁdu dhāreyaneradu koṭṭa silāsādhana || śrī || svada-
ttā[ṁ*] paradattāṁ vā yō harēti vasundharā[ṁ ||*] ṣaṣṭivaruṣasahasrā-
ṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi[ḥ ||*] yo dharmmakke ·āvanānu

inscription.head:
No. 593.
(A.R. No. 302 of 1925.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED NEAR THE TANK-BUND OF CHIKKAKERIYAGINAHALLI,
KUDLIGI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1461, Vikāri, Bhādrapada, śu. 12, corresponding to A.D. 1539
August 26, Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya. It records the
construction of the tank Hiriya-Lakkasamudra by Bayakāṟa Rāmappayya in the
name of his mother Hiriya-Lakkarasamma. It states that Rāmaya, who was the son
of Pedda-Timmaya and Lakkāmbikā, and grandson of Rāma-mantri and Telagamāmbā
and great grandson of Bhāvaya-mantri, who was the son of Śiṅgaya, was the
chief of Koṇḍavīḍu division. He constructed the agrahāras, Tīmmāpura,
Chinna-Timmāpura and Maṅgimāvinahaḷḷi, and constructing a number of temples in
Pedda-Timmāpura, set up the gods Gōpinātha, Bāchēśvara, Kṛishṇēśa, Ammēśa,
Mallikārjuna, Vīrabhadra and Viśvanātha. He made a grant of the village Hoḷagundi
for the service of these gods. Having constructed Achyutammāpura after the name
of his daughter, Achyutamma, he set up the god Kṛishṇa in a temple of his own
construction there; he also caused the construction of the tanks, Achyutamma-
samudra, Bāchasamudra, Rāmasamudra, Akkasamudra, Kāmasamudra, Amma-
samudra, Vīrasamudra, Achyutēndrasamudra, Veṅkatēndrasamudra, Pina-Lakka-
samudra, Chinna-Tippasamudra, Achutammāsamudra, Pedda-Lakkasamudra,
Liṅgālayataṭāka, Venkaṭayyataṭāka, Peda-Timmasamudra and China-Bāchasamudra;
thus he constructed on the whole sixteen tanks. He is also stated to have written a
work on music. His uncles were Timma-mantrī, Sūrīndra, Bhāskara-Chamūpati and
Bhāvaya, all holding high offices under the king. The latter portion describing his
charities is written in Sanskrit language.

l.ka: 1461 neya vikārisaṁvatsarada bhādrapada śu 12 yalu śrī-
matu bayakāṟa rāmappayyanavaru taṁma tāyi hiriyalakha-
trī kṛtī tasyāsītsahadharmiṇī telugamā gaṁgēva dugdhāṁbadhē[ḥ ||*] rāmāṁbudhi-
thābhidaṁ dēvaṁ tatra nidhāya sarvamaśiṣatsēnāṁdhradēśasthalīṁ [||*] tasyāgrajanmā pedatiṁ-
ṁmayēṁdrō lakkāṁbikā tasya bhabhūva patnī | tayōstapasyāparipākamūrtirvi-
cāpi vikārināmani nabhōmāsē sa rāmaprabhu [||*]rgrāmaṁ kṣullakakīḍahaḷḷisahi-
gārāṇyanēkaśaḥ | tēṣu kramātpratiṣṭhāpya gōpīnāthābhidhaṁ hariṁ [||*] bācēśvaraṁ
ca kṛṣṇēśamammēśaṁ mallikārjunaṁ | viśvanāthaṁ ca dēvēśaṁ vīrabhadraṁ jagatpati [ṁ ||]
·acyutaṁmāpuragrāmē dēvāgāraṁ vidhāya ca | tatra kṛṣṇaṁ pratiṣṭhāpya tasyā[ḥ]
rēpi ca | ·acyutaṁmāpurē caiva rāmayāmātyaśēkharaḥ [||*] ramyaṁ bācasamudraṁ ca tathā
rāmasamudrakaṁ | khyātamakkasamudraṁ ca kāntaṁ kāmasamudrakaṁ [||*] kṛdyama[ṁ]-
masamudraṁ ca varaṁ vīrasamudrakaṁ | ·acutēṁdrasamudraṁ ca vēṁkaṭēṁdrasa-
mudrakaṁ | pinalakkasamudraṁ ca cinna[ti]ppasamudrakaṁ | ·acataṁmāsamu[dra]ṁ ca pe-
draṁ ca cinabācasamudrakaṁ [||*] ta[ṭā]kān vividhāṁścakrē vikhyātāniti ṣōḍaśa | ·āpuri-

inscription.head:
No. 594.
(A.R. No. 308 of 1925.)
ON A SLAB LYING BY THE SIDE OF THE WALL OF THE GARBHAGRIHA OF THE
SIDDHESVARA TEMPLE AT UJJINI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka, 1461, Vikṛiti, Bhādrapada, śu. 12, Maṅgaḷavāra,
corresponding to A.D. 1539 August 26, Tuesday. It records the gift of ānandanidhi
made by king Achyutarāya-Mahārāya.

l.ka: kavaruśa 1461 neya vik® • • • rada bhādrapada śu-

dvādaśyabhikhyē tithau | vārē bhūmisutasya [viṣṇvadhipatau] tārēcyutakṣmāpatiḥ kṛtvā-
naṁda-

inscription.head:
No. 595.
(A.R. No. 695 of 1922.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE MANDAPA IN THE PATTABHIRAMA TEMPLE AT
KAMALAPURAM, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1461, Vikāri, Pushya, śu. 1, corresponding to
A.D. 1539 December 11 Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahā-
rāya. It records that Achyutarāya-Mallapaṇṇa made a gift of the toll-revenue on
garden produce amounting to varāhas 1050 in Doravadi-Sōmalāpura, a mānya village
belonging to the god Raghunāthadēva in Vardādēvi-Ammana-paṭṭana, for the service
of the god.

l.ka:
• • • sahita koṭaru • yiṁti-
vu nīu niṁma

inscription.head:
No. 596.
(A.R. No. 176 of 1910.)
ON A SECOND SLAB LYING NEAR A WALL IN THE CENTRE OF ERAGANAHALLI,
GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1462, Vikāri, Pushya, ba. 5, corresponding to A.D.
1539 December 29, Monday (not verifiable), in the reign of Achyutarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that the Pradhāna Mallapayya and Kāḷapa made a gift of land to
Brāhmaṇas and Jaṅgamadēvas in the presence of the god Nañjuṇḍēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 597.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP ON THE TANK-BUND AT TAMMADAHALLI, MADAKSIRA
TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1463, Plava, Kārtika, ba. 2, Śanivāra, corresponding to A.D.
1541 November, 4, Saturday, in the reign of Achyutadēva-Mahārāya. It records that
the tank of the village Tammaḍihaḷḷi having breached at three places, Chenna-Gauṇḍa
and Timma-Gauṇḍa repaired the breaches at their own cost and that they were
granted kaṭṭugoḍagi land for perpetual enjoyment by Tipparāja Mahā-arasu, agent
of Veṅkaṭādri-Nāyaka.

l.ka: maḍihaḷḷi keṟe mūru vaḍavāgi vaḍedu khilavāgi yiralāgi | veṁka-
gōṁḍanavaru yī vaḍadu khilavāda kereyanu taṁma kaya ho(ṁ)-
kaṭṭugoḍagige sarvamānyavāgi koṭṭa gadde kha 1 khaṁḍuga
da staḷadalū marada gade kha 1 ·ā khaṁḍuga ga-
taṁde tāya vāraṇāsili koṁda pāpake hōguvaru
gōva koṁda pāpake hōdavaru nāya māṁsa

inscription.head:
No. 598.
(A.R. No. 715 of 1922.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE NORTH GOPURA OF THE ACHYUTANARAYANASVAMI TEMPLE
AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This undated inscription consists of a Sanskrit verse in praise of king
Achyutēndra and of four of the usual imprecatory verses.

l.ka: śōbhanamastu [||*] sa jayati narapālō ratnasiṁhāsanastō vijeyanagaravāsī kīrtipū-

dānapālaneyōrmadhyē dānātsrēyōnupālanaṁ | dānāsvargamavāpnōti pāla-
nādacyutaṁ padaṁ [||*] svadattādviguṇaṁ pu(ṁ)ṇyaṁ paradattānu-

pālanaṁ | paradattāpahārēṇa svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavēt [||*] svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō
harēti vasuṁdharā[ṁ] | ṣaṣṭirvaruṣasahasrāṇi

viṣṭhāyāṁ jyāyatē krimi[ḥ] [||*] yēkayiva bhaginī lōkē sarvēśāmēva bhūbhujāṁ
na bhōjyāna karagrāhya dēvadattā vasuṁdharā [||*]

sāmā(ṁ)nyōyaṁ dharmasētuṁ nrupāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanīyō bhavadbhiḥ |
sarvānētē bhāvinaḥ pārṭhivēṁdrā(ṁ)n bhūyō

inscription.head:
No. 599.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1913.)
ON THE NANDI-STONE IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT CHITIKINAHALU,
BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is not dated. It records that king Achyutarāya-Mahārāya made a gift of
the village, Chiṭikanahāḷu, for offerings to the god Prasanna-Virūpāksha at
Jentegal. The gift was made for the merit of his father Narasṇṇa-Nāyaka and was
recorded on a copper-plate. Liṅgamudre stones were set up on the boundaries of
the village.

inscription.head:
No. 600.
(A.R. No. 444 of 1920.)
ON A SLAB IN FRONT OF THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT VIDAPANAKALLU,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is very much damaged and the date is lost. It refers itself to the reign of
Achyutadeva-Mahārāya and seems to lay down the method of regulating taxes at
Viḍupanaka[llu].

inscription.head:
No. 601.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1913.)
ON A STONE NEAR A WELL AT RUPANAGUDI, BELLARY TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and not dated. It refers to the Vijayanagara king
Achyutadēva-Mahārāya and states that some lands in the village Yāḷape, together
with a fixed amount of toll-revenue on betel leaves, grains and the like in the ṭhāṇya
of Rūpalaguḍi were granted for the service of the god [Nārā]yaṇadēva.

l.note: Some lines are broken away both at the beginning and the end.

l.ka: da yāḷapeya grāmadalu • • • [ha]ḷake mūḍalu bījavari kari-
ya hola kha 1 || 0 mūvattu • • • da bījavariya holaṁnu
ha mūlavīsavanu yāḷape • • [hu]ṇise voḷage gaurāya basa-

l.note: Some lines are broken away both at the beginning and the end.

inscription.head:
No. 602.
(A.R. No. 262 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT MUSUVANA-KALLAHALLI,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This inscription, which is in Sanskrit, is damaged and undated. It states that a
certain Aṇṇāji who is described as a favourite of the (Vijayanagara) king Achyuta
made a grant of the village Kallahaḷḷi to Siṅgā-Bhaṭṭa, son of Liṅgā-Bhaṭṭa of Bhāra-
dvāja-gōtra, Āpastamba-sūtra and Yajuś-śākhā.

l.ka: bhujāṁ | na bhōjyā na karagrāhyā vipradattā

inscription.head:
No. 603.
(A.R. No. 390 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD AT MANIYUR, A HAMLET OF YELLURU,
UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is very much damaged and dated Śaka 1464, Śubhakṛit, Āshāḍha, ba •
(= A.D. 1542). The name of the king is lost. It mentions Paṇḍaridēva Voḍeya,
governor of Bārakūru and the temple at Yelūru.

inscription.head:
No. 604.
(A.R. No. 554 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE EASTERN WALL OF KANUMA-DODDAYYA'S
TEMPLE NEAR KOTAKALU, ADONI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 14[6]5, Śubhakṛit, Kārtika, śu. 11 corresponding
to A.D. 1542 October 19 Thursday (not verfiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-
Mahārāya. It records that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kōnēṭi-Timmayadēva-Mahā-
arasu, (son of) Rāmarāja, made a gift of gold out of the incomes of bēḍike, birāḍa
and the like in the village Kōṭeyakallu situated in Ādavāni-vaḷita which had been
granted to him by the king for nāyakatana, and a plot of land, for the service of the
god Hanumantadēva at Kōṭeyakallu and for the tīrtha-chhatra. The gift was made
for the merit of the king and for the attainment of Vaikuṇṭha by the donor's ancestors.
Ādavāni-vaḷita is stated to have been a division of Hastināvati in the Mūḍa-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 605.
(A.R. No. 800 of 1917.)
ON A BOULDER NEAR THE DESERTED CHANNAKESAVASVAMI TEMPLE AT
KONDAPURAM, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1465, Śōbhakṛit, Vaiśākha, śu. 15, corresponding to A.D. 1543
April 19, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya. It
records that Daḷavāyi Kṛishṇappa-Nāyaka, son of Daḷavāyi Channama-Nāyaka, the
Kāryakarta of the king, made a gift of the village, Chernūru, surnamed Channakēśava-
pura, for the service of the god Channa-Kēśavadēva, with the prayer that the god may
be pleased to bestow on Sadāśivadēva-Mahāraya the throne of the Vijayanagara
kingdom permanently. The village is stated to have been situated in the Penugoṇḍe-
rājya.

l.ka: tadupari vartamānake saluva śōbhakrutusaṁvatsarada vaiśākha suddha 15 lu śrīma(ṁ)n-

mahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīraśrīsadāsivadēvamahārāyaru
ratnasiṁhvāsanārūḍharāgi prutivīstirasāṁbrajya geyuta yiralu

pūrva śrīvyāsapratiṣṭhevādanaṁtaradalu janamējayana jīrnnōddhāravāda penugoṁḍeya
yaramuṁciya bāgilu śrīcaṁnakēśavadēvarige śrīsādasivadēvamahārāyara kāryyake

karttarāda daḷavāyi caṁnnamanāyakara makkaḷu daḷavāyi kruṣṭapanāya[ka]rū śrī-
sadāśivadēvamahārāyarige vijayanagarada ratnasiṁhāsanada stirasāṁbrājya pa-

ṭavāgabēkeṁdu dēvatāprārthaneya māḍikoṁḍa saṁmaṁdhasvāmi namage naykatanake pāllista-
penugoṁḍaya rājyake saluva bīḍu grāmagaḷoḷagaṇa cernūra grāmakke pratināma-
vāda

śrīca(ṁ)nna kēśavapūraveṁba sarvvamā(ṁ)nyada grāmavanū ·adake saluva kīḷuvaḷi ·ā catusī-
meyoḷagaṇa haḷi paḷi muṁttāda ·aṣṭabhōgatējasvamya sayitavāgi

penagoṁḍeya yaramaṁcibāgila śrīca(ṁ)nna kēśavadēvarige daḷavāyi cennanaykara makkaḷū
daḷavāyi kruṣṇappanāykarū yī tathātithiyalū śrīsadāśivadēmahā-

rāyarige dharmmavāgi sahiraṇyōdakadānadhārapūrvakavāgi dhāreyaneredu koṭa cernura
grāmakke pratināmavāda śrīce(ṁ)nna kēśavapuraveṁba sarvamānyada

grāmakke saluva haḷi paḷi kere kūṁṭe kāluve yēnuṁṭāda sakalasvāmyavanu dēvara
amṛtapaḍi muṁtāda sakala vayibhavagaḷanū ·ācaṁdrārkkasthayigaḷāgi naḍe-

yabēkeṁdu śrīsadāśivadēvamahārāyarige puṇyavāgi daḷavāyi ce(ṁ)nnanāykara
makkaḷu daḷavāyi kruṣṇappanāykaru yaramaṁciya bāgila śrīce(ṁ)nnakēśavadēvarige
samarpista cernura grāmake

pratināmavāda śrīce(ṁ)nnakēśavapuraveṁba sarvamānyada grāmavu nijabhaktisēvakāpāraṁpari-
vāgi ·ācaṁdrārkkāstayi ·āgi naḍeyaluḷadū ·eṁdū samarppista gramada dānapatra-
śāsana |

dānapālaneyōrmadhyē dānayēyōnrupālanaṁ dānāt svargamavāpnōti | pālanā-
dacutaṁ padaṁ [||*] svadattādviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ paradattāpahārēṇa
svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavēt [||*]

svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā jō harēti vasuṁdharāṁ | svaṣṭirvvaruśasahasrāṇi vriṣṭhayāṁ jāya-
tē krimi[ḥ*] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 606.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE MANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE UNDALI SANGAMMANAGUDI AT
HALUGODE, BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1466, Śōbhakṛit, Dvitīya-Śrāvaṇa, śu. 10, corre-
sponding to A.D. 1543 August 9, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśiva-
Mahārāya. It records that the village, Chenakuṇṭe, granted in the reign of
Kṛisṇarāya-Mahārāya for the service of god Saṅgamēśvara of Kurugōḍu being
resumed, Liṅgarāja, son-in-law of Guṇḍaya-Mahārāya, agent of the king, revived the
gift of Chenakuṇṭe and its gardens for the same service.

l.ka: va grāma haḷagi khilivagi yi[ra*]lāgi sadaśivarayara karyakarttara gu-
cenakuṁṭe grāma tōṭa hasaru mānya sahavāgi

inscription.head:
No. 607.
(A.R. No. 707 of 1922.)
ON THE SOUTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE VITTHALASVAMI
TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1465, Śōbhakṛit, Bhādrapada, śu. 12, corresponding to A.D.
1543 September 10, Monday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that the village Niṭūru in Tekkalakōṭe-sīme and some fields and a garden
at Ānegondi granted by the king to Tiruveṅgaḷāchārya, son of Tirumala-Avuku-
Tātāchārya of Śaṭhamarshaṇa-gōtra Āpastamba-sūtra and Yajuś-śākhā were granted
for offerings to the god Viṭṭhaladēva.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrījayādbhudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruśa 1465 neya śōbhakṛtusaṁva-
charada bhādrapada śu | 12 rallū śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīra-
