samarpisida dharmmaśāśana yo dharmmakke ·āru tappidaru śrīviṭhaladēvara śrīpādake tapidavaru ||

inscription.head:
No. 608.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1913.)
ON A ROCK NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT YALPI, BELLARY TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1465, Śōbhakṛit, Phālguṇa, śu. 10, corresponding
to A.D. 1544 March 4, Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that the Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Rāmarājayyadēva-Mahā-arasu, agent to the
king, granted exemption to the barbers of Yaḷappeya-sīme from siddhāya, teṟige,
biṭṭi, birāḍa and other taxes.

l.ka: śōbhakrutusaṁvatsarada phālguṇa śu 10 lu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamē-
śvara śrīvīrapratāpa sadāsivadēvamahārāyara kāryake kartarādaṁthā śrīma • • •

maṁḍalēśvara rāmarājayyadēva mahāarasu[gaḷu] yaḷappeya sīmeya nāyiṁda vō
ja-
gaḷu teruva grāmada kaṭṭu siddhāya terige biṭṭu birāḍa ·a(ṁ)nnu pa(ṁ)nnu yēnuṁṭāda
teṟuvudanellavanū sarvamānyavāgi pālisibiṭṭe·ū ·ārobbaru tappi sidhāya terige-
ya rokkavanu koṁḍare ·ā nāyiṁdarige huṭṭidavaru ·eṁdu baresida śilāśāsana ||

inscription.head:
No. 609.
(A.R. No. 398 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE TEMPLE OF SANKARANARAYANA AT
SANKARANARAYANA, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 14[66], Krōdhi, Chaitra, śu. 6, Śukravāra corre-
sponding to A.D. 1544 March 28, Friday, f.d.t. ·74, in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-
Mahārāya. It records that Dabugeṟe-Voḍeya, disciple of Chenna-Bomma-Voḍeya of
Meṟuvumāye, purchased some plots of land and made a gift of them for the service
of the god Śaṅkaranārāyaṇadēva during the regime of Chennayarasaṇṇa.

l.ka:
śālivāhanaśakavaruṣa 14[66] krōdhisaṁvatsarada cayitra śu 6 śukravāradalu śrīmanmahā-
rājādhirāja rājaparamēśvaraśrī

vīrapratāpa ·uttara dakṣiṇa pūrva paścima catusamudrādhi[pati*] vīrasadāśivarāya
mahārāyaru
vijayanagariya siṁhāsanavanu pratipālisi barutiha samayadali śrīmatu saka[lagu]ṇa-
saṁpaṁnarāda ceṁnayarasaṁṇanavaru taṁma rājyava pratipālisuttiha samayadali śrīma(ṁ)
yaru samarpisida dharmaśāsanada paṭeya vakhaṇeya kramaveṁteṁdare nānu ceṁ-
vivara śaṁkaranārāyaṇadēvara paḍuva bhāgadalli berikeya bhūmiya catusīmeya viva-

inscription.head:
No. 610.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1910.)
ON A SLAB SET UP TO THE EAST OF THE BASAVESVARA TEMPLE AT KUNTHURU,
KOLLEGAL TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1467, Krōdhi, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15 corresponding to A.D.
1544 August 3, Sunday (not verifiable). When the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Nārayyarājayya
of Nandiyāla (a feudatory of the Vijayanagara kings), was ruling over Hadināḍu-sīme,
his agent Kampāraṇṇa is stated to have made a gift to Kempanañjēdēva, head of the
Mahānandīśvara-maṭha. The gift was made for the merit of Timmarājayya,
Nāriyapparājayya and Avubhaḷarājayya.

l.ka: baḷa sarati ·āgi mūvattu varahanu tekoṁḍu baruttiralā-
gi ·ā kuṁtūra siṁhāsanada mahānaṁdīśvara siṁhāsana pu-
ṇyakṣētradalli māḍida dharma ·ā mahā naṁdīsvaramaṭada ma-
nu brāhmaṇahatyava māḍida pāpakke hōhanū | svadattaṁ
rēṇa svadattaṁ ni(ḥ)ṣpalaṁ bhavēt [||*] svadattāṁ vā paradattāṁ vā(ṁ)
jāyatē krimo || na viṣaṁ viṣamatyāhuḥ dēvasvaṁ viṣamu-

inscription.head:
No. 611.
(A.R. No. 451 of 1923.)
ON A SLAB PRESERVED IN THE TEMPLE OF HANUMAN AT MURADI,
RAYADRUG TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1467, Krōdhi, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 7, Śanivāra corresponding to A.D.
1544 August 9, Saturday, in the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya. It records that, under
the orders of the king, Rāmarājaya granted an order to his barber Koṇḍōja, exempting
the barbers from the tax siddhāya.

l.ka: śakavarṣa 1467 krōdhisaṁvatsarada śrāva ba 7 śa lū śrīma-
na ·appaṇe pālisi taṁma kelasanāyiṁda koṁḍōjage taṁma
sīmeya mēlaṇa nāyaṁdarige sidhāya kaṭṭaḷeya sarva[mānya]-

inscription.head:
No. 612.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT AMARAPURAM,
MADAKSIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Kārtika, śu. 1[0], Sōmavāra
corresponding to A.D. 1544 November 10, Monday, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-
Mahārāya. It records that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarājaya-Viṭhalēśvaradēva-
Mahā-arasu granted exemption from such taxes as teṟe, kāṇike and biṭṭi to the
barbers. Koṇḍōja is mentioned.

l.ka: nū nāu nimage biṭṭu nimage sarvamānya-

inscription.head:
No. 613.
(A.R. No. 770 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT PULAMITI,
HINDUPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Kārtika, śu. 10, corresponding
to A.D. 1544 November 10, Monday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-
Mahārāya. It records that Rāmarāja-Arasu granted exemption from taxes such
as terige and kāṇike to the barbers, under the orders of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 614.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP OUTSIDE THE TEMPLE OF VIRABHADRA AT NARASAPURAM,
KALYANADRUG TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1467, Krōdhi, Pushya, śu. 1[0] corresponding
to A.D. 1544 December 23, Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-
Mahārāya. It records the exemption from taxes granted under the orders of
Rāmarājayya to the barbers of Narasāpura by the Mahānāyaṅkāchārya Koṇḍama
Nāyaka of Kundurpe.

inscription.head:
No. 615.
(A.R. No. 723 of 1919.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE POTAPPAN TEMPLE AT KURUKUNDI,
ALUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Māgha, ba. 11 corresponding to A.D. 1545
February 7, Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya. It records that
the king issued orders exempting the barbers of Kuṟukundi from biṭṭi, birāḍa and
other taxes. Koṇḍōja and Bharmōja, the sons of Timmōja of Kandanavōlu are
stated to have been instrumental in getting the exemption.

inscription.head:
No. 616.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1904.)
ON THE SOUTH BASE OF THE MANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
OF THE VITTHALASVAMI TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Phālguṇa, śu. 1 corresponding to A.D. 1545
February 12, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēvarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that Kōnēṭi-Timmarāja, son of Koṇḍarāja, made a gift of the toll-revenue
raised in Ṟavuḍūr for offerings to the god Viṭṭhaladēva on the day of Ratha-
saptamī. The gift was made for the merit of Koṇḍarāja.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaṣakavaruśa 1466 neya krōdhisaṁvatsarada
phālguṇa śu 1 lu śrīmatu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa
sadāśivadēvarāyamahārāyaru pruthvīrājyaṁ geyi·utaṁ yiralu koṁḍarājagaḷa
makkaḷu konēṭi tiṁmarājayanavaru naṁma taṁdegaḷu koṁḍarājagaḷige pu(ṁ)ṇyavāga
bēkū yeṁdu rathasaptamo pu(ṁ)ṇyada kāladalli

śrīviṭhaladēvara pulivaṭṭu naivēdyakke haṭivaḷiya sīmeyoḷage śrīviṭhaladēvara ·amruta-
paḍige saluva ṟauḍūra grāmada staḷa suṁka grāmada prajegaḷu koḍuva ·uḍugoṟeya
rokka gāṇa mara ·aṁgaḍi paṭakāṟaru koḍuva roka ·ī grāmada staḷa suṁkake saluva
ēnuṁṭāda tējōpārjaneyanu nānu śrīviṭaladēvarige sarvamā(ṁ)nyavāgi samarpi
stenū yidakke dinakke naivēdyakke naḍava kaṭaḷe ·aki ¥

hesaru ¥ = ·upu meṇasu ¥ = saṁbhāra śē ¥ || mēlōgara ¥ = tupa ¥ = mosaru
¥ = ·aḍugabu ¥ || ·aḍike • • lekhadalū naḍeyaluḷadū yaṁdu barasida dharma-
śāsana ||

inscription.head:
No. 617.
(A.R. No. 456 of 1923.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BEFORE THE GOPURA OF THE SVAYAMBHUDEVA TEMPLE
AT KANEKKAL, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1467, Viśvāvasu, [Jyē]shṭha, śu. 11, Śukravāra
corresponding to A.D. 1545 May 22, Friday. The name of the king is lost. It mentions
Timmarāja, Timmaṇṇa, Mudaṇa, and Basavarāja, the gods Vīrabhadradēva,
Rāmadēva and Raṅganāthadēva and the village Kaṇeyakallu.

inscription.head:
No. 618.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1904.)
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE RUINED TEMPLE AT HALUGODE NEAR KURUGODU,
BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1467, Viśvāvasu, Pushya, ba. 10, Sōmavāra corresponding to
A.D. 1545 December 28, Monday, in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya. It records
that Rāmarājaya, elder brother of Aḷiya Liṅgarājadēva, and the grandson
of the Mahāmaṇḍalēsvara Koṭagāra Rāmarāja-Voḍeya, made a grant of land for
the service of the god Jinnadēva in the basti at Kuṟigōḍu. The gift was made for the
merit of his father Mallarāja-Voḍeya, with the consent of the Gauḍa, Sēnabōva and
the Paṭṭaṇasvāmis. It also records that in Phālguṇa śu. 8, Sōmavara of the same
year corresponding to A.D. 1546, February 8, Monday, Gōmiseṭṭi son of Sūriyappaseṭṭi
of the Mūla-saṅgha and Balātkāra-gaṇa repaired, along with Padmarasa-Paṇḍiṭa,
the same temple.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrījayādyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣa 1463 neya viśvāvasusaṁ[va]-
tsarada puṣya ba 10 sō lu śrīma(ṁ)nmahādēvadēvōttama

kuṟigōḍa bastiya jiṁnadēvarige dharmamahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrāpratā-
pa śrīvīrasadāśivarāyamahārāyaru pṛthvīrājyaṁ-

geyuta yiralu śrīma(ṁ)nmahāmaṇḍalēśvara koṭagāra rāmarājavoḍeyara moṁmakkaḷu
aḷiya liṁgarājayadēva mahāarasuga-

ḷa ·aṁṇaṁdiru rāmarājayagaḷu taṁma taṁde mallarājavoḍeyarige pu(ṁ)ṇyavāgabēku yeṁdu
dēvara ·amrutapaḍinaivēdyakke

koṭa hola keḷahuṇasēmarada staḷadalu maṁgajīyanavara hōbaḷi [pa]ṭṭavaḷiyavaru mā
ḍuva ·ereyastaḷaṁ va 1 ·idara

holavanu hoṁdida maṁgajīya nīrastaḷada • • bitta bījava 2 ·ubhayaṁ hola bīja
va 3 tōṭa ·ūrige mūḍalu ·uppina

4 ·akṣāradalu nāguḷuda bījavari-

yanu jiṁnadēvara ·amrutapaḍige kuṟugōḍagauḍa sēnabōva paṭaṇasvāmigaḷa ·anumatadiṁda
koṭe·u ·āgi ·ācaṁ-

drarkastayavāgi dēvara ·amrutapaḍi nayivēdyake ·anubhavisabahudu yi dha(ṁ)rmakke ·ārūbaru
tapidarū ta(ṁ)mma taṁde tāyi kāśiyyali koṁda pāta-

kakke hōharu gōbrā(ṁ)hmara koṁda pātakakke hōharu ·eṁdu koṭa dharmmada śilāśāsana
sarvamānya [||*] śrīmūlasaṁgha balātkaragaṇada kaṁmevaisya-

kulōttama sūriyappaseṭṭiyara maga [gōmi]seṭi tatsaṁvatsarada pālguṇa śudha 2 sō lu
jīrṇōddhāraṇeya māḍisi samasta srāvakarige pu(ṁ)ṇyavāgabēkeṁ-
du māḍisida jinaśāsana śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 619.
(A.R. No. 437 of 1923.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BEFORE THE TEMPLE OF HANUMAN AT BAGINAYAKANAHALLI,
RAYADRUG TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka • • • • Parābhava, Chaitra, śu. 10, corresponding
to A.D. 1546 March 12 Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that Vīraṇa-Nāyaka, the agent to the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-
Viṭhalayadēva-Mahā-arasu, made a kaṭṭu-mānya grant of some fields to five persons
for constructing the ruined tank in Bāginā[yakanahaḷḷi].

l.ka: ḷu • • • neya parābhavasaṁvatsarada cayitra śu 10 lu śrī-
nāgireḍi bāvi muddaṇagauḍa saha jana 5 ke koṭa kaṭṭiya ka-
ṭṭumānyakaṁ nele ·uttara • • • • • purada hola mēre hoṁ
di kha | 1 gade kha | 1 keṟekeḷagaṇa gade kha 1 ke dasavaṁda ·āgi

inscription.head:
No. 620.
(A.R. No. 394 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A HOUSE-SITE TO THE SOUTH OF HALAVANAKERE AT BASRUR,
COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1468, Parābhava, Kārtika, śu, 1 corresponding to A.D. 1546
October 25, Monday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya. It records
that, when Achcha-Voḍeya was ruling over Bārakūra-rājya under the orders of the
king, Santāna-Śaṅkaraliṅgaseṭṭi had built a charity-house and granted certain lands
and a tank for the worship of the god Viṭhaladēva and when in the course of time
the charity-house had fallen down, Kuppaṇaseṭṭi-Udare-Sūriseṭṭi got it restored and
endowed it with land and money. It also registers other grants made by some
others for properly conducting the same charity.

l.ka:
ṁ śrīgaṇādhipatayē(ṁ)namaḥ | śrīsarasvatyāeṁnamaḥ | śrīgurubhyō(ṁ) nama[ḥ] nirvighnama-
stu namastuṁgaśi[ra]-

ścuṁbicaṁdracāmaracāravē | trayilō[kya]nagarāraṁbhamulastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē || svasti śrī
jayābhyudaya-

śālivāhana śakavaruṣa 1468 saṁdu vartamāna parābhavasaṁvatsarada kārtika śu 1 dalū śrīma-
drājādhirāja

(rāja) rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa sadāśivamahārāyarū vijayanagariya siṁhvāsana-
dalū cittayisi sakala sāṁbrā-
jyavanu pratipālisuttiha kāladalū ·avara nirūpadiṁda ·accavoḍeyarū bārakūra rā-

bārakūru [pa]-

mu 3 maki mu 13 ·aṁtu bayalu maki saha mu 21 | 0 hakalu haḍu ·anubha [saha ·ā]-

ge ·aki mu 29 ge muvara dha(ṁ)rma naḍe·udu saṁtāna saṁkaraseṭiya maṭha naṣṭa-
ke biṭa bāḷu haṭiyaṁgaḍi voḷage kupaṇaseṭiya gaṇapaseṭi lōkaseṭiya kayali ·a-
rdhādiya māḍikoṁḍa bāḷa gaḍiya vivara muḍalu dugaṇarsana ·udinahaḷiyiṁ mu |
harida bacaliṁda ba | pa | kupaṇaseṭi gaḍi ·ayivatu haneyiṁda mu | ba | beṭṭinagadeyi-
ṁdaṁ teṁ | yīnāku gaḍivoḷage hērigade mu 4 malagade mu 1 || 0 matagade mu 1 || 0
teṁgina tōṭa ·ardha mane kaṇa saha ·ardha ·ādiya koṁḍu koṭa varaha ga 45 yo •
kke teru kaḷaci ·aki nāgaṁḍugadalu mu 10 mataṁ haṭiyaṁgaḍi voḷage ku-

yidanirisi tegedukoṁḍa vara ga 14 yībaḷi dēvasva māīladēvige battamu 1 ||| 0 kaḷaci maṭha-

gaṁḍugadalu mu 20 mataṁ ha[ṭi] kudurapaḍuva kumaravoḷage ·aṁṇauḍupara kayali

ṭa varahā ga 163 yi varahage kaṭida lābha nāgaṁḍugadalu mu 3 | yī ·akiyanu kupaṇase-

ṭi kaṭisida maṭhake dha(ṁ)rmake biṭadu yī ·akiyanu paḍuvakēriya halaru naḍesikoḍuvarū
[ā]
sarvasavanu halaru saṁkaraliṁgaseṭige [nā]yaru mūlava koṭṭaru yī ·akiya saṁka-
raliṁgaseṭi naḍasikoḍuvaṁtāgi halaru koṭa vokalu sātigēli maṭhake naḍaudu
mattaṁ sabilaḍiyoḷage kupaṇaseṭi viṣunāvaḍana maga śaṁkara nāḍanu ·avana kiri-
ya taṁde gōviṁdanāvaḍanu koṭa nāyara mula biḷumaramāḍaneṁba gade mu-
ḍi 4 haṁgāragadeyeṁ muṁ 3 sālagade mu ||| 0 malagade mu 1 | 0 ·ubhaya

koṁ vara ga 32 teru bāḷiyakēri hala ko ·akki mu 2 || 0 kaḷaci mu 8 yi ·aki
voḷage kupaṇaseṭi māraśami modayali biṭa dharma nāgaṁ mu 4 mataṁ
·udare suriyaseṭi dharmake baṭa bāḷina vivara teṁka bayala voḷage baḍaba-
ṭu gōviṁdahaṁmucumane bāḷu hagarugade mu 1 || 0 naligade mu 1 || 0 heṁ
gade voḍi mu | 5 kalanahaḷi mu | 5 nāyigade hāḷi mu | 5 hosagade hāḷi hane 5
siṁgāragade mu || 5 madamaḷahāḷi mu | 5 boḍuvahaḷi mu | 5 kaḷehaḷi mu | 5

[ga]de mu ||| 0 ·ajararahaḷi mu | 5 mane malagade korakegade ·ahaḷi saha mu 2 ha-

bayalu meke maki saha navaṁda mu 13 ||| 5 kūḍi hakalu ·anubha saha

mataṁ tirumaladēvarige ·udaThe sūriseṭi māḍi dharma herikudura hosa-
kaḷi bāḷa mēle naḍava ·akki mu 6 yidake kārtikamāsada sikharada huṁṇa-

tōṁṭada gaḍiyiṁ paḍuvalu tōṭa 1 mataṁ hāḷe kereya tōṁṭa mūḍalu
narenabaḷivara tōṭa paḍuvalu māriṭiya gaḍi baḍagalu teṁginatōṭa [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 621.
(A.R. No. 373 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE WEST SIDE OF THE VOLAGA-MANDAPA IN THE
KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1469, Parābhava, Kārtika, śu. 15, corresponding to A.D. 1546
November 7, Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya. It
records that the king had appointed Veṅkaṭādrirāja-Mahārāya-arasu to govern the
Bārakūra-rājya and that the latter placed Achchapa-Voḍeya in charge of the same.
Achchapa-Voḍeya is stated to have made a gift of varāhas 50 for the service of the
god Kōṭēsvara. The gift was made for the prosperity and long life of the king
and for the merit of Rāmarāja-Veṅkaṭādrirāja-Mahā-arasu.

The second part records that in Virōdhikṛit, Vaiśākha, śu. 5, corresponding
to A.D. 1551 April 11, Saturday, Ekadaḷakhāna-Voḍeya ruling under the orders
of Veṅkaṭādrirāja over Bārakūra-rājya made a gift of varāhas 35 for performing
the rite of purification of the temple of Kōṭēśvara. The gift was made for the well-
being of Veṅkaṭādrirāja. The purification was needed to remove the pollution caused
by the death of Brāhmans and Śūdras and the killing of cows in a religious feud
on the occasion of Tuḍiyahabba, on account of which the door of the temple had
been closed and the worship of the god discontinued for some time.

l.ka: nada parābhavasaṁvatsarada kārttika śu 15 lu śrīmanmahārājā-
śvaradēvarige kārtikamāsa dina 30 ke paṁcāmrutābhiśēka dīpārā-

brāhmaru śūdrara heṇagaḷu dēvasthānadoḷage bidu gōhatya-
vāgi dēvastānada bāgilu kaṭṭi nayivēdya naṁdādīpti ·uḷidu dhanya-

rige dhāreyaneṟadu koṭadu huduruhaḷḷiyali ga 35 varaha-
ge vivara ·aṁṇahattārana hadiyalu ga 10 biḷiya nāraṇahe-

sikoṭṭa śilāśāsana kōṭīśvara grāmada ·aṇihatārara poppa | paḍu
hebārara pūppa bārakūra karaṇika kruṣṇayyagaḷa baraha

inscription.head:
No. 622.
(A.R. No. 106 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP AT THE ENTRANCE TO THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT
BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1468, Plavaṅga, Jyēshṭha, śu. 11, Budhavāra. In the cyclic
year Plavaṅga corresponding to Śaka 1469 expired, the details cited give the equiva-
lent A.D. 1547 May 30 Monday, not Wednesday. The inscription refers itself to
the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya ruling at Vidyānagara and records that Timmōja,
Koṇḍōja, Bhadrōja and other barbers in the service of the crown were exempted
from kāṇike, kaḍḍāya, biṭṭi, birāḍa and other taxes. It also registers a similar
exemption granted to the barbers of Bāguḷi in Koṭṭūra-sīme.

inscription.head:
No. 623.
(A.R. No. 733 of 1922.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN THE COMPOUND OF THE RUINED MALLIKARJUNASVAMI
TEMPLE AT ANANTAPURAM, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1468, Plavaṅga, Āshāḍha, ba. 3, corresponding to A.D. 1547
July 5, Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya. It records
that Harapaṇṇa and other inhabitants of the village of Anantāpura in Āvinamaḍu-
sīme, a district of Penugoṇḍe-rājya restored the dilapidated temple of the god
Mallikārjunadēva in the village and that when a certain Timmadāsari, son of
Kammāra Timmadāsari; claimed that the temple was his own, the people of the
village, with Śrīnivāsāchārya at their head, paid a certain sum to the claimant and
made the temple a common property of the villagers.

l.ka: ḍuvina gauḍagaḷu sēnabhōva prajegaḷa sanmatadali ·ā khi-
ba • • • kaṁmāra tiṁmadāsari maga tiṁmadāsari ba-
ṁdu yo pūrvada ·atistānava taṁma taṁde tiṁmadāsa-

inscription.head:
No. 624.
(A.R. No. 384 of 1920.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE WELL AT KASAVAPURAM, GOOTY TALUK,
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Bhādrapada, śu. 15, Maṁ[gaḷavāra], corre
sponding to A.D. 1547 August 30, Tuesday, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya
It records that at the request of the sons of Puli Appana-Bōva of Kasavāpura, which
had been granted by Kōnēṭi-Timmarājayya, son of Rāmarāja-Koṇḍarāja, as an
umbaḷi to Daḷavāyi Yallappa-Nāyaka, the latter abolished biṭṭi, birāḍa and
other cesses levied from the fishermen. The capture of cocks and pigs was also
prohibited. The gift was made for the merit of Kōnēṭi-Timmarājayya.

inscription.head:
No. 625.
(A.R. No. 390 of 1920.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE OUTER WALL OF THE OBALESVARA TEMPLE
AT GADEKALLU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Plavaṅga, Bhādrapada, ba. 13, in the reign of
Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya, corresponding to A.D. 1547 September 11, Sunday (not
verifiable). It records that Appalarāja-Ayya issued orders to the Gauḍa and Sēnabōvas
of Urvakoṇḍeya-sīme exempting the barbers of certain classes of villages in the
above locality from kāṇike, biṭṭi, birāḍa, kappa and other taxes.

inscription.head:
No. 626.
(A.R. No. 260 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD NEAR THE TANK-BUND AT HAGARANURU,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Kārtika, śu. 2, Budhavāra. In Plavaṅga the
details cited corresponded to A.D. 1547 October 15, Saturday, not Wednesday, as
stated. It refers itself to the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya and records that
Bemma-Nāyaka, son of Muḍugandala-Tammarasa-Nāyaka, made a grant of the village
Amaragere in Raṅgapura-sthala, situated in Koṭūra-sīme, which had been granted by
the king to Kṛishṇapa-Nāyaka, son of Bayapa-Nāyaka, to Varuṇakānta-Voḍeya, son of
Kapilamuni-Heggapa-Śivāchārya-Voḍeya of Mayilāra. The gift was made for the
merit of Kṛishṇapa-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 627.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF CHANDESVARADEVA AT
MALLIPALLI, KALYANADRUG TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is a little damaged and dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Pushya, ba. 2,
Budhavāra, Makara-Saṅkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1547 December 28, Wednesday,
in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya. It records that Yallapa of Kambhadūru
and the son (name lost) of Murārirāvuta-Ayya made a grant of land for the service
of the goddess Banada-Chavuṇḍēśvari at Maruḷanahaḷḷi, for the merit of Kṛishṇapa-
Nāyaka, son of Bayapa-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 628.
(A.R. No. 317 of 1925.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO A FRONT WALL OF THE KARNAM'S HOUSE
AT KOTTUR, KUDLIGI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Māgha, śu. 10, corresponding to A.D. 1548
January 20, Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya. It records that the
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Aḷiya Rāmarāja-Mahā-arasu exempted the barbers of Koṭṭūra-sīme
from teṟige, kaḍḍāya, biṭṭi, koṭṭaṇa and other taxes. The order containing the
gift was made in favour of Timmōja, Bhadrōja, Koṇḍōja and other representatives.

l.note: Lines 21—23 do not seem to be connected with the above record.

l.ka:
• • ḍeyaru barada[ru] virupaṁ
besa[ge]yida śā[sana]ke
maṁgaḷa mahāśrī • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 629.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON A VACANT SITE IN MAIDURU, HARPANAHALLI TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Phālguṇa, śu. • • •
(= A.D. 1548 February), in the reign of Sadāśiva-Ma[hārāya]. It records that
Timmōja, Koṇḍōja and Bhadrōja got a grant from the king exempting the barbers
of Maidūru from the payment of kāṇike, kaḍḍāya and other taxes.

inscription.head:
No. 630.
(A.R. No. 508 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB LYING TO THE EAST OF THE VILLAGE NAKKARAHALU,
HADAGALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1470, Kīlaka, Vaiśākha, ba • • • Ādivāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1548 April 29 or May 6, Sunday, in the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya. It records
that Kṛishṇapa-Nāyaka made a gift of the village Nagarehaḷi, surnamed Nagarūru,
situated in [Kuṇṭe]nūra-sīme in the Kōgaḷi-vaḷita to Timapa of Bommapura. The
record is damaged.

l.ka: mava ko[ṭṭe*]vu yidake ·āru tapidare gaṁ[ge*] taḍīli
gōva koṁda pāpake hōru śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 631.
(A.R. No. 583 of 1912.)
ON THE NAGALABANDA ROCK NEAR THE PEDDACHERUVU TANK AT OLD LEPAKSHI,
NEAR LEPAKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1471, Kīlaka, Bhādrapada, śu. 1, corresponding
to A.D. 1548 August 4, Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya.
It registers a gift made for the merit of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-
Viṭhaladēva-Mahā-arasu, Rāmarājaya-Raṅgarāja and the donor's (name lost) parents
Koṇḍamarasaya and Narasamma. Mentions Hiriya-Limga-Bōva.

inscription.head:
No. 632.
(A.R. No. 432 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A PRIVATE HOUSE SOUTH OF THE TANK CALLED
SANNAKEREKATTE AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1471; Kīlaka, Kārtika, śu. 1 corresponding to A.D. 1548
October 2, Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya ruling at Vijayanagara.
It records that Bemmachaseṭṭi of the family of Kalluseṭṭi restored the maṭha built
by Kalluseṭṭi and endowed it with some plots of land and money.

l.ka:
svasti [||*] śrīgaṇādhipati·ēṁnama[ḥ*] śrīsarasvatyāeṁ namaḥ śrīgurubhyō(ṁ) nama[ḥ*]-

mu 1 gōḷiyagade mu 1 meke teṁgina

3 ||| 0 ge hoṁna moda ga 81 |||

lūra voḷage jaṁni nāraṇakaṁbaḷiya hiriyara

inscription.head:
No. 633.
(A.R. No. 449 of 1920.)
ON A ROCK IN A FIELD TO THE SOUTH-EAST OF VELPUMADUGU,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1470, Kīlaka, Kārtika, śu. 5 corresponding to A.D. 1548
October 7, Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāyadēva-Mahārāya. It
records that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-Appayyadēva-Mahā-arasu fixed the
rate of rent payable by the people of the village Apalapura for cultivating the fields
at Velupaḍige which had been lying fallow and that he exempted them from bitti,
birāḍa and other taxes. Velupaḍige is stated to have been situated in the
Urvakoṇḍe-sīme which had been granted to Appayyadēva as amaranāyaka by the
king.

l.ka:
śrī śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1470 neya kīlaka-
saṁvatsarada kārttika śu 5 lu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśva[ra] śrīvīrāpra-
tāpa śrīsadāśivarāyadēvamahārāyaru prutvīrājyaṁge-

yi·utta yiralu śrīmaṁmāhamaṁḍalēśvara rāmarāja·appayyadēvamahāarasugaḷu velu-
paḍigeya holadoḷage paligrāmavāgi kaṭṭida ·apalapurada gauḍasēnabōvaru vakkalu
timmiṟaḍi muṁtāda samasta prajegaḷi-

ge koṭa sāguvaḷiya śāṣanada kramaveṁtaṁdare sadāśivamahārāyaru namage ·amaranāyaka-
tanake pālisida ·urvakoṁḍeya sīmege saluva velupaḍigeya grāmada holanu pāṁḍuva
bīḷu bidu yiralāgi nāu nimage koṭa

sāguvaḷiya vivara yo tātātiti ·ārabhyavāgi bituvudu varuṣa 1 ke hola gutage ·āgi ko-
ṭudu ·ereya bījavari ¥ 1 kkegutage gaṁ jōḷa ¥ 0 || ·oṁdu hoṁnu yibaḷa jōḷa
maḍeya holada bījavari ¥ 1 ke cakra ga 3 ru mū-

ru haṇa ·eraḍaneya varuśake niṁta gutage yereya bījavari ¥ 1 ke ga 1 voṁdu varaha jōḷa
¥ 1 vokuḷa jōḷa maḍeya bījavari ¥ 1 ke ga 0 || ·oṁdu hoṁnu yi mariyādelu
yeraḍu yeṁdu yeṁdige tapade na-

ḍasikoṁbe·u yo mariyādelū vevaśāyigaḷanu māḍi yo śasanada pramāṇadalu ·ācaṁdrā-
arkkastāyigaḷu ·āgi sukadalu vevaśāyava māḍuvadu yi ·apalapurakke kāṇike kapa
biṭi birāḍa ·urvakoṁḍe sīmege baṁda • • •

danu yalavanu sarvamānyava māḍide·u yi sāguvaḷiyanu mālayaḷagutage·agi naḍesi-
koṁbe·u yiṣṭake naṁmanu sarvāta naṁbi sukadalu vevaśāyava māḍikoṁḍu yihu-
du yeṁdu koṭa śilāsāsana [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 634.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1920.)
ON TWO DETACHED STONES IN THE SOUTH WALL OF THE KRISHNASVAMI
TEMPLE AT DHARAPURAM, DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and incomplete and is dated Śaka 1471, Saumya, Vaiśākha, ba.
30 corresponding to A.D. 1549 April 27, Saturday or if Nija-Vaiśākha is meant,
May 26, Sunday (not verifiable). It refers itself to the reign of Sadāśivadēva-
Mahārāya and registers the grant of a village (name lost) made to Vīrā-Paṇḍita, son
of Śivanappa-Paṇḍita of Bhāradvāja-gōtra, Āśvalāyana-sūtra, and Ṛik-śākhā by
Nañjayya-Liṅgarasa.

l.note: Some lines seem to have been lost after this.

inscription.head:
No. 635.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1904.)
ON TWO PILLARS IN A MANDAPA ON THE WAY TO THE JAMBUNATHA TEMPLE
NEAR HOSPET, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1471, Saumya, Pushya, śu. 9 corresponding to A.D. 1549
December 28, Saturday (not verifiable). It refers itself to the reign of Sadāśivadēva-
Mahārāya and records that the king made a gift. of 4 paḍis of rice per day out of the
provision made for the service of god Anantaśayanadēva, for offerings to the god
Hanumantadēva on the Jambukēśvara hill.

inscription.head:
No. 636.
(A.R. No. 23-A of 1904.)
IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This undated record written in the same hand as the above states that the
Gauḍa, Sēnabōva and the people of Chikavāḍi made a gift to the same god.

inscription.head:
No. 637.
(A.R. No. 682 of 1922.)
ON THE EAST WALL OF THE MANDAPA OF THE ANANTASAYANA TEMPLE AT
ANANTASAYANAGUDI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1471, Saumya, Pushya, śu. 9 corresponding to A.D. 1549
December 28, Saturday (not verifiable). It records that Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya
made a grant of 4 paḍis of rice per day out of the provision for the Anantaśayana
temple, for the service of the god Hanumantadēva of Jambukēśvara hill.

l.ka: śubhamastu [||*] śālivāhanaśakavaruṣa 1471 saumyasaṁvatsarada puśya śu 9 lu
saluva svāsteya voḷage nāu kaṭṭaḷeya māḍidu dina 1 kke ·akki paḍi 4
nāluku paḍi ·akkiyanu kaṭaḷeyanu māḍi ·ācaṁdrārkavāgi naḍeva hāge
śilāśāsanava hākisi koṭe·u [||] svādatādviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradatānupālanaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 638.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1926.)
ON A STONE PLANTED BEFORE THE HANUMAN TEMPLE AT KANAGANIPALLE,
DHARMAVARAM TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription is dated Śaka 1472, Sādhāraṇa, Bhādrapada, śu.
15, corresponding to A.D. 1550 August 27, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of
Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya. It records that Kāḍavīra, a Liṁgadhara (Liṅgayat) of
Ānegondi made a grant of the Liṅgadeṟige payable by the Liṅgadhārins of Kanaka-
goṇḍanahaḷḷi, belonging to the nāyakatana of Rāmarājayya, to god Vīrēśvaradēva
of the village for dīpārādhane.

l.ka: ḷu koḍuvaṁtha liṁgaderigeyanu samarpaṇe māḍi koṭa
koṭadu varuṣa 1 ke ga 2 yeraḍu varaha • •

inscription.head:
No. 639.
(A.R. No. 590 of 1912.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE CHAUDESVARI TEMPLE AT KOTNURU,
HINDUPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the cyclic year Sādhāraṇa, Mārgaśira, ba. 13 corre-
sponding to A.D. 1550 December 6, Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of
Sadāśiva-Mahārāya. It records the exemption from the taxes biṭṭi, birāḍa, teṟige and
hoṟige granted to the servants of the temple of Chauḍēśvarī at Koṭnūru by Guṇḍa-
marasaya, the agent of Siriyapa-Nāyaka, for the merit of Daḷavāyi Kṛishṇayya-
Nāyaka. The names of these temple servants are given.

edition.h4: On the back side of the stone.

inscription.head:
No. 640.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE LEFT SIDE OF THE ENTRANCE TO THE KALLESVARA
TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1472, Sādhāraṇa, Pushya, śu. pūrṇimā, Sōmavāra corresponding
to A.D. 1550 December 23, Tuesday, not Monday, in the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya
ruling at Vidyānagarī. It records that while Kṛishṇappa-Nāyaka, son of Haḍapada
Bayyapa-Nāyaka, was ruling over Koṭṭūru in Kōgaḷi district, and the thirty-two-
thousand province, and Haḷavi-Nāyaka was ruling over Bāguḷi in the Koṭṭūra-vaḷita,
Liṅgamma, wife of Lakkiseṭṭi, the paṭṭaṇasvāmi of Bāḷguḷi, made a gift of paddy
and gold for the service of the god Kalidēva with the prayer that her son
Liṅgayya may prosper. The gift was made into the hands of Dēvarāśijīya, the
head of the temple.

l.ka: ya śaṁbhavē [||*] svasti samastabhuvanāśrayaṁ śrīpruthvīvallabha mahā(rā)-
pālisida kōgaḷige saluva koṭṭūrumaṁ mūvattichāsiramumaṁ duṣṭa-
koṭṭūra vaḷitake saluva bāguḷiya sīmeyanu tatpādapadmōpajīvi haḷavināya-
ṣya śu pūrnami sōmavāra puṇyadinadalu bāḷguḷiya śrīsvayaṁbhukalidēva-
ṇasvāmi lakkiseṭṭiyara madavaḷige liṁgaṁmanu || satiyoḷu sarasvatiyenaluṁ
patiyoḷsīlatvavenaltaṁna suta liṁgaiyyage satisutadhanadhānyaṁgaḷa satāyuṁ samru-
ddhiyeṁdu nānatanādaḷu [||*] ·antenisi kalidēvatānamaskāravaṁ māḍi tamma makkaḷu
lu tiṁgaḷtiṁgaḷige nellu 1 | 0 mēluvecca bāḍukāyi lavaṇa kaṭughṛtakaṁ
ra naḍavaṁtāgi mattaṁ śivagñānasaṁpannarappa liṁgaṁmanu liṁgiseṭṭiyaru
luvaṁta stānika dēvarāsijiyyarige kaṭṭaḷeyaṁ māḍi dēvara śrīpādada-
llu samarppisidaḷiṁtī dharmamamanayvadiṁbaru pratipālisidavarge vāraṇāsi ku-
rukṣētradallu sahasra brāhmaṇarige sāvira kapileya koṭṭa phalamakku ||

inscription.head:
No. 641.
(A.R. No. 85 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1472, Sādhāraṇa, Phālguṇa, śu. 5 corresponding to A.D.
1551 February 11, wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya
ruling at Vidyānagara. It records that Heḷavi-Nāyaka, son of Chāmi-Nāyaka of Bāguḷi,
in the sub-division of Koṭūra-sīme which had been granted to Haḍapada-Kṛishṇappa-
Nāyaka, exempted the shepherds of Bāguḷi and its hamlets from tax on their sheep.
Koṭūra-sīme is stated to have been situated in Kōgaḷi-vēṇṭhe which was a sub-
division of Pāṇḍya-nāḍu.

l.ka: na maga liṁgagoṁḍa muṁtāda kuṟuba gau[ḍa]-
gaḷige kuṟideṟige yillā ·eṁdu biṭṭu koṭṭa śā
ya vo[ḷa]gaṇa bāguḷiya grāma 1 ·ā grāmakke saluva

inscription.head:
No. 642.
(A.R. No. 734 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE RUINED MALLIKARJUNA TEMPLE AT
ANTAPURAM, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1473, Virōdhikṛit, Āshāḍha, śu. 12 corresponding
to A.D. 1551, June 15, Monday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that Viṭhalarājayya, son of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-
Tirumalarājayya, purchased a fruit-garden at Āvinamaḍuvu and made a gift of
it for the service of the god Mallikārjuna at Hiriya-Anantāpura.

inscription.head:
No. 643.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP AT DODDAKATTA-CHERUVU NEAR HALUKUR,
MADAKASIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1473, Virōdhikṛit, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 1 corresponding to A.D. 1551
July 4, Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya. It records
that, under the orders of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-Viṭhalayadēva-Mahā-
arasu, the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tirumalayadēva-Mahā-arasu of Manubōlu made a
kaṭṭu-koḍagi gift of land to Māḷi-Gauḍa and Parusa-Gauḍa for repairing the tank at
Doḍḍagaṭṭa in Niḍugal-nāḍu.

l.ka: ḍana maga māḷigauḍa dēvagauḍana maga paru-
du vaḍakiyanu kaṭidakke nimage koṭa kaṭu-
goḍagi gade modala kaṭeya staḷadalu gade
gige ·āru tapidaru taṁma teṁdeyalu hattutāyalu hanno-
da dōśake hōharū sāvira gōu sāvira brāṁ-
hara koṁda dōśake hōharū ·aṁdu koṭa kaṭugo-
ḍagiya mānyada dharmaśāśana yi mānyava koṭa

inscription.head:
No. 644.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1920.)
ON TWO DETACHED STONES IN THE NORTH WALL OF THE SIXTEEN-PILLARED MANDAPA
IN THE UTTARA-VIRARAGHAVA PERUMAL TEMPLE IN THE FORT AT DHARAPURAM,
DHARAPURAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1473, Virōdhikṛit, Bhādrapada, śu. 12, Guruvāra, corresponding
to A.D. 1551 September (Nija-Bhādrapada), 10, Thursday, f.d.t. ·68, in the reign of
Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya. It records that at the suggestion of Nañjappayya, son of
Kandāchāra-Timmarasayya, the Nāyakas, Gollas, Bestas, workmen, and others of
Dhārāpura agreed to pay a certain sum of money for the palanquin (pallakki) service
of the god Rāghavadēva of the village once a year. The inscription is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 645.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE MAILARESVARA TEMPLE AT
PATASIVARAM, MADAKASIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1473, Virōdhikṛit, Āśvīja, śu. 10 corresponding
to A.D. 1551 October 9, Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that Koṇḍarājayya's son (name lost) made a gift of land, free from all
taxes, in the presence of the god Viṭhaladēva.

l.ka: śvīja śu 10 lu śrīma(ṁ)n mahāmaṁḍalēśvara rājādhirā-

inscription.head:
No. 646.
(A.R. No. 490 of 1915.)
ON A ROCK AT YERIGERI, BELLARY TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and incomplete. It is dated Śaka 1473, Virōdhikṛit, Kārtika,
śu. 12, corresponding to A.D. 1551 November 10, Tuesday (not verifiable), in the
reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 647.
(A.R. Nos. 305 and 306 of 1925.)
ON A BROKEN PILLAR LYING BEFORE THE RUINED TEMPLE OF VASANTA-MALLIKARJUNA
AT DEVALAPURA, A HAMLET OF GAJAPURA, KUDLIGI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT. These two numbers together form one inscription.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 147[4], Virōdhikṛit, Pushya, ba. 30, Sōmavāra corresponding to
A.D. 1552 January 25, Monday, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya. It records
the gift of the village Pannula-Nūṅkanahaḷḷi to the god Vasanta-Mallikārjuna of
Dēvalāpura made by Rāmappa-Nāyaka, son of Timmappa-Nāyaka of Vēlūr and
agent to Kṛishṇapa-Nāyaka, son of Bayyapa-Nāyaka. The village was to be enjoyed
by the piper Musiya-Rāvuta, son of Kōnayya-Rāvuta, for playing on two nāgasvaras
and two drums in the temple. It also states that the goldsmith Siriyaṇṇa who
engraved the inscription was granted a mānya land in the village.

l.ka: neya virōdhikṛtusaṁvatsarada puṣya ba 30 lu sōmavāra ·a-
nu dēvalāpurada vasaṁtamallikārjunadēvarige yeraḍu ḍōḷu yera-
pu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalu koṭṭa grāma ·ā grāmake saluva catusī-
meyoḷagaṇa jala pāṣāṇa nidhi nikṣēpa ·akṣīṇi ·āgāmi siddha

inscription.head:
No. 648.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP TO THE EAST OF THE PAMPAPATI TEMPLE AT KAMPILI,
HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1474, Parīdhāvi, Vaiśākha, śu. 1, Sō[mavāra] corresponding
to A.D. 1552 April 24, Sunday (not Monday). It refers itself to the reign of
Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya and records that Saṅkarasa, son of Suṅkada-Banadarasa
and head of Karibēḍu, caused the construction of a shrine in the temple of the god
Virūpāksha in Kampili, set up the goddess Hampādēvi in it and made a gift of land
at the village of Karibēḍu, for the service of the goddess.

l.ka: parīdhāvisa[ṁ]vatsarada vaiśākha śu 1 sō dalu śrīmatu śrīmanmahā-
ḍanāḍa suṁkada banadarasara makkaḷu ka[ri]bēḍa yajamāna saṁkara-
hāradalu tamage vuṁṭāda vṛttigaḷoḷage haṁpādēvige ha-
ttu vṛttiyanu dhāreneredu koṭevu yo hattuvṛttige vivara vu-
ttama hatu hatu koḷaga madhyama hattu koḷaga kanisa yikaḷavu
tōṭasthaḷa vokkaḷa vubhayaṁ khaṇḍugada mūgaḷada holadali
huṭṭidaṁtthā rasadhānya jōḷa nelu muṁtāda dhānyagaḷanu nō-
ḍikoṁḍu hotare solage bayige solage voṁdu naṁdādīpti
hotare voṁdu bayigoṁdu vīḷaya mahānavami nava-
rātri pūje danurmāsada pūje kārtikadīpāradhane paṁca-
parva muṁtāda tithigaḷanu māḍikoṁḍu yo hatu vṛttiya phala-

rēṇa svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavēt [||*] svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēca va-

inscription.head:
No. 649.
(A.R. No. 787 of 1917.)
ON A NANDI-PILLAR SET UP IN A FIELD TO THE EAST OF
CHALIVENDLA, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1474, Parīdhāvi, Kārtika, śu. 1 corresponding
to A.D. 1552 October 18, Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Vīra-[Dhama]rāya.
It records that the cess raised on marriages at Cheluviṇḍli was granted for the
service of a god (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 650.
(A.R. No. 775 of 1917.)
ON A BOULDER NEAR THE DILAPIDATED ISVARA TEMPLE AT KANCHISAMUDRAM,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1476, Pramādi, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 5 corresponding
to A.D. 1553 July 29, Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-
Mahārāya. It records that Vīraṇṇa-Voḍeya's pupil (name lost) made a gift of gold
for the service of the god Sōmēśvaradēva in the village of Kaṁchisamudra
surnamed Pratāpadēvarāyapura.

inscription.head:
No. 651.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1912.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A GARDEN AT KAGGALLU, SAME TALUK
AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This record is damaged and dated Śaka 147[5], Pramādīcha, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 10,
Śu[kravāra] corresponding probably to A.D. 1553 August 4, Friday, in the reign of
Sadāsivadēva-Mahārāya. It records a gift for burning lamps before the god
Mallikārjunadēva of Kaggallu, surnamed Timmasamudra, in Kōkāpura-sthaḷa situated
in Penugoṇḍe-rājya, a division of Ṟodda-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 652.
(A.R. No. 402 of 1920.)
ON A STONE SET UP BEHIND THE ANJANEYASVAMI TEMPLE AT RAGULAPADU,
GOOTY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1476, Pramādi, Māgha, śu. 7, Maṅgaḷavāra,
Makara-saṅkrānti corresponding to A.D. 1554 January 9 Tuesday, f.d.t. ·39, in the
reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya. It records that the Vipravinōdi-Brāhmans Padma-
nābhayya, Kēśavayya and Mādhavayya agreed to conduct the worship of the god
Hanumantadēva in the village Rāganahāḷu, surnamed Liṅgasamudra, in the
Urukoṇḍe-sīme a sub-division of Rāyadurga-vēṇṭhe in the district of Mūḍa-nāḍu
in Hastināvati-vaḷita. The worship was to be conducted for the merit of the
Vipravinōdins Gōvindayya, Kēśavayya, Mantramūrti-Basavayya, Dakshiṇāmūrti and
Suṅkada-Vallabhayya.

l.ka: jege vipravinōdigaḷige mukhyasvāmigaḷāda vēda śāstra purāṇa cauṣaṣṭi-
basavayya dakṣiṇāmūrti suṁkada vallabhayya jana ·ayavaru modalāda
tra gōviṁdayana komāra padmanābhayanu kēśavayyana maga kēśavayyanu
kausikagōtrada yajusākha valabhasōmayā[ja]ra komāra mādhavayyanu jana
mūvaru ·ēkastarāgi koṭa rāganahāḷa hanumaṁtadēvara kārtikada pūjege
makarasaṁkrānti puṇyakāladalli dāreyanaṟadu koṭṭa dharmaśaśana yī dharma-
ka vipravinōdi jātiyoḷage ·āru tapidarū kāśiyoḷage gō brāhmara
vadhiśida pāpake hōharu gōmāṁsa śunakamāṁsa bhakṣistavaru su-

inscription.head:
No. 653.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1904.)
ON THE NORTH BASE OF THE MANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
OF THE VITTHALASVAMI TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK,
BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1476, Ānanda, Nija-Āshāḍha, śu. 12, Guruvāra corresponding
to A.D. 1554 July 12 Thursday, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya. It records that
Udagiri-Timmarājaya-Mahā-arasu, son of Kōnēṭayadēva-Mahā-arasu and grandson
of Ārivīṭi Rāmarājaya-Koṇḍayadēva-Mahā-arasu, made a gift of the village,
Tirumalāpura, a suburb of Koṇḍakundi situated in Puliyēru-māgaṇe, a division of
Guttiya-sīme, for mounting the god Viṭṭhalasvāmi on the see-saw, in the maṇḍapa
erected by him for the purpose, on the Prathama-Ēkādaśī and Dvādaśī festival days
and for offerings to the god on those days. The revenue of the village was gadyāṇas
600 per year.

inscription.head:
No. 654.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1904.)
ON THE NORTH BASE OF THE MANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE
VITTHALASVAMI TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1476, • • • • Nija-Āshāḍha, ba. 2, Sō[mavāra] correspond-
ing to A.D. 1554 July 16, Monday, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya ruling
in Vidyānagara. It records that a subordinate of the king (name lost), son of
Rāmarāja-Timmarājaya of Musalimaḍuvu, made a gift of the village Anantāpura,
together with some vṛittis in Marukombanahaḷḷi and other villages, yielding 300
varāhas per annum, for the services of the god Viṭhaladēva. The record is stated
to have been engraved by the orders of Aḷiya-Rāmapayyadēva.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśaka[varuṣaṁgaḷu] 1476 neya • • •
saṁvatsarada nija ·āṣāḍha ba 2 sō lu śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara
śrīvīrapratāpa śrīvīrasadāśivadēvamahārāyaru vidyānagaradalu ratnasiṁhāsanā-
rūḍharāgi pruthvīsāṁbrājyaṁgeyi·uttaṁ yiralu śrīmatu ·anēkakōṭi bra(ṁ)hmāṁḍa-
nāyaka purāṇapuruśōttama bhaktajanavatsala śrīviṭhaladēvarige haritasagōtra ·āpa-
staṁbasūtrada yajuḥśākhādhyāyigaḷāda [mu]salimaḍuvina vīrarāma-

rāja tiṁmarājayanavara makkaḷu rāma • • • • koṭṭa śilāśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare
namage ·amaramāgaṇige naḍeva vijayanāgarada mūḍalu ·udagiriya bāgila muṁdaṇa
anaṁtāpurada grāmavanū sadāśivadēvamahārāyara kayiyalu dhārenerasi vahi ·utā-
ravanū māḍisida saṁmaṁdha ·ā ·anaṁtāpurada grāma 1 kaṁ huṭuvaḷi gaṭiga 200 varaha
namage sadāśivadēvamahārāyariṁdalu pratigrāhalabdhavāgi śā-

sanasthavāgi baṁda dūrūru • • • • • • • • • • vijayasamudrada ·agrahāradalu vritti
1 phalaguttige va ga 10 saha • • • marakoṁbanahaḷḷige pratināmavāda timmasā-
garada ·agrāhāradalu vritti 4 kke phalaguttige va ga 20 ·aṁtu va gaṭi ga 300 ·akṣaradallu
yivē ghaṭi mu(ṁ)nnūru varahakke pālu[ṭu] kaṭṭida vivara | viṭhaladēvaru lakṣmīdēvi
a(ṁ)manavaru dina 1 kke ·udayavāda jāvada ·avasaradalu ·avadharisuva harivāṇa 1 ke varaha
ga 35 daṟa lekhadalu va ga 280 naṁdādīptige di 1

kke ·udayavāda jāvada ·avasaradalu • • • • • • lekhadalū va ga 30 naṁdādīptige
di 1 ke tuppa sēru 1 lekhadalu varuṣa 1 ke sēru 360 kke kraya varaha ga 1 kke sēru
30 lekhadalu va ga 12 gaṁdha di 1 kkeci pu 0 || lekhadalu ci pu varuṣa 1 kke ci pu 180
kke gaṁdhada sekke sēru 18 kke kra va ga 3 gaṁdhada parimaḷake va 1 kke varaha ga 0 || 2 || 0
hūvina daṁḍe viṭhaladēvarige 1 ·aṁmanavarige 1 ·ubhayaṁ di 1 kke daṁḍe 2 lekhadalu varuṣa
1 kke varaha ga 1 hōḷu di 1 kke (ṁ) se 0 = lekhadalu va 1 ke se 45 kke

varuṣa 1 ke sū 2 ṟa lekhadalu va ga 1 • • • • • 500 lekhadalu varaha ga 110 ·antu
varaṣa 1 ke ghaṭi varaha ga 300 yo prakāradalu pratidivasaū ·ācaṁdrārkavāgi
naḍeyaluḷadu yidarali • • • rāyasada veṁkaṭādrige di 1 ke prasāda harivāṇa
2 gaṁdha ci pu 0 = • • • • • • • • • • ḍake yele 60 yo lekhadalu putra-
pautraparaṁpareyalu ·anubhavisaluḷavaru yeṁdu ·aḷiya rāmapayyadēvamahāarasugaḷa
apaṇeyiṁdalu māḍida silāśāśanau

svadattāddviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ | • • • • • • • • • •
dharmasēturnrupāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālaniyō bhavadbhiḥ | sarvānētāṁ [bhāvinaḥ*]
pārthivēṁdrān bhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmacaṁdraḥ || maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī
[||*]

inscription.head:
No. 655.
(A.R. No. 433 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE ROAD-SIDE TO THE WEST OF THE TANK CALLED
SANNAKEREKATTE AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1476, Ānanda, Kārtika, śu. 1 corresponding to A.D. 1554
October 27 Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya. While
Mallapa-Voḍeya was the ruler of Bārakūra-rājya, under the orders of Sadāśiva-
Nāyaka (of Keḷadi), who had been appointed governor of the Tuḷu-rājya by the king,
Timmayyaseṭṭi, nephew (aḷiya) of Hosabuseṭṭi, is stated to have purchased a plot of
land, constructed a maṭha thereon and having purchased from Aṇṇa-Uḍupa a
brahmadāya land made a gift of the same for the maintenance of the chhatra attached
to the maṭha. The record is incomplete.

l.ka:
śa 1476 saṁda ·ānaṁdasaṁvatsarada kārtika śuddha [1] li śrīmadrājādhirāja rājapara-
mēśva-
ra śrīvīrapratāpa sadāśivarāyamahārāyaru vijayanagariya siṁhāsanadalli ci-
ttavisi sakala sā[ṁ]brājyavanāḷutiha kāladalli ·avara nirūpadiṁda sadāśivarāyanāya-
karū tuḷurājya maḍikoṁḍu ·avara nirūpadiṁda mallappapoḍēru bārukūru siṁhāsana-
ya tiṁmayyaseṭṭiyu basarūra paḍuvakēriya voḷage halaru seṭṭikārara kai-
da catussīmeya vivara mūḍalu ho[ḷe]ya baḷiyavara maṭhadiṁda paḍuvalu teṁ-
kalu baḷagāra baḷiyavara dharmada kaṭṭeyiṁda baḍagalu paḍuvalu rāja-
bīdiyiṁda mūḍalu baḍagalu kauḍici baḷiyavara maṭhadiṁda teṁkalu yi[ṁ]-
ttī cattussīme voḷaguḷa maṭhava kaṭṭisi svasti koṁḍu chatrakke biṭa vivara
ṇi ·aṁṇauḍuparu koṭṭa biḍāruvārada patrada kramaveṁteṁdare nāu

inscription.head:
No. 656.
(A.R. No. 769 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT PULAMITI,
HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1458 (mistake for 1478), Rākshasa, Jyēshṭha,
ba. 10 corresponding to A.D. 1555 June 14, Friday (not verifiable) in the reign of
Sadāśivarāya. It records that Timmarāja, son of Dombara-Timarāja, made a gift
for the service of a god (name lost), for the prosperity of his family.

inscription.head:
No. 657.
(A.R. No. 468 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE PLATFORM OF THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE
AT TIMMALAPURA, HADAGALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1477, Rākshasa, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 5 corresponding to A.D. 1555
July 23 Tuesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya ruling at Vidyānagara.
It records that Aḷiya-Rāmarāja-Arasu made a gift of the village Hakahandiganūru
in Guttavoḷala-sīme which was in charge of Viṭhalappa-Oḍeya, for the service of the
god Āḷvāru near Viṭhala.

inscription.head:
No. 658.
(A.R. No. 340 of 1920.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYASVAMI TEMPLE AT AVULAMPALLI,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1478, Rākshasa, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 5, Guruvāra
corresponding to A.D. 1555 August 7, Wednesday, not Thursday, in the reign of
Sadāśiva-Mahārāya. It records that Komaravīra, son of Komaravīrayya of
Ānegondi, made a grant of land for the service of a god (name lost) in
Virūpākshapura.

l.ka: [tsara śrā]vaṇa ba 5 gu lu śrīmadrājādhirāja paramēśvara śrīvī-

diya komaravī[ra]yagaḷa makaḷu komaravīragaḷu • • • • virūpākṣa-
purada
• • • • dēvarige kārtika pūje dīpārādhanege dēvā pṛthvī

inscription.head:
No. 659.
(A.R. No. 392 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BY THE SIDE OF A HOUSE TO THE EAST OF THE TANK,
DEVARAKERE, AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1477, Rākshasa, Kārtika, śu. 1 corresponding to A.D. 1555
October 16, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya.
It records that while Sadāśivarāya-Nāyaka was the governor of Tuḷu-rājya under the
orders of the king, and that under the latter's orders Mallappoḍeya was ruling
over Bārakūra-rājya, Kōṭīsaraseṭṭi, grandson of Śaṅkara Haḍupāḷe of Baḷābaḷi, a
resident of Basarūru, made a grant of land for maintaining the sattra attached to
his maṭha. The record is incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 660.
(A.R. No. 304 of 1925.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE TANK-BUND AT HULIGUNTA,
KUDLIGI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1478, Naḷa, Vaiśākha, śu. 3, corresponding to A.D. 1556 April
12, Sunday (not verifiable). Though the record does not refer itself to the reign of
any king its date falls in the reign of Sadāśivarāya. It records, in addition to what
has been stated in No. 593 the construction of the village Achyutammāpura and
of the temple of Bāla-Kṛishṇa by Bayakāra-Rāmappayya for the merit of his
daughter Achyutamma. He is identical with Rāmayāmātya.

l.ka: neya naḷasaṁvatsarada vaiśākha śu 3 llu śrīmatu bayakāra
kṛtī tasyāsītsahadha[r]miṇī telugamā gaṁgēva dugdhāṁbudhēḥ [||*] rāmāṁbudhēsteluga-

tra nidhāya sarvamaśiṣatsēnāṁdhradēśasthalīṁ [||*] tasyāgrajanmā pedatiṁmayēṁdrō
lakkā-
ṁbikā tasya babhūva patnī | tayōstapasyāparipākamūrtirvirājatē rāmayamaṁtri-

lē dēvabrāhmaṇamaṁḍalāya bahuḷānnavyāgrahārān dadau || śākābdēvanikārtikē-
yava-

danāṁbhōdhīṁdusaṁkhyānvitē varṣē cāpi vikārināmani nabhōmāsē sa rāmaprabhuḥ |
grā-
rōt khyātāṁ cigōḍābhidhaṁ || tiṁmāpurāgrahārasya grāmagrāsatayā kṛtaṁ cinatiṁ-
māpuramiti pratināma samāśritaṁ || maṁgimāvinahaḷḷyākhyaṁ grāmaṁ sasyōpaśōbhitaṁ |

ka[śaḥ] | tēṣu kramātpratiṣṭhāpya gōpīnāthābhidhaṁ hariṁ [||*] bācēśvaraṁ ca kṛṣṭē-
śamaṁmēśaṁ ma-
llikārjunaṁ | viśvanāthaṁ ca dēvēśaṁ vīrabhadraṁ jagatpatiṁ || hoḷagoṁdima-
ṁmāpuragrāmē dēvāgāraṁ vidhāya ca | tatra kṛṣṇaṁ pratiṣṭhāpya tasyā[ḥ*] pu-
myaṁ bācasamudraṁ ca tathā rāmasamudrakaṁ | khyātamakkasamudraṁ ca kā[ṁ]-
ccutēṁdrasamudraṁ ca vēṁkaṭēṁdra samadakaṁ [||*] pina[la]kkasamudraṁ ca cinya
yataṭākaṁ ca ve[ṁ]kaṭayyataṭākakaṁ | pedatiṁmasamudraṁ ca cina-

inscription.head:
No. 660.
(A.R. No. 407 of 1920.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT CHYABALA,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1478, Naḷa, Vaiśākha, śu. 15, corresponding to A.D. 1556 April
24, Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya ruling at
Vidyānagara. It records that Vipravinōdi-Gōvindayya and his brother Titāraya,
sons of Rudraya and grandsons of Pralayakāla-Gōvindayya of Kāśyapa-gōtra,
Āpastamba-sūtra and Yajus-śākhā and Vipravinodi-Valabhayya, son of Vīraya and
grandson of Hirivallabhayya of Vasishṭha-gōtra, Āpastamba-sūtra and Yajuś-śāḵhā
made a grant of the income due to the Vipravinōdins in the sarvaamānya-agrahāra
Tāvaregoḷa, surnamed Kṛishṇarāyapura, in Urvakoṇḍe, a division of Rāyadurga-nāḍu,
for the service of the god Channakēśavadēva of the place.

l.ka: khavaruṣa 1478 ya naḷasaṁvatsarada vayiśākha śu 15 lu
pautrarāda vīrayagaḷa putrarāda valabhaiyanū jana mūvarū va-
pi koṭa dharmaśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare tāvaragoḷake pratinā-

inscription.head:
No. 662.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1913.)
ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE MUD WALL IN FRONT OF THE LAKSHMINARAYANA
TEMPLE AT SOMASAMUDRA, BELLARY TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is incomplete and dated Śaka 1478, Naḷa, Āshāḍha, śu. 11, Gu[ruvāra]
corresponding to A.D. 1556 June 18, Thursday, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya
ruling from Vidyānagara. It records that the Mahājanas of the sarvamānya-agrhahāra
Sōmasamudra made a grant of land for the service of the god Lakshmīnārāyaṇa in
the village and that Nāganātha-Sōmayāji granted also some vṛittis. Sōmasamudra
is stated to have been situated in Kuṟugōḍa-sīme, which was a sub-division of
Mūganāḍa-vēṇṭhe in Hastināvati-vaḷita.

inscription.head:
No. 663.
(A.R. No. 423 of 1920.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE NARASIMHASVAMI TEMPLE AT PENNAHOBILAM,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1478, Naḷa, Āshāḍha, śu. 11, corresponding to A.D. 1556 June
18, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya, ruling from
Vidyānagara. It records that Kōnapadēva-Mahā-arasu, son of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Rāmarāja-Appayadēva-Mahā-arasu, exempted the Mahājanas of the agrahāras of
Ānadale, Niṁbegalu, Rāyapanahaḷḷi, Kalidēvanahaḷḷi, Sirivara, Rēvināyakanahaḷḷi,
Yaraguḍi, Tumbeganūru, Rāganahāḷu, Tāvaregoḷa, Chikapēpali, Timmāpura, Hōtūru,
Benakānahaḷḷi, Aṇṭarasanahāḷu and Kamaḷahāḷu, situated in Uruvakoṇḍe-sīme, a
sub-division of Rāyadurga-vēṇṭhe, from such taxes as taḷavārike, bēḍike, rāyasada-
vartane, karaṇikara-vartane, taḷavārike-vartane, yeleya-pammu, maharnavami-ulupe,
Henne-dēvara-ulupe, kammatada-kūli, soppe, birāḍa, daṇḍina-kāṇike, janasāle and
haraḷabiṭṭi. The gift was made in the presence of the god Narasimhasvāmi of Henne-
Ahōbaḷa on the bank of the river Pinākinī, for the merit of the donor's father
Appalarājayya and his brother Tirumalarāja.

l.ka: yaṁ śrīnrusiṁhaṁ bhajāmyahaṁ [||*] svasti śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavarṣaṁgaḷu sā
1478 neya naḷasaṁvatsarada ·āśāḍha śudha 11 lu śrīmadrājādhirāja rājapa-
yapanahaḷi kalidēvanahaḷi sirivara rēvināyakanahaḷi yaraguḍi tuṁbeganūru
rāganahāḷu tāvaregoḷa cikapēpali tiṁmāpura hōtūru benakānahaḷi ·aṁṭa-
rasanahāḷu kamaḷahāḷu yivu muṁtāda ·uruvakoṁḍeya sīmeya voḷagaṇa ·agra-
maṁḍalēśvara rāmarāja·apayadēva mahāarasugaḷa kumā[ra]ru kōnapadēva ma-
sīmeya karaṇikaravartane ·uruvakoṁḍeya sthaḷada karaṇikara varttane nā-
lupe rokka heṁnedēvara ·ulupe roka kaṁmatadakūli rokka sūppe birāḍada ro-
kka daṁḍinakāṇike roka jānasāle ·auḍalada haraḷa biṭi yi·u moda-
lāda yēnuṁṭāda cinivāna biṭigaḷu kāṇike kappagaḷu sahavāgi
nyavāgi dhāreneredu koṭe·u yeṁdu barasi hākisikoṭṭa dharmaśilāśāsana
yo śilāśāsanadalli bareda ·arthake nāu modalāgi ·ārobaru tappidaru ta-
ṁma mātāpitrugaḷanu taṁma guru·ugaḷanu gōbrā[hma]ranu kāśiyali va-
dhiśidaṁthā pātakake hōgaluḷavaru yeṁdu barasi hākiśi koṭa śilāśāsana
tiṁmāpurada ·agrahārake grāma grāsavāgi vellaḍagi holadoḷage vīśvaranā-
yakara tiṁmapanāyakaru koṭa hola sahavāgi biṭe·u | dānapālanayō-
svadatāṁ paradatāṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharāṁ śaṣṭivaruṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyā
yī śāsanaṁ veyiṁcavacina mudrakarta pusuri rāma-

inscription.head:
No. 664.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE MALLIKARJUNASVAMI TEMPLE
AT KAMBADURU, KALYANADRUG TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1478, Naḷa, Āshāḍha, śu. 12 corresponding to A.D. 1556 June
19, Friday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya. It records that
Virupana-Nāyaka, made a grant of varāhas 50, enjoyed as mānyagrāsa by the
Pārapatyagāra, to the temple of Mallikārjunadēva of Kambhadūru, for the merit
of his father Tammapa-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 665.
(A.R. No. 1 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE RANGANATHA TEMPLE
AT PAILABANDA, MADAKASIRA TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1478; Naḷa, Pushya, śu 8 corresponding to A.D. 1556 December
10, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya. It records
that, under the orders of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-Tirumalarājayyadēva-Mahā-
arasu, the Mahānāyakāchārya Timmaṇa-Nāyaka of Niḍugal, the Gauḍas and
Sēnabōvas of Niḍugalla-sīme, together with the merchants, made a gift of the village
of Tūmukuṇṭe in Niḍugal-rājya for the service of the god Tiruveṅgaḷanātha of
Peyalabaṇḍe in the presence of Gutti-Tirumalarājayyadēva and Keṁchappa-Nāyaka.
The gift was made for the merit of Gutti-Tirumalarājayyadēva; and the date on
which it was actually made was Pusyha, śu. 11.

l.ka: gaḷu 1428 neya naḷasaṁvatsarada puṣya śu 8 lu sva-
rasa tavara mūruvāgila tippagōṁḍa jājura cika-
gade beddalu saha sakala suvarnnadāya sakala

inscription.head:
No. 666.
(A.R. No. 270 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT MUTTIGI,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1479, Piṅgaḷa, Kārtika, śu. 11 Maṅgalavāra corresponding to
A.D. 1557 November 2 Tuesday, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya ruling at
Vidyānagarī. It records that the agent of the [Mahā]nāyakāchārya Kṛishṇapa-
Nāyaka fixed the rate of the tax on sheep payable by the cultivators of the village
Muttigi. The record is damaged. The writer was a Sēnabōva of Gobaḷiyabāvi and
the engraver Dēmōja.

inscription.head:
No. 667.
(A.R. No. 328 of 1925.)
ON ONE OF THE PILLARS IN THE TEMPLE OF CHENNAKESAVA IN THE DESERTED
FORT OF THE VILLAGE RAKATLA, RAYADRUG TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and incomplete. It is dated Śaka 1480, Piṅgala, Māgha, śu. 12
corresponding to A.D. 1558 January 31 Monday (not verifiable).

inscription.head:
No. 668.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1904.)
ON THE WESTERN BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE VITTHALASVAMI
TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1480, Kālayukti, Phālguṇa, śu. 15 corresponding to A.D. 1559
February 22, Wednesday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that Nammāḷuvār made an exchange of a garden land in his enjoyment
for another garden land, of specified boundaries, for meeting the expenses of the
festivals of the god Viṭhaladēva when taken in procession to the Parāṅkuśa-
maṇḍapa.

inscription.head:
No. 669.
(A.R. No. 705 of 1922.)
Same as A.R. No. 7 of 1904 above.

No. 670.
(A.R. No. 510 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB IN A FIELD TO THE NORTH OF THE VILLAGE CHATRADAHALLI,
HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1481, Siddhārthi, Jyēshṭha, ba. 7 corresponding to A.D. 1559
May 28 Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya. It records
that the Gauḍas and others of the village Chhatradahaḷḷi, which was a mānya of the
god Mālyavanta-Raghunāthadēva, made a gift of a field in the village for filling up
a charity water-trough with water by means of a charity water-lift. It records
also that the Upāṟas (named) of the village made a gift on the same date of a
fixed quantity of salt for the man lifting water from the well for the charity water-
trough. It further records that the iron-smiths of the village (named) agreed to
supply an iron bucket to bale out water for the charity water-trough. Certain
Gauḍas (named) of the village are stated to have made a gift of the toddy-palm
trees, in the area defined, to the person lifting water for the charity water-trough.

l.ka: ṣa 1481 ne śrīmatu sidhārthisaṁvacharada jēṣṭha ba 7 lū śrīmaṁ-
ge saluva chatradahaḷiya rāyapagauḍaru busaṇagauḍaru jeḍagaṇa-

nīrahoyivadake koṭa silāśāsanada dānaśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare nāu

śrīmatu kāḷakādēvi kamaṭēśvara dēvara varaprasādigaḷādaṁthā sōmala ·ē-

inscription.head:
No. 671.
(A.R. No. 543 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE KESAVASVAMI TEMPLE AT
BUDURU, ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1482, Raudri, Vaiśākha, śu. 15 corresponding to
A.D. 1560 May 9, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya
ruling at Vidyānagara. It records that Ādavāni-sīme was the amaramāgaṇi of
the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Veṅkaṭādrirāja-Ayya, that Būdūru in that district was the
amaramāgaṇi of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gobūri-Avubhalarājaya and that the latter
made a gift of some plots of land for the service of the god Ahōbala-Nārasiṁha
in the village.

inscription.head:
No. 672.
(A.R. No. 739 of 1919.)
ON A SLAB LYING NEAR THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT SULUVAYI,
ALUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1483, Durmati, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15 corresponding to
A.D. 1561 July 26, Saturday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya.
It records that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tirumalarāja-Rāmarājaya-Mahā-arasu made
a grant of a fixed sum of toll-revenue of two villages (names lost) for the service of
the god Kalināthadēva at Suruḷuvāyi.

l.ka: ya du(ṁ)rmmatisaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa śu 15 lu | śrīma(ṁ)nmahārā[jā]-
bhōva gauḍa • • • • seṭi paṭaṇasvāmi muṁtāda sama-
yo dēvarige saluvaṁtha mā(ṁ)nya nīrāraṁbha kāḍā-
śiyalli koṁda pāpakke hōharu maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 673.
(A.R. No. 385 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE ENTRANCE INTO THE
KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVAR, COONDAPUR TALUK,
SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1484, Dundubhi, Chaitra, śu. 10, corresponding
to A.D. 1562 March 15, Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahā-
rāya ruling at Vijayanagarī. It records that, when Sadāśivarāya-Nāyaka was governing
the Tuḷu-rājya under the king's orders and Kalleya-Yellappa-Voḍeya was ruling
over Bārakūru under the latter's command, Chituseṭṭi, son of Gaṇapaseṭṭi of
Basarūru, caused the construction of a maṭha and granted land for the service of
the god Umāmahēśvara in the maṭha, for feeding 6 persons daily and for the service
of the god Kōṭēśvara, in whose presence the gift was made.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 674.
(A.R. No. 397 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE TEMPLE OF SANKARANARAYANA
AT THE VILLAGE, SANKARANARAYANA, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1485, Dundubhi, Jyēshṭha, śu. [7], Ādivāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1562 May 10 Sunday, in the reign of Sadāśivarāya-Mahārāya. It records that
when the king granted as amaramāgaṇi, to Yeḍavamurāri, Kōṭekōlāhala, Paḍuvaṇa-
samudrādhīśvara Sadāśivarāya-Nāyaka of Keḷadi, son of Chavuḍa-Gauṇḍa of Keḷadi,
the districts of Āraga, Gutti, Tuḷurājya, and Bārakūru and Maṅgalūru, 56 gāvudas
in length, Sadāśiva-Nāyaka constructed a maṭha for Virūpākshadēva and having
purchased some land from Kupāra-Kēśava-Hebāruva made a gift of it for bhikshā
in the maṭha. The gift was made in the presence of the god Śaṅkara-Nārāyaṇa,
a sacred place in the Tuḷu-rājya, for the final beatitude of the parents of Sadāśiva-
Nāyaka viz., Chauḍapa and Tirumamma.

l.ka: nāyakarige ·āraga gutti muvattāṟu kaṁpaṇa tuḷurājya bā-
ru taṁma taṁde cauḍapagu taṁma tāyi tirumaṁmanava-
kaṭisi ·ā maṭake bhikṣa svastikupāra kēśavahebāru[va*]na kaya
ko[ṁ*]ḍa bhūmi krayaśāsanada vivarā || tatsaṁvatsarada jēṣṭa śu-
baṁdaṁtha mūlasarvasvada bhūmiyanā dēvarige krayamūlavāgi ko-
ṭṭenāgi yo bhūmiya gadeya vivarā | gaṁgādve kūḍaṇa hā-
ḷi saha bittuva bījavari nāghaṁḍugadallū mu 5 vottire naḍuvaṇa-

de bittuva bījavari nāghaṁḍugadalu mu 3 baṁkana gade bāḷeya hā-
ḷi saha bittuva bījavari nāghaṁḍugadallu muḍi 4 kēdageya gadde bitu va
bījavari nāghaṁḍugadalū mu 4 hastānaṁba gade paḍuva beṭṭasa-
ha bittuva bījavari nāghaṁḍugadallu mu 5 kiriyaṇahe-
gadeya keḷage hūvina maki nāghaṁḍugadalū mu 2 mallagare-
ya paḍuvalu diṁbadamakki mēlaṇa mallagade saha nāghaṁḍu-
gadalu mu 6 ·aṁttu bayalu mu 27 mallagade makkiyida mu
12 ·ubhayaṁ mu 36 kke catusīmeya vivarā mūḍalū harida
muḍalū baḍagalu teṁganavoḍeya bayala gaddeyiṁdalu vōḍe-
marana makiya vōṇiya gaḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalū yiṁtiya ca-
tusīmeyoḷage saluva bituva bījavari nāghaṁḍugadalu mu 39 ·a-
kṣaradalu muvattoṁbhattu muḍi gadde yī bhūmige saluva
gēṇi varuśa 1 kke savaḍada ·aṁgaḍiya koḷagada lekkadali nāgha-
kiya muḍi 319 śudha dēvarige salisuva guttige muḍi
yī śāsanadalli barada bhūmiya mūlacalitada kraya-
mūlakke niṁma kayya tegedukoṁḍadu bārakūra pariva-

grāmada ·appaṁḍadali teṟu birāḍavellava nānē tettu ba 4 mū-
ḍe 50 nu koṭṭubahenu yī bhūmiya parivokka[linikki] neṁ
• • bāḷa biḍuvenu yī bhūmiyoḷagaṇa phala nidhi nikṣēpa
hāḷa ·ā karttana suhastada voppa kuppāṟa vināyakada
yanaḷipidavaru mātru guru gō strī brahmāhatyaga-

inscription.head:
No. 675.
(A.R. No. 387 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TANK IN THE HARIHARA TEMPLE
AT KUMBHAKASI, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1485, Dundubhi, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 1, Gu[ruvāra], corresponding to
A.D. 1562 July 2 (Adhika-Śrāvaṇa), Thursday, in the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya.
It records that Kaleya Elapa-Voḍeya who was ruling over the province of Bārkūru-rājya
under the orders of Sadāśivarāya-Nāyaka of Keḷadi who had been appointed governor
of the Tuḷu-rājya by Rāmarāja-Veṅkaṭādrirāja-Mahā-arasu made tax-free those plots
of land which Mahābalaliṅga-sēnabōva granted to the chhatra in the maṭha attached
to the temples of Umāmahēśvara and Lakshmīnārāyaṇadēva in Kumbhakāśi. The
gift was made for the prosperity of the king, under the orders of Sadāśivarāya-
Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 676.
(A.R. No. 507 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT NAKKARAHALU,
HADAGALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1483, Dundubhi, Nija-Śrāvaṇa, śu. 10 corres-
ponding to A.D. 1562 August 9, Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśiva-
rāya. It records that Pedapa, son of Gājulla-Nāgayya and the Adhikāri of
Nagarehāḷ fixed the dasavandha payment on a canal at Nagarehāḷ and granted
mānya lands to Sēnabōva Timmarasa, the Gauḍa, Taḷāra and Jōyisa.

l.ka:
tiṁdu kaḷa kuḍidu gōva koṁda pāta
[ka]-

inscription.head:
No. 677.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1913.)
ON A STONE IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT YALPI-KAGGALLU,
BELLARY TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1485, Rudhirōdgāri, Chaitra, śu. 10, Śu[kravāra], corresponding
to A.D. 1563 April 2, Friday, f.d.t. ·36, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya who was
ruling at Vidyānagara. It records that Raghunātharājayya-Mahā-arasu, son of the
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja-Tirumalarājayadēva-Mahā-arasu, confirmed the grant
of a land made by him in the cyclic year Naḷa, Kārtika, śu. 12, corresponding to
A.D. 1556 November 12, for the service of the god Siddhēśvaradēva at Kēmāna-
Beḷugallu. The land granted was situated in Kaggallu on the bank of the river
Hagarī, in the Rāyadurga-sīme.

l.ka:

dēvamahā-
koṭa bhūdānada silāśāsanada vivara rāyadurgada sīmege saluva hagarī tīrada kagga[lla]

hasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi[ḥ ||*] maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī śrī |

inscription.head:
No. 678.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1904.)
ON THE NORTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE VITTHALASVAMI
TEMPLE AT HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1485, Rudhirōdgāri, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 2, corresponding to A.D.
1563 August 5, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya
who was ruling at Vidyānagara. It records that Nammāḷvārulu of the Viṭhala
temple made a gift of some lands to the treasury of god Viṭhaladēva for sending
to him, every day with music band, the offerings made to the god in the morning.

inscription.head:
No. 679.
(A.R. No. 706 of 1922.)
ON THE SOUTH BASE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and incomplete. It is dated Śaka 1485, Rudhi[rōdgāri] (= A.D.
1563-64), with no further details, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti śrījayābrudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruśaṁbalu 1485 ne rudhi
• • • śrīmadrā [jā*]dhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrāpratāpa śrīsadāsivadēva-
mahārāyaru pruthivīsāṁbrājyaṁgaiyyuta

inscription.head:
No. 680.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1910.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE ENTRANCE OF THE LAKSHMINARAYANA
TEMPLE AT KOLLEGAL, KOLLEGAL TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1491, Śukla, Pushya, śu. 10 corresponding to
A.D. 1569 December 18, Sunday (not verifiable), in the reign of Sadāśiva-Mahārāya. It
records that Rāmarājayadēva-Mahā-arasu, son of the Mahāmaṇḍalēsvara Rāmarāja-
Tirumalarājayadēva, made a gift of the village Koḷogāla in Śivasamudra-sthala,
a sub-division of Hadināḍa-sīme, as pallakki-umbaḷi to Rāmarāja-Nāyaka, the cheif
of Hadināḍa-sīme.

l.ka: ra komāra rāmarājayadēvamahāarasugaḷu hadināḍa sīmeya prabhu
ḍa sīmeya śivasamudrada staḷada koḷogālada staḷavanu ninage pallakiya ·uṁ-
baḷiyāgi koṭṭevāgi ·ā staḷake saluva haḷehiriyūra catussīmeyoḷa[gaṇa]
gade bedalu suṁka suvarnādāya tōṭa modalāda sakala svāmyavanu pū-

inscription.head:
No. 681.
(A.R. No. 408 of 1920.)
ON A STONE IN FRONT OF THE CHENNAKESAVA TEMPLE AT CHYABALA,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription whose date is lost refers itself to the reign of
Sadāśivarāya. It records that, not capable of bearing the enhanced and
numerous taxes, the people of the village Chebale had deserted it and that Daḷavāayi
Appala-Nāyaka, the agent to Koṇḍayadēva-Mahā-arasu, to whom the district
containing the village was given as amaramāgaṇi; invited the people and persuaded
them to resettle in the village giving them some concessions. They were that they
need not pay for three years to come sēnabōva-vartane, rāyasa-vartane, fees to royal
servants, kāṇike, kaḍāya, cess on marriage and other domestic festivals, free work for
the service of the god Henneyadēva and other imposts; that after three years such
taxes as were current elsewhere would be justly levied; and that the rate of rent
payable to the palace would be according to the quality of the soil.

inscription.head:
No. 682.
(A.R. No. 464 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD AT NANDIHALLI, HADAGALLI TALUK,
BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is not dated. It records that Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya made a gift of the
village Voḷalagundi-Bhairāpura, surnamed Gōpināthapura, for the service of the gods
Bandēśvaradēva and Gōpināthadēva in the agrahāra Timmalāpura, set up by
Bayikāṟa-Rāmapaya in the temples which the latter had caused to be constructed.
A copper-plate charter recording this gift is also stated to have been granted.

inscription.head:
No. 683.
(A.R. No. 409 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE OUTER PRAKARA OF THE MAHALINGESVARA TEMPLE
AT BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and the date and the king's name are lost. It mentions
Sadāśivarāya-Nāyaka and Bārakūra-rājya and records that Timmaṇṇaseṭṭi and his
elder brother Chemmaṇa-Heggaḍe lent 350 varāhas, of which they had made a gift
for the worship of the god Mahādēva at Paḍuvakēri, to the residents of Ānegaḷi
village. The interest on the amount was payable to the temple of Mahādēva for
conducting the worship of the god.

l.ka: varige dharmake hākida varaha ga 300 tiṁmaṇaseṭi [taṁma] dharmake ko-
ṭṭa varaha ga 50 ·aṁtu varaha ga 35[0] ·akṣaradalū yive mu-

gi koṭa varaha ga 350 yī hoṁnige baḍḍi prati varuṣa 1 kke do-
ḍa varaha ga 35 varahana niṁma hoṁna baḍḍige hēḷi ko-
ṭṭadake naḍava dhar(ṁ)ma niṁna ·aṁṇa beṁmaṇahegaḍe dharmmake ko-
ṭa varaha ga 300 ke ·adara baḍi varaha ga 30 naḍava dharmma kṣa-

dēvara ·amrutapaḍi dina 1 ke ·aki hā 1 ra lekadalū kālakā-

darū halaru seṭṭigāraru pereyiṁda hoṁna koṭu
yo dharma ·uḷiyada hāge naḍasikoḍuvarū yo dha-
dalū kāśiyali sāvira kavileya koṁda dōśake hōharu
gi māḍisi koṭanu ·a dharma tiṁmaṇaseṭiyadu [||*] dānapālana-

sēnabō[va]ra maga nāgayyasēnabō[va]na baraha kālakālada kārtika śu
1 ·arabhya ·a śu 14 varege dina hadināku dinada rātriyalli naḍa-

bbaradu salladū maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 684.
(A.R. No. 391 of 1920.)
ON A STONE NEAR A WELL TO THE NORTH OF THE VILLAGE KADADARABENCHI,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1500, Īśvara, Jyēshṭha, ba. 12 corresponding
to A.D. 1577 June 13, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Śrīraṅgarāyadēva-
Mahārāya. It records that the king made a grant of a village (name lost) in Ādavani-
durga to Haṇḍeya-Hanumanāyaka-Ayya for the repairs of a tank.

inscription.head:
No. 685.
(A.R. No. 318 of 1925.)
ON A STONE LYING BEFORE THE KARNAM'S HOUSE AT KOTTUR
KUDLIGI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1502, Vikrama, Mārgaśira, ba. 10, corresponding to A.D. 1580
December 1, Thursday (not verifiable), in the reign of Timmarājaya-Mahā-arasu, son
of Rāmarājayyadēva-Mahā-arasu ruling from Ānegondi. It records that Vādi-Nāyaka,
son of Māchi-Nāyaka of Bāguḷi, agent to the king, exempted the shepherds of Koṭṭūra-
sīme from tax on their sheep. The gift was made in the presence of the god
Kallinātha of Bāguḷi, for the merit of his parents. Koṭṭūra-sīme is stated to have
been situated in Kōgaḷi-vēṇṭhe, a sub-division of Pāṇḍya-nāḍu in Hastināvati-vaḷita.

l.ka:
gaḷu 1502 neya vikramasaṁvatsarada mārgaśira ba 10 llu śrīma(ṁ)nmahārājādhirāja
rājapa-

ramēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa rāmarājayyadēvamahāarasugaḷa komāra tiṁmarājayamahā-
arasugaḷu ·āneyagoṁ-

diya paṭaṇadalu ratnasiṁhāsanārūḍharāgi [pru]thvīrājyaṁgeyyuttiruva kāladalli ·ā tiṁma-
rāja-

yyadēvama[hā*]arsarige kāryyakarttarāda śrīma(ṁ)nmahānāyakācāryyabhāṣetappu-
vanāyakara gaṁḍa maṟehokkare kāva

māṟāṁtaraṁ koluva bāguḷiya śrīmācināyakara makaḷu vādināyaka[nava]ru koṭṭūra
sīmeya kuṟu[ba]-
maga cikkaṇagauḍa guravagauṁḍa yivaru muṁtāda koṭūra sīmeya [sa]mastakuṟuba pra-

jegaḷige koṭṭa dharmaśāsanada paṭṭeya kramaveṁteṁdare nāu ·ādavani • • hastināvati-

ḍyanāḍa kō[gaḷi]ya vēṁṭheyake saluva koṭūra sīmeyoḷagaṇa • • • • grāma-
gaḷoḷage yihaṁthā kuṟu-
• • • • • • • • • • pakṣada samaya prasaṁgagaḷu baṁdu mahāparvada pu(ṁ)-

mātāpi-

pautrapā-

naḍa-

naṁ |

inscription.head:
No. 686.
(A.R. No. 732 of 1916.)
ON A STONE FIXED INTO THE FLOOR OF THE TANK-BUND AT HARASAMUDRAM,
MADAKSIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and fragmentary. It is dated Śaka 1505, Svabhānu, Mārgaśira,
ba. 30, Budhavāra, Sūryōparāga, corresponding to A.D. 1583 December 4, Wednesday,
in the reign of Śrīraṅgarāyadēva-Mahārāya ruling at Penugoṇḍe.

l.note: The stone is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 687.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1917.)
ON THE SECOND FACE OF THE PILLAR SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE
RANGANATHA TEMPLE AT PAILABANDA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the cyclic year Svabhānu, Māgha, śu. 1, corresponding
probably to A.D. 1584 January 4, Saturday (not verifiable). It records that Immaḍi-
Timmaṇa-Nāyaka of Niḍugal gave the kāṇāchi of the tank, Narasāmbudhikuṇṭe, to
Samani-Pāpaya under the orders of the god Tiruvēṁgaḷanātha of Pēyalakoṇḍe for
carrying a chappara every year to Tirumale.

inscription.head:
No. 688.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE SOUTH WALL OF THE RANGASVAMI TEMPLE AT
SRIRANGAPURAM, KALYANADRUG TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 15[05], Svabhānu, Māgha, śu. 7, Guruvāra, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1584 January 9, Thursday, in the reign of Śrīraṅgadēva-Mahārāya
ruling at Penugoṇḍe. It refers to the report made by the learned men of Eṟaguḍi
to the east of Hagare in Vēparāla-vēṇṭhe, a sub-division of Rāyadurga-rājya to the king
Śrīraṅgarāya about the gift made to them of the village Śrīraṅgasamudra during the
reign of king Kṛishṇarāya by Śrīraṅgayya, son of Tirumalanātha of Chaturtha-gōtra,
who had also constructed for their use the temple of Śrīraṅganātha, a tank and
a canal connecting the tank with a river for the supply of water and provided for the
worship of the god by granting lands to the temple; and that these had been ruined
by the Turukas. The rest of the record is lost.

l.ka:
·avighnamastu | śrīgaṇādhipatāē śrīsaraśvatithāē [na]maḥ śrīraṁganāthanē gati śrīkāḷaṁmā
viśvanāthā[ya] namaḥ | śrīnīlakaṁṭāē namaḥ [||*]

svasti śrījayābhyudayaśālivāhanaśakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 15[05] neyā śvabhānu[saṁva]tsara
[māga] śu 7 guruvāradalu śrīmadrājādhirāja paramēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa vīra (śrīraṁga)

śrīraṁgadēvamahārāyaru penugoṁḍe paṭṭaṇadali vajrasiṁhāsanārūḍharāgi pruthvīrā-
jyaṁgaiutiralu rāyadurgadarājyada vēparāla vēṁṭeyada hagerege mūḍagaḍeya ·eṟagu-
ḍige pratināma

śrī • • • • puraveṁba ·agrahārada kēśavasōmayadigaḷa tirumalabhaṭṭaru muṁ-
tāda stitāśēṣavidvā(ṁ)nmahājanaṁgaḷu caturdaśabhuvanādhīśvaranāda śrīraṁganātha-
dēvara divyaśrīpādapadmaṁgaḷige sāṣṭāṁga nama-

skāravaṁ māḍi māḍuva biṁnahau prāku vijayanagarada siṁhāsanadali ·aṣṭhadikurāyama-
nōbhayaṁkaranāda kriṣṇadēvamahārāyara kayyali caturthagōtrada vidvachyēbya-
rāyaraṁjakada tirumalanātana makkaḷu śrīraṁ-

gayyanu namage śrīraṁgasamudravanu ·agrahārava māḍisi taṁma kulasvāmi ·ādaṁtā śrī-
raṁganāthage pu(ṁ)ṇyavāgabēkeṁdu kere kaṭṭisi ·ākerege hoḷekāluvenagesi śrīraṁganātha-
dēvara guḍiya kaṭṭisi svāmiya prathiṣṭhemāḍi ·ā svāmige kerekeḷa-

ge hattukoḷaga kāluve keḷage gadde hattukoḷaga beḷimarada staḷadali koṭṭa gade vokaḷa yare
ikaḷake nela 0 || kaḷa śrīraṁgasamudrada staḷada ceṁnāyaka haṁje grāmadali vritti ·aṁśa
dēvarige pālukoṁḍu yidanu samarpaṇeya māḍalāgi ·āyūra cepuḍigeya ho • •
